modules.label: aerospike_migrations
modules.tooltip: Check or wait for migrations between nodes

fields.label: connect timeout
fields.description: How long to try to connect before giving up (milliseconds).

fields.label: consecutive good checks
fields.description: How many times should the cluster report "no migrations" consecutively before returning OK back to ansible?

fields.label: fail on cluster change
fields.description: Fail if the cluster key changes if something else is changing the cluster, we may want to fail.

fields.label: local only
fields.description: Do you wish to only check for migrations on the local node before returning, or do you want all nodes in the cluster to finish before returning?

fields.label: migrate rx key
fields.description: The metric key used to determine if we have rx migrations remaining. Changeable due to backwards compatibility.

fields.label: migrate tx key
fields.description: The metric key used to determine if we have tx migrations remaining. Changeable due to backwards compatibility.

fields.label: min cluster size
fields.description: Check fails until cluster size is met or until tries is exhausted.

fields.label: sleep between checks
fields.description: How long to sleep between each check (seconds).

fields.label: target cluster size
fields.description: When all aerospike builds in the cluster are greater than version 4.3, then the C(cluster-stable) info command is used. Inside this command, you can optionally specify what the target cluster size is - but it is not necessary. You can still rely on O(min_cluster_size) if you do not want to use this option. If this option is specified on a cluster that has at least one host <4.3 then it is ignored until the min version reaches 4.3.

fields.label: tries limit
fields.description: How many times do we poll before giving up and failing?

fields.label: environment
fields.description: The airbrake environment name, typically v(production), V(staging), and so on.

fields.label: project id
fields.description: Airbrake PROJECT_ID.

fields.label: project key
fields.description: Airbrake PROJECT_KEY.

fields.label: repo
fields.description: URL of the project repository.

fields.label: revision
fields.description: A hash, number, tag, or other identifier showing what revision from version control was deployed.

fields.label: url
fields.description: Optional URL to submit the notification to. Use to send notifications to Airbrake-compliant tools like Errbit.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The username of the person doing the deployment.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates for the target URL is not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: version
fields.description: A string identifying what version was deployed.

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: A list of device attributes.

fields.label: device
fields.description: The name of the device. V(all) is valid to rescan C(available) all devices (AIX C(cfgmgr) command).

fields.label: force
fields.description: Forces action.

fields.label: recursive
fields.description: Removes or defines a device and children devices.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Controls the device state. V(available) (alias V(present)) rescan a specific device or all devices (when O(device) is not specified). V(removed) (alias V(absent) removes a device. V(defined) changes device to Defined state.

modules.label: aix_filesystem
modules.tooltip: Configure LVM and NFS file systems for AIX

fields.label: account subsystem
fields.description: Specifies whether the file system is to be processed by the accounting subsystem.

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: Specifies attributes for files system separated by comma.

fields.label: auto mount
fields.description: File system is automatically mounted at system restart.

fields.label: device
fields.description: Logical volume (LV) device name or remote export device to create a NFS file system. It is used to create a file system on an already existing logical volume or the exported NFS file system. If not mentioned a new logical volume name is created following AIX standards (LVM).

fields.label: filesystem
fields.description: Specifies the mount point, which is the directory where the file system will be mounted.

fields.label: fs type
fields.description: Specifies the virtual file system type.

fields.label: mount group
fields.description: Specifies the mount group.

fields.label: nfs server
fields.description: Specifies a Network File System (NFS) server.

fields.label: permissions
fields.description: Set file system permissions. V(rw) (read-write) or V(ro) (read-only).

fields.label: rm mount point
fields.description: Removes the mount point directory when used with state V(absent).

fields.label: size
fields.description: Specifies the file system size. For already present it resizes the filesystem. 512-byte blocks, megabytes or gigabytes. If the value has M specified it is in megabytes. If the value has G specified it is in gigabytes. If no M or G the value is 512-byte blocks. If V(+) is specified in begin of value, the value is added. If V(-) is specified in begin of value, the value is removed. If neither V(+) nor V(-) is specified, then the total value is the specified. Size respects the LVM AIX standards.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Controls the file system state. V(present) check if file system exists, creates or resize. V(absent) removes existing file system if already V(unmounted). V(mounted) checks if the file system is mounted or mount the file system. V(unmounted) check if the file system is unmounted or unmount the file system.

fields.label: vg
fields.description: Specifies an existing volume group (VG).

fields.label: action
fields.description: Action the C(init) process performs for this entry.

fields.label: command
fields.description: Command to be executed.

fields.label: insertafter
fields.description: After which C(inittab) line should the new entry inserted.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the C(inittab) entry.

fields.label: runlevel
fields.description: Runlevel of the entry.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the entry should be present or absent in the C(inittab) file.

modules.label: aix_lvg
modules.tooltip: Manage LVM volume groups on AIX

fields.label: force
fields.description: Force volume group creation.

fields.label: pp size
fields.description: The size of the physical partition in megabytes.

fields.label: pvs
fields.description: List of comma-separated devices to use as physical devices in this volume group. Required when creating or extending (V(present) state) the volume group. If not informed reducing (V(absent) state) the volume group is removed.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Control if the volume group exists and volume group AIX state varyonvg V(varyon) or varyoffvg V(varyoff).

fields.label: vg
fields.description: The name of the volume group.

fields.label: vg type
fields.description: The type of the volume group.

fields.label: copies
fields.description: The number of copies of the logical volume. Maximum copies are 3.

fields.label: lv
fields.description: The name of the logical volume.

fields.label: lv type
fields.description: The type of the logical volume.

fields.label: opts
fields.description: Free-form options to be passed to the mklv command.

fields.label: policy
fields.description: Sets the interphysical volume allocation policy. V(maximum) allocates logical partitions across the maximum number of physical volumes. V(minimum) allocates logical partitions across the minimum number of physical volumes.

fields.label: pvs
fields.description: A list of physical volumes, for example V([hdisk1, hdisk2]).

fields.label: size
fields.description: The size of the logical volume with one of the [MGT] units.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Control if the logical volume exists. If V(present) and the volume does not already exist then the O(size) option is required.

fields.label: vg
fields.description: The volume group this logical volume is part of.

fields.label: alerta url
fields.description: The Alerta API endpoint.

fields.label: api key
fields.description: The access token for the API.

fields.label: api password
fields.description: The password for the API using basic auth.

fields.label: api username
fields.description: The username for the API using basic auth.

fields.label: customer
fields.description: Name of the customer.

fields.label: match
fields.description: The matching logged in user for the customer.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the customer should exist or not. Both O(customer) and O(match) identify a customer that should be added or removed.

modules.label: ali_instance
modules.tooltip: Create, Start, Stop, Restart or Terminate an Instance in ECS; Add or Remove Instance to/from a Security Group

fields.label: alicloud access key
fields.description: Alibaba Cloud access key. If not set then the value of environment variable E(ALICLOUD_ACCESS_KEY), E(ALICLOUD_ACCESS_KEY_ID) is used instead.

fields.label: alicloud assume role
fields.description: If provided with a role ARN, Ansible attempts to assume this role using the supplied credentials. The nested assume_role block supports C(alicloud_assume_role_arn), C(alicloud_assume_role_session_name), C(alicloud_assume_role_session_expiration) and C(alicloud_assume_role_policy).

fields.label: alicloud assume role arn
fields.description: The Alibaba Cloud C(role_arn). The ARN of the role to assume. If ARN is set to an empty string, it does not perform role switching. It supports environment variable E(ALICLOUD_ASSUME_ROLE_ARN). Ansible executes with provided credentials.

fields.label: alicloud assume role session expiration
fields.description: The Alibaba Cloud C(session_expiration). The time after which the established session for assuming role expires. Valid value range 900-3600 seconds. Default to 3600 (in this case Alicloud use own default value). It supports environment variable E(ALICLOUD_ASSUME_ROLE_SESSION_EXPIRATION).

fields.label: alicloud assume role session name
fields.description: The Alibaba Cloud session_name. The session name to use when assuming the role. If omitted, 'ansible' is passed to the AssumeRole call as session name. It supports environment variable E(ALICLOUD_ASSUME_ROLE_SESSION_NAME).

fields.label: alicloud region
fields.description: The Alibaba Cloud region to use. If not specified then the value of environment variable E(ALICLOUD_REGION), E(ALICLOUD_REGION_ID) is used instead.

fields.label: alicloud secret key
fields.description: Alibaba Cloud secret key. If not set then the value of environment variable E(ALICLOUD_SECRET_KEY), E(ALICLOUD_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) is used instead.

fields.label: alicloud security token
fields.description: The Alibaba Cloud security token. If not specified then the value of environment variable E(ALICLOUD_SECURITY_TOKEN) is used instead.

fields.label: allocate public ip
fields.description: Whether allocate a public IP for the new instance.

fields.label: auto renew
fields.description: Whether automate renew the charge of the instance.

fields.label: auto renew period
fields.description: The duration of the automatic renew the charge of the instance. Required when O(auto_renew=true).

fields.label: availability zone
fields.description: Aliyun availability zone ID in which to launch the instance. If it is not specified, it is allocated by system automatically.

fields.label: count
fields.description: The number of the new instance. An integer value which indicates how many instances that match O(count_tag) should be running. Instances are either created or terminated based on this value.

fields.label: count tag
fields.description: O(count) determines how many instances based on a specific tag criteria should be present. This can be expressed in multiple ways and is shown in the EXAMPLES section. The specified count_tag must already exist or be passed in as the O(tags) option. If it is not specified, it is replaced by O(instance_name).

fields.label: description
fields.description: The description of ECS instance, which is a string of 2 to 256 characters. It cannot begin with V(http://) or V(https://).

fields.label: dry run
fields.description: Specifies whether to send a dry-run request. If O(dry_run=true), Only a dry-run request is sent and no instance is created. The system checks whether the required parameters are set, and validates the request format, service permissions, and available ECS instances. If the validation fails, the corresponding error code is returned. If the validation succeeds, the DryRunOperation error code is returned. If O(dry_run=false), a request is sent. If the validation succeeds, the instance is created.

fields.label: ecs role name
fields.description: The RAM Role Name attached on a ECS instance for API operations. You can retrieve this from the 'Access Control' section of the Alibaba Cloud console. If you are running Ansible from an ECS instance with RAM Instance using RAM Role, Ansible just accesses the metadata U(http://100.100.100.200/latest/meta-data/ram/security-credentials/<ecs_role_name>) to obtain the STS credential. This is a preferred approach over any other when running in ECS as you can avoid hard coding credentials. Instead these are leased on-the-fly by Ansible which reduces the chance of leakage.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Whether the current operation needs to be execute forcibly.

fields.label: host name
fields.description: Instance host name. Ordered hostname is not supported.

fields.label: image id
fields.description: Image ID used to launch instances. Required when O(state=present) and creating new ECS instances.

fields.label: include data disks
fields.description: Whether to change instance disks charge type when changing instance charge type.

fields.label: instance charge type
fields.description: The charge type of the instance.

fields.label: instance ids
fields.description: A list of instance IDs. It is required when need to operate existing instances. If it is specified, O(count) is ignored.

fields.label: instance name
fields.description: The name of ECS instance, which is a string of 2 to 128 Chinese or English characters. It must begin with an uppercase/lowercase letter or a Chinese character and can contain numerals, V(.), V(_) or V(-). It cannot begin with V(http://) or V(https://).

fields.label: instance type
fields.description: Instance type used to launch instances. Required when O(state=present) and creating new ECS instances.

fields.label: internet charge type
fields.description: Internet charge type of ECS instance.

fields.label: key name
fields.description: The name of key pair which is used to access ECS instance in SSH.

fields.label: max bandwidth in
fields.description: Maximum incoming bandwidth from the public network, measured in Mbps (Megabits per second).

fields.label: max bandwidth out
fields.description: Maximum outgoing bandwidth to the public network, measured in Mbps (Megabits per second). Required when O(allocate_public_ip=true). Ignored when O(allocate_public_ip=false).

fields.label: period
fields.description: The charge duration of the instance, in months. Required when O(instance_charge_type=PrePaid). The valid value are [V(1-9), V(12), V(24), V(36)].

fields.label: period unit
fields.description: The duration unit that you are buying the resource. It is valid when O(instance_charge_type=PrePaid).

fields.label: profile
fields.description: This is the Alicloud profile name as set in the shared credentials file. It can also be sourced from the E(ALICLOUD_PROFILE) environment variable.

fields.label: purge tags
fields.description: Delete any tags not specified in the task that are on the instance. If V(true), it means you have to specify all the desired tags on each task affecting an instance.

fields.label: ram role name
fields.description: The name of the instance RAM role.

fields.label: security groups
fields.description: A list of security group IDs.

fields.label: shared credentials file
fields.description: This is the path to the shared credentials file. It can also be sourced from the E(ALICLOUD_SHARED_CREDENTIALS_FILE) environment variable. If this is not set and a profile is specified, C(~/.aliyun/config.json) is used.

fields.label: spot price limit
fields.description: The maximum hourly price for the preemptible instance. This parameter supports a maximum of three decimal places and takes effect when the SpotStrategy parameter is set to SpotWithPriceLimit.

fields.label: spot strategy
fields.description: The bidding mode of the pay-as-you-go instance. This parameter is valid when O(instance_charge_type=PostPaid).

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of the instance after operating.

fields.label: system disk category
fields.description: Category of the system disk.

fields.label: system disk description
fields.description: Description of the system disk.

fields.label: system disk name
fields.description: Name of the system disk.

fields.label: system disk size
fields.description: Size of the system disk, in GB. The valid values are V(40)~V(500).

fields.label: tags
fields.description: A hash/dictionaries of instance tags, to add to the new instance or for starting/stopping instance by tag. V({"key":"value"}).

fields.label: unique suffix
fields.description: Specifies whether to add sequential suffixes to the host_name. The sequential suffix ranges from 001 to 999.

fields.label: user data
fields.description: User-defined data to customize the startup behaviors of an ECS instance and to pass data into an ECS instance. It only takes effect when launching the new ECS instances.

fields.label: vswitch id
fields.description: The subnet ID in which to launch the instances (VPC).

modules.label: ali_instance_info
modules.tooltip: Gather information on instances of Alibaba Cloud ECS

fields.label: alicloud access key
fields.description: Alibaba Cloud access key. If not set then the value of environment variable E(ALICLOUD_ACCESS_KEY), E(ALICLOUD_ACCESS_KEY_ID) is used instead.

fields.label: alicloud assume role
fields.description: If provided with a role ARN, Ansible attempts to assume this role using the supplied credentials. The nested assume_role block supports C(alicloud_assume_role_arn), C(alicloud_assume_role_session_name), C(alicloud_assume_role_session_expiration) and C(alicloud_assume_role_policy).

fields.label: alicloud assume role arn
fields.description: The Alibaba Cloud C(role_arn). The ARN of the role to assume. If ARN is set to an empty string, it does not perform role switching. It supports environment variable E(ALICLOUD_ASSUME_ROLE_ARN). Ansible executes with provided credentials.

fields.label: alicloud assume role session expiration
fields.description: The Alibaba Cloud C(session_expiration). The time after which the established session for assuming role expires. Valid value range 900-3600 seconds. Default to 3600 (in this case Alicloud use own default value). It supports environment variable E(ALICLOUD_ASSUME_ROLE_SESSION_EXPIRATION).

fields.label: alicloud assume role session name
fields.description: The Alibaba Cloud session_name. The session name to use when assuming the role. If omitted, 'ansible' is passed to the AssumeRole call as session name. It supports environment variable E(ALICLOUD_ASSUME_ROLE_SESSION_NAME).

fields.label: alicloud region
fields.description: The Alibaba Cloud region to use. If not specified then the value of environment variable E(ALICLOUD_REGION), E(ALICLOUD_REGION_ID) is used instead.

fields.label: alicloud secret key
fields.description: Alibaba Cloud secret key. If not set then the value of environment variable E(ALICLOUD_SECRET_KEY), E(ALICLOUD_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) is used instead.

fields.label: alicloud security token
fields.description: The Alibaba Cloud security token. If not specified then the value of environment variable E(ALICLOUD_SECURITY_TOKEN) is used instead.

fields.label: ecs role name
fields.description: The RAM Role Name attached on a ECS instance for API operations. You can retrieve this from the 'Access Control' section of the Alibaba Cloud console. If you are running Ansible from an ECS instance with RAM Instance using RAM Role, Ansible just accesses the metadata U(http://100.100.100.200/latest/meta-data/ram/security-credentials/<ecs_role_name>) to obtain the STS credential. This is a preferred approach over any other when running in ECS as you can avoid hard coding credentials. Instead these are leased on-the-fly by Ansible which reduces the chance of leakage.

fields.label: filters
fields.description: A dict of filters to apply. Each dict item consists of a filter key and a filter value. The filter keys can be all of request parameters. See U(https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/25506.htm) for parameter details. Filter keys can be same as request parameter name or be lower case and use underscore (V("_")) or dash (V("-")) to connect different words in one parameter. C(InstanceIds) should be a list. C(Tag.n.Key) and C(Tag.n.Value) should be a dict and using O(tags) instead.

fields.label: name prefix
fields.description: Use a instance name prefix to filter ECS instances.

fields.label: profile
fields.description: This is the Alicloud profile name as set in the shared credentials file. It can also be sourced from the E(ALICLOUD_PROFILE) environment variable.

fields.label: shared credentials file
fields.description: This is the path to the shared credentials file. It can also be sourced from the E(ALICLOUD_SHARED_CREDENTIALS_FILE) environment variable. If this is not set and a profile is specified, C(~/.aliyun/config.json) is used.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: A hash/dictionaries of instance tags. C({"key":"value"}).

modules.label: alternatives
modules.tooltip: Manages alternative programs for common commands

fields.label: family
fields.description: The family groups similar alternatives. This option is available only on RHEL-based distributions.

fields.label: link
fields.description: The path to the symbolic link that should point to the real executable. This option is always required on RHEL-based distributions. On Debian-based distributions this option is required when the alternative O(name) is unknown to the system.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The generic name of the link.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The path to the real executable that the link should point to.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: The priority of the alternative. If no priority is given for creation V(50) is used as a fallback.

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(present) - install the alternative (if not already installed), but do not set it as the currently selected alternative for the group. V(selected) - install the alternative (if not already installed), and set it as the currently selected alternative for the group. V(auto) - install the alternative (if not already installed), and set the group to auto mode. Added in community.general 5.1.0. V(absent) - removes the alternative. Added in community.general 5.1.0.

fields.label: accept licenses
fields.description: If this is set to V(true), the module attempts to accept license prompts generated by C(sdkmanager) during package installation. Otherwise, every license prompt is rejected.

fields.label: channel
fields.description: Indicates what channel must C(sdkmanager) use for installation of packages.

fields.label: name
fields.description: A name of an Android SDK package (for instance, V(build-tools;34.0.0)).

fields.label: sdk root
fields.description: Provides path for an alternative directory to install Android SDK packages to. By default, all packages are installed to the directory where C(sdkmanager) is installed.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicates the desired package(s) state. V(present) ensures that package(s) is/are present. V(absent) ensures that package(s) is/are absent. V(latest) ensures that package(s) is/are installed and updated to the latest version(s).

modules.label: ansible_galaxy_install
modules.tooltip: Install Ansible roles or collections using ansible-galaxy

fields.label: dest
fields.description: The path to the directory containing your collections or roles, according to the value of O(type). Please notice that C(ansible-galaxy) does not install collections with O(type=both), when O(requirements_file) contains both roles and collections and O(dest) is specified.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: Path to the C(ansible-galaxy) executable. When not specified, the module uses C(ansible-galaxy) found by Ansible.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Force overwriting existing roles and/or collections. It can be used for upgrading, but the module output always reports RV(ignore:changed=true). Using O(force=true) is mandatory when downgrading.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the collection or role being installed. Versions can be specified with C(ansible-galaxy) usual formats. For example, the collection V(community.docker:1.6.1) or the role V(ansistrano.deploy,3.8.0). O(name) and O(requirements_file) are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: no deps
fields.description: Refrain from installing dependencies.

fields.label: requirements file
fields.description: Path to a file containing a list of requirements to be installed. It works for O(type) equals to V(collection) and V(role). O(name) and O(requirements_file) are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: state
fields.description: If O(state=present) then the collection or role is installed. Note that the collections and roles are not updated with this option. Currently the O(state=latest) is ignored unless O(type=collection), and it ensures the collection is installed and updated to the latest available version. Please note that O(force=true) can be used to perform upgrade regardless of O(type).

fields.label: type
fields.description: The type of installation performed by C(ansible-galaxy). If O(type=both), then O(requirements_file) must be passed and it may contain both roles and collections. Note however that the opposite is not true: if using a O(requirements_file), then O(type) can be any of the three choices.

modules.label: apache2_mod_proxy
modules.tooltip: Set and/or get members' attributes of an Apache httpd 2.4 mod_proxy balancer pool

fields.label: balancer url suffix
fields.description: Suffix of the balancer pool URL required to access the balancer pool status page (for example V(balancer_vhost[:port]/balancer_url_suffix)).

fields.label: balancer vhost
fields.description: (IPv4|IPv6|FQDN):port of the Apache httpd 2.4 mod_proxy balancer pool.

fields.label: member host
fields.description: (IPv4|IPv6|FQDN) of the balancer member to get or to set attributes to. Port number is autodetected and should not be specified here. If undefined, the M(community.general.apache2_mod_proxy) module returns a members list of dictionaries of all the current balancer pool members' attributes.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of the member host. States can be simultaneously invoked by separating them with a comma (for example V(state=drained,ignore_errors)), but it is recommended to specify them as a proper YAML list. States V(present) and V(absent) must be used without any other state.

fields.label: tls
fields.description: Use https to access balancer management page.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Validate ssl/tls certificates.

modules.label: apache2_module
modules.tooltip: Enables/disables a module of the Apache2 webserver

fields.label: force
fields.description: Force disabling of default modules and override Debian warnings.

fields.label: identifier
fields.description: Identifier of the module as listed by C(apache2ctl -M). This is optional and usually determined automatically by the common convention of appending V(_module) to O(name) as well as custom exception for popular modules.

fields.label: ignore configcheck
fields.description: Ignore configuration checks about inconsistent module configuration. Especially for mpm_* modules.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the module to enable/disable as given to C(a2enmod)/C(a2dismod).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of the module.

fields.label: warn mpm absent
fields.description: Control the behavior of the warning process for MPM modules.

fields.label: available
fields.description: During upgrade, reset versioned world dependencies and change logic to prefer replacing or downgrading packages (instead of holding them) if the currently installed package is no longer available from any repository.

fields.label: name
fields.description: A package name, like V(foo), or multiple packages, like V(foo,bar). Do not include additional whitespace when specifying multiple packages as a string. Prefer YAML lists over comma-separating multiple package names.

fields.label: no cache
fields.description: Do not use any local cache path.

fields.label: repository
fields.description: A package repository or multiple repositories. Unlike with the underlying apk command, this list overrides the system repositories rather than supplement them.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicates the desired package(s) state. V(present) ensures the package(s) is/are present. V(installed) can be used as an alias. V(absent) ensures the package(s) is/are absent. V(removed) can be used as an alias. V(latest) ensures the package(s) is/are present and the latest version(s).

fields.label: update cache
fields.description: Update repository indexes. Can be run with other steps or on its own.

fields.label: upgrade
fields.description: Upgrade all installed packages to their latest version.

fields.label: world
fields.description: Use a custom world file when checking for explicitly installed packages. The file is used only when a value is provided for O(name), and O(state) is set to V(present) or V(latest).

fields.label: remove others
fields.description: Remove other then added repositories. Used if O(state=present).

fields.label: repo
fields.description: Name of the repository to add or remove.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicates the desired repository state.

fields.label: update
fields.description: Update the package database after changing repositories.

fields.label: clean
fields.description: Run the equivalent of C(apt-get clean) to clear out the local repository of retrieved package files. It removes everything but the lock file from C(/var/cache/apt/archives/) and C(/var/cache/apt/archives/partial/). Can be run as part of the package installation (clean runs before install) or as a separate step.

fields.label: dist upgrade
fields.description: If true performs an C(apt-get dist-upgrade) to upgrade system.

fields.label: package
fields.description: List of packages to install, upgrade, or remove. Since community.general 8.0.0, may include paths to local C(.rpm) files if O(state=installed) or O(state=present), requires C(rpm) Python module.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicates the desired package state. The states V(latest) and V(present_not_latest) have been added in community.general 8.6.0. Please note before community.general 11.0.0, V(present) and V(installed) were equivalent to V(latest). This changed in community.general 11.0.0. Now they are equivalent to V(present_not_latest).

fields.label: update cache
fields.description: Run the equivalent of C(apt-get update) before the operation. Can be run as part of the package installation or as a separate step. Default is not to update the cache.

fields.label: update kernel
fields.description: If true performs an C(update-kernel) to upgrade kernel packages.

modules.label: archive
modules.tooltip: Creates a compressed archive of one or more files or trees

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: The attributes the resulting filesystem object should have. To get supported flags look at the man page for I(chattr) on the target system. This string should contain the attributes in the same order as the one displayed by I(lsattr). The C(=) operator is assumed as default, otherwise C(+) or C(-) operators need to be included in the string.

fields.label: dest
fields.description: The file name of the destination archive. The parent directory must exists on the remote host. This is required when O(path) refers to multiple files by either specifying a glob, a directory or multiple paths in a list. If the destination archive already exists, it is truncated and overwritten.

fields.label: exclude path
fields.description: Remote absolute path, glob, or list of paths or globs for the file or files to exclude from O(path) list and glob expansion. Use O(exclusion_patterns) to instead exclude files or subdirectories below any of the paths from the O(path) list.

fields.label: exclusion patterns
fields.description: Glob style patterns to exclude files or directories from the resulting archive. This differs from O(exclude_path) which applies only to the source paths from O(path).

fields.label: force archive
fields.description: Allows you to force the module to treat this as an archive even if only a single file is specified. By default when a single file is specified it is compressed only (not archived). Enable this if you want to use M(ansible.builtin.unarchive) on an archive of a single file created with this module.

fields.label: format
fields.description: The type of compression to use.

fields.label: group
fields.description: Name of the group that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current group of the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership.

fields.label: mode
fields.description: The permissions the resulting filesystem object should have. For those used to I(/usr/bin/chmod) remember that modes are actually octal numbers. You must give Ansible enough information to parse them correctly. For consistent results, quote octal numbers (for example, C('644') or C('1777')) so Ansible receives a string and can do its own conversion from string into number. Adding a leading zero (for example, C(0755)) works sometimes, but can fail in loops and some other circumstances. Giving Ansible a number without following either of these rules will end up with a decimal number which will have unexpected results. As of Ansible 1.8, the mode may be specified as a symbolic mode (for example, C(u+rwx) or C(u=rw,g=r,o=r)). If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does not) exist, the default C(umask) on the system will be used when setting the mode for the newly created filesystem object. If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does) exist, the mode of the existing filesystem object will be used. Specifying C(mode) is the best way to ensure filesystem objects are created with the correct permissions. See CVE-2020-1736 for further details.

fields.label: owner
fields.description: Name of the user that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership. Specifying a numeric username will be assumed to be a user ID and not a username. Avoid numeric usernames to avoid this confusion.

fields.label: path
fields.description: Remote absolute path, glob, or list of paths or globs for the file or files to compress or archive.

fields.label: remove
fields.description: Remove any added source files and trees after adding to archive.

fields.label: selevel
fields.description: The level part of the SELinux filesystem object context. This is the MLS/MCS attribute, sometimes known as the C(range). When set to C(_default), it will use the C(level) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: serole
fields.description: The role part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(role) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: setype
fields.description: The type part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(type) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: seuser
fields.description: The user part of the SELinux filesystem object context. By default it uses the C(system) policy, where applicable. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(user) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: unsafe writes
fields.description: Influence when to use atomic operation to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem object. By default this module uses atomic operations to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem objects, but sometimes systems are configured or just broken in ways that prevent this. One example is docker mounted filesystem objects, which cannot be updated atomically from inside the container and can only be written in an unsafe manner. This option allows Ansible to fall back to unsafe methods of updating filesystem objects when atomic operations fail (however, it doesn't force Ansible to perform unsafe writes). IMPORTANT! Unsafe writes are subject to race conditions and can lead to data corruption.

fields.label: activate
fields.description: Activate the new firewall rules. Can be run with other steps or on its own. Idempotency is affected if O(activate=true), as the module always reports a changed state.

fields.label: name
fields.description: One or more policy names.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the policies should be enabled or disabled.

modules.label: beadm
modules.tooltip: Manage ZFS boot environments on FreeBSD/Solaris/illumos systems

fields.label: description
fields.description: Associate a description with a new boot environment. This option is available only on Solarish platforms.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Specifies if the unmount should be forced.

fields.label: mountpoint
fields.description: Path where to mount the ZFS boot environment.

fields.label: name
fields.description: ZFS boot environment name.

fields.label: options
fields.description: Create the datasets for new BE with specific ZFS properties. Multiple options can be specified. This option is available only on Solarish platforms.

fields.label: snapshot
fields.description: If specified, the new boot environment is cloned from the given snapshot or inactive boot environment.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Create or delete ZFS boot environment.

fields.label: component
fields.description: The name of the component being deployed. Ex: V(billing).

fields.label: deployment message
fields.description: Message about the deployment.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Free text description of the deployment.

fields.label: env
fields.description: The environment name, typically V(production), V(staging), and so on.

fields.label: hosts
fields.description: Name of affected host name. Can be a list. If not specified, it defaults to the remote system's hostname.

fields.label: owner
fields.description: The person responsible for the deployment.

fields.label: source system
fields.description: Source system used in the requests to the API.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the deployment.

fields.label: token
fields.description: API token.

fields.label: url
fields.description: Base URL of the API server.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates for the target URL are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: version
fields.description: The deployment version.

fields.label: client id
fields.description: The OAuth consumer key. If not set the environment variable E(BITBUCKET_CLIENT_ID) is used.

fields.label: client secret
fields.description: The OAuth consumer secret. If not set the environment variable E(BITBUCKET_CLIENT_SECRET) is used.

fields.label: key
fields.description: The SSH public key.

fields.label: label
fields.description: The key label.

fields.label: repository
fields.description: The repository name.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicates desired state of the access key.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The username. If not set the environment variable E(BITBUCKET_USERNAME) is used. O(ignore:username) is an alias of O(user) since community.general 6.0.0. It was an alias of O(workspace) before.

fields.label: workspace
fields.description: The repository owner. B(Note:) O(ignore:username) used to be an alias of this option. Since community.general 6.0.0 it is an alias of O(user).

modules.label: bitbucket_pipeline_key_pair
modules.tooltip: Manages Bitbucket pipeline SSH key pair

fields.label: client id
fields.description: The OAuth consumer key. If not set the environment variable E(BITBUCKET_CLIENT_ID) is used.

fields.label: client secret
fields.description: The OAuth consumer secret. If not set the environment variable E(BITBUCKET_CLIENT_SECRET) is used.

fields.label: private key
fields.description: The private key.

fields.label: public key
fields.description: The public key.

fields.label: repository
fields.description: The repository name.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicates desired state of the key pair.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The username. If not set the environment variable E(BITBUCKET_USERNAME) is used. O(ignore:username) is an alias of O(user) since community.general 6.0.0. It was an alias of O(workspace) before.

fields.label: workspace
fields.description: The repository owner. B(Note:) O(ignore:username) used to be an alias of this option. Since community.general 6.0.0 it is an alias of O(user).

fields.label: client id
fields.description: The OAuth consumer key. If not set the environment variable E(BITBUCKET_CLIENT_ID) is used.

fields.label: client secret
fields.description: The OAuth consumer secret. If not set the environment variable E(BITBUCKET_CLIENT_SECRET) is used.

fields.label: key
fields.description: The public key.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The FQDN of the known host.

fields.label: repository
fields.description: The repository name.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicates desired state of the record.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The username. If not set the environment variable E(BITBUCKET_USERNAME) is used. O(ignore:username) is an alias of O(user) since community.general 6.0.0. It was an alias of O(workspace) before.

fields.label: workspace
fields.description: The repository owner. B(Note:) O(ignore:username) used to be an alias of this option. Since community.general 6.0.0 it is an alias of O(user).

fields.label: client id
fields.description: The OAuth consumer key. If not set the environment variable E(BITBUCKET_CLIENT_ID) is used.

fields.label: client secret
fields.description: The OAuth consumer secret. If not set the environment variable E(BITBUCKET_CLIENT_SECRET) is used.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The pipeline variable name.

fields.label: repository
fields.description: The repository name.

fields.label: secured
fields.description: Whether to encrypt the variable value.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicates desired state of the variable.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The username. If not set the environment variable E(BITBUCKET_USERNAME) is used. O(ignore:username) is an alias of O(user) since community.general 6.0.0. It was an alias of O(workspace) before.

fields.label: value
fields.description: The pipeline variable value.

fields.label: workspace
fields.description: The repository owner. B(Note:) O(ignore:username) used to be an alias of this option. Since community.general 6.0.0 it is an alias of O(user).

fields.label: image
fields.description: The image to switch to. This is required when O(state=switch).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Control whether to apply the latest image or switch the image. B(Note:) This does not reboot the system. Please use M(ansible.builtin.reboot) to reboot the system.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of a bower package to install.

fields.label: offline
fields.description: Install packages from local cache, if the packages were installed before.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The base path where to install the bower packages.

fields.label: production
fields.description: Install with C(--production) flag.

fields.label: relative execpath
fields.description: Relative path to bower executable from install path.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of the bower package.

fields.label: version
fields.description: The version to be installed.

fields.label: automount
fields.description: Allow the module to temporarily mount the targeted btrfs filesystem in order to validate the current state and make any required changes.

fields.label: default
fields.description: Make the subvolume specified by O(name) the filesystem's default subvolume.

fields.label: filesystem device
fields.description: A block device contained within the btrfs filesystem to be targeted. Useful when multiple btrfs filesystems are present to specify which filesystem should be targeted.

fields.label: filesystem label
fields.description: A descriptive label assigned to the btrfs filesystem to be targeted. Useful when multiple btrfs filesystems are present to specify which filesystem should be targeted.

fields.label: filesystem uuid
fields.description: A unique identifier assigned to the btrfs filesystem to be targeted. Useful when multiple btrfs filesystems are present to specify which filesystem should be targeted.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the subvolume/snapshot to be targeted.

fields.label: recursive
fields.description: When true, indicates that parent/child subvolumes should be created/removedas necessary to complete the operation (for O(state=present) and O(state=absent) respectively).

fields.label: snapshot conflict
fields.description: Policy defining behavior when a subvolume already exists at the path of the requested snapshot. V(skip) - Create a snapshot only if a subvolume does not yet exist at the target location, otherwise indicate that no change is required. Warning, this option does not yet verify that the target subvolume was generated from a snapshot of the requested source. V(clobber) - If a subvolume already exists at the requested location, delete it first. This option is not idempotent and results in a new snapshot being generated on every execution. V(error) - If a subvolume already exists at the requested location, return an error. This option is not idempotent and results in an error on replay of the module.

fields.label: snapshot source
fields.description: Identifies the source subvolume for the created snapshot. Infers that the created subvolume is a snapshot.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicates the current state of the targeted subvolume.

modules.label: bundler
modules.tooltip: Manage Ruby Gem dependencies with Bundler

fields.label: binstub directory
fields.description: Only applies if O(state=present). Specifies the directory to install any gem bins files to. When executed the bin files run within the context of the Gemfile and fail if any required gem dependencies are not installed. If O(chdir) is set then this path is relative to O(chdir).

fields.label: chdir
fields.description: The directory to execute the bundler commands from. This directory needs to contain a valid Gemfile or .bundle/ directory. If not specified, it defaults to the temporary working directory.

fields.label: clean
fields.description: Only applies if O(state=present). If set removes any gems on the target host that are not in the gemfile.

fields.label: deployment mode
fields.description: Only applies if O(state=present). If set it installs gems in C(./vendor/bundle) instead of the default location. Requires a C(Gemfile.lock) file to have been created prior.

fields.label: exclude groups
fields.description: A list of Gemfile groups to exclude during operations. This only applies when O(state=present). Bundler considers this a 'remembered' property for the Gemfile and automatically excludes groups in future operations even if O(exclude_groups) is not set.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: The path to the bundler executable.

fields.label: extra args
fields.description: A space separated string of additional commands that can be applied to the Bundler command. Refer to the Bundler documentation for more information.

fields.label: gem path
fields.description: Only applies if O(state=present). Specifies the directory to install the gems into. If O(chdir) is set then this path is relative to O(chdir). If not specified the default RubyGems gem paths are used.

fields.label: gemfile
fields.description: Only applies if O(state=present). The path to the gemfile to use to install gems. If not specified it defaults to the Gemfile in current directory.

fields.label: local
fields.description: If set only installs gems from the cache on the target host.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The desired state of the Gem bundle. V(latest) updates gems to the most recent, acceptable version.

fields.label: user install
fields.description: Only applies if O(state=present). Installs gems in the local user's cache or for all users.

modules.label: bzr
modules.tooltip: Deploy software (or files) from bzr branches

fields.label: dest
fields.description: Absolute path of where the branch should be cloned to.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: Path to C(bzr) executable to use. If not supplied, the normal mechanism for resolving binary paths is used.

fields.label: force
fields.description: If V(true), any modified files in the working tree is discarded.

fields.label: name
fields.description: SSH or HTTP protocol address of the parent branch.

fields.label: version
fields.description: What version of the branch to clone. This can be the bzr revno or revid.

fields.label: msg
fields.description: The message body.

fields.label: notify
fields.description: Send a notification sound before the message.

fields.label: room
fields.description: Room number to which the message should be sent.

fields.label: subscription
fields.description: The subscription name to use.

fields.label: token
fields.description: API token.

fields.label: capability
fields.description: Desired capability to set (with operator and flags, if O(state=present)) or remove (if O(state=absent)).

fields.label: path
fields.description: Specifies the path to the file to be managed.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the entry should be present or absent in the file's capabilities.

fields.label: directory
fields.description: Path to the source directory to install the Rust package from. This is only used when installing packages.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: Path to the C(cargo) installed in the system. If not specified, the module looks for C(cargo) in E(PATH).

fields.label: features
fields.description: List of features to activate. This is only used when installing packages.

fields.label: locked
fields.description: Install with locked dependencies. This is only used when installing packages.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of a Rust package to install.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The base path where to install the Rust packages. Cargo automatically appends V(/bin). In other words, V(/usr/local) becomes V(/usr/local/bin).

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of the Rust package.

fields.label: version
fields.description: The version to install. If O(name) contains multiple values, the module tries to install all of them in this version.

fields.label: api key
fields.description: Circonus API key.

fields.label: category
fields.description: Annotation Category.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Description of annotation.

fields.label: duration
fields.description: Duration in seconds of annotation.

fields.label: start
fields.description: Unix timestamp of event start. If not specified, it defaults to "now".

fields.label: stop
fields.description: Unix timestamp of event end. If not specified, it defaults to "now" + O(duration).

fields.label: title
fields.description: Title of annotation.

modules.label: cisco_webex
modules.tooltip: Send a message to a Cisco Webex Teams Room or Individual

fields.label: msg
fields.description: The message you would like to send.

fields.label: msg type
fields.description: Specifies how you would like the message formatted.

fields.label: personal token
fields.description: Your personal access token required to validate the Webex Teams API.

fields.label: recipient id
fields.description: The unique identifier associated with the supplied O(recipient_type).

fields.label: recipient type
fields.description: The request parameter you would like to send the message to. Messages can be sent to either a room or individual (by ID or E-Mail).

fields.label: filter
fields.description: Filter facts.

fields.label: account api key
fields.description: Account API key. Required for API keys authentication. You can obtain your API key from the bottom of the Cloudflare 'My Account' page, found here: U(https://dash.cloudflare.com/).

fields.label: account email
fields.description: Account email. Required for API keys authentication.

fields.label: algorithm
fields.description: Algorithm number. Required for O(type=DS) and O(type=SSHFP) when O(state=present).

fields.label: api token
fields.description: API token. Required for API token authentication. You can obtain your API token from the bottom of the Cloudflare 'My Account' page, found here: U(https://dash.cloudflare.com/). Can be specified in E(CLOUDFLARE_TOKEN) environment variable since community.general 2.0.0.

fields.label: cert usage
fields.description: Certificate usage number. Required for O(type=TLSA) when O(state=present).

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Comments or notes about the DNS record.

fields.label: flag
fields.description: Issuer Critical Flag. Should be set either to V(0) for not critical, or to V(128) for critical. Before community.general 12.3.0, only V(0) and V(1) were valid options. The value V(1) is still available for backwards-compatibility reasons. Required for O(type=CAA) when O(state=present).

fields.label: hash type
fields.description: Hash type number. Required for O(type=DS), O(type=SSHFP) and O(type=TLSA) when O(state=present).

fields.label: key tag
fields.description: DNSSEC key tag. Needed for O(type=DS) when O(state=present).

fields.label: priority
fields.description: Record priority. Required for O(type=MX) and O(type=SRV).

fields.label: proto
fields.description: Service protocol. Required for O(type=SRV) and O(type=TLSA). Common values are TCP and UDP.

fields.label: proxied
fields.description: Proxy through Cloudflare network or just use DNS.

fields.label: record
fields.description: Record to add. Required if O(state=present). Default is V(@) (that is, the zone name).

fields.label: selector
fields.description: Selector number. Required for O(type=TLSA) when O(state=present).

fields.label: service
fields.description: Record service. Required for O(type=SRV).

fields.label: solo
fields.description: Whether the record should be the only one for that record type and record name. Only use with O(state=present). This deletes all other records with the same record name and type.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the record(s) should exist or not.

fields.label: tag
fields.description: CAA issue restriction. Required for O(type=CAA) when O(state=present).

fields.label: tags
fields.description: Custom tags for the DNS record.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: The TTL to give the new record. Must be between V(120) and V(2,147,483,647) seconds, or V(1) for automatic.

fields.label: type
fields.description: The type of DNS record to create. Required if O(state=present). Support for V(SPF) has been removed from community.general 9.0.0 since that record type is no longer supported by CloudFlare. Support for V(PTR) has been added in community.general 11.1.0.

fields.label: value
fields.description: The record value. Required for O(state=present).

fields.label: weight
fields.description: Service weight. Required for O(type=SRV).

fields.label: zone
fields.description: The name of the Zone to work with (for example V(example.com)). The Zone must already exist.

fields.label: use ssl
fields.description: If V(false), an HTTP connection is used instead of the default HTTPS connection.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only set to V(false) when used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: interfaces
fields.description: A list of dictionaries containing interface options.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The system name to manage.

fields.label: properties
fields.description: A dictionary with system properties.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the system should be present, absent or a query is made.

fields.label: sync
fields.description: Sync on changes. Concurrently syncing Cobbler is bound to fail.

fields.label: use ssl
fields.description: If V(false), an HTTP connection is used instead of the default HTTPS connection.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only set to V(false) when used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: apcu autoloader
fields.description: Uses APCu to cache found/not-found classes.

fields.label: arguments
fields.description: Composer arguments like required package, version and so on.

fields.label: classmap authoritative
fields.description: Autoload classes from classmap only. Implicitly enable optimize_autoloader. Recommended especially for production, but can take a bit of time to run.

fields.label: command
fields.description: Composer command like V(install), V(update) and so on.

fields.label: composer executable
fields.description: Path to composer executable on the remote host, if composer is not in E(PATH) or a custom composer is needed.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: Path to PHP executable on the remote host, if PHP is not in E(PATH).

fields.label: force
fields.description: When O(command) is V(create-project), the module checks whether a V(composer.json) already exists in O(working_dir) and skips the command if it does, making the task idempotent. Set to V(true) to always run the command regardless.

fields.label: global command
fields.description: Runs the specified command globally.

fields.label: ignore platform reqs
fields.description: Ignore C(php), C(hhvm), C(lib-*) and C(ext-*) requirements and force the installation even if the local machine does not fulfill these.

fields.label: no dev
fields.description: Disables installation of require-dev packages (see C(--no-dev)).

fields.label: no plugins
fields.description: Disables all plugins (see C(--no-plugins)).

fields.label: no scripts
fields.description: Skips the execution of all scripts defined in composer.json (see C(--no-scripts)).

fields.label: optimize autoloader
fields.description: Optimize autoloader during autoloader dump (see C(--optimize-autoloader)). Convert PSR-0/4 autoloading to classmap to get a faster autoloader. Recommended especially for production, but can take a bit of time to run.

fields.label: prefer dist
fields.description: Forces installation from package dist even for dev versions (see C(--prefer-dist)).

fields.label: prefer source
fields.description: Forces installation from package sources when possible (see C(--prefer-source)).

fields.label: working dir
fields.description: Directory of your project (see C(--working-dir)). This is required when the command is not run globally. This is ignored if O(global_command=true).

modules.label: consul
modules.tooltip: Add, modify & delete services within a Consul cluster

fields.label: check host
fields.description: Host name.

fields.label: check id
fields.description: An ID for the service check. If O(state=absent), defaults to O(check_name). Ignored if part of a service definition.

fields.label: check name
fields.description: Name for the service check. Required if standalone, ignored if part of service definition.

fields.label: check node
fields.description: Node name.

fields.label: http
fields.description: Checks can be registered with an HTTP endpoint. This means that Consul checks that the http endpoint returns a successful HTTP status. Requires O(interval) to be provided. Mutually exclusive with O(script), O(ttl) and O(tcp).

fields.label: interval
fields.description: The interval at which the service check is run. This is a number with a V(s) or V(m) suffix to signify the units of seconds or minutes, for example V(15s) or V(1m). If no suffix is supplied V(s) is used by default, for example V(10) is V(10s). Required if one of the parameters O(script), O(http), or O(tcp) is specified.

fields.label: notes
fields.description: Notes to attach to check when registering it.

fields.label: scheme
fields.description: The protocol scheme on which the Consul agent is running.

fields.label: script
fields.description: The script/command that is run periodically to check the health of the service. Requires O(interval) to be provided. Mutually exclusive with O(ttl), O(tcp) and O(http).

fields.label: service address
fields.description: The address to advertise that the service is listening on. This value is passed as the C(address) parameter to Consul's C(/v1/agent/service/register) API method, so refer to the Consul API documentation for further details.

fields.label: service id
fields.description: The ID for the service, must be unique per node. If O(state=absent), defaults to the service name if supplied.

fields.label: service name
fields.description: Unique name for the service on a node, must be unique per node, required if registering a service. May be omitted if registering a node level check.

fields.label: service port
fields.description: The port on which the service is listening. Can optionally be supplied for registration of a service, that is if O(service_name) or O(service_id) is set.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Register or deregister the Consul service, defaults to present.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: Tags that are attached to the service registration.

fields.label: tcp
fields.description: Checks can be registered with a TCP port. This means that Consul checks if the connection attempt to that port is successful (that is, the port is currently accepting connections). The format is V(host:port), for example V(localhost:80). Requires O(interval) to be provided. Mutually exclusive with O(script), O(ttl) and O(http).

fields.label: token
fields.description: The token key identifying an ACL rule set. May be required to register services.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: Checks can be registered with a TTL instead of a O(script) and O(interval) this means that the service checks in with the agent before the TTL expires. If it does not the check is considered failed. Required if registering a check and the script an interval are missing Similar to the interval this is a number with a V(s) or V(m) suffix to signify the units of seconds or minutes, for example V(15s) or V(1m). If no suffix is supplied V(s) is used by default, for example V(10) is equivalent to V(10s). Mutually exclusive with O(script), O(tcp) and O(http).

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to verify the TLS certificate of the Consul agent.

fields.label: bootstrap secret
fields.description: The secret to be used as secret ID for the initial token. Needs to be an UUID.

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: The CA bundle to use for https connections.

fields.label: scheme
fields.description: The protocol scheme on which the Consul agent is running. Defaults to V(http) and can be set to V(https) for secure connections.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the token should be present or absent.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to verify the TLS certificate of the Consul agent.

modules.label: consul_agent_check
modules.tooltip: Add, modify, and delete checks within a Consul cluster

fields.label: args
fields.description: Specifies command arguments to run to update the status of the check. Requires O(interval) to be provided. Mutually exclusive with O(ttl), O(tcp) and O(http).

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: The CA bundle to use for https connections.

fields.label: http
fields.description: Checks can be registered with an HTTP endpoint. This means that Consul checks that the HTTP endpoint returns a successful HTTP status. Requires O(interval) to be provided. Mutually exclusive with O(args), O(ttl) and O(tcp).

fields.label: id
fields.description: Specifies a unique ID for this check on the node. This defaults to the O(name) parameter, but it may be necessary to provide an ID for uniqueness. This value is returned in the response as V(CheckId).

fields.label: interval
fields.description: The interval at which the service check is run. This is a number with a V(s) or V(m) suffix to signify the units of seconds or minutes, for example V(15s) or V(1m). If no suffix is supplied V(s) is used by default, for example V(10) is equivalent to V(10s). Required if one of the parameters O(args), O(http), or O(tcp) is specified.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Required name for the service check.

fields.label: notes
fields.description: Notes to attach to check when registering it.

fields.label: scheme
fields.description: The protocol scheme on which the Consul agent is running. Defaults to V(http) and can be set to V(https) for secure connections.

fields.label: service id
fields.description: The ID for the service, must be unique per node. If O(state=absent), defaults to the service name if supplied.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the check should be present or absent.

fields.label: tcp
fields.description: Checks can be registered with a TCP port. This means that Consul will check if the connection attempt to that port is successful (that is, the port is currently accepting connections). The format is V(host:port), for example V(localhost:80). Requires O(interval) to be provided. Mutually exclusive with O(args), O(ttl) and O(http).

fields.label: token
fields.description: The token to use for authorization.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: Checks can be registered with a TTL instead of a O(args) and O(interval) this means that the service checks in with the agent before the TTL expires. If it does not the check is considered failed. Required if registering a check and the script an interval are missing Similar to the interval this is a number with a V(s) or V(m) suffix to signify the units of seconds or minutes, for example V(15s) or V(1m). If no suffix is supplied V(s) is used by default, for example V(10) is equivalent to V(10s). Mutually exclusive with O(args), O(tcp) and O(http).

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to verify the TLS certificate of the Consul agent.

modules.label: consul_agent_service
modules.tooltip: Add, modify and delete services within a Consul cluster

fields.label: address
fields.description: The address to advertise that the service listens on. This value is passed as the C(address) parameter to Consul's C(/v1/agent/service/register) API method, so refer to the Consul API documentation for further details.

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: The CA bundle to use for https connections.

fields.label: enable tag override
fields.description: Specifies to disable the anti-entropy feature for this service's tags. If C(EnableTagOverride) is set to true then external agents can update this service in the catalog and modify the tags.

fields.label: id
fields.description: Specifies a unique ID for this service. This must be unique per agent. This defaults to the O(name) parameter if not provided. If O(state=absent), defaults to the service name if supplied.

fields.label: meta
fields.description: Optional meta data used for filtering. For keys, the characters C(A-Z), C(a-z), C(0-9), C(_), C(-) are allowed. Not allowed characters are replaced with underscores.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Unique name for the service on a node, must be unique per node, required if registering a service.

fields.label: scheme
fields.description: The protocol scheme on which the Consul agent is running. Defaults to V(http) and can be set to V(https) for secure connections.

fields.label: service port
fields.description: The port on which the service is listening. Can optionally be supplied for registration of a service, that is if O(name) or O(id) is set.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the service should be present or absent.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: Tags that are attached to the service registration.

fields.label: token
fields.description: The token to use for authorization.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to verify the TLS certificate of the Consul agent.

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: The CA bundle to use for https connections.

fields.label: config
fields.description: The raw configuration to use for the chosen auth method. Contents vary depending upon the O(type) chosen. Required when the auth method is created.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Free form human readable description of the auth method.

fields.label: display name
fields.description: An optional name to use instead of O(name) when displaying information about this auth method.

fields.label: max token ttl
fields.description: This specifies the maximum life of any token created by this auth method. Can be specified in the form of V(60s) or V(5m) (that is, 60 seconds or 5 minutes, respectively).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies a name for the ACL auth method. The name can contain alphanumeric characters, dashes C(-), and underscores C(_).

fields.label: scheme
fields.description: The protocol scheme on which the Consul agent is running. Defaults to V(http) and can be set to V(https) for secure connections.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the token should be present or absent.

fields.label: token
fields.description: The token to use for authorization.

fields.label: token locality
fields.description: Defines the kind of token that this auth method should produce.

fields.label: type
fields.description: The type of auth method being configured. This field is immutable. Required when the auth method is created.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to verify the TLS certificate of the Consul agent.

fields.label: auth method
fields.description: The name of the auth method that this rule applies to.

fields.label: bind name
fields.description: The name to bind to a token at login-time. What it binds to can be adjusted with different values of the O(bind_type) parameter.

fields.label: bind type
fields.description: Specifies the way the binding rule affects a token created at login.

fields.label: bind vars
fields.description: Specifies the templated policy variables when O(bind_type) is set to V(templated-policy).

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: The CA bundle to use for https connections.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Free form human readable description of the binding rule.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies a name for the binding rule. Note: This is used to identify the binding rule. But since the API does not support a name, it is prefixed to the description.

fields.label: scheme
fields.description: The protocol scheme on which the Consul agent is running. Defaults to V(http) and can be set to V(https) for secure connections.

fields.label: selector
fields.description: Specifies the expression used to match this rule against valid identities returned from an auth method validation. If empty this binding rule matches all valid identities returned from the auth method.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the binding rule should be present or absent.

fields.label: token
fields.description: The token to use for authorization.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to verify the TLS certificate of the Consul agent.

modules.label: consul_kv
modules.tooltip: Manipulate entries in the key/value store of a Consul cluster

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: The CA bundle to use for HTTPS connections.

fields.label: cas
fields.description: Used when acquiring a lock with a session. If the O(cas) is V(0), then Consul only puts the key if it does not already exist. If the O(cas) value is non-zero, then the key is only set if the index matches the ModifyIndex of that key.

fields.label: datacenter
fields.description: The name of the datacenter to query. If unspecified, the query defaults to the datacenter of the Consul agent on O(host).

fields.label: flags
fields.description: Opaque positive integer value that can be passed when setting a value.

fields.label: key
fields.description: The key at which the value should be stored.

fields.label: recurse
fields.description: If the key represents a prefix, each entry with the prefix can be retrieved by setting this to V(true).

fields.label: retrieve
fields.description: If the O(state) is V(present) and O(value) is set, perform a read after setting the value and return this value.

fields.label: scheme
fields.description: The protocol scheme on which the Consul agent is running.

fields.label: session
fields.description: The session that should be used to acquire or release a lock associated with a key/value pair.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The action to take with the supplied key and value. If the state is V(present) and O(value) is set, the key contents is set to the value supplied and RV(ignore:changed) is set to V(true) only if the value was different to the current contents. If the state is V(present) and O(value) is not set, the existing value associated to the key is returned. The state V(absent) is used to remove the key/value pair, again RV(ignore:changed) is set to V(true) only if the key actually existed prior to the removal. An attempt can be made to obtain or free the lock associated with a key/value pair with the states V(acquire) or V(release) respectively. A valid session must be supplied to make the attempt RV(ignore:changed) is V(true) if the attempt is successful, V(false) otherwise.

fields.label: token
fields.description: The token key identifying an ACL rule set that controls access to the key value pair.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to verify the TLS certificate of the Consul agent. Instead of setting this to V(false), please consider using O(ca_path) instead.

fields.label: value
fields.description: The value should be associated with the given key, required if O(state) is V(present).

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: The CA bundle to use for https connections.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Description of the policy.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name that should be associated with the policy, this is opaque to Consul.

fields.label: rules
fields.description: Rule document that should be associated with the current policy.

fields.label: scheme
fields.description: The protocol scheme on which the Consul agent is running. Defaults to V(http) and can be set to V(https) for secure connections.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the policy should be present or absent.

fields.label: token
fields.description: The token to use for authorization.

fields.label: valid datacenters
fields.description: Valid datacenters for the policy. All if list is empty.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to verify the TLS certificate of the Consul agent.

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: The CA bundle to use for https connections.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Description of the role. If not specified, the assigned description is not changed.

fields.label: name
fields.description: A name used to identify the role.

fields.label: scheme
fields.description: The protocol scheme on which the Consul agent is running. Defaults to V(http) and can be set to V(https) for secure connections.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the role should be present or absent.

fields.label: token
fields.description: The token to use for authorization.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to verify the TLS certificate of the Consul agent.

fields.label: behavior
fields.description: The optional behavior that can be attached to the session when it is created. This controls the behavior when a session is invalidated.

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: The CA bundle to use for https connections.

fields.label: checks
fields.description: Checks that are used to verify the session health. If all the checks fail, the session is invalidated and any locks associated with the session are released and can be acquired once the associated lock delay has expired.

fields.label: datacenter
fields.description: The name of the datacenter in which the session exists or should be created.

fields.label: delay
fields.description: The optional lock delay that can be attached to the session when it is created. Locks for invalidated sessions ar blocked from being acquired until this delay has expired. Durations are in seconds.

fields.label: id
fields.description: ID of the session, required when O(state) is either V(info) or V(remove).

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name that should be associated with the session. Required when O(state=node) is used.

fields.label: node
fields.description: The name of the node that with which the session is associated. By default this is the name of the agent.

fields.label: scheme
fields.description: The protocol scheme on which the Consul agent is running. Defaults to V(http) and can be set to V(https) for secure connections.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the session should be present, in other words it should be created if it does not exist, or absent, removed if present. If created, the O(id) for the session is returned in the output. If V(absent), O(id) is required to remove the session. Info for a single session, all the sessions for a node or all available sessions can be retrieved by specifying V(info), V(node) or V(list) for the O(state); for V(node) or V(info), the node O(name) or session O(id) is required as parameter.

fields.label: token
fields.description: The token to use for authorization.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: Specifies the duration of a session in seconds (between 10 and 86400).

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to verify the TLS certificate of the Consul agent.

fields.label: accessor id
fields.description: Specifies a UUID to use as the token's Accessor ID. If not specified a UUID is generated for this field.

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: The CA bundle to use for https connections.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Free form human readable description of the token.

fields.label: expiration ttl
