fields.description: This is a convenience field and if set it initializes the C(expiration_time). Can be specified in the form of V(60s) or V(5m) (that is, 60 seconds or 5 minutes, respectively). Ingored when the token is updated!

fields.label: local
fields.description: If true, indicates that the token should not be replicated globally and instead be local to the current datacenter.

fields.label: scheme
fields.description: The protocol scheme on which the Consul agent is running. Defaults to V(http) and can be set to V(https) for secure connections.

fields.label: secret id
fields.description: Specifies a UUID to use as the token's Secret ID. If not specified a UUID is generated for this field.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the token should be present or absent.

fields.label: token
fields.description: The token to use for authorization.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to verify the TLS certificate of the Consul agent.

modules.label: copr
modules.tooltip: Manage one of the Copr repositories

fields.label: chroot
fields.description: The name of the chroot that you want to enable/disable/remove in the project, for example V(epel-7-x86_64). Default chroot is determined by the operating system, version of the operating system, and architecture on which the module is run.

fields.label: excludepkgs
fields.description: List of packages in this repository to exclude from all operations.  Matches a name or a glob.

fields.label: includepkgs
fields.description: List of packages to include in all operations.  Inverse of O(excludepkgs), DNF will exclude any package in the repository that does not match this list.  Matches a name or a glob.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Copr directory name, for example C(@copr/copr-dev).

fields.label: protocol
fields.description: This indicate which protocol to use with the host.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to set this project as V(enabled), V(disabled), or V(absent).

fields.label: executable
fields.description: Override the path to the C(cpanm) executable.

fields.label: from path
fields.description: The local directory or C(tar.gz) file to install from.

fields.label: install recommendations
fields.description: If V(true), installs dependencies declared as recommends per META spec. If V(false), it ensures the dependencies declared as recommends are not installed, overriding any decision made earlier in E(PERL_CPANM_OPT). If parameter is not set, C(cpanm) uses its existing defaults. When these dependencies fail to install, cpanm continues the installation, since they are just recommendation.

fields.label: install suggestions
fields.description: If V(true), installs dependencies declared as suggests per META spec. If V(false), it ensures the dependencies declared as suggests are not installed, overriding any decision made earlier in E(PERL_CPANM_OPT). If parameter is not set, C(cpanm) uses its existing defaults. When these dependencies fail to install, C(cpanm) continues the installation, since they are just suggestions.

fields.label: installdeps
fields.description: Only install dependencies.

fields.label: locallib
fields.description: Specify the install base to install modules.

fields.label: mirror
fields.description: Specifies the base URL for the CPAN mirror to use.

fields.label: mirror only
fields.description: Use the mirror's index file instead of the CPAN Meta DB.

fields.label: mode
fields.description: Controls the module behavior. See notes below for more details. The default changed from V(compatibility) to V(new) in community.general 9.0.0. V(compatibility) was removed from community.general 13.0.0. O(mode=new): The O(name) parameter may refer to a module name, a distribution file, a HTTP URL or a git repository URL as described in C(cpanminus) documentation. C(cpanm) version specifiers are recognized. This is the default mode from community.general 9.0.0 onwards.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The Perl library to install. Valid values change according to the O(mode), see notes for more details. Note that for installing from a local path the parameter O(from_path) should be used.

fields.label: name check
fields.description: When O(mode=new), this parameter can be used to check if there is a module O(name) installed (at O(version), when specified).

fields.label: notest
fields.description: Do not run unit tests.

fields.label: version
fields.description: Version specification for the perl module. When O(mode=new), C(cpanm) version operators are accepted.

fields.label: backup
fields.description: If set, create a backup of the crontab before it is modified. The location of the backup is returned in the C(backup) variable by this module.

fields.label: cron file
fields.description: If specified, uses this file instead of an individual user's crontab. Without a leading V(/), this is assumed to be in C(/etc/cron.d). With a leading V(/), this is taken as absolute.

fields.label: insertafter
fields.description: If specified, the variable is inserted after the variable specified. Used with O(state=present).

fields.label: insertbefore
fields.description: Used with O(state=present). If specified, the variable is inserted just before the variable specified.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the crontab variable.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to ensure that the variable is present or absent.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The specific user whose crontab should be modified. This parameter defaults to V(root) when unset.

fields.label: value
fields.description: The value to set this variable to. Required if O(state=present).

fields.label: backing device
fields.description: Path to the underlying block device or file, or the UUID of a block-device prefixed with V(UUID=).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the encrypted block device as it appears in the C(/etc/crypttab) file, or optionally prefixed with V(/dev/mapper/), as it appears in the filesystem. V(/dev/mapper/) is stripped from O(name).

fields.label: opts
fields.description: A comma-delimited list of options. See V(crypttab(5\)) for details.

fields.label: path
fields.description: Path to file to use instead of V(/etc/crypttab). This might be useful in a chroot environment.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Use V(present) to add a line to C(/etc/crypttab) or update its definition if already present. Use V(absent) to remove a line with matching O(name). Use V(opts_present) to add options to those already present; options with different values are updated. Use V(opts_absent) to remove options from the existing set.

fields.label: api host
fields.description: The URL to the Datadog API. This value can also be set with the E(DATADOG_HOST) environment variable.

fields.label: api key
fields.description: Your Datadog API key.

fields.label: app key
fields.description: Your Datadog app key.

fields.label: downtime message
fields.description: A message to include with notifications for this downtime. Email notifications can be sent to specific users by using the same "@username" notation as events.

fields.label: end
fields.description: POSIX timestamp to end the downtime. If not provided, the downtime is in effect until you cancel it.

fields.label: id
fields.description: The identifier of the downtime. If empty, a new downtime gets created, otherwise it is either updated or deleted depending of the O(state). To keep your playbook idempotent, you should save the identifier in a file and read it in a lookup.

fields.label: monitor id
fields.description: The ID of the monitor to mute. If not provided, the downtime applies to all monitors.

fields.label: monitor tags
fields.description: A list of monitor tags to which the downtime applies. The resulting downtime applies to monitors that match ALL provided monitor tags.

fields.label: rrule
fields.description: The C(RRULE) standard for defining recurring events. For example, to have a recurring event on the first day of each month, select a type of rrule and set the C(FREQ) to C(MONTHLY) and C(BYMONTHDAY) to C(1). Most common rrule options from the iCalendar Spec are supported. Attributes specifying the duration in C(RRULE) are not supported (for example C(DTSTART), C(DTEND), C(DURATION)).

fields.label: scope
fields.description: A list of scopes to which the downtime applies. The resulting downtime applies to sources that matches ALL provided scopes.

fields.label: start
fields.description: POSIX timestamp to start the downtime. If not provided, the downtime starts the moment it is created.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The designated state of the downtime.

fields.label: timezone
fields.description: The timezone for the downtime.

fields.label: aggregation key
fields.description: An arbitrary string to use for aggregation.

fields.label: alert type
fields.description: Type of alert.

fields.label: api host
fields.description: DataDog API endpoint URL.

fields.label: api key
fields.description: Your DataDog API key.

fields.label: app key
fields.description: Your DataDog app key.

fields.label: date happened
fields.description: POSIX timestamp of the event. Default value is now.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: The priority of the event.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: Comma separated list of tags to apply to the event.

fields.label: text
fields.description: The body of the event.

fields.label: title
fields.description: The event title.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: api host
fields.description: The URL to the Datadog API. Default value is V(https://api.datadoghq.com). This value can also be set with the E(DATADOG_HOST) environment variable.

fields.label: api key
fields.description: Your Datadog API key.

fields.label: app key
fields.description: Your Datadog app key.

fields.label: escalation message
fields.description: A message to include with a re-notification. Supports the '@username' notification we allow elsewhere. Not applicable if O(renotify_interval=none).

fields.label: evaluation delay
fields.description: Time to delay evaluation (in seconds). Effective for sparse values.

fields.label: id
fields.description: The ID of the alert. If set, it is used instead of O(name) to locate the alert.

fields.label: include tags
fields.description: Whether notifications from this monitor automatically inserts its triggering tags into the title.

fields.label: locked
fields.description: Whether changes to this monitor should be restricted to the creator or admins.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the alert.

fields.label: new host delay
fields.description: A positive integer representing the number of seconds to wait before evaluating the monitor for new hosts. This gives the host time to fully initialize.

fields.label: no data timeframe
fields.description: The number of minutes before a monitor notifies when data stops reporting. Must be at least 2x the monitor timeframe for metric alerts or 2 minutes for service checks. If not specified, it defaults to 2x timeframe for metric, 2 minutes for service.

fields.label: notification message
fields.description: A message to include with notifications for this monitor. Email notifications can be sent to specific users by using the same '@username' notation as events. Monitor message template variables can be accessed by using double square brackets, in other words C([[) and C(]]).

fields.label: notification preset name
fields.description: Toggles the display of additional content sent in the monitor notification.

fields.label: notify audit
fields.description: Whether tagged users are notified on changes to this monitor.

fields.label: notify no data
fields.description: Whether this monitor notifies when data stops reporting.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: Integer from V(1) (high) to V(5) (low) indicating alert severity.

fields.label: query
fields.description: The monitor query to notify on. Syntax varies depending on what type of monitor you are creating.

fields.label: renotify interval
fields.description: The number of minutes after the last notification before a monitor re-notifies on the current status. It only re-notifies if it is not resolved.

fields.label: renotify occurrences
fields.description: The number of times re-notification messages should be sent on the current status at the provided re-notification interval.

fields.label: renotify statuses
fields.description: The types of monitor statuses for which re-notification messages are sent.

fields.label: require full window
fields.description: Whether this monitor needs a full window of data before it gets evaluated. We highly recommend you set this to V(false) for sparse metrics, otherwise some evaluations are skipped.

fields.label: silenced
fields.description: Dictionary of scopes to silence, with timestamps or None. Each scope is muted until the given POSIX timestamp or forever if the value is V(None).

fields.label: state
fields.description: The designated state of the monitor. The values V(mute) and V(unmute) are deprecated since community.general 13.0.0 and will be removed in 15.0.0. Use the M(community.general.datadog_downtime) module to manage monitor downtimes instead.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: A list of tags to associate with your monitor when creating or updating. This can help you categorize and filter monitors.

fields.label: thresholds
fields.description: A dictionary of thresholds by status. Only available for service checks and metric alerts. Because each of them can have multiple thresholds, we do not define them directly in the query. If not specified, it defaults to: V({'ok': 1, 'critical': 1, 'warning': 1}).

fields.label: timeout h
fields.description: The number of hours of the monitor not reporting data before it automatically resolves from a triggered state.

fields.label: type
fields.description: The type of the monitor. The types V(query alert), V(trace-analytics alert) and V(rum alert) were added in community.general 2.1.0. The type V(composite) was added in community.general 3.4.0. The type V(event-v2 alert) was added in community.general 4.8.0.

fields.label: key
fields.description: A dconf key to modify or read from the dconf database.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The action to take upon the key/value.

fields.label: value
fields.description: Value to set for the specified dconf key. Value should be specified in GVariant format. Due to complexity of this format, it is best to have a look at existing values in the dconf database. Required for O(state=present). Although the type is specified as "raw", it should typically be specified as a string. However, boolean values in particular are handled properly even when specified as booleans rather than strings (in fact, handling booleans properly is why the type of this parameter is "raw").

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: The attributes the resulting filesystem object should have. To get supported flags look at the man page for I(chattr) on the target system. This string should contain the attributes in the same order as the one displayed by I(lsattr). The C(=) operator is assumed as default, otherwise C(+) or C(-) operators need to be included in the string.

fields.label: dest
fields.description: The file name of the destination file where the compressed file is decompressed. If the destination file exists, it is truncated and overwritten. If not specified, the destination filename is derived from O(src) by removing the compression format extension. For example, when O(src) is V(/path/to/file.txt.gz) and O(format) is V(gz), O(dest) is V(/path/to/file.txt). If the O(src) file does not have an extension for the current O(format), the O(dest) filename is made by appending C(_decompressed) to the O(src) filename. For instance, when O(src) is V(/path/to/file.myextension), the (dest) filename is V(/path/to/file.myextension_decompressed).

fields.label: format
fields.description: The type of compression to use to decompress.

fields.label: group
fields.description: Name of the group that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current group of the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership.

fields.label: mode
fields.description: The permissions the resulting filesystem object should have. For those used to I(/usr/bin/chmod) remember that modes are actually octal numbers. You must give Ansible enough information to parse them correctly. For consistent results, quote octal numbers (for example, C('644') or C('1777')) so Ansible receives a string and can do its own conversion from string into number. Adding a leading zero (for example, C(0755)) works sometimes, but can fail in loops and some other circumstances. Giving Ansible a number without following either of these rules will end up with a decimal number which will have unexpected results. As of Ansible 1.8, the mode may be specified as a symbolic mode (for example, C(u+rwx) or C(u=rw,g=r,o=r)). If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does not) exist, the default C(umask) on the system will be used when setting the mode for the newly created filesystem object. If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does) exist, the mode of the existing filesystem object will be used. Specifying C(mode) is the best way to ensure filesystem objects are created with the correct permissions. See CVE-2020-1736 for further details.

fields.label: owner
fields.description: Name of the user that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership. Specifying a numeric username will be assumed to be a user ID and not a username. Avoid numeric usernames to avoid this confusion.

fields.label: remove
fields.description: Remove original compressed file after decompression.

fields.label: selevel
fields.description: The level part of the SELinux filesystem object context. This is the MLS/MCS attribute, sometimes known as the C(range). When set to C(_default), it will use the C(level) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: serole
fields.description: The role part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(role) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: setype
fields.description: The type part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(type) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: seuser
fields.description: The user part of the SELinux filesystem object context. By default it uses the C(system) policy, where applicable. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(user) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: src
fields.description: Remote absolute path for the file to decompress.

fields.label: unsafe writes
fields.description: Influence when to use atomic operation to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem object. By default this module uses atomic operations to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem objects, but sometimes systems are configured or just broken in ways that prevent this. One example is docker mounted filesystem objects, which cannot be updated atomically from inside the container and can only be written in an unsafe manner. This option allows Ansible to fall back to unsafe methods of updating filesystem objects when atomic operations fail (however, it doesn't force Ansible to perform unsafe writes). IMPORTANT! Unsafe writes are subject to race conditions and can lead to data corruption.

modules.label: deploy_helper
modules.tooltip: Manages some of the steps common in deploying projects

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: The attributes the resulting filesystem object should have. To get supported flags look at the man page for I(chattr) on the target system. This string should contain the attributes in the same order as the one displayed by I(lsattr). The C(=) operator is assumed as default, otherwise C(+) or C(-) operators need to be included in the string.

fields.label: clean
fields.description: Whether to run the clean procedure in case of O(state=finalize).

fields.label: current path
fields.description: The name of the symlink that is created when the deploy is finalized. Used in O(state=finalize) and O(state=clean). Returned in the C(deploy_helper.current_path) fact.

fields.label: group
fields.description: Name of the group that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current group of the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership.

fields.label: keep releases
fields.description: The number of old releases to keep when cleaning. Used in O(state=finalize) and O(state=clean). Any unfinished builds are deleted first, so only correct releases count. The current version does not count.

fields.label: mode
fields.description: The permissions the resulting filesystem object should have. For those used to I(/usr/bin/chmod) remember that modes are actually octal numbers. You must give Ansible enough information to parse them correctly. For consistent results, quote octal numbers (for example, C('644') or C('1777')) so Ansible receives a string and can do its own conversion from string into number. Adding a leading zero (for example, C(0755)) works sometimes, but can fail in loops and some other circumstances. Giving Ansible a number without following either of these rules will end up with a decimal number which will have unexpected results. As of Ansible 1.8, the mode may be specified as a symbolic mode (for example, C(u+rwx) or C(u=rw,g=r,o=r)). If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does not) exist, the default C(umask) on the system will be used when setting the mode for the newly created filesystem object. If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does) exist, the mode of the existing filesystem object will be used. Specifying C(mode) is the best way to ensure filesystem objects are created with the correct permissions. See CVE-2020-1736 for further details.

fields.label: owner
fields.description: Name of the user that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership. Specifying a numeric username will be assumed to be a user ID and not a username. Avoid numeric usernames to avoid this confusion.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The root path of the project. Returned in the C(deploy_helper.project_path) fact.

fields.label: release
fields.description: The release version that is being deployed. Defaults to a timestamp format C(%Y%m%d%H%M%S) (for example V(20141119223359)). This parameter is optional during O(state=present), but needs to be set explicitly for O(state=finalize). You can use the generated fact C(release={{ deploy_helper.new_release }}).

fields.label: releases path
fields.description: The name of the folder that holds the releases. This can be relative to O(path) or absolute. Returned in the C(deploy_helper.releases_path) fact.

fields.label: selevel
fields.description: The level part of the SELinux filesystem object context. This is the MLS/MCS attribute, sometimes known as the C(range). When set to C(_default), it will use the C(level) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: serole
fields.description: The role part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(role) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: setype
fields.description: The type part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(type) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: seuser
fields.description: The user part of the SELinux filesystem object context. By default it uses the C(system) policy, where applicable. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(user) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: shared path
fields.description: The name of the folder that holds the shared resources. This can be relative to O(path) or absolute. If this is set to an empty string, no shared folder is created. Returned in the C(deploy_helper.shared_path) fact.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of the project. V(query) gathers facts. V(present) creates the project C(root) folder, and in it the C(releases) and C(shared) folders. V(finalize) removes the unfinished_filename file, creates a symlink to the newly deployed release and optionally cleans old releases. V(clean) removes failed & old releases. V(absent) removes the project folder (synonymous to the M(ansible.builtin.file) module with O(state=absent)).

fields.label: unfinished filename
fields.description: The name of the file that indicates a deploy has not finished. All folders in the O(releases_path) that contain this file are deleted on O(state=finalize) with O(clean=true), or O(state=clean). This file is automatically deleted from the C(new_release_path) during O(state=finalize).

fields.label: unsafe writes
fields.description: Influence when to use atomic operation to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem object. By default this module uses atomic operations to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem objects, but sometimes systems are configured or just broken in ways that prevent this. One example is docker mounted filesystem objects, which cannot be updated atomically from inside the container and can only be written in an unsafe manner. This option allows Ansible to fall back to unsafe methods of updating filesystem objects when atomic operations fail (however, it doesn't force Ansible to perform unsafe writes). IMPORTANT! Unsafe writes are subject to race conditions and can lead to data corruption.

fields.label: avatar url
fields.description: Overrides the default avatar of the webhook.

fields.label: content
fields.description: Content of the message to the Discord channel. At least one of O(content) and O(embeds) must be specified.

fields.label: embeds
fields.description: Send messages as Embeds to the Discord channel. Embeds can have a colored border, embedded images, text fields and more. Allowed parameters are described in the Discord Docs: U(https://discord.com/developers/docs/resources/channel#embed-object). At least one of O(content) and O(embeds) must be specified.

fields.label: tts
fields.description: Set this to V(true) if this is a TTS (Text to Speech) message.

fields.label: webhook id
fields.description: The webhook ID. Format from Discord webhook URL: C(/webhooks/{webhook.id}/{webhook.token}).

fields.label: webhook token
fields.description: The webhook token. Format from Discord webhook URL: C(/webhooks/{webhook.id}/{webhook.token}).

fields.label: apps
fields.description: Restrict checks to specific applications. Default is to check all applications.

fields.label: databases
fields.description: Specify databases to run checks against. If not specified, Django does not run database tests. The parameter has been renamed to O(databases) in community.general 11.3.0. The old name is still available as an alias.

fields.label: deploy
fields.description: Include additional checks relevant in a deployment setting.

fields.label: fail level
fields.description: Message level that triggers failure. Default is the Django default value. Check the documentation for the version being used.

fields.label: pythonpath
fields.description: Adds the given filesystem path to the Python import search path. The value is passed as is to the C(--pythonpath) argument in C(django-admin).

fields.label: settings
fields.description: Specifies the settings module to use. The value is passed as is to the C(--settings) argument in C(django-admin).

fields.label: skip checks
fields.description: Skips running system checks prior to running the command.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: Restrict checks to specific tags.

fields.label: traceback
fields.description: Provides a full stack trace in the output when a C(CommandError) is raised.

fields.label: venv
fields.description: Use the Python interpreter from this virtual environment. Pass the path to the root of the virtualenv, not the C(bin/) directory nor the C(python) executable.

fields.label: verbosity
fields.description: Specifies the amount of notification and debug information in the output of C(django-admin).

fields.label: command
fields.description: Django admin command. It must be a valid command accepted by C(python -m django) at the target system.

fields.label: extra args
fields.description: List of extra arguments passed to the django admin command.

fields.label: pythonpath
fields.description: Adds the given filesystem path to the Python import search path. The value is passed as is to the C(--pythonpath) argument in C(django-admin).

fields.label: settings
fields.description: Specifies the settings module to use. The value is passed as is to the C(--settings) argument in C(django-admin).

fields.description: Skips running system checks prior to running the command.

fields.label: traceback
fields.description: Provides a full stack trace in the output when a C(CommandError) is raised.

fields.label: venv
fields.description: Use the Python interpreter from this virtual environment. Pass the path to the root of the virtualenv, not the C(bin/) directory nor the C(python) executable.

fields.label: verbosity
fields.description: Specifies the amount of notification and debug information in the output of C(django-admin).

fields.label: database
fields.description: Specify the database to be used.

fields.label: pythonpath
fields.description: Adds the given filesystem path to the Python import search path. The value is passed as is to the C(--pythonpath) argument in C(django-admin).

fields.label: settings
fields.description: Specifies the settings module to use. The value is passed as is to the C(--settings) argument in C(django-admin).

fields.label: skip checks
fields.description: Skips running system checks prior to running the command.

fields.label: traceback
fields.description: Provides a full stack trace in the output when a C(CommandError) is raised.

fields.label: venv
fields.description: Use the Python interpreter from this virtual environment. Pass the path to the root of the virtualenv, not the C(bin/) directory nor the C(python) executable.

fields.label: verbosity
fields.description: Specifies the amount of notification and debug information in the output of C(django-admin).

fields.label: all
fields.description: Dump all records, including those which might otherwise be filtered or modified by a custom manager.

fields.label: apps models
fields.description: Dump only the applications and models listed in the dump. Format must be either V(app_label) or V(app_label.ModelName). If not passed, all applications and models are to be dumped.

fields.label: database
fields.description: Specify the database to be used.

fields.label: excludes
fields.description: Applications or models to be excluded. Format must be either V(app_label) or V(app_label.ModelName).

fields.label: fixture
fields.description: Path to the output file. The fixture filename may end with V(.bz2), V(.gz), V(.lzma) or V(.xz), in which case the corresponding compression format is used. This corresponds to the C(--output) parameter for the C(django-admin dumpdata) command.

fields.label: format
fields.description: Serialization format of the output data.

fields.label: indent
fields.description: Indentation size for the output. Default is not to indent, so the output is generated in one single line.

fields.label: natural foreign
fields.description: Use natural keys when serializing for foreign keys.

fields.label: natural primary
fields.description: Omit primary keys when serializing.

fields.label: primary keys
fields.description: List of primary keys to include in the dump. Only available when dumping one single model.

fields.label: pythonpath
fields.description: Adds the given filesystem path to the Python import search path. The value is passed as is to the C(--pythonpath) argument in C(django-admin).

fields.label: settings
fields.description: Specifies the settings module to use. The value is passed as is to the C(--settings) argument in C(django-admin).

fields.label: skip checks
fields.description: Skips running system checks prior to running the command.

fields.label: traceback
fields.description: Provides a full stack trace in the output when a C(CommandError) is raised.

fields.label: venv
fields.description: Use the Python interpreter from this virtual environment. Pass the path to the root of the virtualenv, not the C(bin/) directory nor the C(python) executable.

fields.label: verbosity
fields.description: Specifies the amount of notification and debug information in the output of C(django-admin).

fields.label: app
fields.description: Specifies a single app to look for fixtures in rather than looking in all apps.

fields.label: database
fields.description: Specify the database to be used.

fields.label: excludes
fields.description: Applications or models to be excluded. Format must be either V(app_label) or V(app_label.ModelName).

fields.label: fixtures
fields.description: List of paths to the fixture files.

fields.label: format
fields.description: Serialization format of the output data.

fields.label: ignore non existent
fields.description: Ignores fields and models that may have been removed since the fixture was originally generated.

fields.label: pythonpath
fields.description: Adds the given filesystem path to the Python import search path. The value is passed as is to the C(--pythonpath) argument in C(django-admin).

fields.label: settings
fields.description: Specifies the settings module to use. The value is passed as is to the C(--settings) argument in C(django-admin).

fields.label: skip checks
fields.description: Skips running system checks prior to running the command.

fields.label: traceback
fields.description: Provides a full stack trace in the output when a C(CommandError) is raised.

fields.label: venv
fields.description: Use the Python interpreter from this virtual environment. Pass the path to the root of the virtualenv, not the C(bin/) directory nor the C(python) executable.

fields.label: verbosity
fields.description: Specifies the amount of notification and debug information in the output of C(django-admin).

fields.label: apps
fields.description: A list of space-delimited apps to target. Used by the V(test) command.

fields.label: cache table
fields.description: The name of the table used for database-backed caching. Used by the V(createcachetable) command.

fields.label: clear
fields.description: Clear the existing files before trying to copy or link the original file. Used only with the V(collectstatic) command. The C(--noinput) argument is added automatically.

fields.label: command
fields.description: The name of the Django management command to run. The commands listed below are built in this module and have some basic parameter validation. V(collectstatic) - Collects the static files into C(STATIC_ROOT). V(createcachetable) - Creates the cache tables for use with the database cache backend. V(flush) - Removes all data from the database. V(loaddata) - Searches for and loads the contents of the named O(fixtures) into the database. V(migrate) - Synchronizes the database state with models and migrations. V(test) - Runs tests for all installed apps. Custom commands can be entered, but they fail unless they are known to Django. Custom commands that may prompt for user input should be run with the C(--noinput) flag. Support for the values V(cleanup), V(syncdb), V(validate) was removed in community.general 9.0.0. See note about supported versions of Django.

fields.label: database
fields.description: The database to target. Used by the V(createcachetable), V(flush), V(loaddata), V(syncdb), and V(migrate) commands.

fields.label: failfast
fields.description: Fail the command immediately if a test fails. Used by the V(test) command.

fields.label: fixtures
fields.description: A space-delimited list of fixture file names to load in the database. B(Required) by the V(loaddata) command.

fields.label: link
fields.description: Creates links to the files instead of copying them, you can only use this parameter with V(collectstatic) command.

fields.label: merge
fields.description: Runs out-of-order or missing migrations as they are not rollback migrations, you can only use this parameter with V(migrate) command.

fields.label: project path
fields.description: The path to the root of the Django application where C(manage.py) lives.

fields.label: pythonpath
fields.description: A directory to add to the Python path. Typically used to include the settings module if it is located external to the application directory. This would be equivalent to adding O(pythonpath)'s value to the E(PYTHONPATH) environment variable.

fields.label: settings
fields.description: The Python path to the application's settings module, such as V(myapp.settings).

fields.label: skip
fields.description: Skips over out-of-order missing migrations, you can only use this parameter with V(migrate) command.

fields.label: testrunner
fields.description: Controls the test runner class that is used to execute tests. This parameter is passed as-is to C(manage.py).

fields.label: virtualenv
fields.description: An optional path to a C(virtualenv) installation to use while running the manage application. The virtual environment must exist, otherwise the module fails.

modules.label: dnf_config_manager
modules.tooltip: Enable or disable dnf repositories using config-manager

fields.label: name
fields.description: Repository ID, for example V(crb).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the repositories should be V(enabled) or V(disabled).

modules.label: dnf_versionlock
modules.tooltip: Locks package versions in C(dnf) based systems

fields.label: name
fields.description: Package name spec to add or exclude to or delete from the C(locklist) using the format expected by the C(dnf repoquery) command. This parameter is mutually exclusive with O(state=clean).

fields.label: raw
fields.description: Do not resolve package name specs to NEVRAs to find specific version to lock to. Instead the package name specs are used as they are. This enables locking to not yet available versions of the package.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to add (V(present) or V(excluded)) to or remove (V(absent) or V(clean)) from the C(locklist). V(present) adds a package name spec to the C(locklist). If there is a installed package that matches, then only that version is added. Otherwise, all available package versions are added. V(excluded) adds a package name spec as excluded to the C(locklist). It means that packages represented by the package name spec are excluded from transaction operations. All available package versions are added. V(absent) deletes entries in the C(locklist) that match the package name spec. V(clean) deletes all entries in the C(locklist). This option is mutually exclusive with O(name).

modules.label: dnsimple
modules.tooltip: Interface with dnsimple.com (a DNS hosting service)

fields.label: account api token
fields.description: Account API token. See O(account_email) for more information.

fields.label: account email
fields.description: Account email. If omitted, the environment variables E(DNSIMPLE_EMAIL) and E(DNSIMPLE_API_TOKEN) are looked for. If those variables are not found, a C(.dnsimple) file is looked for, see: U(https://github.com/mikemaccana/dnsimple-python#getting-started). C(.dnsimple) config files are only supported in dnsimple-python<2.0.0.

fields.label: domain
fields.description: Domain to work with. Can be the domain name (for example V(mydomain.com)) or the numeric ID of the domain in DNSimple. If omitted, a list of domains is returned. If domain is present but the domain does not exist, it is created.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: Record priority.

fields.label: record
fields.description: Record to add, if blank a record for the domain is created, supports the wildcard (*).

fields.label: record ids
fields.description: List of records to ensure they either exist or do not exist.

fields.label: sandbox
fields.description: Use the DNSimple sandbox environment. Requires a dedicated account in the dnsimple sandbox environment. Check U(https://developer.dnsimple.com/sandbox/) for more information.

fields.label: solo
fields.description: Whether the record should be the only one for that record type and record name. Only use with O(state) is set to V(present) on a record.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the record should exist or not.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: The TTL to give the new record in seconds.

fields.label: type
fields.description: The type of DNS record to create.

fields.label: value
fields.description: Record value. Must be specified when trying to ensure a record exists.

modules.label: dnsimple_info
modules.tooltip: Pull basic info from DNSimple API

fields.label: account id
fields.description: The account ID to query.

fields.label: api key
fields.description: The API key to use.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The domain name to retrieve info from. Returns all associated records for this domain if specified. If not specified, returns all domains associated with the account ID.

fields.label: record
fields.description: The record to find. If specified, only this record is returned instead of all records.

fields.label: sandbox
fields.description: Whether or not to use sandbox environment.

modules.label: dnsmadeeasy
modules.tooltip: Interface with dnsmadeeasy.com (a DNS hosting service)

fields.label: account key
fields.description: Account API Key.

fields.label: account secret
fields.description: Account Secret Key.

fields.label: autoFailover
fields.description: If true, fallback to the primary IP address is manual after a failover. If false, fallback to the primary IP address is automatic after a failover.

fields.label: contactList
fields.description: Name or ID of the contact list that the monitor notifies. The default V('') means the Account Owner.

fields.label: domain
fields.description: Domain to work with. Can be the domain name (for example V(mydomain.com)) or the numeric ID of the domain in DNS Made Easy (for example V(839989)) for faster resolution.

fields.label: failover
fields.description: If V(true), add or change the failover. This is applicable only for A records.

fields.label: httpFile
fields.description: The file at the Fqdn that the monitor queries for HTTP or HTTPS.

fields.label: httpFqdn
fields.description: The fully qualified domain name used by the monitor.

fields.label: httpQueryString
fields.description: The string in the httpFile that the monitor queries for HTTP or HTTPS.

fields.label: ip1
fields.description: Primary IP address for the failover. Required if adding or changing the monitor or failover.

fields.label: ip2
fields.description: Secondary IP address for the failover. Required if adding or changing the failover.

fields.label: ip3
fields.description: Tertiary IP address for the failover.

fields.label: ip4
fields.description: Quaternary IP address for the failover.

fields.label: ip5
fields.description: Quinary IP address for the failover.

fields.label: maxEmails
fields.description: Number of emails sent to the contact list by the monitor.

fields.label: monitor
fields.description: If V(true), add or change the monitor. This is applicable only for A records.

fields.label: protocol
fields.description: Protocol used by the monitor.

fields.label: record name
fields.description: Record name to get/create/delete/update. If O(record_name) is not specified; all records for the domain are returned in "result" regardless of the state argument.

fields.label: record ttl
fields.description: Record's "Time-To-Live". Number of seconds the record remains cached in DNS servers.

fields.label: record type
fields.description: Record type.

fields.label: record value
fields.description: Record value. HTTPRED: <redirection URL>, MX: <priority> <target name>, NS: <name server>, PTR: <target name>, SRV: <priority> <weight> <port> <target name>, TXT: <text value>". If O(record_value) is not specified; no changes are made and the record is returned in RV(ignore:result) (in other words, this module can be used to fetch a record's current ID, type, and TTL).

fields.label: sandbox
fields.description: Decides if the sandbox API should be used. Otherwise (default) the production API of DNS Made Easy is used.

fields.label: sensitivity
fields.description: Number of checks the monitor performs before a failover occurs where Low = 8, Medium = 5,and High = 3.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the record should exist or not.

fields.label: systemDescription
fields.description: Description used by the monitor.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

modules.label: dpkg_divert
modules.tooltip: Override a debian package's version of a file

fields.label: divert
fields.description: The location where the versions of file are diverted. Default is to add suffix C(.distrib) to the file path. This parameter is ignored when O(state=absent).

fields.label: force
fields.description: When O(rename=true) and O(force=true), renaming is performed even if the target of the renaming exists, in other words the existing contents of the file at this location are lost. This parameter is ignored when O(rename=false).

fields.label: holder
fields.description: The name of the package whose copy of file is not diverted, also known as the diversion holder or the package the diversion belongs to. The actual package does not have to be installed or even to exist for its name to be valid. If not specified, the diversion is hold by 'LOCAL', that is reserved by/for dpkg for local diversions. This parameter is ignored when O(state=absent).

fields.label: path
fields.description: The original and absolute path of the file to be diverted or undiverted. This path is unique, in other words it is not possible to get two diversions for the same O(path).

fields.label: rename
fields.description: Actually move the file aside (when O(state=present)) or back (when O(state=absent)), but only when changing the state of the diversion. This parameter has no effect when attempting to add a diversion that already exists or when removing an unexisting one. Unless O(force=true), renaming fails if the destination file already exists (this lock being a dpkg-divert feature, and bypassing it being a module feature).

fields.label: state
fields.description: When O(state=absent), remove the diversion of the specified O(path); when O(state=present), create the diversion if it does not exist, or update its package O(holder) or O(divert) location, if it already exists.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: The explicit executable or a pathname to the executable to be used to run easy_install for a specific version of Python installed in the system. For example V(easy_install-3.3), if there are both Python 2.7 and 3.3 installations in the system and you want to run easy_install for the Python 3.3 installation.

fields.label: name
fields.description: A Python library name.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The desired state of the library. V(latest) ensures that the latest version is installed.

fields.label: virtualenv
fields.description: An optional O(virtualenv) directory path to install into. If the O(virtualenv) does not exist, it is created automatically.

fields.label: virtualenv command
fields.description: The command to create the virtual environment with. For example V(pyvenv), V(virtualenv), V(virtualenv2).

fields.label: virtualenv site packages
fields.description: Whether the virtual environment inherits packages from the global site-packages directory. Note that this setting has no effect on an already existing virtual environment, so if you want to change it, the environment must be deleted and newly created.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Describe the desired state of the user to be managed.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Force batch mode when installing plugins. This is only necessary if a plugin requires additional permissions and console detection fails.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the plugin to install.

fields.label: plugin bin
fields.description: Location of the plugin binary. If this file is not found, the default plugin binaries are used.

fields.label: plugin dir
fields.description: Your configured plugin directory specified in Elasticsearch.

fields.label: proxy host
fields.description: Proxy host to use during plugin installation.

fields.label: proxy port
fields.description: Proxy port to use during plugin installation.

fields.label: src
fields.description: Optionally set the source location to retrieve the plugin from. This can be a C(file://) URL to install from a local file, or a remote URL. If this is not set, the plugin location is just based on the name. The name parameter must match the descriptor in the plugin ZIP specified. Is only used if the state would change, which is solely checked based on the name parameter. If, for example, the plugin is already installed, changing this has no effect. For ES 1.x use O(url).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of a plugin.

fields.label: url
fields.description: Set exact URL to download the plugin from (Only works for ES 1.x). For ES 2.x and higher, use src.

fields.label: version
fields.description: Version of the plugin to be installed. If plugin exists with previous version, it is NOT updated.

modules.label: emc_vnx_sg_member
modules.tooltip: Manage storage group member on EMC VNX

fields.label: lunid
fields.description: LUN ID to be added.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the Storage group to manage.

fields.label: sp address
fields.description: Address of the SP of target/secondary storage.

fields.label: sp password
fields.description: password for accessing SP.

fields.label: sp user
fields.description: Username for accessing SP.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicates the desired lunid state. V(present) ensures specified O(lunid) is present in the Storage Group. V(absent) ensures specified O(lunid) is absent from Storage Group.

modules.label: etcd3
modules.tooltip: Set or delete key value pairs from an etcd3 cluster

fields.label: ca cert
fields.description: The Certificate Authority to use to verify the etcd host. Required if O(client_cert) and O(client_key) are defined.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: PEM formatted certificate chain file to be used for SSL client authentication. Required if O(client_key) is defined.

fields.label: client key
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains your private key to be used for SSL client authentication. Required if O(client_cert) is defined.

fields.label: key
fields.description: The key where the information is stored in the cluster.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of the value for the key. Can be present or absent.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The etcd user to authenticate with.

fields.label: value
fields.description: The information stored.

modules.label: facter_facts
modules.tooltip: Runs the discovery program C(facter) on the remote system and return Ansible facts

fields.label: arguments
fields.description: Specifies arguments for facter.

modules.label: file_remove
modules.tooltip: Remove files matching a pattern from a directory

fields.label: file type
fields.description: Type of files to remove.

fields.label: path
fields.description: Path to the directory where files should be removed. This must be an existing directory.

fields.label: pattern
fields.description: Pattern to match files for removal. Supports wildcards (V(*), V(?), V([seq]), V([!seq])) for glob-style matching. Use O(use_regex=true) to interpret this as a regular expression instead.

fields.label: recursive
fields.description: If V(true), search for files recursively in subdirectories. If V(false), only files in the specified directory are removed.

fields.label: use regex
fields.description: If V(true), O(pattern) is interpreted as a regular expression. If V(false), O(pattern) is interpreted as a glob-style wildcard pattern.

modules.label: filesize
modules.tooltip: Create a file with a given size, or resize it if it exists

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: The attributes the resulting filesystem object should have. To get supported flags look at the man page for I(chattr) on the target system. This string should contain the attributes in the same order as the one displayed by I(lsattr). The C(=) operator is assumed as default, otherwise C(+) or C(-) operators need to be included in the string.

fields.label: blocksize
fields.description: Size of blocks, in bytes if not followed by a multiplicative suffix. The numeric value (before the unit) B(MUST) be an integer (or a C(float) if it equals an integer). If not set, the size of blocks is guessed from the OS and commonly results in V(512) or V(4096) bytes, that is used internally by the module or when O(size) has no unit.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Whether or not to overwrite the file if it exists, in other words, to truncate it from 0. When V(true), the module is not idempotent, that means it always reports RV(ignore:changed=true). O(force=true) and O(sparse=true) are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: group
fields.description: Name of the group that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current group of the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership.

fields.label: mode
fields.description: The permissions the resulting filesystem object should have. For those used to I(/usr/bin/chmod) remember that modes are actually octal numbers. You must give Ansible enough information to parse them correctly. For consistent results, quote octal numbers (for example, C('644') or C('1777')) so Ansible receives a string and can do its own conversion from string into number. Adding a leading zero (for example, C(0755)) works sometimes, but can fail in loops and some other circumstances. Giving Ansible a number without following either of these rules will end up with a decimal number which will have unexpected results. As of Ansible 1.8, the mode may be specified as a symbolic mode (for example, C(u+rwx) or C(u=rw,g=r,o=r)). If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does not) exist, the default C(umask) on the system will be used when setting the mode for the newly created filesystem object. If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does) exist, the mode of the existing filesystem object will be used. Specifying C(mode) is the best way to ensure filesystem objects are created with the correct permissions. See CVE-2020-1736 for further details.

fields.label: owner
fields.description: Name of the user that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership. Specifying a numeric username will be assumed to be a user ID and not a username. Avoid numeric usernames to avoid this confusion.

fields.label: path
fields.description: Path of the regular file to create or resize.

fields.label: selevel
fields.description: The level part of the SELinux filesystem object context. This is the MLS/MCS attribute, sometimes known as the C(range). When set to C(_default), it will use the C(level) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: serole
fields.description: The role part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(role) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: setype
fields.description: The type part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(type) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: seuser
fields.description: The user part of the SELinux filesystem object context. By default it uses the C(system) policy, where applicable. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(user) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: size
fields.description: Requested size of the file. The value is a number (either C(int) or C(float)) optionally followed by a multiplicative suffix, that can be one of V(B) (bytes), V(KB) or V(kB) (= 1000B), V(MB) or V(mB) (= 1000kB), V(GB) or V(gB) (= 1000MB), and so on for V(T), V(P), V(E), V(Z) and V(Y); or alternatively one of V(K), V(k) or V(KiB) (= 1024B); V(M), V(m) or V(MiB) (= 1024KiB); V(G), V(g) or V(GiB) (= 1024MiB); and so on. If the multiplicative suffix is not provided, the value is treated as an integer number of blocks of O(blocksize) bytes each (float values are rounded to the closest integer). When the O(size) value is equal to the current file size, does nothing. When the O(size) value is bigger than the current file size, bytes from O(source) (if O(sparse) is not V(false)) are appended to the file without truncating it, in other words, without modifying the existing bytes of the file. When the O(size) value is smaller than the current file size, it is truncated to the requested value without modifying bytes before this value. That means that a file of any arbitrary size can be grown to any other arbitrary size, and then resized down to its initial size without modifying its initial content.

fields.label: source
fields.description: Device or file that provides input data to provision the file. This parameter is ignored when O(sparse=true).

fields.label: sparse
fields.description: Whether or not the file to create should be a sparse file. This option is effective only on newly created files, or when growing a file, only for the bytes to append. This option is not supported on OSes or filesystems not supporting sparse files. O(force=true) and O(sparse=true) are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: unsafe writes
fields.description: This option is silently ignored. This module always modifies file size in-place.

fields.label: dev
fields.description: Target path to block device (Linux) or character device (FreeBSD) or regular file (both). When setting Linux-specific filesystem types on FreeBSD, this module only works when applying to regular files, also known as disk images. Currently V(lvm) (Linux-only) and V(ufs) (FreeBSD-only) do not support a regular file as their target O(dev). Support for character devices on FreeBSD has been added in community.general 3.4.0.

fields.label: force
fields.description: If V(true), allows to create new filesystem on devices that already has filesystem.

fields.label: fstype
fields.description: Filesystem type to be created. This option is required with O(state=present) (or if O(state) is omitted). V(ufs) support has been added in community.general 3.4.0. V(bcachefs) support has been added in community.general 8.6.0. V(gfs2) support has been added in community.general 13.1.0.

fields.label: label
fields.description: Set filesystem's label to the given value. The label options specified in O(opts) take precedence over this value. Supported for O(fstype) being one of V(gfs2). Support for other filesystems might be added later. On GFS2 this sets the lock table with the C(-t) option of mkfs.gfs2(8). It has to be in the form V(CLUSTERNAME:LOCKSPACE).

fields.label: opts
fields.description: List of options to be passed to C(mkfs) command.

fields.label: resizefs
fields.description: If V(true), if the block device and filesystem size differ, grow the filesystem into the space. Supported when O(fstype) is one of: V(bcachefs), V(btrfs), V(ext2), V(ext3), V(ext4), V(ext4dev), V(f2fs), V(lvm), V(xfs), V(ufs) and V(vfat). Attempts to resize other filesystem types fail. XFS only grows if mounted. Currently, the module is based on commands from C(util-linux) package to perform operations, so resizing of XFS is not supported on FreeBSD systems. VFAT is likely to fail if C(fatresize < 1.04). Mutually exclusive with O(uuid).

fields.label: state
fields.description: If O(state=present), the filesystem is created if it does not already exist, that is the default behaviour if O(state) is omitted. If O(state=absent), filesystem signatures on O(dev) are wiped if it contains a filesystem (as known by C(blkid)). When O(state=absent), all other options but O(dev) are ignored, and the module does not fail if the device O(dev) does not actually exist.

fields.label: uuid
fields.description: Set filesystem's UUID to the given value. The UUID options specified in O(opts) take precedence over this value. See xfs_admin(8) (C(xfs)), tune2fs(8) (C(ext2), C(ext3), C(ext4), C(ext4dev)) for possible values. For O(fstype=lvm) the value is ignored, it resets the PV UUID if set. Supported for O(fstype) being one of V(bcachefs), V(ext2), V(ext3), V(ext4), V(ext4dev), V(lvm), V(gfs2) or V(xfs). This is B(not idempotent). Specifying this option always results in a change. Mutually exclusive with O(resizefs).

fields.label: executable
fields.description: The path to the C(flatpak) executable to use. By default, this module looks for the C(flatpak) executable on the path.

fields.label: from url
fields.description: A C(http://) or C(https://) URL pointing to a C(.flatpakref) file to install from. When this option is set, O(name) must contain exactly one entry specifying the reverse DNS application ID of the flatpak (for example V(com.onepassword.OnePassword)). This is used to check whether the flatpak is already installed. O(name) and O(from_url) cannot both contain URLs. This option is recommended instead of passing a URL in O(name); passing URLs in O(name) will be deprecated in the future.

fields.label: method
fields.description: The installation method to use. Defines if the C(flatpak) is supposed to be installed globally for the whole V(system) or only for the current V(user).

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the flatpak to manage. To operate on several packages this can accept a list of packages. Should be specified as the unique reverse DNS name that identifies a flatpak (for example V(org.gnome.gedit)). When supplying a reverse DNS name, you can use the O(remote) option to specify on what remote to look for the flatpak. When used with O(state=present), O(name) can also be specified as a C(https://) or C(http://) URL to a C(flatpakref) file. However, it is recommended you use O(from_url) instead to get reliable idempotency. Passing URLs in O(name) will be deprecated in the future. When used with O(state=absent) or O(state=latest), always specify the name in the reverse DNS format. When supplying a URL with O(state=absent) or O(state=latest), the module tries to match the installed flatpak based on the name of the flatpakref to remove or update it. However, there is no guarantee that the names of the flatpakref file and the reverse DNS name of the installed flatpak do match.

fields.label: no dependencies
fields.description: If installing runtime dependencies should be omitted or not. This parameter is primarily implemented for integration testing this module. There might however be some use cases where you would want to have this, like when you are packaging your own flatpaks.

fields.label: remote
fields.description: The flatpak remote (repository) to install the flatpak from. By default, V(flathub) is assumed, but you do need to add the flathub flatpak_remote before you can use this. See the M(community.general.flatpak_remote) module for managing flatpak remotes.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicates the desired package state. The value V(latest) is supported since community.general 8.6.0.

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Indicates whether this remote is enabled.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: The path to the C(flatpak) executable to use. By default, this module looks for the C(flatpak) executable on the path.

fields.label: flatpakrepo url
fields.description: The URL to the C(flatpakrepo) file representing the repository remote to add. When used with O(state=present), the flatpak remote specified under the O(flatpakrepo_url) is added using the specified installation O(method). When used with O(state=absent), this is not required. Required when O(state=present).

fields.label: method
fields.description: The installation method to use. Defines if the C(flatpak) is supposed to be installed globally for the whole V(system) or only for the current V(user).

fields.label: name
fields.description: The desired name for the flatpak remote to be registered under on the managed host. When used with O(state=present), the remote is added to the managed host under the specified O(name). When used with O(state=absent) the remote with that name is removed.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicates the desired package state.

fields.label: api key
fields.description: Account API token. Note that these type of keys are deprecated and might stop working at some point. Use personal access tokens instead. One of O(personal_access_token) and O(api_key) must be specified.

fields.label: domain
fields.description: The name of the Domain to work with (for example, V(example.com)).

fields.label: personal access token
fields.description: Scoped API token. One of O(personal_access_token) and O(api_key) must be specified.

fields.label: record
fields.description: Record to add.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the record(s) should exist or not.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: The TTL to give the new record. Required when O(state=present).

fields.label: type
fields.description: The type of DNS record to create.

fields.label: values
fields.description: The record values. Required when O(state=present).

fields.label: config source
fields.description: Specify a configuration source to use rather than the default path.

fields.label: direct
fields.description: Access the config database directly, bypassing server. If O(direct) is specified then the O(config_source) must be specified as well.

fields.label: key
fields.description: A GConf preference key is an element in the GConf repository that corresponds to an application preference.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The action to take upon the key/value.

fields.label: value
fields.description: Preference keys typically have simple values such as strings, integers, or lists of strings and integers. This is ignored unless O(state=present).

fields.label: value type
fields.description: The type of value being set. This is ignored unless O(state=present).

fields.label: key
fields.description: The key name for an element in the GConf database.

fields.label: bindir
fields.description: Install executables into a specific directory.

fields.label: build flags
fields.description: Allow adding build flags for gem compilation.

fields.label: env shebang
fields.description: Rewrite the shebang line on installed scripts to use /usr/bin/env.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: Override the path to the gem executable.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Force gem to (un-)install, bypassing dependency checks.

fields.label: gem source
fields.description: The path to a local gem used as installation source.

fields.label: include dependencies
fields.description: Whether to include dependencies or not.

fields.label: include doc
fields.description: Install with or without docs.

fields.label: install dir
fields.description: Install the gems into a specific directory. These gems are independent from the global installed ones. Specifying this requires user_install to be false.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the gem to be managed.

fields.label: norc
fields.description: Avoid loading any C(.gemrc) file. Ignored for RubyGems prior to 2.5.2. The default changed from V(false) to V(true) in community.general 6.0.0.

fields.label: override platform install dir
fields.description: Resolve the user gem installation directory via C(gem environment) and pass it explicitly as C(--install-dir) to both C(gem install) and C(gem uninstall), instead of using C(--user-install). This is needed on distributions (such as Fedora) where a platform-specific C(operating_system.rb) injects C(--install-dir) as a default for all gem commands, which conflicts with C(--user-install) and causes C(gem uninstall) to search the wrong directory. Cannot be combined with O(user_install=false) or O(install_dir).

fields.label: pre release
fields.description: Allow installation of pre-release versions of the gem.

fields.label: repository
fields.description: The repository from which the gem is installed.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The desired state of the gem. V(latest) ensures that the latest version is installed.

fields.label: user install
fields.description: Install gem in user's local gems cache or for all users.

fields.label: version
fields.description: Version of the gem to be installed/removed.

modules.label: gio_mime
modules.tooltip: Set default handler for MIME type, for applications using Gnome GIO

fields.label: handler
fields.description: Default handler set for the MIME type.

fields.label: mime type
fields.description: MIME type for which a default handler is set.

fields.label: add mode
fields.description: Specify if a value should replace the existing value(s) or if the new value should be added alongside other values with the same name. This option is only relevant when adding/replacing values. If O(state=absent) or values are just read out, this option is not considered.

fields.label: file
fields.description: Path to an adhoc git configuration file to be managed using the V(file) scope.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the setting.

fields.label: repo
fields.description: Path to a git repository for reading and writing values from a specific repo.

fields.label: scope
fields.description: Specify which scope to read/set values from. This is required when setting config values. If this is set to V(local), you must also specify the O(repo) parameter. If this is set to V(file), you must also specify the O(file) parameter. It defaults to system.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicates the setting should be set/unset. This parameter has higher precedence than O(value) parameter: when O(state=absent) and O(value) is defined, O(value) is discarded.

fields.label: value
fields.description: When specifying the name of a single setting, supply a value to set that setting to the given value. From community.general 11.0.0 on, O(value) is required if O(state=present). To read values, use the M(community.general.git_config_info) module instead.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the setting to read. If not provided, all settings are returned as RV(config_values).

fields.label: path
fields.description: Path to a git repository or file for reading values from a specific repo. If O(scope) is V(local), this must point to a repository to read from. If O(scope) is V(file), this must point to specific git config file to read from. Otherwise O(path) is ignored if set.

fields.label: scope
fields.description: Specify which scope to read values from. If set to V(global), the global git config is used. O(path) is ignored. If set to V(system), the system git config is used. O(path) is ignored. If set to V(local), O(path) must be set to the repo to read from. If set to V(file), O(path) must be set to the config file to read from.

modules.label: github_deploy_key
modules.tooltip: Manages deploy keys for GitHub repositories

fields.label: force
fields.description: If V(true), forcefully adds the deploy key by deleting any existing deploy key with the same public key or title.

fields.label: github url
fields.description: The base URL of the GitHub API.

fields.label: key
fields.description: The SSH public key to add to the repository as a deploy key.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name for the deploy key.

fields.label: otp
fields.description: The 6 digit One Time Password for 2-Factor Authentication. Required together with O(username) and O(password).

fields.label: owner
fields.description: The name of the individual account or organization that owns the GitHub repository.

fields.label: read only
fields.description: If V(true), the deploy key is only able to read repository contents. Otherwise, the deploy key is able to read and write.

fields.label: repo
fields.description: The name of the GitHub repository.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of the deploy key.

fields.label: token
fields.description: The OAuth2 token or personal access token to authenticate with. Mutually exclusive with O(password).

fields.label: action
fields.description: Get various details about issue depending upon action specified.

fields.label: issue
fields.description: Issue number for which information is required.

fields.label: organization
fields.description: Name of the GitHub organization in which the repository is hosted.

fields.label: repo
fields.description: Name of repository from which issue needs to be retrieved.

fields.label: api url
fields.description: URL to the GitHub API if not using github.com but your own GitHub Enterprise instance.

fields.label: force
fields.description: The default is V(true), which replaces the existing remote key if it is different than O(pubkey). If V(false), the key is only set if no key with the given O(name) exists.

fields.label: name
fields.description: SSH key name.

fields.label: pubkey
fields.description: SSH public key value. Required when O(state=present).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to remove a key, ensure that it exists, or update its value.

fields.label: token
fields.description: GitHub Access Token with permission to list and create public keys.

fields.label: action
fields.description: Action to perform.

fields.label: body
fields.description: Description of the release when creating a release.

fields.label: draft
fields.description: Sets if the release is a draft or not. (boolean).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of release when creating a release.

fields.label: prerelease
fields.description: Sets if the release is a prerelease or not. (boolean).

fields.label: repo
fields.description: Repository name.

fields.label: tag
fields.description: Tag name when creating a release. Required when using O(action=create_release).

fields.label: target
fields.description: Target of release when creating a release.

fields.label: token
fields.description: GitHub Personal Access Token for authenticating. Mutually exclusive with O(password).

fields.label: user
fields.description: The GitHub account that owns the repository.

fields.label: access token
fields.description: Token parameter for authentication. This is only needed when not using O(username) and O(password).

fields.label: api url
fields.description: URL to the GitHub API if not using github.com but you own instance.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Description for the repository. Defaults to empty if O(force_defaults=true), which is the default in this module. Defaults to empty if O(force_defaults=false) when creating a new repository. This is only used when O(state) is V(present).

fields.label: force defaults
fields.description: If V(true), overwrite current O(description) and O(private) attributes with defaults. The default value changed from V(true) to V(false) in community.general 13.0.0.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Repository name.

fields.label: organization
fields.description: Organization for the repository. When O(state=present), the repository is created in the current user profile.

fields.label: private

fields.label: vars
fields.description: When the list element is a simple key-value pair, C(masked), C(hidden), C(raw), and C(protected) are set to V(false). When the list element is a dict with the keys C(value), C(masked), C(hidden), C(raw), and C(protected), the user can have full control about whether a value should be masked, hidden, raw, protected, or a combination. Support for group variables requires GitLab >= 9.5. Support for environment_scope requires GitLab Premium >= 13.11. Support for protected values requires GitLab >= 9.3. Support for masked values requires GitLab >= 11.10. Support for hidden values requires GitLab >= 17.4, and was added in community.general 11.3.0. Support for raw values requires GitLab >= 15.7. A C(value) must be a string or a number. Field C(variable_type) must be a string with either V(env_var), which is the default, or V(file). When a value is masked, it must be in Base64 and have a length of at least 8 characters. See GitLab documentation on acceptable values for a masked variable (U(https://docs.gitlab.com/ce/ci/variables/#masked-variables)).

fields.description: Absolute path image to configure avatar. File size should not exceed 200 kb. This option is only used on creation, not for updates.

fields.label: build timeout
fields.description: Maximum number of seconds a CI job can run. If not specified on creation, GitLab imposes a default value.

fields.label: builds access level
fields.description: V(private) means that repository CI/CD is allowed only to project members. V(disabled) means that repository CI/CD is disabled. V(enabled) means that repository CI/CD is enabled.
