fields.description: The CA certificates bundle to use to verify GitLab server certificate.

fields.label: ci config path
fields.description: Custom path to the CI configuration file for this project.

fields.label: container registry access level
fields.description: V(private) means that container registry is allowed only to project members. V(disabled) means that container registry is disabled. V(enabled) means that container registry is enabled.

fields.label: default branch
fields.description: The default branch name for this project. For project creation, this option requires O(initialize_with_readme=true). For project update, the branch must exist. Supports project's default branch update since community.general 8.0.0.

fields.label: description
fields.description: An description for the project.

fields.label: environments access level
fields.description: V(private) means that deployment to environment is allowed only to project members. V(disabled) means that deployment to environment is disabled. V(enabled) means that deployment to environment is enabled.

fields.label: feature flags access level
fields.description: V(private) means that feature rollout is allowed only to project members. V(disabled) means that feature rollout is disabled. V(enabled) means that feature rollout is enabled.

fields.label: forking access level
fields.description: V(private) means that repository forks is allowed only to project members. V(disabled) means that repository forks are disabled. V(enabled) means that repository forks are enabled.

fields.label: group
fields.description: ID or the full path of the group of which this projects belongs to.

fields.label: import url
fields.description: Git repository which is imported into gitlab. GitLab server needs read access to this git repository.

fields.label: infrastructure access level
fields.description: V(private) means that configuring infrastructure is allowed only to project members. V(disabled) means that configuring infrastructure is disabled. V(enabled) means that configuring infrastructure is enabled.

fields.label: initialize with readme
fields.description: Initializes the project with a default C(README.md). Is only used when the project is created, and ignored otherwise.

fields.label: issues access level
fields.description: V(private) means that accessing issues tab is allowed only to project members. V(disabled) means that accessing issues tab is disabled. V(enabled) means that accessing issues tab is enabled. O(issues_access_level) and O(issues_enabled) are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: issues enabled
fields.description: Whether you want to create issues or not. O(issues_access_level) and O(issues_enabled) are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: lfs enabled
fields.description: Enable Git large file systems to manages large files such as audio, video, and graphics files.

fields.label: merge method
fields.description: What requirements are placed upon merges. Possible values are V(merge), V(rebase_merge) merge commit with semi-linear history, V(ff) fast-forward merges only.

fields.label: merge requests enabled
fields.description: If merge requests can be made or not.

fields.label: model registry access level
fields.description: V(private) means that accessing model registry tab is allowed only to project members. V(disabled) means that accessing model registry tab is disabled. V(enabled) means that accessing model registry tab is enabled.

fields.label: monitor access level
fields.description: V(private) means that monitoring health is allowed only to project members. V(disabled) means that monitoring health is disabled. V(enabled) means that monitoring health is enabled.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the project.

fields.label: only allow merge if all discussions are resolved
fields.description: All discussions on a merge request (MR) have to be resolved.

fields.label: only allow merge if pipeline succeeds
fields.description: Only allow merges if pipeline succeeded.

fields.label: packages enabled
fields.description: Enable GitLab package repository.

fields.label: pages access level
fields.description: V(private) means that accessing pages tab is allowed only to project members. V(disabled) means that accessing pages tab is disabled. V(enabled) means that accessing pages tab is enabled.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The path of the project you want to create, this is server_url/O(group)/O(path). If not supplied, O(name) is used.

fields.label: releases access level
fields.description: V(private) means that accessing release is allowed only to project members. V(disabled) means that accessing release is disabled. V(enabled) means that accessing release is enabled.

fields.label: remove source branch after merge

fields.label: repository access level

fields.label: security and compliance access level
fields.description: V(private) means that accessing security and complicance tab is allowed only to project members. V(disabled) means that accessing security and complicance tab is disabled. V(enabled) means that accessing security and complicance tab is enabled.

fields.label: service desk enabled

fields.label: snippets enabled

fields.label: squash option

fields.label: state

fields.label: topics
fields.description: A topic or list of topics to be assigned to a project. It is compatible with old GitLab server releases (versions before 14, correspond to C(tag_list)).

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether or not to validate SSL certs when supplying a HTTPS endpoint.

fields.label: visibility
fields.description: V(private) Project access must be granted explicitly for each user. V(internal) The project can be cloned by any logged in user. V(public) The project can be cloned without any authentication.

fields.label: wiki enabled
fields.description: If an wiki for this project should be available or not.

fields.label: access level
fields.description: Access level of the access token. The value V(planner) was added in community.general 11.3.0.

fields.label: api job token

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: The CA certificates bundle to use to verify GitLab server certificate.

fields.label: expires at
fields.description: Expiration date of the access token in C(YYYY-MM-DD) format. Make sure to quote this value in YAML to ensure it is kept as a string and not interpreted as a YAML date.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Access token's name.

fields.label: project
fields.description: ID or full path of project in the form of group/name.

fields.label: recreate
fields.description: Whether the access token is recreated if it already exists. When V(never) the token is never recreated. When V(always) the token is always recreated. When V(state_change) the token is recreated if there is a difference between desired state and actual state.

fields.label: scopes
fields.description: Scope of the access token. The values V(manage_runner) and V(self_rotate) were added in community.general 11.3.0.

fields.label: state
fields.description: When V(present) the access token is added to the project if it does not exist. When V(absent) it is removed from the project if it exists.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether or not to validate SSL certs when supplying a HTTPS endpoint.

fields.label: access level
fields.description: The access level for the user. Required if O(state=present), user state is set to present. V(owner) was added in community.general 10.6.0.

fields.label: api job token

fields.label: project
fields.description: The name (or full path) of the GitLab project the member is added to/removed from.

fields.label: purge users
fields.description: Adds/remove users of the given access_level to match the given O(gitlab_user)/O(gitlab_users_access) list. If omitted do not purge orphaned members. Is only used when O(state=present). V(owner) was added in community.general 10.6.0.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the member in the project. On V(present), it adds a user to a GitLab project. On V(absent), it removes a user from a GitLab project.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether or not to validate SSL certs when supplying a HTTPS endpoint.

fields.description: GitLab CI job token for logging in.

fields.label: api oauth token

fields.label: api password
fields.description: The password to use for authentication against the API.

fields.label: ca path

fields.label: project
fields.description: The path and name of the project.

fields.label: purge
fields.description: When set to V(true), all variables which are not untouched in the task are deleted.

fields.label: state

fields.label: validate certs

fields.label: vars
fields.description: When the list element is a simple key-value pair, C(masked), C(hidden), C(raw), and C(protected) are set to V(false). When the list element is a dict with the keys C(value), C(masked), C(hidden), C(raw), and C(protected), the user can have full control about whether a value should be masked, hidden, raw, protected, or a combination. Support for protected values requires GitLab >= 9.3. Support for masked values requires GitLab >= 11.10. Support for hidden values requires GitLab >= 17.4, and was added in community.general 11.3.0. Support for raw values requires GitLab >= 15.7. Support for environment_scope requires GitLab Premium >= 13.11. Support for variable_type requires GitLab >= 11.11. A C(value) must be a string or a number. Field C(variable_type) must be a string with either V(env_var), which is the default, or V(file). Field C(environment_scope) must be a string defined by scope environment. When a value is masked, it must be in Base64 and have a length of at least 8 characters. See GitLab documentation on acceptable values for a masked variable (https://docs.gitlab.com/ce/ci/variables/#masked-variables).

fields.label: allow force push
fields.description: Whether or not to allow force pushes to the protected branch.

fields.description: The name of the branch that needs to be protected. Can make use a wildcard character for like V(production/*) or just have V(main) or V(develop) as value.

fields.label: project
fields.description: The path and name of the project.

fields.label: push access level
fields.description: Access levels allowed to push.

fields.label: validate certs

modules.label: gitlab_runner
modules.tooltip: Create, modify and delete GitLab Runners

fields.label: access level
fields.description: Determines if a runner can pick up jobs only from protected branches. If O(access_level_on_creation) is not explicitly set to V(true), this option is ignored on registration and is only applied on updates. If set to V(not_protected), runner can pick up jobs from both protected and unprotected branches. If set to V(ref_protected), runner can pick up jobs only from protected branches. Before community.general 8.0.0 the default was V(ref_protected). This was changed to no default in community.general 8.0.0. If this option is not specified explicitly, GitLab uses V(not_protected) on creation, and the value set is not changed on any updates.

fields.label: access level on creation
fields.description: Whether the runner should be registered with an access level or not. If set to V(true), the value of O(access_level) is used for runner registration. If set to V(false), GitLab registers the runner with the default access level. The default of this option changed to V(true) in community.general 7.0.0. Before, it was V(false).

fields.label: active
fields.description: Define if the runners is immediately active after creation. Mutually exclusive with O(paused).

fields.label: api job token
fields.description: GitLab CI job token for logging in.

fields.label: api oauth token
fields.description: GitLab OAuth token for logging in.

fields.label: api password
fields.description: The password to use for authentication against the API.

fields.label: api token
fields.description: GitLab access token with API permissions.

fields.label: api url
fields.description: The resolvable endpoint for the API.

fields.label: api username
fields.description: The username to use for authentication against the API.

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: The CA certificates bundle to use to verify GitLab server certificate.

fields.label: description
fields.description: The unique name of the runner.

fields.label: group
fields.description: ID or full path of the group in the form group/subgroup. Mutually exclusive with O(owned) and O(project). Must be group's numeric ID if O(registration_token) is not set and O(state=present).

fields.label: locked
fields.description: Determines if the runner is locked or not.

fields.label: maximum timeout
fields.description: The maximum time that a runner has to complete a specific job. Use V(0) to disable the timeout. This is available since community.general 12.1.0.

fields.label: owned
fields.description: Searches only runners available to the user when searching for existing, when false admin token required. Mutually exclusive with O(project) since community.general 4.5.0. Mutually exclusive with O(group).

fields.label: paused
fields.description: Define if the runners is active or paused after creation. Mutually exclusive with O(active).

fields.label: project
fields.description: ID or full path of the project in the form of group/name. Mutually exclusive with O(owned) since community.general 4.5.0. Mutually exclusive with O(group). Must be project's numeric ID if O(registration_token) is not set and O(state=present).

fields.label: registration token
fields.description: The registration token is used to register new runners before GitLab 16.0. Required if O(state=present) for GitLab < 16.0. If set, the runner is created using the old runner creation workflow. If not set, the runner is created using the new runner creation workflow, introduced in GitLab 16.0. If not set, requires python-gitlab >= 4.0.0.

fields.label: run untagged
fields.description: Run untagged jobs or not.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Make sure that the runner with the same name exists with the same configuration or delete the runner with the same name.

fields.label: tag list
fields.description: The tags that apply to the runner.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether or not to validate SSL certs when supplying a HTTPS endpoint.

fields.label: access level
fields.description: The access level to the group. The value V(master) is an alias for V(maintainer).

fields.label: api job token
fields.description: GitLab CI job token for logging in.

fields.label: api oauth token
fields.description: GitLab OAuth token for logging in.

fields.label: api password
fields.description: The password to use for authentication against the API.

fields.label: api token
fields.description: GitLab access token with API permissions.

fields.label: api url
fields.description: The resolvable endpoint for the API.

fields.label: api username
fields.description: The username to use for authentication against the API.

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: The CA certificates bundle to use to verify GitLab server certificate.

fields.label: confirm
fields.description: Require confirmation.

fields.label: email
fields.description: The email that belongs to the user. Required only if O(state=present).

fields.label: external
fields.description: Define external parameter for this user.

fields.label: group
fields.description: ID or Full path of parent group in the form of group/name. Add user as a member to this group.

fields.label: isadmin
fields.description: Grant admin privileges to the user.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the user you want to create. Required only if O(state=present).

fields.label: overwrite identities
fields.description: Overwrite identities with identities added in this module. This means that all identities that the user has and that are not listed in O(identities) are removed from the user. This is only done if a list is provided for O(identities). To remove all identities, provide an empty list.

fields.label: reset password
fields.description: Whether the user can change its password or not.

fields.label: sshkey expires at
fields.description: The expiration date of the SSH public key in ISO 8601 format C(YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ). This is only used when creating or replacing SSH public keys.

fields.label: sshkey file
fields.description: The SSH public key itself.

fields.label: sshkey name
fields.description: The name of the SSH public key.

fields.label: sshkey update mode
fields.description: Controls how existing SSH keys with the same O(sshkey_name) are handled. Use V(create) to preserve the historical behavior and leave existing keys with the same name unchanged. Use V(update) to replace an existing key only when exactly one key with the same name exists and its key material differs. Use V(deduplicate) to delete all keys with the same name before creating the requested key. SSH key comments are ignored when comparing key material.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Create, delete or block a user.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether or not to validate SSL certs when supplying a HTTPS endpoint.

modules.label: golang_package
modules.tooltip: Manage Go packages with C(go install)

fields.label: executable
fields.description: Path to the C(go) binary. If not specified, the module looks for C(go) in E(PATH).

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of a Go package to install. Must be a full Go package path, for example V(golang.org/x/tools/cmd/stringer). A version can be specified inline using the V(@) suffix, for example V(golang.org/x/tools/cmd/stringer@v0.29.0). This allows pinning different versions when installing multiple packages. The inline V(@version) syntax cannot be combined with the O(version) parameter.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The desired state of the Go package.

fields.label: version
fields.description: The version to install, for example V(v1.55.2). The version must include the V(v) prefix as required by Go module versioning. Can only be used when O(name) contains a single package without an inline V(@version). When not specified and O(state=present), the latest version is installed for new packages.

fields.label: create new thread
fields.description: Controls behavior when O(thread_key) is set but no matching thread exists. When V(true), a new thread is started if no matching thread is found. When V(false), the message is only posted if a matching thread already exists, otherwise it fails. Only used when O(thread_key) is set.

fields.label: key
fields.description: The C(key) request parameter from the incoming webhook URL. Keep this value secret as it grants the ability to post to the space.

fields.label: space
fields.description: The identifier of the Chat space to post to, taken from the incoming webhook URL. For a webhook URL of the form C(https://chat.googleapis.com/v1/spaces/AAAA/messages?key=...&token=...), this is the C(AAAA) part.

fields.label: text
fields.description: The text of the message to send. Emoji must be supplied as Unicode characters (for example V(🚀)). The Chat API does not render C(:shortcode:) style emoji in plain text messages as they appear as literal text.

fields.label: thread key
fields.description: An arbitrary key used to start or reply to a message thread. When set, O(create_new_thread) controls the behavior when the thread is not found.

fields.label: token
fields.description: The C(token) request parameter from the incoming webhook URL. Keep this value secret as it grants the ability to post to the space.

modules.label: grove
modules.tooltip: Sends a notification to a grove.io channel

fields.label: channel token
fields.description: Token of the channel to post to.

fields.label: icon url
fields.description: Icon for the service.

fields.label: message content
fields.description: Message content. The alias O(ignore:message) has been removed in community.general 4.0.0.

fields.label: service
fields.description: Name of the service (displayed as the "user" in the message).

fields.label: url
fields.description: Service URL for the web client.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: app
fields.description: The app module. A name refers to a WSGI callable that should be found in the specified module.

fields.label: chdir
fields.description: Chdir to specified directory before apps loading.

fields.label: config
fields.description: Path to the gunicorn configuration file.

fields.label: pid
fields.description: A filename to use for the PID file. If not set and not found on the configuration file a tmp pid file is created to check a successful run of gunicorn.

fields.label: user
fields.description: Switch worker processes to run as this user.

fields.label: venv
fields.description: Path to the virtualenv directory.

fields.label: worker
fields.description: The type of workers to use. The default class (sync) should handle most "normal" types of workloads.

modules.label: haproxy
modules.tooltip: Enable, disable, and set weights for HAProxy backend servers using socket commands

fields.label: agent
fields.description: Disable/enable agent checks (depending on O(state) value).

fields.label: backend
fields.description: Name of the HAProxy backend pool. If this parameter is unset, it is auto-detected.

fields.label: drain
fields.description: Wait until the server has no active connections or until the timeout determined by O(wait_interval) and O(wait_retries) is reached. Continue only after the status changes to C(MAINT). This overrides the shutdown_sessions option.

fields.label: fail on not found
fields.description: Fail whenever trying to enable/disable a backend host that does not exist.

fields.label: health
fields.description: Disable/enable health checks (depending on O(state) value).

fields.label: shutdown sessions
fields.description: When disabling a server, immediately terminate all the sessions attached to the specified server. This can be used to terminate long-running sessions after a server is put into maintenance mode. Overridden by the drain option.

fields.label: socket
fields.description: Path to the HAProxy socket file.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of the provided backend host. Note that V(drain) state is supported only by HAProxy version 1.5 or later. When used on versions < 1.5, it is ignored.

fields.label: wait
fields.description: Wait until the server reports a status of C(UP) when O(state=enabled), status of C(MAINT) when O(state=disabled) or status of C(DRAIN) when O(state=drain).

fields.label: wait interval
fields.description: Number of seconds to wait between retries.

fields.label: wait retries
fields.description: Number of times to check for status after changing the state.

fields.label: weight
fields.description: The value passed in argument. If the value ends with the V(%) sign, then the new weight is relative to the initially configured weight. Relative weights are only permitted between 0 and 100% and absolute weights are permitted between 0 and 256.

modules.label: heroku_collaborator
modules.tooltip: Add or delete app collaborators on Heroku

fields.label: api key
fields.description: Heroku API key.

fields.label: apps
fields.description: List of Heroku App names.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Create or remove the heroku collaborator.

fields.label: suppress invitation
fields.description: Suppress email invitation when creating collaborator.

fields.label: user
fields.description: User ID or e-mail.

fields.label: clone
fields.description: If V(false), do not clone the repository if it does not exist locally.

fields.label: dest
fields.description: Absolute path of where the repository should be cloned to. This parameter is required, unless clone and update are set to no.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: Path to C(hg) executable to use. If not supplied, the normal mechanism for resolving binary paths is used.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Discards uncommitted changes. Runs C(hg update -C).

fields.label: purge
fields.description: Deletes untracked files. Runs C(hg purge).

fields.label: repo
fields.description: The repository address.

fields.label: revision
fields.description: Equivalent C(-r) option in hg command which could be the changeset, revision number, branch name or even tag.

fields.label: update
fields.description: If V(false), do not retrieve new revisions from the origin repository.

fields.label: force formula
fields.description: Force the package(s) to be treated as a formula (equivalent to C(brew --formula)). To install a cask, use the M(community.general.homebrew_cask) module.

fields.label: install options
fields.description: Options flags to install a package.

fields.label: name
fields.description: A list of names of packages to install/remove.

fields.label: path
fields.description: A V(:) separated list of paths to search for C(brew) executable. Since a package (I(formula) in homebrew parlance) location is prefixed relative to the actual path of C(brew) command, providing an alternative C(brew) path enables managing different set of packages in an alternative location in the system.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the package.

fields.label: update homebrew
fields.description: Update homebrew itself first.

fields.label: upgrade all
fields.description: Upgrade all homebrew packages.

fields.label: upgrade options
fields.description: Option flags to upgrade.

fields.label: accept external apps
fields.description: Allow external apps.

fields.label: greedy
fields.description: Upgrade casks that auto update. Passes C(--greedy) to C(brew outdated --cask) when checking if an installed cask has a newer version available, or to C(brew upgrade --cask) when upgrading all casks.

fields.label: install options
fields.description: Options flags to install a package.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of cask to install or remove.

fields.label: path
fields.description: ':' separated list of paths to search for 'brew' executable.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the cask.

fields.label: sudo password
fields.description: The sudo password to be passed to E(SUDO_ASKPASS).

fields.label: update homebrew
fields.description: Update homebrew itself first. Note that C(brew cask update) is a synonym for C(brew update).

fields.label: upgrade all
fields.description: Upgrade all casks. Mutually exclusive with C(upgraded) state.

fields.label: name
fields.description: An installed homebrew package whose service is to be updated.

fields.label: path
fields.description: A V(:) separated list of paths to search for C(brew) executable. Since a package (I(formula) in homebrew parlance) location is prefixed relative to the actual path of C(brew) command, providing an alternative C(brew) path enables managing different set of packages in an alternative location in the system.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the package's service.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The GitHub user/organization repository to tap.

fields.label: path
fields.description: A V(:) separated list of paths to search for C(brew) executable.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the repository.

fields.label: url
fields.description: The optional git URL of the repository to tap. The URL is not assumed to be on GitHub, and the protocol does not have to be HTTP. Any location and protocol that git can handle is fine. O(name) option may not be a list of multiple taps (but a single tap instead) when this option is provided.

fields.label: disksize
fields.description: The intended home directory disk space. Human readable value such as V(10G), V(10M), or V(10B).

fields.label: email
fields.description: The email address of the user.

fields.label: environment
fields.description: String separated by comma each containing an environment variable and its value to set for the user's login session, in a format compatible with C(putenv(\)). Any environment variable listed here is automatically set by pam_systemd for all login sessions of the user.

fields.label: gid
fields.description: Sets the gid of the user. If using O(uid) homed requires the value to be the same. Only used when a user is first created.

fields.label: homedir
fields.description: Path to use as home directory for the user. This is the directory the user's home directory is mounted to while the user is logged in. This is not where the user's data is actually stored, see O(imagepath) for that. Only used when a user is first created.

fields.label: iconname
fields.description: The name of an icon picked by the user, for example for the purpose of an avatar. Should follow the semantics defined in the Icon Naming Specification. See U(https://specifications.freedesktop.org/icon-naming-spec/icon-naming-spec-latest.html) for specifics.

fields.label: imagepath
fields.description: Path to place the user's home directory. See U(https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/homectl.html#--image-path=PATH) for more information. Only used when a user is first created.

fields.label: language
fields.description: The preferred language/locale for the user. This should be in a format compatible with the E(LANG) environment variable.

fields.label: location
fields.description: A free-form location string describing the location of the user.

fields.label: locked
fields.description: Whether the user account should be locked or not.

fields.label: memberof
fields.description: String separated by comma each indicating a UNIX group this user shall be a member of. Groups the user should be a member of should be supplied as comma separated list.

fields.label: mountopts
fields.description: String separated by comma each indicating mount options for a users home directory. Valid options are V(nosuid), V(nodev) or V(noexec). Homed by default uses V(nodev) and V(nosuid) while V(noexec) is off.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The user name to create, remove, or update.

fields.label: notafter
fields.description: A time since the UNIX epoch after which the record should be considered invalid for the purpose of logging in.

fields.label: notbefore
fields.description: A time since the UNIX epoch before which the record should be considered invalid for the purpose of logging in.

fields.label: passwordhint
fields.description: Password hint for the given user.

fields.label: realm
fields.description: The 'realm' a user is defined in.

fields.label: realname
fields.description: The user's real ('human') name. This can also be used to add a comment to maintain compatibility with C(useradd).

fields.label: resize
fields.description: When used with O(disksize) this attempts to resize the home directory immediately.

fields.label: shell
fields.description: Shell binary to use for terminal logins of given user. If not specified homed by default uses V(/bin/bash).

fields.label: skeleton
fields.description: The absolute path to the skeleton directory to populate a new home directory from. This is only used when a home directory is first created. If not specified homed by default uses V(/etc/skel).

fields.label: sshkeys
fields.description: String separated by comma each listing a SSH public key that is authorized to access the account. The keys should follow the same format as the lines in a traditional C(~/.ssh/authorized_key) file.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The operation to take on the user.

fields.label: storage
fields.description: Indicates the storage mechanism for the user's home directory. If the storage type is not specified, C(homed.conf(5\)) defines which default storage to use. Only used when a user is first created.

fields.label: timezone
fields.description: Preferred timezone to use for the user. Should be a tzdata compatible location string such as V(America/New_York).

fields.label: uid
fields.description: Sets the UID of the user. If using O(gid) homed requires the value to be the same. Only used when a user is first created.

fields.label: umask
fields.description: Sets the umask for the user's login sessions. Value from V(0000) to V(0777).

fields.label: environment
fields.description: The environment name, typically V(production), V(staging), and so on.

fields.label: repo
fields.description: URL of the project repository.

fields.label: revision
fields.description: A hash, number, tag, or other identifier showing what revision was deployed.

fields.label: token
fields.description: API token.

fields.label: url
fields.description: Optional URL to submit the notification to.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The username of the person doing the deployment.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates for the target URL are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

modules.label: hpilo_boot
modules.tooltip: Boot system using specific media through HP iLO interface

fields.label: force
fields.description: Whether to force a reboot (even when the system is already booted). As a safeguard, without force, M(community.general.hpilo_boot) refuses to reboot a server that is already running.

fields.label: idempotent boot once
fields.description: This option makes O(state=boot_once) succeed instead of failing when the server is already powered on.

fields.label: image
fields.description: The URL of a cdrom, floppy or usb boot media image in the form V(protocol://username:password@hostname:port/filename). V(protocol) is either V(http) or V(https). V(username:password) is optional. V(port) is optional.

fields.label: login
fields.description: The login name to authenticate to the HP iLO interface.

fields.label: media
fields.description: The boot media to boot the system from.

fields.label: ssl version
fields.description: Change the ssl_version used.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of the boot media. V(no_boot): Do not boot from the device. V(boot_once): Boot from the device once and then notthereafter. V(boot_always): Boot from the device each time the server is rebooted. V(connect): Connect the virtual media device and set to boot_always. V(disconnect): Disconnects the virtual media device and set to no_boot. V(poweroff): Power off the server.

modules.label: hpilo_info
modules.tooltip: Gather information through an HP iLO interface

fields.label: login
fields.description: The login name to authenticate to the HP iLO interface.

fields.label: ssl version
fields.description: Change the ssl_version used.

modules.label: hponcfg
modules.tooltip: Configure HP iLO interface using C(hponcfg)

fields.label: executable
fields.description: Path to the hponcfg executable (C(hponcfg) which uses E(PATH)).

fields.label: minfw
fields.description: The minimum firmware level needed.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The XML file as accepted by C(hponcfg).

fields.label: verbose
fields.description: Run C(hponcfg) in verbose mode (-v).

modules.label: htpasswd
modules.tooltip: Manage user files for basic authentication

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: The attributes the resulting filesystem object should have. To get supported flags look at the man page for I(chattr) on the target system. This string should contain the attributes in the same order as the one displayed by I(lsattr). The C(=) operator is assumed as default, otherwise C(+) or C(-) operators need to be included in the string.

fields.label: create
fields.description: Used with O(state=present). If V(true), the file is created if it does not exist. Conversely, if set to V(false) and the file does not exist, it fails.

fields.label: group
fields.description: Name of the group that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current group of the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership.

fields.label: hash scheme
fields.description: Hashing scheme to be used. As well as the four choices listed here, you can also use any other hash supported by passlib, such as V(portable_apache22) and V(host_apache24); or V(md5_crypt) and V(sha256_crypt), which are Linux passwd hashes. Only some schemes in addition to the four choices below are compatible with Apache or Nginx, and supported schemes depend on C(passlib) version and its dependencies. See U(https://passlib.readthedocs.io/en/stable/lib/passlib.apache.html#passlib.apache.HtpasswdFile) parameter C(default_scheme). Some of the available choices might be: V(apr_md5_crypt), V(des_crypt), V(ldap_sha1), V(plaintext). B(WARNING): The module has no mechanism to determine the O(hash_scheme) of an existing entry, therefore, it does not detect whether the O(hash_scheme) has changed. If you want to change the scheme, you must remove the existing entry and then create a new one using the new scheme.

fields.label: mode
fields.description: The permissions the resulting filesystem object should have. For those used to I(/usr/bin/chmod) remember that modes are actually octal numbers. You must give Ansible enough information to parse them correctly. For consistent results, quote octal numbers (for example, C('644') or C('1777')) so Ansible receives a string and can do its own conversion from string into number. Adding a leading zero (for example, C(0755)) works sometimes, but can fail in loops and some other circumstances. Giving Ansible a number without following either of these rules will end up with a decimal number which will have unexpected results. As of Ansible 1.8, the mode may be specified as a symbolic mode (for example, C(u+rwx) or C(u=rw,g=r,o=r)). If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does not) exist, the default C(umask) on the system will be used when setting the mode for the newly created filesystem object. If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does) exist, the mode of the existing filesystem object will be used. Specifying C(mode) is the best way to ensure filesystem objects are created with the correct permissions. See CVE-2020-1736 for further details.

fields.label: name
fields.description: User name to add or remove.

fields.label: owner
fields.description: Name of the user that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership. Specifying a numeric username will be assumed to be a user ID and not a username. Avoid numeric usernames to avoid this confusion.

fields.label: path
fields.description: Path to the file that contains the usernames and passwords.

fields.label: selevel
fields.description: The level part of the SELinux filesystem object context. This is the MLS/MCS attribute, sometimes known as the C(range). When set to C(_default), it will use the C(level) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: serole
fields.description: The role part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(role) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: setype
fields.description: The type part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(type) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: seuser
fields.description: The user part of the SELinux filesystem object context. By default it uses the C(system) policy, where applicable. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(user) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the user entry should be present or not.

fields.label: unsafe writes
fields.description: Influence when to use atomic operation to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem object. By default this module uses atomic operations to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem objects, but sometimes systems are configured or just broken in ways that prevent this. One example is docker mounted filesystem objects, which cannot be updated atomically from inside the container and can only be written in an unsafe manner. This option allows Ansible to fall back to unsafe methods of updating filesystem objects when atomic operations fail (however, it doesn't force Ansible to perform unsafe writes). IMPORTANT! Unsafe writes are subject to race conditions and can lead to data corruption.

modules.label: hwc_ecs_instance
modules.tooltip: Creates a resource of Ecs/Instance in Huawei Cloud

fields.label: admin pass
fields.description: Specifies the initial login password of the administrator account for logging in to an ECS using password authentication. The Linux administrator is root, and the Windows administrator is Administrator. Password complexity requirements, consists of 8 to 26 characters. The password must contain at least three of the following character types 'uppercase letters, lowercase letters, digits, and special characters (V(!@$%^-_=+[{}]:,./?))'. The password cannot contain the username or the username in reverse. The Windows ECS password cannot contain the username, the username in reverse, or more than two consecutive characters in the username.

fields.label: availability zone
fields.description: Specifies the name of the AZ where the ECS is located.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Specifies the description of an ECS, which is a null string by default. Can contain a maximum of 85 characters. Cannot contain special characters, such as V(<) and V(>).

fields.label: domain
fields.description: The name of the Domain to scope to (Identity v3). Currently only domain names are supported, and not domain IDs.

fields.label: eip id
fields.description: Specifies the ID of the elastic IP address assigned to the ECS. Only elastic IP addresses in the DOWN state can be assigned.

fields.label: enable auto recovery
fields.description: Specifies whether automatic recovery is enabled on the ECS.

fields.label: enterprise project id
fields.description: Specifies the ID of the enterprise project to which the ECS belongs.

fields.label: flavor name
fields.description: Specifies the name of the system flavor.

fields.label: id
fields.description: The ID of resource to be managed.

fields.label: identity endpoint
fields.description: The Identity authentication URL.

fields.label: image id
fields.description: Specifies the ID of the system image.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the ECS name. Value requirements consists of 1 to 64 characters, including letters, digits, underscores (V(_)), hyphens (V(-)), periods (V(.)).

fields.label: project
fields.description: The name of the Tenant (Identity v2) or Project (Identity v3). Currently only project names are supported, and not project IDs.

fields.label: region
fields.description: The region to which the project belongs.

fields.label: security groups
fields.description: Specifies the security groups of the ECS. If this parameter is left blank, the default security group is bound to the ECS by default.

fields.label: server metadata
fields.description: Specifies the metadata of ECS to be created.

fields.label: server tags
fields.description: Specifies the tags of an ECS. When you create ECSs, one ECS supports up to 10 tags.

fields.label: ssh key name
fields.description: Specifies the name of the SSH key used for logging in to the ECS.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the given object should exist in Huawei Cloud.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The user name to login with. Currently only user names are supported, and not user IDs.

fields.label: user data
fields.description: Specifies the user data to be injected during the ECS creation process. Text, text files, and gzip files can be injected. The content to be injected must be encoded with base64. The maximum size of the content to be injected (before encoding) is 32 KB. For Linux ECSs, this parameter does not take effect when adminPass is used.

fields.label: vpc id
fields.description: Specifies the ID of the VPC to which the ECS belongs.

modules.label: hwc_evs_disk
modules.tooltip: Creates a resource of Evs/Disk in Huawei Cloud

fields.label: availability zone
fields.description: Specifies the AZ where you want to create the disk.

fields.label: backup id
fields.description: Specifies the ID of the backup that can be used to create a disk. This parameter is mandatory when you use a backup to create the disk.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Specifies the disk description. The value can contain a maximum of 255 bytes.

fields.label: domain
fields.description: The name of the Domain to scope to (Identity v3). Currently only domain names are supported, and not domain IDs.

fields.label: enable full clone
fields.description: If the disk is created from a snapshot and linked cloning needs to be used, set this parameter to True.

fields.label: enable scsi
fields.description: If this parameter is set to V(true), the disk device type is SCSI, which allows ECS OSs to directly access underlying storage media. SCSI reservation command is supported. If this parameter is set to V(false), the disk device type is VBD, which supports only simple SCSI read/write commands. If parameter enable_share is set to True and this parameter is not specified, shared SCSI disks are created. SCSI EVS disks cannot be created from backups, which means that this parameter cannot be True if backup_id has been specified.

fields.label: enable share
fields.description: Specifies whether the disk is shareable. The default value is False.

fields.label: encryption id
fields.description: Specifies the encryption ID. The length of it fixes at 36 bytes.

fields.label: enterprise project id
fields.description: Specifies the enterprise project ID. This ID is associated with the disk during the disk creation. If it is not specified, the disk is bound to the default enterprise project.

fields.label: id
fields.description: The ID of resource to be managed.

fields.label: identity endpoint
fields.description: The Identity authentication URL.

fields.label: image id
fields.description: Specifies the image ID. If this parameter is specified, the disk is created from an image. BMS system disks cannot be created from BMS images.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the disk name. The value can contain a maximum of 255 bytes.

fields.label: project
fields.description: The name of the Tenant (Identity v2) or Project (Identity v3). Currently only project names are supported, and not project IDs.

fields.label: region
fields.description: The region to which the project belongs.

fields.label: size
fields.description: Specifies the disk size, in GB. Its values are as follows, System disk 1 GB to 1024 GB, Data disk 10 GB to 32768 GB. This parameter is mandatory when you create an empty disk or use an image or a snapshot to create a disk. If you use an image or a snapshot to create a disk, the disk size must be greater than or equal to the image or snapshot size. This parameter is optional when you use a backup to create a disk. If this parameter is not specified, the disk size is equal to the backup size.

fields.label: snapshot id
fields.description: Specifies the snapshot ID. If this parameter is specified, the disk is created from a snapshot.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the given object should exist in Huawei Cloud.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The user name to login with. Currently only user names are supported, and not user IDs.

fields.label: volume type
fields.description: Specifies the disk type. Currently, the value can be SSD, SAS, or SATA. SSD specifies the ultra-high I/O disk type. SAS specifies the high I/O disk type. SATA specifies the common I/O disk type. If the specified disk type is not available in the AZ, the disk creation fails. If the EVS disk is created from a snapshot, the volume_type field must be the same as that of the snapshot's source disk.

fields.label: cidr
fields.description: The range of available subnets in the VPC.

fields.label: domain
fields.description: The name of the Domain to scope to (Identity v3). Currently only domain names are supported, and not domain IDs.

fields.label: id
fields.description: The ID of resource to be managed.

fields.label: identity endpoint
fields.description: The Identity authentication URL.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of vpc.

fields.label: project
fields.description: The name of the Tenant (Identity v2) or Project (Identity v3). Currently only project names are supported, and not project IDs.

fields.label: region
fields.description: The region to which the project belongs.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the given object should exist in VPC.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The user name to login with. Currently only user names are supported, and not user IDs.

fields.label: project

fields.label: next hop

fields.label: enterprise project id
fields.description: Specifies the enterprise project ID. When creating a security group, associate the enterprise project ID with the security group.s.

fields.label: id

fields.label: identity endpoint

fields.label: name

fields.label: project

modules.label: hwc_vpc_security_group_rule
modules.tooltip: Creates a resource of VPC/SecurityGroupRule in Huawei Cloud

fields.label: description
fields.description: Provides supplementary information about the security group rule. The value is a string of no more than 255 characters that can contain letters and digits.

fields.label: direction
fields.description: Specifies the direction of access control. The value can be egress or ingress.

fields.label: domain
fields.description: The name of the Domain to scope to (Identity v3). Currently only domain names are supported, and not domain IDs.

fields.label: ethertype
fields.description: Specifies the IP protocol version. The value can be IPv4 or IPv6. If you do not set this parameter, IPv4 is used by default.

fields.label: id
fields.description: The ID of resource to be managed.

fields.label: identity endpoint
fields.description: The Identity authentication URL.

fields.label: port range max
fields.description: Specifies the end port number. The value ranges from 1 to 65535. If the protocol is not icmp, the value cannot be smaller than the port_range_min value. An empty value indicates all ports.

fields.label: port range min
fields.description: Specifies the start port number. The value ranges from 1 to 65535. The value cannot be greater than the port_range_max value. An empty value indicates all ports.

fields.label: project
fields.description: The name of the Tenant (Identity v2) or Project (Identity v3). Currently only project names are supported, and not project IDs.

fields.label: protocol
fields.description: Specifies the protocol type. The value can be icmp, tcp, or udp. If the parameter is left blank, the security group supports all protocols.

fields.label: region
fields.description: The region to which the project belongs.

fields.label: remote group id
fields.description: Specifies the ID of the peer security group. The value is exclusive with parameter remote_ip_prefix.

fields.label: remote ip prefix
fields.description: Specifies the remote IP address. If the access control direction is set to egress, the parameter specifies the source IP address. If the access control direction is set to ingress, the parameter specifies the destination IP address. The value can be in the CIDR format or IP addresses. The parameter is exclusive with parameter remote_group_id.

fields.label: security group id
fields.description: Specifies the security group rule ID, which uniquely identifies the security group rule.

fields.label: state

modules.label: hwc_vpc_subnet
modules.tooltip: Creates a resource of VPC/Subnet in Huawei Cloud

fields.label: availability zone
fields.description: Specifies the AZ to which the subnet belongs. Cannot be changed after creating the subnet.

fields.label: cidr
fields.description: Specifies the subnet CIDR block. The value must be within the VPC CIDR block and be in CIDR format. The subnet mask cannot be greater than 28. Cannot be changed after creating the subnet.

fields.label: dhcp enable
fields.description: Specifies whether DHCP is enabled for the subnet. The value can be true (enabled) or false(disabled), and default value is true. If this parameter is set to false, newly created ECSs cannot obtain IP addresses, and usernames and passwords cannot be injected using Cloud-init.

fields.label: dns address
fields.description: Specifies the DNS server addresses for subnet. The address in the head is used first.

fields.label: domain
fields.description: The name of the Domain to scope to (Identity v3). Currently only domain names are supported, and not domain IDs.

fields.label: gateway ip
fields.description: Specifies the gateway of the subnet. The value must be an IP address in the subnet. Cannot be changed after creating the subnet.

fields.label: id
fields.description: The ID of resource to be managed.

fields.label: identity endpoint
fields.description: The Identity authentication URL.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specifies the subnet name. The value is a string of 1 to 64 characters that can contain letters, digits, underscores (V(_)), hyphens (V(-)), and periods (V(.)).

fields.label: project
fields.description: The name of the Tenant (Identity v2) or Project (Identity v3). Currently only project names are supported, and not project IDs.

fields.label: region
fields.description: The region to which the project belongs.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the given object should exist in Huawei Cloud.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The user name to login with. Currently only user names are supported, and not user IDs.

fields.label: vpc id
fields.description: Specifies the ID of the VPC to which the subnet belongs. Cannot be changed after creating the subnet.

fields.label: all services
fields.description: Whether downtimes should be created for all services of the matched host objects. If omitted, Icinga 2 does not create downtimes for all services of the matched host objects by default.

fields.description: Name of the author.

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: CA certificates bundle to use to verify the Icinga 2 server certificate.

fields.description: Schedule child downtimes.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: PEM formatted certificate chain file to be used for SSL client authentication. This file can also include the key as well, and if the key is included, C(client_key) is not required.

fields.label: client key
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains your private key to be used for SSL client authentication. If C(client_cert) contains both the certificate and key, this option is not required.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: A descriptive comment.

fields.label: duration

fields.description: End time of the downtime as UNIX timestamp.

fields.label: filter
fields.description: Filter expression limiting the objects to operate on.

fields.description: Variable names and values used in the filter expression.

fields.label: fixed
fields.description: Whether the downtime is fixed or flexible. If omitted, Icinga 2 creates a fixed downtime by default.

fields.label: force
fields.description: If C(yes) do not get a cached copy.

fields.label: force basic auth
fields.description: Credentials specified with I(url_username) and I(url_password) should be passed in HTTP Header.

fields.label: http agent
fields.description: Header to identify as, generally appears in web server logs.

fields.description: Name of the downtime object. This option has no effect for states other than V(absent).

fields.description: Use V(Host) for a host downtime and V(Service) for a service downtime. Use V(Downtime) and give the name of the downtime object you want to remove.

fields.label: start time
fields.description: Start time of the downtime as UNIX timestamp.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the downtime.

fields.label: trigger name
fields.description: Name of the downtime trigger.

fields.label: url

fields.label: url password
fields.description: The password for use in HTTP basic authentication. If the I(url_username) parameter is not specified, the I(url_password) parameter will not be used.

fields.label: url username
fields.description: The username for use in HTTP basic authentication. This parameter can be used without I(url_password) for sites that allow empty passwords

fields.label: use gssapi
fields.description: Use GSSAPI to perform the authentication, typically this is for Kerberos or Kerberos through Negotiate authentication. Requires the Python library L(gssapi,https://github.com/pythongssapi/python-gssapi) to be installed. Credentials for GSSAPI can be specified with I(url_username)/I(url_password) or with the GSSAPI env var C(KRB5CCNAME) that specified a custom Kerberos credential cache. NTLM authentication is C(not) supported even if the GSSAPI mech for NTLM has been installed.

fields.label: use proxy
fields.description: If C(no), it will not use a proxy, even if one is defined in an environment variable on the target hosts.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If C(no), SSL certificates will not be validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: name
fields.description: This is the feature name to enable or disable.

fields.label: state
fields.description: If set to V(present) and feature is disabled, then feature is enabled. If set to V(present) and feature is already enabled, then nothing is changed. If set to V(absent) and feature is enabled, then feature is disabled. If set to V(absent) and feature is already disabled, then nothing is changed.

fields.label: check command
fields.description: The command used to check if the host is alive.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: PEM formatted certificate chain file to be used for SSL client authentication. This file can also include the key as well, and if the key is included, O(client_key) is not required.

fields.label: client key
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains your private key to be used for SSL client authentication. If O(client_cert) contains both the certificate and key, this option is not required.

fields.label: display name
fields.description: The name used to display the host. If not specified, it defaults to the value of the O(name) parameter.

fields.label: force
fields.description: If C(yes) do not get a cached copy.

fields.label: force basic auth
fields.description: C(httplib2), the library used by Ansible's HTTP request code only sends authentication information when a webservice responds to an initial request with a 401 status. Since some basic auth services do not properly send a 401, logins may fail. This option forces the sending of the Basic authentication header upon initial request.

fields.label: http agent
fields.description: Header to identify as, generally appears in web server logs.

fields.label: ip
fields.description: The IP address of the host. This is no longer required since community.general 8.0.0.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name used to create / delete the host. This does not need to be the FQDN, but does needs to be unique.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Apply feature state.

fields.label: template
fields.description: The template used to define the host. Template cannot be modified after object creation.

fields.label: url
fields.description: HTTP, HTTPS, or FTP URL in the form V((http|https|ftp\)://[user[:pass]]@host.domain[:port]/path).

fields.label: url password
fields.description: The password for use in HTTP basic authentication. If the O(url_username) parameter is not specified, the O(url_password) parameter is not used.

fields.label: url username
fields.description: The username for use in HTTP basic authentication. This parameter can be used without O(url_password) for sites that allow empty passwords.

fields.label: use gssapi
fields.description: Use GSSAPI to perform the authentication, typically this is for Kerberos or Kerberos through Negotiate authentication. Requires the Python library L(gssapi,https://github.com/pythongssapi/python-gssapi) to be installed. Credentials for GSSAPI can be specified with I(url_username)/I(url_password) or with the GSSAPI env var C(KRB5CCNAME) that specified a custom Kerberos credential cache. NTLM authentication is C(not) supported even if the GSSAPI mech for NTLM has been installed.

fields.label: use proxy
fields.description: If V(false), it does not use a proxy, even if one is defined in an environment variable on the target hosts.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: variables
fields.description: Dictionary of variables.

fields.label: zone
fields.description: The zone from where this host should be polled.

This may be convenient to template simple requests, for anything complex use the M(ansible.builtin.template) module. You can collate multiple IMC XML fragments and they are processed sequentially in a single stream, the Cisco IMC output is subsequently merged. Parameter O(content) is mutual exclusive with parameter O(path).
