fields.label: bootdev
fields.description: Set boot device to use on next reboot. The choices for the device are: V(network) -- Request network boot. V(floppy) -- Boot from floppy. V(hd) -- Boot from hard drive. V(safe) -- Boot from hard drive, requesting 'safe mode'. V(optical) -- boot from CD/DVD/BD drive. V(setup) -- Boot into setup utility. V(default) -- remove any IPMI directed boot device request.

fields.label: key
fields.description: Encryption key to connect to the BMC in hex format.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Hostname or IP address of the BMC.

fields.label: persistent
fields.description: If set, ask that system firmware uses this device beyond next boot. Be aware many systems do not honor this.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to ensure that boot devices is desired. The choices for the state are: - present -- Request system turn on - absent -- Request system turn on.

fields.label: uefiboot
fields.description: If set, request UEFI boot explicitly. Strictly speaking, the spec suggests that if not set, the system should BIOS boot and offers no "do not care" option. In practice, this flag not being set does not preclude UEFI boot on any system I have encountered.

fields.label: user
fields.description: Username to use to connect to the BMC.

fields.label: key
fields.description: Encryption key to connect to the BMC in hex format.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Hostname or IP address of the BMC.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to ensure that the machine in desired state. The choices for state are: V(on) -- Request system turn on. V(off) -- Request system turn off without waiting for OS to shutdown. V(shutdown) -- Have system request OS proper shutdown. V(reset) -- Request system reset without waiting for OS. V(boot) -- If system is off, then V(on), else V(reset). Either this option or O(machine) is required.

fields.label: user
fields.description: Username to use to connect to the BMC.

modules.label: iptables_state
modules.tooltip: Save iptables state into a file or restore it from a file

fields.label: counters
fields.description: Save or restore the values of all packet and byte counters. When V(true), the module is not idempotent.

fields.label: ip version
fields.description: Which version of the IP protocol this module should apply to.

fields.label: modprobe
fields.description: Specify the path to the C(modprobe) program internally used by iptables related commands to load kernel modules. By default, V(/proc/sys/kernel/modprobe) is inspected to determine the executable's path.

fields.label: noflush
fields.description: For O(state=restored), ignored otherwise. If V(false), restoring iptables rules from a file flushes (deletes) all previous contents of the respective table(s). If V(true), the previous rules are left untouched (but policies are updated anyway, for all built-in chains).

fields.label: path
fields.description: The file the iptables state should be saved to. The file the iptables state should be restored from.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the firewall state should be saved (into a file) or restored (from a file).

fields.label: table
fields.description: When O(state=restored), restore only the named table even if the input file contains other tables. Fail if the named table is not declared in the file. When O(state=saved), restrict output to the specified table. If not specified, output includes all active tables.

fields.label: wait
fields.description: Wait N seconds for the xtables lock to prevent instant failure in case multiple instances of the program are running concurrently.

modules.label: ipwcli_dns
modules.tooltip: Manage DNS Records for Ericsson IPWorks using C(ipwcli)

fields.label: address
fields.description: The IP address for the A or AAAA record. Required for O(type=A) or O(type=AAAA).

fields.label: container
fields.description: Sets the container zone for the record.

fields.label: dnsname
fields.description: Name of the record.

fields.label: flags
fields.description: Sets one of the possible flags of NAPTR record. Required for O(type=NAPTR).

fields.label: order
fields.description: Sets the order of the NAPTR record. Required for O(type=NAPTR).

fields.label: preference
fields.description: Sets the preference of the NAPTR record. Required for O(type=NAPTR).

fields.label: priority
fields.description: Sets the priority of the SRV record.

fields.label: replacement
fields.description: Sets the replacement of the NAPTR record. Required for O(type=NAPTR).

fields.label: service
fields.description: Sets the service of the NAPTR record. Required for O(type=NAPTR).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the record should exist or not.

fields.label: target
fields.description: Sets the target of the SRV record. Required for O(type=SRV).

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: Sets the TTL of the record.

fields.label: type
fields.description: Type of the record.

fields.label: weight
fields.description: Sets the weight of the SRV record.

modules.label: irc
modules.tooltip: Send a message to an IRC channel or a nick

fields.label: channel
fields.description: Channel name. One of O(nick_to) or O(channel) needs to be set. When both are set, the message is sent to both of them.

fields.label: color
fields.description: Text color for the message.

fields.label: key
fields.description: Channel key.

fields.label: msg
fields.description: The message body.

fields.label: nick
fields.description: Nickname to send the message from. May be shortened, depending on server's NICKLEN setting.

fields.label: nick to
fields.description: A list of nicknames to send the message to. One of O(nick_to) or O(channel) needs to be set. When both are defined, the message is sent to both of them.

fields.label: part
fields.description: Designates whether user should part from channel after sending message or not. Useful for when using a mock bot and not wanting join/parts between messages.

fields.label: passwd
fields.description: Server password.

fields.label: server
fields.description: IRC server name/address.

fields.label: style
fields.description: Text style for the message. Note italic does not work on some clients.

fields.label: topic
fields.description: Set the channel topic.

fields.label: use tls
fields.description: Designates whether TLS/SSL should be used when connecting to the IRC server. O(use_tls) is available since community.general 8.1.0, before the option was exclusively called O(use_ssl). The latter is now an alias of O(use_tls). B(Note:) for security reasons, you should always set O(use_tls=true) and O(validate_certs=true) whenever possible. The default of this option changed to V(true) in community.general 10.0.0.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If set to V(false), the SSL certificates are not validated. This should always be set to V(true). Using V(false) is unsafe and should only be done if the network between between Ansible and the IRC server is known to be safe. B(Note:) for security reasons, you should always set O(use_tls=true) and O(validate_certs=true) whenever possible. The default of this option changed to V(true) in community.general 10.0.0.

modules.label: iso_create
modules.tooltip: Generate ISO file with specified files or folders

fields.label: dest iso
fields.description: The absolute path with file name of the new generated ISO file on local machine. It creates intermediate folders when they do not exist.

fields.label: interchange level
fields.description: The ISO9660 interchange level to use, it dictates the rules on the names of files. Levels and valid values V(1), V(2), V(3), V(4) are supported. The default value is level V(1), which is the most conservative, level V(3) is recommended. ISO9660 file names at interchange level V(1) cannot have more than 8 characters or 3 characters in the extension.

fields.label: joliet
fields.description: Support levels and valid values are V(1), V(2), or V(3). Level V(3) is by far the most common. If not specified, then no Joliet support is added.

fields.label: rock ridge
fields.description: Whether to make this ISO have the Rock Ridge extensions or not. Valid values are V(1.09), V(1.10) or V(1.12), means adding the specified Rock Ridge version to the ISO. If unsure, set V(1.09) to ensure maximum compatibility. If not specified, then not add Rock Ridge extension to the ISO.

fields.label: src files
fields.description: This is a list of absolute paths of source files or folders to be contained in the new generated ISO file. The module fails if specified file or folder in O(src_files) does not exist on local machine. Note: With all ISO9660 levels from 1 to 3, all file names are restricted to uppercase letters, numbers and underscores (_). File names are limited to 31 characters, directory nesting is limited to 8 levels, and path names are limited to 255 characters.

fields.label: udf
fields.description: Whether to add UDF support to this ISO. If set to V(true), then version 2.60 of the UDF spec is used. If not specified or set to V(false), then no UDF support is added.

fields.label: vol ident
fields.description: The volume identification string to use on the new generated ISO image.

fields.label: delete files
fields.description: Absolute paths for files inside the ISO file that should be removed.

fields.label: dest iso
fields.description: The path of the customized ISO file.

fields.label: src iso
fields.description: This is the path of source ISO file.

modules.label: iso_extract
modules.tooltip: Extract files from an ISO image

fields.label: dest
fields.description: The destination directory to extract files to.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: The path to the C(7z) executable to use for extracting files from the ISO. If not provided, it assumes the value V(7z).

fields.label: files
fields.description: A list of files to extract from the image. Extracting directories does not work. File paths should not include a leading V(/); any leading path separator is automatically stripped.

fields.label: force
fields.description: If V(true), it replaces the remote file when contents are different than the source. If V(false), the file is only extracted and copied if the destination does not already exist.

fields.label: image
fields.description: The ISO image to extract files from.

modules.label: jabber
modules.tooltip: Send a message to jabber user or chat room

fields.label: encoding
fields.description: Message encoding.

fields.label: msg
fields.description: The message body.

fields.label: to
fields.description: User ID or name of the room, when using room use a slash to indicate your nick.

fields.label: user
fields.description: User as which to connect.

modules.label: java_cert
modules.tooltip: Uses keytool to import/remove certificate to/from java keystore (cacerts)

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: The attributes the resulting filesystem object should have. To get supported flags look at the man page for I(chattr) on the target system. This string should contain the attributes in the same order as the one displayed by I(lsattr). The C(=) operator is assumed as default, otherwise C(+) or C(-) operators need to be included in the string.

fields.label: cert alias
fields.description: Imported certificate alias. The alias is used when checking for the presence of a certificate in the keystore.

fields.label: cert content
fields.description: Content of the certificate used to create the keystore. Exactly one of O(cert_url), O(cert_path), O(cert_content), or O(pkcs12_path) is required to load certificate.

fields.label: cert path
fields.description: Local path to load certificate from. Exactly one of O(cert_url), O(cert_path), O(cert_content), or O(pkcs12_path) is required to load certificate.

fields.label: cert port
fields.description: Port to connect to URL. This is used to create server URL:PORT.

fields.label: cert url
fields.description: Basic URL to fetch SSL certificate from. Exactly one of O(cert_url), O(cert_path), O(cert_content), or O(pkcs12_path) is required to load certificate.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: Path to keytool binary if not used we search in PATH for it.

fields.label: group
fields.description: Name of the group that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current group of the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership.

fields.label: keystore create
fields.description: Create keystore if it does not exist.

fields.label: keystore pass
fields.description: Keystore password.

fields.label: keystore path
fields.description: Path to keystore.

fields.label: keystore type
fields.description: Keystore type (JCEKS, JKS).

fields.label: mode
fields.description: The permissions the resulting filesystem object should have. For those used to I(/usr/bin/chmod) remember that modes are actually octal numbers. You must give Ansible enough information to parse them correctly. For consistent results, quote octal numbers (for example, C('644') or C('1777')) so Ansible receives a string and can do its own conversion from string into number. Adding a leading zero (for example, C(0755)) works sometimes, but can fail in loops and some other circumstances. Giving Ansible a number without following either of these rules will end up with a decimal number which will have unexpected results. As of Ansible 1.8, the mode may be specified as a symbolic mode (for example, C(u+rwx) or C(u=rw,g=r,o=r)). If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does not) exist, the default C(umask) on the system will be used when setting the mode for the newly created filesystem object. If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does) exist, the mode of the existing filesystem object will be used. Specifying C(mode) is the best way to ensure filesystem objects are created with the correct permissions. See CVE-2020-1736 for further details.

fields.label: owner
fields.description: Name of the user that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership. Specifying a numeric username will be assumed to be a user ID and not a username. Avoid numeric usernames to avoid this confusion.

fields.label: pkcs12 alias
fields.description: Alias in the PKCS12 keystore.

fields.label: pkcs12 password
fields.description: Password for importing from PKCS12 keystore.

fields.label: pkcs12 path
fields.description: Local path to load PKCS12 keystore from. Unlike O(cert_url), O(cert_path) and O(cert_content), the PKCS12 keystore embeds the private key matching the certificate, and is used to import both the certificate and its private key into the java keystore. Exactly one of O(cert_url), O(cert_path), O(cert_content), or O(pkcs12_path) is required to load certificate.

fields.label: selevel
fields.description: The level part of the SELinux filesystem object context. This is the MLS/MCS attribute, sometimes known as the C(range). When set to C(_default), it will use the C(level) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: serole
fields.description: The role part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(role) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: setype
fields.description: The type part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(type) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: seuser
fields.description: The user part of the SELinux filesystem object context. By default it uses the C(system) policy, where applicable. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(user) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Defines action which can be either certificate import or removal. When O(state=present), the certificate is always inserted into the keystore, even if there already exists a cert alias that is different.

fields.label: trust cacert
fields.description: Trust imported cert as CAcert.

fields.label: unsafe writes
fields.description: Influence when to use atomic operation to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem object. By default this module uses atomic operations to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem objects, but sometimes systems are configured or just broken in ways that prevent this. One example is docker mounted filesystem objects, which cannot be updated atomically from inside the container and can only be written in an unsafe manner. This option allows Ansible to fall back to unsafe methods of updating filesystem objects when atomic operations fail (however, it doesn't force Ansible to perform unsafe writes). IMPORTANT! Unsafe writes are subject to race conditions and can lead to data corruption.

modules.label: java_keystore
modules.tooltip: Create a Java keystore in JKS format

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: The attributes the resulting filesystem object should have. To get supported flags look at the man page for I(chattr) on the target system. This string should contain the attributes in the same order as the one displayed by I(lsattr). The C(=) operator is assumed as default, otherwise C(+) or C(-) operators need to be included in the string.

fields.label: certificate
fields.description: Content of the certificate used to create the keystore. If the fingerprint of the provided certificate does not match the fingerprint of the certificate bundled in the keystore, the keystore is regenerated with the provided certificate. Exactly one of O(certificate) or O(certificate_path) is required.

fields.label: certificate path
fields.description: Location of the certificate used to create the keystore. If the fingerprint of the provided certificate does not match the fingerprint of the certificate bundled in the keystore, the keystore is regenerated with the provided certificate. Exactly one of O(certificate) or O(certificate_path) is required.

fields.label: dest
fields.description: Absolute path of the generated keystore.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Keystore is created even if it already exists.

fields.label: group
fields.description: Name of the group that should own jks file.

fields.label: keystore type
fields.description: Type of the Java keystore. When this option is omitted and the keystore does not already exist, the behavior follows C(keytool)'s default store type which depends on Java version; V(pkcs12) since Java 9 and V(jks) prior (may also be V(pkcs12) if new default has been backported to this version). When this option is omitted and the keystore already exists, the current type is left untouched, unless another option leads to overwrite the keystore (in that case, this option behaves like for keystore creation). When O(keystore_type) is set, the keystore is created with this type if it does not already exist, or is overwritten to match the given type in case of mismatch.

fields.label: mode
fields.description: Mode the file should be.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the certificate in the keystore. If the provided name does not exist in the keystore, the module re-creates the keystore. This behavior changed in community.general 3.0.0, before that the module would fail when the name did not match.

fields.label: owner
fields.description: Name of the user that should own jks file.

fields.label: private key
fields.description: Content of the private key used to create the keystore. Exactly one of O(private_key) or O(private_key_path) is required.

fields.label: private key passphrase
fields.description: Passphrase used to read the private key, if required.

fields.label: private key path
fields.description: Location of the private key used to create the keystore. Exactly one of O(private_key) or O(private_key_path) is required.

fields.label: selevel
fields.description: The level part of the SELinux filesystem object context. This is the MLS/MCS attribute, sometimes known as the C(range). When set to C(_default), it will use the C(level) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: serole
fields.description: The role part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(role) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: setype
fields.description: The type part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(type) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: seuser
fields.description: The user part of the SELinux filesystem object context. By default it uses the C(system) policy, where applicable. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(user) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: ssl backend
fields.description: Backend for loading private keys and certificates.

fields.label: unsafe writes
fields.description: Influence when to use atomic operation to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem object. By default this module uses atomic operations to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem objects, but sometimes systems are configured or just broken in ways that prevent this. One example is docker mounted filesystem objects, which cannot be updated atomically from inside the container and can only be written in an unsafe manner. This option allows Ansible to fall back to unsafe methods of updating filesystem objects when atomic operations fail (however, it doesn't force Ansible to perform unsafe writes). IMPORTANT! Unsafe writes are subject to race conditions and can lead to data corruption.

fields.label: deploy path
fields.description: The location in the filesystem where the deployment scanner listens.

fields.label: deployment
fields.description: The name of the deployment.

fields.label: src
fields.description: The remote path of the application ear or war to deploy. Required when O(state=present). Ignored when O(state=absent).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the application should be deployed or undeployed.

fields.label: args
fields.description: A list of parameters to pass to the build.

fields.label: build number
fields.description: An integer which specifies a build of a job. Is required to remove a build from the queue.

fields.label: detach
fields.description: Enable detached mode to not wait for the build end.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the Jenkins job to build.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Attribute that specifies if the build is to be created, deleted or stopped. The V(stopped) state has been added in community.general 3.3.0.

fields.label: time between checks
fields.description: Time in seconds to wait between requests to the Jenkins server. This times must be higher than the configured quiet time for the job.

fields.label: token
fields.description: API token used to authenticate with the Jenkins server.

fields.label: url
fields.description: URL of the Jenkins server.

fields.label: user
fields.description: User to authenticate with the Jenkins server.

fields.label: build number
fields.description: An integer which specifies a build of a job. If not specified the last build information is returned.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the Jenkins job to which the build belongs.

fields.label: token
fields.description: API token used to authenticate with the Jenkins server.

fields.label: url
fields.description: URL of the Jenkins server.

fields.label: user
fields.description: User to authenticate with the Jenkins server.

modules.label: jenkins_credential
modules.tooltip: Manage Jenkins credentials and domains through API

fields.label: api uri
fields.description: Link to Github API.

fields.label: appID
fields.description: GitHub App ID.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Description of the credential or domain.

fields.label: exc hostname
fields.description: List of hostnames to exclude from scope. If a hostname appears in both this list and O(inc_hostname), the hostname is excluded.

fields.label: exc hostname port
fields.description: List of host:port to exclude from scope. If a hostname and port appears in both this list and O(inc_hostname_port), it is excluded.

fields.label: exc path
fields.description: List of URL paths to exclude. If a path is also matched by O(exc_path), it is excluded. If you exclude a subpath of a path previously included, that subpath alone is excluded.

fields.label: file path
fields.description: File path to secret file (for example O(type=file) or O(type=certificate)). For O(type=certificate), this can be a V(.p12) or V(.pem) file.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Force update if the credential already exists, used with O(state=present). If set to V(true), it deletes the existing credential before creating a new one. Always returns RV(ignore:changed=true).

fields.label: id
fields.description: The ID of the Jenkins credential or domain. When generating a new token, do not pass O(id). It is generated automatically.

fields.label: inc hostname
fields.description: List of hostnames to include in scope.

fields.label: inc hostname port
fields.description: List of V(host:port) to include in scope.

fields.label: inc path
fields.description: List of URL paths to include when matching credentials to domains. B(Matching is hierarchical): subpaths of excluded paths are also excluded, even if explicitly included.

fields.label: jenkins password
fields.description: Jenkins password for token creation. Required if O(type=token).

fields.label: jenkins user
fields.description: Jenkins user for authentication.

fields.label: location
fields.description: Location of the credential. Either V(system) or V(folder). If O(location=folder) then O(url) must be set to V(<jenkins-server>/job/<folder_name>).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the token to generate. Required if O(type=token). When generating a new token, do not pass O(id). It is generated automatically. Creating two tokens with the same name generates two distinct tokens with different RV(token_uuid) values. Replacing a token with another one of the same name requires deleting the original first using O(force=True).

fields.label: owner
fields.description: GitHub App owner.

fields.label: passphrase
fields.description: SSH passphrase if needed.

fields.label: private key path
fields.description: Path to private key file for PEM certificates or GitHub Apps.

fields.label: schemes
fields.description: List of schemes (for example V(http) or V(https)) to match.

fields.label: scope
fields.description: Jenkins credential domain scope. Deleting a domain scope deletes all credentials within it.

fields.label: secret
fields.description: Secret text (used when O(type=text)).

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of the credential.

fields.label: token
fields.description: Jenkins API token. Required unless O(type=token).

fields.label: type
fields.description: Type of the credential or action.

fields.label: url
fields.description: Jenkins server URL.

fields.label: config
fields.description: Config in XML format. Required if job does not yet exist. Mutually exclusive with O(enabled). Considered if O(state=present).

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Whether the job should be enabled or disabled. Mutually exclusive with O(config). Considered if O(state=present).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the Jenkins job.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Attribute that specifies if the job has to be created or deleted.

fields.label: token
fields.description: API token used to authenticate alternatively to password.

fields.label: url
fields.description: URL where the Jenkins server is accessible.

fields.label: user
fields.description: User to authenticate with the Jenkins server.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If set to V(false), the SSL certificates are not validated. This should only set to V(false) used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates as it avoids verifying the source site. The C(python-jenkins) library only handles this by using the environment variable E(PYTHONHTTPSVERIFY).

fields.label: color
fields.description: Only fetch jobs with the given status color.

fields.label: glob
fields.description: A shell glob of Jenkins job names to fetch information about.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Exact name of the Jenkins job to fetch information about.

fields.label: token
fields.description: API token used to authenticate with the Jenkins server. This is mutually exclusive with O(password).

fields.label: url
fields.description: URL where the Jenkins server is accessible.

fields.label: user
fields.description: User to authenticate with the Jenkins server.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If set to V(false), the SSL certificates are not validated. This should only set to V(false) used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: labels
fields.description: When specified, sets the Jenkins node labels.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the Jenkins node to manage.

fields.label: num executors
fields.description: When specified, sets the Jenkins node executor count.

fields.label: offline message
fields.description: Specifies the offline reason message to be set when configuring the Jenkins node state. If O(offline_message) is given and requested O(state) is not V(disabled), an error is raised. Internally O(offline_message) is set using the V(toggleOffline) API, so updating the message when the node is already offline (current state V(disabled)) is not possible. In this case, a warning is issued.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Specifies whether the Jenkins node should be V(present) (created), V(absent) (deleted), V(enabled) (online) or V(disabled) (offline).

fields.label: token
fields.description: API token to authenticate with the Jenkins server.

fields.label: url
fields.description: URL of the Jenkins server.

fields.label: user
fields.description: User to authenticate with the Jenkins server.

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: The attributes the resulting filesystem object should have. To get supported flags look at the man page for I(chattr) on the target system. This string should contain the attributes in the same order as the one displayed by I(lsattr). The C(=) operator is assumed as default, otherwise C(+) or C(-) operators need to be included in the string.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: PEM formatted certificate chain file to be used for SSL client authentication. This file can also include the key as well, and if the key is included, C(client_key) is not required.

fields.label: client key
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains your private key to be used for SSL client authentication. If C(client_cert) contains both the certificate and key, this option is not required.

fields.label: force
fields.description: If C(yes) do not get a cached copy.

fields.label: force basic auth
fields.description: Credentials specified with I(url_username) and I(url_password) should be passed in HTTP Header.

fields.label: group
fields.description: GID or name of the Jenkins group on the OS.

fields.label: http agent
fields.description: Header to identify as, generally appears in web server logs.

fields.label: jenkins home
fields.description: Home directory of the Jenkins user.

fields.label: latest plugins url segments
fields.description: Path inside the O(updates_url) to get latest plugins from.

fields.label: mode
fields.description: File mode applied on versioned plugins.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Plugin name.

fields.label: owner
fields.description: UID or name of the Jenkins user on the OS.

fields.label: plugin versions url segment
fields.description: A list of URL segment(s) to retrieve the plugin versions JSON file from.

fields.label: selevel
fields.description: The level part of the SELinux filesystem object context. This is the MLS/MCS attribute, sometimes known as the C(range). When set to C(_default), it will use the C(level) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: serole
fields.description: The role part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(role) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: setype
fields.description: The type part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(type) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: seuser
fields.description: The user part of the SELinux filesystem object context. By default it uses the C(system) policy, where applicable. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(user) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired plugin state. If set to V(latest), the check for new version is performed every time. This is suitable to keep the plugin up-to-date.

fields.label: unsafe writes
fields.description: Influence when to use atomic operation to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem object. By default this module uses atomic operations to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem objects, but sometimes systems are configured or just broken in ways that prevent this. One example is docker mounted filesystem objects, which cannot be updated atomically from inside the container and can only be written in an unsafe manner. This option allows Ansible to fall back to unsafe methods of updating filesystem objects when atomic operations fail (however, it doesn't force Ansible to perform unsafe writes). IMPORTANT! Unsafe writes are subject to race conditions and can lead to data corruption.

fields.label: update json url segment
fields.description: A list of URL segment(s) to retrieve the update center JSON file from.

fields.label: updates expiration
fields.description: Number of seconds after which a new copy of the C(update-center.json) file is downloaded. This is used to avoid the need to download the plugin to calculate its checksum when O(state=latest) is specified. Set it to V(0) if no cache file should be used. In that case, the plugin file is always downloaded to calculate its checksum when O(state=latest) is specified.

fields.label: updates url
fields.description: A list of base URL(s) to retrieve C(update-center.json), and direct plugin files from. This can be a list since community.general 3.3.0.

fields.label: updates url password
fields.description: If using a custom O(updates_url), set this as the password of the user with access to the URL. If the custom O(updates_url) does not require authentication, this can be left empty.

fields.label: updates url username
fields.description: If using a custom O(updates_url), set this as the username of the user with access to the URL. If the custom O(updates_url) does not require authentication, this can be left empty.

fields.label: url
fields.description: URL of the Jenkins server.

fields.label: url password
fields.description: The password for use in HTTP basic authentication. If the I(url_username) parameter is not specified, the I(url_password) parameter will not be used.

fields.label: url username
fields.description: The username for use in HTTP basic authentication. This parameter can be used without I(url_password) for sites that allow empty passwords

fields.label: use gssapi
fields.description: Use GSSAPI to perform the authentication, typically this is for Kerberos or Kerberos through Negotiate authentication. Requires the Python library L(gssapi,https://github.com/pythongssapi/python-gssapi) to be installed. Credentials for GSSAPI can be specified with I(url_username)/I(url_password) or with the GSSAPI env var C(KRB5CCNAME) that specified a custom Kerberos credential cache. NTLM authentication is C(not) supported even if the GSSAPI mech for NTLM has been installed.

fields.label: use proxy
fields.description: If C(no), it will not use a proxy, even if one is defined in an environment variable on the target hosts.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If C(no), SSL certificates will not be validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: version
fields.description: Plugin version number. If this option is specified, all plugin dependencies must be installed manually. It might take longer to verify that the correct version is installed. This is especially true if a specific version number is specified. Quote the version to prevent the value to be interpreted as float. For example if V(1.20) would be unquoted, it would become V(1.2).

fields.label: versioned plugins url segments
fields.description: Path inside the O(updates_url) to get specific version of plugins from.

fields.label: with dependencies
fields.description: Defines whether to install plugin dependencies. In earlier versions, this option had no effect when a specific O(version) was set. Since community.general 11.2.0, dependencies are also installed for versioned plugins.

modules.label: jenkins_script
modules.tooltip: Executes a groovy script in the jenkins instance

fields.label: args
fields.description: A dict of key-value pairs used in formatting the script using string.Template (see https://docs.python.org/2/library/string.html#template-strings).

fields.label: script
fields.description: The groovy script to be executed. This gets passed as a string Template if args is defined.

fields.label: url
fields.description: The jenkins server to execute the script against. The default is a local jenkins instance that is not being proxied through a webserver.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The username to connect to the jenkins server with.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If set to V(false), the SSL certificates are not validated. This should only set to V(false) used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates as it avoids verifying the source site.

modules.label: jira
modules.tooltip: Create and modify issues in a JIRA instance

fields.label: account id
fields.description: Sets the account identifier for the assignee when O(operation) is V(create), V(transition), or V(edit). Note that JIRA may not allow changing field values on specific transitions or states.

fields.label: assignee
fields.description: Sets the assignee when O(operation) is V(create), V(transition), or V(edit). Jira Cloud requires account IDs for all user-type fields. When O(cloud=true), a string value containing C(@) is resolved as an email to an account ID automatically. A string without C(@) is assumed to be an account ID. See also O(account_id) for a dedicated assignee account ID parameter. Added in community.general 12.6.0. Jira Server and Jira Data Center may still accept O(assignee) as a username. Note that JIRA may not allow changing field values on specific transitions or states.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: Client certificate if required. In addition to O(username) and O(password) or O(token). Not mutually exclusive.

fields.label: client key
fields.description: Client certificate key if required. In addition to O(username) and O(password) or O(token). Not mutually exclusive.

fields.label: cloud
fields.description: Enable when using Jira Cloud. When set to V(true), the module uses Jira Cloud compatible behavior. When set to V(false) (the default), the module uses Jira Server / Data Center compatible behavior. In the future, this option might affect additional operations for Jira Cloud compatibility.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: The comment text to add. Note that JIRA may not allow changing field values on specific transitions or states.

fields.label: custom user fields
fields.description: A list of field names (for example V(customfield_10050)) that hold user values. When O(cloud=true) and a listed field is present in O(fields) as a string containing C(@), the module resolves the email to a Jira Cloud account ID automatically. The built-in user fields O(assignee) and V(reporter) are always resolved when present; list here any further user-typed fields (from other Jira products or extensions) that should receive the same resolution.

fields.label: description
fields.description: The issue description, where appropriate. Note that JIRA may not allow changing field values on specific transitions or states.

fields.label: fields
fields.description: This is a free-form data structure that can contain arbitrary data. This is passed directly to the JIRA REST API (possibly after merging with other required data, as when passed to create). See examples for more information, and the JIRA REST API for the structure required for various fields. When O(cloud=true), user-type fields (V(assignee), V(reporter), and any fields listed in O(custom_user_fields)) that contain a string value with C(@) are automatically resolved from email to account ID. String values without C(@) are assumed to be account IDs. Added in community.general 12.6.0. When passed to comment, the data structure is merged at the first level since community.general 4.6.0. Useful to add JIRA properties for example. Note that JIRA may not allow changing field values on specific transitions or states.

fields.label: inwardissue
fields.description: Set issue from which link is created.

fields.label: issue
fields.description: An existing issue key to operate on.

fields.label: issuetype
fields.description: The issue type, for issue creation.

fields.label: jql
fields.description: Query JIRA in JQL Syntax, for example V("CMDB Hostname" = test.example.com).

fields.label: linktype
fields.description: Set type of link, when action 'link' selected.

fields.label: maxresults
fields.description: Limit the result of O(operation=search). If no value is specified, the default JIRA limit is used. Used when O(operation=search) only, ignored otherwise.

fields.label: operation
fields.description: The operation to perform. V(worklog) was added in community.general 6.5.0.

fields.label: outwardissue
fields.description: Set issue to which link is created.

fields.label: project
fields.description: The project for this operation. Required for issue creation.

fields.label: status
fields.description: Only used when O(operation) is V(transition), and a bit of a misnomer, it actually refers to the transition name. This is mutually exclusive with O(status_id).

fields.label: status id
fields.description: Only used when O(operation) is V(transition), and refers to the transition ID. This is mutually exclusive with O(status).

fields.label: summary
fields.description: The issue summary, where appropriate. Note that JIRA may not allow changing field values on specific transitions or states.

fields.label: token
fields.description: The personal access token to log-in with. Mutually exclusive with O(username) and O(password).

fields.label: uri
fields.description: Base URI for the JIRA instance.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Require valid SSL certificates (set to V(false) if you would like to use self-signed certificates).

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: The attributes the resulting filesystem object should have. To get supported flags look at the man page for I(chattr) on the target system. This string should contain the attributes in the same order as the one displayed by I(lsattr). The C(=) operator is assumed as default, otherwise C(+) or C(-) operators need to be included in the string.

fields.label: backup
fields.description: Create a backup file.

fields.label: group
fields.description: Name of the group that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current group of the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership.

fields.label: kwriteconfig path
fields.description: Path to the kwriteconfig executable. If not specified, Ansible tries to discover it.

fields.label: mode
fields.description: The permissions the resulting filesystem object should have. For those used to I(/usr/bin/chmod) remember that modes are actually octal numbers. You must give Ansible enough information to parse them correctly. For consistent results, quote octal numbers (for example, C('644') or C('1777')) so Ansible receives a string and can do its own conversion from string into number. Adding a leading zero (for example, C(0755)) works sometimes, but can fail in loops and some other circumstances. Giving Ansible a number without following either of these rules will end up with a decimal number which will have unexpected results. As of Ansible 1.8, the mode may be specified as a symbolic mode (for example, C(u+rwx) or C(u=rw,g=r,o=r)). If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does not) exist, the default C(umask) on the system will be used when setting the mode for the newly created filesystem object. If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does) exist, the mode of the existing filesystem object will be used. Specifying C(mode) is the best way to ensure filesystem objects are created with the correct permissions. See CVE-2020-1736 for further details.

fields.label: owner
fields.description: Name of the user that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership. Specifying a numeric username will be assumed to be a user ID and not a username. Avoid numeric usernames to avoid this confusion.

fields.label: path
fields.description: Path to the config file. If the file does not exist it is created.

fields.label: selevel
fields.description: The level part of the SELinux filesystem object context. This is the MLS/MCS attribute, sometimes known as the C(range). When set to C(_default), it will use the C(level) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: serole
fields.description: The role part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(role) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: setype
fields.description: The type part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(type) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: seuser
fields.description: The user part of the SELinux filesystem object context. By default it uses the C(system) policy, where applicable. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(user) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: unsafe writes
fields.description: Influence when to use atomic operation to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem object. By default this module uses atomic operations to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem objects, but sometimes systems are configured or just broken in ways that prevent this. One example is docker mounted filesystem objects, which cannot be updated atomically from inside the container and can only be written in an unsafe manner. This option allows Ansible to fall back to unsafe methods of updating filesystem objects when atomic operations fail (however, it doesn't force Ansible to perform unsafe writes). IMPORTANT! Unsafe writes are subject to race conditions and can lead to data corruption.

modules.label: kea_command
modules.tooltip: Submits generic command to ISC KEA server on target

fields.label: arguments
fields.description: The arguments sent along with the command, if any. Use V({}) to send an empty arguments dict/object instead of omitting it.

fields.label: command
fields.description: The name of the command to send, for example V(status-get).

fields.label: rv changed
fields.description: A list of C(result) codes to indicate success and changed system state. Omit this for most acquisition commands. Set it to V([0]) for O(command=lease4-del) and similar which return changed system state that way. Any C(result) codes not listed in either O(rv_unchanged) or O(rv_changed) are interpreted as indicating an error result. O(rv_unchanged) has precedence over O(rv_changed) if a result code is in both lists.

fields.label: rv unchanged
fields.description: A list of C(result) codes to indicate success but unchanged system state. Set this to V([0]) for most acquisition commands. Use V([3]) for O(command=lease4-del) and similar which have a separate code for this. Any C(result) codes not listed in either O(rv_unchanged) or O(rv_changed) are interpreted as indicating an error result. O(rv_unchanged) has precedence over O(rv_changed) if a result code is in both lists.

fields.label: socket
fields.description: The full pathname of the Unix Domain Socket to connect to. The default value is suitable for C(kea-dhcp4-server) on Debian trixie. This module directly interfaces using UDS; the HTTP wrappers are not supported.

fields.label: blacklist file
fields.description: If specified, use this blacklist file instead of C(/etc/modprobe.d/blacklist-ansible.conf).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of kernel module to black- or whitelist.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the module should be present in the blacklist or absent.

fields.label: alias
fields.description: Alias for the authentication flow.

fields.label: auth client id
fields.description: OpenID Connect C(client_id) to authenticate to the API with.

fields.label: auth client secret
fields.description: Client Secret to use in conjunction with O(auth_client_id) (if required).

fields.label: auth keycloak url
fields.description: URL to the Keycloak instance.

fields.label: auth password
fields.description: Password to authenticate for API access with.

fields.label: auth realm
fields.description: Keycloak realm name to authenticate to for API access.

fields.label: auth username
fields.description: Username to authenticate for API access with.

fields.label: connection timeout
fields.description: Controls the HTTP connections timeout period (in seconds) to Keycloak API.

fields.label: copyFrom
fields.description: C(flowAlias) of the authentication flow to use for the copy.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Description of the flow.

fields.label: force
fields.description: If V(true), allows to remove the authentication flow and recreate it.

fields.label: http agent
fields.description: Configures the HTTP User-Agent header.

fields.label: providerId
fields.description: C(providerId) for the new flow when not copied from an existing flow.

fields.label: realm
fields.description: The name of the realm in which is the authentication.

fields.label: refresh token
fields.description: Authentication refresh token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Control if the authentication flow must exists or not.

fields.label: token
fields.description: Authentication token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Verify TLS certificates (do not disable this in production).

modules.label: keycloak_authentication_required_actions
modules.tooltip: Allows administration of Keycloak authentication required actions

fields.label: auth client id
fields.description: OpenID Connect C(client_id) to authenticate to the API with.

fields.label: auth client secret
fields.description: Client Secret to use in conjunction with O(auth_client_id) (if required).

fields.label: auth keycloak url
fields.description: URL to the Keycloak instance.

fields.label: auth password
fields.description: Password to authenticate for API access with.

fields.label: auth realm

fields.label: auth username

fields.label: realm
fields.description: The name of the realm in which are the authentication required actions.

fields.label: refresh token
fields.description: Authentication refresh token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Control if the realm authentication required actions are going to be registered/updated (V(present)) or deleted (V(absent)).

fields.label: token
fields.description: Authentication token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Verify TLS certificates (do not disable this in production).

modules.label: keycloak_authentication_v2
modules.tooltip: Configure authentication flows in Keycloak in an idempotent and safe manner.

fields.label: alias
fields.description: The name of the authentication flow.

fields.label: auth client id
fields.description: OpenID Connect C(client_id) to authenticate to the API with.

fields.label: auth client secret
fields.description: Client Secret to use in conjunction with O(auth_client_id) (if required).

fields.label: auth keycloak url
fields.description: URL to the Keycloak instance.

fields.label: auth password
fields.description: Password to authenticate for API access with.

fields.label: auth realm
fields.description: Keycloak realm name to authenticate to for API access.

fields.label: auth username
fields.description: Username to authenticate for API access with.

fields.label: connection timeout
fields.description: Controls the HTTP connections timeout period (in seconds) to Keycloak API.

fields.label: description
fields.description: A human-readable description of the flow.

fields.label: force temporary swap flow deletion
fields.description: If C(true), any pre-existing temporary swap flow (identified by the original alias plus O(temporary_swap_flow_suffix)) is deleted before the Safe Swap procedure begins. Set this to C(false) to cause the module to fail instead of silently removing a pre-existing temporary flow, for example to avoid accidental data loss after an interrupted run.

fields.label: http agent
fields.description: Configures the HTTP User-Agent header.

fields.label: providerId
fields.description: The C(providerId) for the new flow.

fields.label: realm
fields.description: The name of the realm in which the authentication flow resides.

fields.label: refresh token
fields.description: Authentication refresh token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the authentication flow should exist or not.

fields.label: temporary swap flow suffix
fields.description: The suffix appended to the alias of the temporary flow created during a Safe Swap update. The temporary flow exists only for the duration of the swap procedure and is renamed to the original alias once all bindings have been successfully transferred.

fields.label: token
fields.description: Authentication token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Verify TLS certificates (do not disable this in production).

fields.label: auth client id

fields.label: display name

fields.label: realm

fields.label: refresh token

fields.description: URL that will cause the client to log itself out when a logout request is sent to this realm. This is stored as C(backchannel.logout.url) in the client attributes.

fields.label: base url
fields.description: Default URL to use when the auth server needs to redirect or link back to the client This is C(baseUrl) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: bearer only
fields.description: The access type of this client is bearer-only. This is C(bearerOnly) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: client authenticator type
fields.description: How do clients authenticate with the auth server? Either V(client-secret), V(client-jwt), or V(client-x509) can be chosen. When using V(client-secret), the module parameter O(secret) can set it, for V(client-jwt), you can use the keys C(use.jwks.url), C(jwks.url), and C(jwt.credential.certificate) in the O(attributes) module parameter to configure its behavior. For V(client-x509) you can use the keys C(x509.allow.regex.pattern.comparison) and C(x509.subjectdn) in the O(attributes) module parameter to configure which certificate(s) to accept. This is C(clientAuthenticatorType) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: client id
fields.description: Client ID of client to be worked on. This is usually an alphanumeric name chosen by you. Either this or O(id) is required. If you specify both, O(id) takes precedence. This is C(clientId) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: client scopes behavior
fields.description: Determine how O(default_client_scopes) and O(optional_client_scopes) behave when updating an existing client. V(ignore): Do not change the client scopes of an existing client. This is the default for backward compatibility. V(patch): Add missing scopes, do not remove any missing scopes. V(idempotent): Make the client scopes exactly as specified, adding and removing scopes as needed.

fields.label: client template
fields.description: Client template to use for this client. If it does not exist this field is silently dropped. This is C(clientTemplate) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: connection timeout
fields.description: Controls the HTTP connections timeout period (in seconds) to Keycloak API.

fields.label: consent required
fields.description: If enabled, users have to consent to client access. This is C(consentRequired) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: default client scopes
fields.description: List of default client scopes. See O(client_scopes_behavior) for how this behaves when updating an existing client.

fields.label: default roles
fields.description: List of default roles for this client. If the client roles referenced do not exist yet, they are created. This is C(defaultRoles) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Description of the client in Keycloak.

fields.label: direct access grants enabled
fields.description: Are direct access grants enabled for this client or not (OpenID connect). This is C(directAccessGrantsEnabled) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Is this client enabled or not?

fields.label: frontchannel logout
fields.description: Is frontchannel logout enabled for this client or not. This is C(frontchannelLogout) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: full scope allowed
fields.description: Is the "Full Scope Allowed" feature set for this client or not. This is C(fullScopeAllowed) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: http agent
fields.description: Configures the HTTP User-Agent header.

fields.label: id
fields.description: ID of client to be worked on. This is usually an UUID. Either this or O(client_id) is required. If you specify both, this takes precedence.

fields.label: implicit flow enabled
fields.description: Enable implicit flow for this client or not (OpenID connect). This is C(implicitFlowEnabled) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the client (this is not the same as O(client_id)).

fields.label: node re registration timeout
fields.description: Cluster node re-registration timeout for this client. This is C(nodeReRegistrationTimeout) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: not before
fields.description: Revoke any tokens issued before this date for this client (this is a UNIX timestamp). This is C(notBefore) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: optional client scopes
fields.description: List of optional client scopes. See O(client_scopes_behavior) for how this behaves when updating an existing client.

fields.label: protocol
fields.description: Type of client. At creation only, default value is V(openid-connect) if O(protocol) is omitted. The V(docker-v2) value was added in community.general 8.6.0.

fields.label: public client
fields.description: Is the access type for this client public or not. This is C(publicClient) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: realm
fields.description: The realm to create the client in.

fields.label: redirect uris
fields.description: Acceptable redirect URIs for this client. This is C(redirectUris) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: refresh token
fields.description: Authentication refresh token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: registered nodes
fields.description: Dict of registered cluster nodes (with C(nodename) as the key and last registration time as the value). This is C(registeredNodes) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: registration access token
fields.description: The registration access token provides access for clients to the client registration service. This is C(registrationAccessToken) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: root url
fields.description: Root URL appended to relative URLs for this client. This is C(rootUrl) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: secret
fields.description: When using O(client_authenticator_type=client-secret) (the default), you can specify a secret here (otherwise one is generated if it does not exit). If changing this secret, the module does not register a change currently (but the changed secret is saved).

fields.label: service accounts enabled
fields.description: Are service accounts enabled for this client or not (OpenID connect). This is C(serviceAccountsEnabled) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: standard flow enabled
fields.description: Enable standard flow for this client or not (OpenID connect). This is C(standardFlowEnabled) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the client. On V(present), the client are created (or updated if it exists already). On V(absent), the client are removed if it exists.

fields.label: surrogate auth required
fields.description: Whether or not surrogate auth is required. This is C(surrogateAuthRequired) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: token
fields.description: Authentication token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: use template config
fields.description: Whether or not to use configuration from the O(client_template). This is C(useTemplateConfig) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: use template mappers
fields.description: Whether or not to use mapper configuration from the O(client_template). This is C(useTemplateMappers) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: use template scope
fields.description: Whether or not to use scope configuration from the O(client_template). This is C(useTemplateScope) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: valid post logout redirect uris
fields.description: Valid post logout redirect URIs for this client. This is stored as C(post.logout.redirect.uris) in the client attributes. Use V(+) as a single list element to allow all redirect URIs.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Verify TLS certificates (do not disable this in production).

fields.label: web origins
fields.description: List of allowed CORS origins. This is C(webOrigins) in the Keycloak REST API.

fields.label: auth client id
fields.description: OpenID Connect C(client_id) to authenticate to the API with.

fields.label: auth client secret
fields.description: Client Secret to use in conjunction with O(auth_client_id) (if required).

fields.label: auth keycloak url
fields.description: URL to the Keycloak instance.

fields.label: auth password
fields.description: Password to authenticate for API access with.

fields.label: auth realm
fields.description: Keycloak realm name to authenticate to for API access.

fields.label: auth username
fields.description: Username to authenticate for API access with.

fields.label: cid
fields.description: ID of the client to be mapped. This parameter is not required for updating or deleting the rolemapping but providing it reduces the number of API calls required.

fields.label: client id
fields.description: Name of the client to be mapped (different than O(cid)). This parameter is required (can be replaced by cid for less API call).

fields.label: connection timeout
fields.description: Controls the HTTP connections timeout period (in seconds) to Keycloak API.

fields.label: gid
fields.description: ID of the group to be mapped. This parameter is not required for updating or deleting the rolemapping but providing it reduces the number of API calls required.

fields.label: group name
fields.description: Name of the group to be mapped. This parameter is required (can be replaced by gid for less API call).

fields.label: http agent
fields.description: Configures the HTTP User-Agent header.

fields.label: realm
fields.description: They Keycloak realm under which this role_representation resides.

fields.label: refresh token
fields.description: Authentication refresh token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the client_rolemapping. On V(present), the client_rolemapping is created if it does not yet exist, or updated with the parameters you provide. On V(absent), the client_rolemapping is removed if it exists.

fields.label: token
fields.description: Authentication token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Verify TLS certificates (do not disable this in production).

fields.description: If true, users cannot log in through this provider. They can only link to this provider. This is useful if you do not want to allow login from the provider, but want to integrate with a provider.

fields.label: mappers
fields.description: A list of dicts defining mappers associated with this Identity Provider.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the identity provider. On V(present), the identity provider is created if it does not yet exist, or updated with the parameters you provide. On V(absent), the identity provider is removed if it exists.

fields.label: store token
fields.description: Enable/disable whether tokens must be stored after authenticating users.

fields.label: token
fields.description: Authentication token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: trust email
fields.description: If enabled, email provided by this provider is not verified even if verification is enabled for the realm.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Verify TLS certificates (do not disable this in production).

fields.label: authorizationUrl
fields.description: The Authorization URL.

fields.label: backchannelSupported

fields.label: clientAuthMethod

fields.description: The client or client secret registered within the identity provider.

fields.label: defaultScope
fields.description: The scopes to be sent when asking for authorization.

fields.label: entityId
fields.description: The Entity ID that is used to uniquely identify this SAML Service Provider.

fields.label: fromUrl
fields.description: IDP well-known OpenID Connect configuration URL. Support only O(provider_id=oidc). O(config.fromUrl) is mutually exclusive with O(config.userInfoUrl), O(config.authorizationUrl), O(config.tokenUrl), O(config.logoutUrl), O(config.issuer) and O(config.jwksUrl).

fields.label: gui order
fields.description: Number defining order of the provider in GUI (for example, on Login page).

fields.label: hide on login page
fields.description: If hidden, login with this provider is possible only if requested explicitly, for example using the C(kc_idp_hint) parameter.

fields.label: issuer
fields.description: The issuer identifier for the issuer of the response. If not provided, no validation is performed.

fields.label: jwksUrl
fields.description: URL where identity provider keys in JWK format are stored. See JWK specification for more details.

fields.label: logoutUrl
fields.description: End session endpoint to use to logout user from external IDP.

fields.label: nameIDPolicyFormat
fields.description: Specifies the URI reference corresponding to a name identifier format.

fields.label: principalType
fields.description: Way to identify and track external users from the assertion.

fields.label: singleLogoutServiceUrl
fields.description: The URL that must be used to send logout requests.

fields.label: singleSignOnServiceUrl
fields.description: The URL that must be used to send authentication requests (SAML AuthnRequest).

fields.label: sync mode
fields.description: Default sync mode for all mappers. The sync mode determines when user data is synced using the mappers.

fields.label: tokenUrl
fields.description: The Token URL.

fields.label: useJwksUrl
fields.description: If V(true), identity provider public keys are downloaded from given JWKS URL.

fields.label: userInfoUrl
fields.description: The User Info URL.

fields.label: validateSignature
fields.description: Enable/disable signature validation of external IDP signatures.

modules.label: keycloak_realm_key
modules.tooltip: Allows administration of Keycloak realm keys using Keycloak API

fields.label: auth client id
fields.description: OpenID Connect C(client_id) to authenticate to the API with.

fields.label: auth client secret
fields.description: Client Secret to use in conjunction with O(auth_client_id) (if required).

fields.label: auth keycloak url
fields.description: URL to the Keycloak instance.

fields.label: auth password
fields.description: Password to authenticate for API access with.

fields.label: auth realm
fields.description: Keycloak realm name to authenticate to for API access.

fields.label: auth username
fields.description: Username to authenticate for API access with.

fields.label: connection timeout
fields.description: Controls the HTTP connections timeout period (in seconds) to Keycloak API.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Enforce the state of the private key and certificate. This is not automatically the case as this module is unable to determine the current state of the private key and thus cannot trigger an update based on an actual divergence. That said, a private key update may happen even if force is false as a side-effect of other changes.

fields.label: http agent
fields.description: Configures the HTTP User-Agent header.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the realm key to create.

fields.label: parent id
fields.description: The parent_id of the realm key. In practice the name of the realm.

fields.label: provider id
fields.description: The name of the "provider ID" for the key. The value V(rsa-enc) has been added in community.general 8.2.0. The value V(java-keystore) has been added in community.general 12.4.0. This provider imports keys from a Java Keystore (JKS or PKCS12) file located on the Keycloak server filesystem. The values V(rsa-generated), V(hmac-generated), V(aes-generated), and V(ecdsa-generated) have been added in community.general 12.4.0. These are auto-generated key providers where Keycloak manages the key material. The values V(rsa-enc-generated), V(ecdh-generated), and V(eddsa-generated) have been added in community.general 12.4.0. These correspond to the auto-generated key providers available in Keycloak 26.

fields.label: refresh token
fields.description: Authentication refresh token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the keycloak realm key. On V(present), the realm key is created (or updated if it exists already). On V(absent), the realm key is removed if it exists.

fields.label: token
fields.description: Authentication token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: update password
fields.description: Controls when passwords are sent to Keycloak for V(java-keystore) provider. V(always) - Always send passwords. Keycloak will update the component even if passwords have not changed. Use when you need to ensure passwords are updated. V(on_create) - Only send passwords when creating a new component. When updating an existing component, send the masked value to preserve existing passwords. This makes the module idempotent for password fields. This is necessary because Keycloak masks passwords in API responses (returns C(**********)), making comparison impossible. Has no effect for providers other than V(java-keystore).

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Verify TLS certificates (do not disable this in production).

fields.label: active
fields.description: Whether they key is active or inactive. Not to be confused with the state of the Ansible resource managed by the O(state) parameter.

fields.label: algorithm
fields.description: Key algorithm. The values V(RS384), V(RS512), V(PS256), V(PS384), V(PS512), V(RSA1_5), V(RSA-OAEP), V(RSA-OAEP-256) have been added in community.general 8.2.0. The values V(HS256), V(HS384), V(HS512) (for HMAC), V(ES256), V(ES384), V(ES512) (for ECDSA), and V(AES) have been added in community.general 12.4.0. The values V(ECDH_ES), V(ECDH_ES_A128KW), V(ECDH_ES_A192KW), V(ECDH_ES_A256KW) (for ECDH key exchange), and V(Ed25519), V(Ed448) (for EdDSA signing) have been added in community.general 12.4.0. For O(provider_id=rsa), O(provider_id=rsa-generated), and O(provider_id=java-keystore), defaults to V(RS256). For O(provider_id=rsa-enc) and O(provider_id=rsa-enc-generated), must be one of V(RSA1_5), V(RSA-OAEP), V(RSA-OAEP-256) (required, no default). For O(provider_id=hmac-generated), must be one of V(HS256), V(HS384), V(HS512) (required, no default). For O(provider_id=ecdsa-generated), must be one of V(ES256), V(ES384), V(ES512) (required, no default). For O(provider_id=ecdh-generated), must be one of V(ECDH_ES), V(ECDH_ES_A128KW), V(ECDH_ES_A192KW), V(ECDH_ES_A256KW) (required, no default). For O(provider_id=eddsa-generated), this option is not used (the algorithm is determined by O(config.elliptic_curve)). For O(provider_id=aes-generated), this option is not used (AES is always used).

fields.label: certificate
fields.description: A certificate signed with the private key as an ASCII string. Contents of the key must match O(config.algorithm) and O(provider_id). If you want Keycloak to automatically generate a certificate using your private key then set this to an empty string. Required when O(provider_id) is V(rsa) or V(rsa-enc). Not used for auto-generated providers.

fields.label: elliptic curve
fields.description: The elliptic curve to use for ECDSA, ECDH, or EdDSA keys. For O(provider_id=ecdsa-generated) and O(provider_id=ecdh-generated), valid values are V(P-256), V(P-384), V(P-521). Default is V(P-256). For O(provider_id=eddsa-generated), valid values are V(Ed25519), V(Ed448). Default is V(Ed25519).

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Whether the key is enabled or disabled. Not to be confused with the state of the Ansible resource managed by the O(state) parameter.

fields.label: key alias
fields.description: Alias of the key within the keystore. Required when O(provider_id=java-keystore).

fields.label: key password
fields.description: Password for the key within the keystore. If not specified, the O(config.keystore_password) is used. Only applicable to O(provider_id=java-keystore).

fields.label: key size
fields.description: The size of the generated key in bits. Only applicable to O(provider_id=rsa-generated) and O(provider_id=rsa-enc-generated). Valid values are V(1024), V(2048), V(4096). Default is V(2048).

fields.label: keystore
fields.description: Path to the Java Keystore file on the Keycloak server filesystem. Required when O(provider_id=java-keystore).

fields.label: keystore password
fields.description: Password for the Java Keystore. Required when O(provider_id=java-keystore).

fields.label: priority
fields.description: The priority of the key.

fields.label: private key
fields.description: The private key as an ASCII string. Contents of the key must match O(config.algorithm) and O(provider_id). Please note that the module cannot detect whether the private key specified differs from the current state's private key. Use O(force=true) to force the module to update the private key if you expect it to be updated. Required when O(provider_id) is V(rsa) or V(rsa-enc). Not used for auto-generated providers.

fields.label: secret size
fields.description: The size of the generated secret key in bytes. Only applicable to O(provider_id=hmac-generated) and O(provider_id=aes-generated). Valid values are V(16), V(24), V(32), V(64), V(128), V(256), V(512). Default is V(64) for HMAC, V(16) for AES.

fields.label: auth client id
fields.description: OpenID Connect C(client_id) to authenticate to the API with.

fields.label: auth client secret
fields.description: Client Secret to use in conjunction with O(auth_client_id) (if required).

fields.description: URL to the Keycloak instance.

fields.label: auth username
fields.description: Username to authenticate for API access with.

fields.label: connection timeout
fields.description: Controls the HTTP connections timeout period (in seconds) to Keycloak API.

fields.label: force
fields.description: If V(false), only the keys listed in the O(overrides) are modified by this module. Any other pre-existing keys are ignored. If V(true), all locale overrides are made to match configuration of this module. For example any keys missing from the O(overrides) are removed regardless of O(state) value.

fields.label: http agent
fields.description: Configures the HTTP User-Agent header.

fields.label: locale
fields.description: Locale code for which the overrides apply (for example, V(en), V(fi), V(de)).

fields.label: parent id
fields.description: Name of the realm that owns the locale overrides.

fields.label: refresh token
fields.description: Authentication refresh token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of localization overrides for the given locale. On V(present), the set of overrides for the locale are made to match O(overrides). If O(force) is V(true) keys not listed in O(overrides) are removed, and the listed keys are created or updated. If O(force) is V(false)  keys not listed in O(overrides) are ignored, and the listed keys are created or updated. On V(absent), overrides for the locale is removed. If O(force) is V(true), all keys are removed. If O(force) is V(false), only the keys listed in O(overrides) are removed.

fields.label: token
fields.description: Authentication token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Verify TLS certificates (do not disable this in production).

fields.description: Client Secret to use in conjunction with O(auth_client_id) (if required).
