fields.description: URL to the Keycloak instance.

fields.label: auth password
fields.description: Password to authenticate for API access with.

fields.label: auth realm
fields.description: Keycloak realm name to authenticate to for API access.

fields.label: auth username
fields.description: Username to authenticate for API access with.

fields.label: bind credential update mode
fields.description: The value of the config parameter O(config.bindCredential) is redacted in the Keycloak responses. Comparing the redacted value with the desired value always evaluates to not equal. This means the before and desired states are never equal if the parameter is set. Set to V(always) to include O(config.bindCredential) in the comparison of before and desired state. Because of the redacted value returned by Keycloak the module always detects a change and make an update if a O(config.bindCredential) value is set. Set to V(only_indirect) to exclude O(config.bindCredential) when comparing the before state with the desired state. The value of O(config.bindCredential) is only updated if there are other changes to the user federation that require an update.

fields.label: connection timeout
fields.description: Controls the HTTP connections timeout period (in seconds) to Keycloak API.

fields.label: http agent
fields.description: Configures the HTTP User-Agent header.

fields.label: id
fields.description: The unique ID for this user federation. If left empty, the user federation is searched by its O(name).

fields.label: mappers
fields.description: A list of dicts defining mappers associated with this Identity Provider.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Display name of provider when linked in admin console.

fields.label: parent id
fields.description: Unique ID for the parent of this user federation. Realm ID is automatically used if left blank.

fields.label: provider id
fields.description: Provider for this user federation. Built-in providers are V(ldap), V(kerberos), and V(sssd). Custom user storage providers can also be used.

fields.label: provider type
fields.description: Component type for user federation (only supported value is V(org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageProvider)).

fields.label: realm
fields.description: The Keycloak realm under which this user federation resides.

fields.label: refresh token
fields.description: Authentication refresh token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: remove unspecified mappers
fields.description: Remove mappers that are not specified in the configuration for this federation. Set to V(false) to keep mappers that are not listed in O(mappers).

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the user federation. On V(present), the user federation is created if it does not yet exist, or updated with the parameters you provide. On V(absent), the user federation is removed if it exists.

fields.label: token
fields.description: Authentication token for Keycloak API.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Verify TLS certificates (do not disable this in production).

fields.label: allowKerberosAuthentication
fields.description: Enable/disable HTTP authentication of users with SPNEGO/Kerberos tokens. The data about authenticated users is provisioned from this LDAP server.

fields.label: allowPasswordAuthentication
fields.description: Enable/disable possibility of username/password authentication against Kerberos database.

fields.label: authType
fields.description: Type of the Authentication method used during LDAP Bind operation. It is used in most of the requests sent to the LDAP server.

fields.label: batchSizeForSync
fields.description: Count of LDAP users to be imported from LDAP to Keycloak within a single transaction.

fields.label: bindCredential
fields.description: Password of LDAP admin.

fields.label: bindDn
fields.description: DN of LDAP user which is used by Keycloak to access LDAP server.

fields.label: cachePolicy
fields.description: Cache Policy for this storage provider.

fields.label: changedSyncPeriod
fields.description: Period for synchronization of changed or newly created LDAP users in seconds.

fields.label: connectionPooling
fields.description: Determines if Keycloak should use connection pooling for accessing LDAP server.

fields.label: connectionPoolingAuthentication
fields.description: A list of space-separated authentication types of connections that may be pooled.

fields.label: connectionPoolingDebug
fields.description: A string that indicates the level of debug output to produce. Example valid values are V(fine) (trace connection creation and removal) and V(all) (all debugging information).

fields.label: connectionPoolingInitSize
fields.description: The number of connections per connection identity to create when initially creating a connection for the identity.

fields.label: connectionPoolingMaxSize
fields.description: The maximum number of connections per connection identity that can be maintained concurrently.

fields.label: connectionPoolingPrefSize
fields.description: The preferred number of connections per connection identity that should be maintained concurrently.

fields.label: connectionPoolingProtocol
fields.description: A list of space-separated protocol types of connections that may be pooled. Valid types are V(plain) and V(ssl).

fields.label: connectionPoolingTimeout
fields.description: The number of milliseconds that an idle connection may remain in the pool without being closed and removed from the pool.

fields.label: connectionTimeout
fields.description: LDAP Connection Timeout in milliseconds.

fields.label: connectionUrl
fields.description: Connection URL to your LDAP server.

fields.label: customUserSearchFilter
fields.description: Additional LDAP Filter for filtering searched users. Leave this empty if you do not need additional filter.

fields.label: debug
fields.description: Enable/disable debug logging to standard output for Krb5LoginModule.

fields.label: editMode
fields.description: V(READ_ONLY) is a read-only LDAP store. V(WRITABLE) means data is synced back to LDAP on demand. V(UNSYNCED) means user data is imported, but not synced back to LDAP.

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Enable/disable this user federation.

fields.label: evictionDay
fields.description: Day of the week the entry is set to become invalid on.

fields.label: evictionHour
fields.description: Hour of day the entry is set to become invalid on.

fields.label: evictionMinute
fields.description: Minute of day the entry is set to become invalid on.

fields.label: fullSyncPeriod
fields.description: Period for full synchronization in seconds.

fields.label: importEnabled
fields.description: If V(true), LDAP users are imported into Keycloak DB and synced by the configured sync policies.

fields.label: kerberosRealm
fields.description: Name of kerberos realm.

fields.label: keyTab
fields.description: Location of Kerberos KeyTab file containing the credentials of server principal. For example V(/etc/krb5.keytab).

fields.label: krbPrincipalAttribute
fields.description: Name of the LDAP attribute, which refers to Kerberos principal. This is used to lookup appropriate LDAP user after successful Kerberos/SPNEGO authentication in Keycloak. When this is empty, the LDAP user is looked up based on LDAP username corresponding to the first part of his Kerberos principal. For instance, for principal C(john@KEYCLOAK.ORG), it assumes that LDAP username is V(john).

fields.label: maxLifespan
fields.description: Max lifespan of cache entry in milliseconds.

fields.label: pagination
fields.description: Does the LDAP server support pagination.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: Priority of provider when doing a user lookup. Lowest first.

fields.label: rdnLDAPAttribute
fields.description: Name of LDAP attribute, which is used as RDN (top attribute) of typical user DN. Usually it is the same as Username LDAP attribute, however it is not required. For example for Active directory, it is common to use V(cn) as RDN attribute when username attribute might be V(sAMAccountName).

fields.label: readTimeout
fields.description: LDAP Read Timeout in milliseconds. This timeout applies for LDAP read operations.

fields.label: referral
fields.description: Specifies if LDAP referrals should be followed or ignored. Please note that enabling referrals can slow down authentication as it allows the LDAP server to decide which other LDAP servers to use. This could potentially include untrusted servers.

fields.label: searchScope
fields.description: For one level, the search applies only for users in the DNs specified by User DNs. For subtree, the search applies to the whole subtree. See LDAP documentation for more details.

fields.label: serverPrincipal
fields.description: Full name of server principal for HTTP service including server and domain name. For example V(HTTP/host.foo.org@FOO.ORG). Use V(*) to accept any service principal in the KeyTab file.

fields.label: startTls
fields.description: Encrypts the connection to LDAP using STARTTLS, which disables connection pooling.

fields.label: syncRegistrations
fields.description: Should newly created users be created within LDAP store? Priority effects which provider is chosen to sync the new user.

fields.label: trustEmail
fields.description: If enabled, email provided by this provider is not verified even if verification is enabled for the realm.

fields.label: updateProfileFirstLogin
fields.description: Update profile on first login.

fields.label: useKerberosForPasswordAuthentication
fields.description: Use Kerberos login module for authenticate username/password against Kerberos server instead of authenticating against LDAP server with Directory Service API.

fields.label: usePasswordModifyExtendedOp
fields.description: Use the LDAPv3 Password Modify Extended Operation (RFC-3062). The password modify extended operation usually requires that LDAP user already has password in the LDAP server. So when this is used with 'Sync Registrations', it can be good to add also 'Hardcoded LDAP attribute mapper' with randomly generated initial password.

fields.label: useTruststoreSpi
fields.description: Specifies whether LDAP connection uses the truststore SPI with the truststore configured in standalone.xml/domain.xml. V(always) means that it always uses it. V(never) means that it does not use it. V(ldapsOnly) means that it uses if your connection URL use ldaps. Note even if standalone.xml/domain.xml is not configured, the default Java cacerts or certificate specified by C(javax.net.ssl.trustStore) property is used.

fields.label: userObjectClasses
fields.description: All values of LDAP objectClass attribute for users in LDAP divided by comma. For example V(inetOrgPerson, organizationalPerson). Newly created Keycloak users are written to LDAP with all those object classes and existing LDAP user records are found just if they contain all those object classes.

fields.label: usernameLDAPAttribute
fields.description: Name of LDAP attribute, which is mapped as Keycloak username. For many LDAP server vendors it can be V(uid). For Active directory it can be V(sAMAccountName) or V(cn). The attribute should be filled for all LDAP user records you want to import from LDAP to Keycloak.

fields.label: usersDn
fields.description: Full DN of LDAP tree where your users are. This DN is the parent of LDAP users.

fields.label: uuidLDAPAttribute
fields.description: Name of LDAP attribute, which is used as unique object identifier (UUID) for objects in LDAP. For many LDAP server vendors, it is V(entryUUID); however some are different. For example for Active directory it should be V(objectGUID). If your LDAP server does not support the notion of UUID, you can use any other attribute that is supposed to be unique among LDAP users in tree.

fields.label: validatePasswordPolicy
fields.description: Determines if Keycloak should validate the password with the realm password policy before updating it.

fields.label: vendor
fields.description: LDAP vendor (provider). Use short name. For instance, write V(rhds) for "Red Hat Directory Server".

If neither O(cid) nor O(client_id) is specified, a B(realm) role is mapped instead.

fields.label: keyring password
fields.description: Password to unlock keyring.

fields.label: service
fields.description: The name of the service.

fields.label: allow root
fields.description: Whether to allow C(kibana) and C(kibana-plugin) to be run as root. Passes the C(--allow-root) flag to these commands.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Delete and re-install the plugin. It can be useful for plugins update.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the plugin to install.

fields.label: plugin bin
fields.description: Location of the Kibana binary.

fields.label: plugin dir
fields.description: Your configured plugin directory specified in Kibana.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of a plugin.

fields.label: url
fields.description: Set exact URL to download the plugin from. For local file, prefix its absolute path with C(file://).

fields.label: version
fields.description: Version of the plugin to be installed. If the plugin is installed with in a previous version, it is B(not) updated unless O(force=true).

fields.label: config
fields.description: Path to the Kopia config file for this repository connection. Defaults to the Kopia default config path when not set.

fields.label: fingerprint tls
fields.description: TLS certificate fingerprint of the Kopia server. Required if O(state=connected) and O(backend.provider=server).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of the Kopia repository.

fields.label: url
fields.description: URL of the Kopia server to connect to. Required if O(state=connected) and O(backend.provider=server).

modules.label: kopia_repository_info
modules.tooltip: Gather information about a Kopia repository

fields.label: config
fields.description: Path to the Kopia config file for this repository connection. Defaults to the Kopia default config path when not set.

fields.label: address restricted
fields.description: Request tickets restricted to the host's local address or non-restricted.

fields.label: anonymous
fields.description: Requests anonymous processing.

fields.label: cache name
fields.description: Use O(cache_name) as the ticket cache name and location. If this option is not used, the default cache name and location are used. The default credentials cache may vary between systems. If not set the value of E(KRB5CCNAME) environment variable is used instead, its value is used to name the default ticket cache.

fields.label: canonicalization
fields.description: Requests canonicalization of the principal name, and allows the KDC to reply with a different client principal from the one requested.

fields.label: enterprise
fields.description: Treats the principal name as an enterprise name (implies the O(canonicalization) option).

fields.label: forwardable
fields.description: Request forwardable or non-forwardable tickets.

fields.label: kdestroy all
fields.description: When O(state=absent) destroys all credential caches in collection. Equivalent of running C(kdestroy -A).

fields.label: keytab
fields.description: Requests a ticket, obtained from a key in the local host's keytab. If O(keytab_path) is not specified it tries to use default client keytab path (C(-i) option).

fields.label: keytab path
fields.description: Use when O(keytab=true) to specify path to a keytab file. It is required to specify O(password) or O(keytab_path).

fields.label: lifetime
fields.description: Requests a ticket with the lifetime, if the O(lifetime) is not specified, the default ticket lifetime is used. Specifying a ticket lifetime longer than the maximum ticket lifetime (configured by each site) does not override the configured maximum ticket lifetime. The value for O(lifetime) must be followed by one of the following suffixes: V(s) - seconds, V(m) - minutes, V(h) - hours, V(d) - days. You cannot mix units; a value of V(3h30m) results in an error. See U(https://web.mit.edu/kerberos/krb5-1.12/doc/basic/date_format.html) for reference.

fields.label: principal
fields.description: The principal name. If not set, the user running this module is used.

fields.label: proxiable
fields.description: Request proxiable or non-proxiable tickets.

fields.label: renewable
fields.description: Requests renewable tickets, with a total lifetime equal to O(renewable). The value for O(renewable) must be followed by one of the following delimiters: V(s) - seconds, V(m) - minutes, V(h) - hours, V(d) - days. You cannot mix units; a value of V(3h30m) results in an error. See U(https://web.mit.edu/kerberos/krb5-1.12/doc/basic/date_format.html) for reference.

fields.label: renewal
fields.description: Requests renewal of the ticket-granting ticket. Note that an expired ticket cannot be renewed, even if the ticket is still within its renewable life.

fields.label: start time
fields.description: Requests a postdated ticket. Postdated tickets are issued with the invalid flag set, and need to be resubmitted to the KDC for validation before use. O(start_time) specifies the duration of the delay before the ticket can become valid. You can use absolute time formats, for example V(July 27, 2012 at 20:30) you would neet to set O(start_time=20120727203000). You can also use time duration format similar to O(lifetime) or O(renewable). See U(https://web.mit.edu/kerberos/krb5-1.12/doc/basic/date_format.html) for reference.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of the Kerberos ticket. V(present) is equivalent of C(kinit) command. V(absent) is equivalent of C(kdestroy) command.

fields.label: validate
fields.description: Requests that the ticket-granting ticket in the cache (with the invalid flag set) be passed to the KDC for validation. If the ticket is within its requested time range, the cache is replaced with the validated ticket.

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Whether the service should start on boot. B(At least one of state and enabled are required).

fields.label: force stop
fields.description: Whether the service should not be restarted automatically by launchd. Services might have the C(KeepAlive) attribute set to V(true) in a launchd configuration. In case this is set to V(true), stopping a service causes that C(launchd) starts the service again. Set this option to V(true) to let this module change the C(KeepAlive) attribute to V(false).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the service.

fields.label: plist
fields.description: Name of the V(.plist) file for the service. Defaults to V({name}.plist).

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(started)/V(stopped) are idempotent actions that do not run commands unless necessary. C(launchd) does not support V(restarted) nor V(reloaded) natively. These states trigger a stop/start (restarted) or an unload/load (reloaded). V(restarted) unloads and loads the service before start to ensure that the latest job definition (plist) is used. V(reloaded) unloads and loads the service to ensure that the latest job definition (plist) is used. Whether a service is started or stopped depends on the content of the definition file.

fields.label: list url
fields.description: An URL of the alternative overlays list that defines the overlay to install. This list is fetched and saved under C(${overlay_defs}/${name}.xml), where C(overlay_defs) is read from the Layman's configuration.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The overlay ID to install, synchronize, or uninstall. Use V(ALL) to sync all of the installed overlays (can be used only when O(state=updated)).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to install (V(present)), sync (V(updated)), or uninstall (V(absent)) the overlay.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be set to V(false) when no other option exists.

modules.label: lbu
modules.tooltip: Local Backup Utility for Alpine Linux

fields.label: commit
fields.description: Control whether to commit changed files.

fields.label: exclude
fields.description: List of paths to exclude.

fields.label: include
fields.description: List of paths to include.

modules.label: ldap_attrs
modules.tooltip: Add or remove multiple LDAP attribute values

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: The attribute(s) and value(s) to add or remove. Each attribute value can be a string for single-valued attributes or a list of strings for multi-valued attributes. If you specify values for this option in YAML, please note that you can improve readability for long string values by using YAML block modifiers as seen in the examples for this module. Note that when using values that YAML/ansible-core interprets as other types, like V(yes), V(no) (booleans), or V(2.10) (float), make sure to quote them if these are meant to be strings. Otherwise the wrong values may be sent to LDAP.

fields.label: binary attributes
fields.description: If O(state=present), attributes whose values must be handled as raw sequences of bytes must be listed here. The values provided for the attributes will be converted from Base64.

fields.label: bind dn
fields.description: A DN to bind with. Try to use a SASL bind with the EXTERNAL mechanism as default when this parameter is omitted. Use an anonymous bind if the parameter is blank.

fields.label: bind pw
fields.description: The password to use with O(bind_dn).

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: Set the path to PEM file with CA certs.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: PEM formatted certificate chain file to be used for SSL client authentication. Required if O(client_key) is defined.

fields.label: client key
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains your private key to be used for SSL client authentication. Required if O(client_cert) is defined.

fields.label: dn
fields.description: The DN of the entry to add or remove.

fields.label: honor binary
fields.description: If O(state=present) and this option is V(true), attributes whose name include the V(binary) option will be treated as Base64-encoded byte sequences automatically, even if they are not listed in O(binary_attributes).

fields.label: ordered
fields.description: If V(true), prepend list values with X-ORDERED index numbers in all attributes specified in the current task. This is useful mostly with C(olcAccess) attribute to easily manage LDAP Access Control Lists.

fields.label: referrals chasing
fields.description: Set the referrals chasing behavior. V(anonymous) follow referrals anonymously. This is the default behavior. V(disabled) disable referrals chasing. This sets C(OPT_REFERRALS) to off.

fields.label: sasl class
fields.description: The class to use for SASL authentication.

fields.label: server uri
fields.description: The O(server_uri) parameter may be a comma- or whitespace-separated list of URIs containing only the schema, the host, and the port fields. The default value lets the underlying LDAP client library look for a UNIX domain socket in its default location. Note that when using multiple URIs you cannot determine to which URI your client gets connected. For URIs containing additional fields, particularly when using commas, behavior is undefined.

fields.label: start tls
fields.description: Use the START_TLS LDAP extension if set to V(true).

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of the attribute values. If V(present), all given attribute values are added if they are missing. If V(absent), all given attribute values are removed if present. If V(exact), the set of attribute values is forced to exactly those provided and no others. If O(state=exact) and the attribute value is empty, all values for this attribute are removed.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If set to V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: xorder discovery
fields.description: Set the behavior on how to process Xordered DNs. V(enable) performs a C(ONELEVEL) search below the superior RDN to find the matching DN. V(disable) always uses the DN unmodified (as passed by the O(dn) parameter). V(auto) only performs a search if the first RDN does not contain an index number (C({x})).

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: If O(state=present), attributes necessary to create an entry. Existing entries are never modified. To assert specific attribute values on an existing entry, use M(community.general.ldap_attrs) module instead. Each attribute value can be a string for single-valued attributes or a list of strings for multi-valued attributes. If you specify values for this option in YAML, please note that you can improve readability for long string values by using YAML block modifiers as seen in the examples for this module. Note that when using values that YAML/ansible-core interprets as other types, like V(yes), V(no) (booleans), or V(2.10) (float), make sure to quote them if these are meant to be strings. Otherwise the wrong values may be sent to LDAP. If O(honor_binary=true), an attribute that includes the C(binary) option as per L(RFC 4522, https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc4522.html#section-3) will be considered as binary. Its contents must be specified as Base64 and sent to the LDAP after decoding. If an attribute must be handled as binary without including the C(binary) option, it can be listed in O(binary_attributes). Support for binary values was added in community.general 12.5.0.

fields.label: binary attributes
fields.description: If O(state=present), attributes whose values must be handled as raw sequences of bytes must be listed here. The values provided for the attributes will be converted from Base64.

fields.label: bind dn
fields.description: A DN to bind with. Try to use a SASL bind with the EXTERNAL mechanism as default when this parameter is omitted. Use an anonymous bind if the parameter is blank.

fields.label: bind pw
fields.description: The password to use with O(bind_dn).

fields.description: Set the path to PEM file with CA certs.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: PEM formatted certificate chain file to be used for SSL client authentication. Required if O(client_key) is defined.

fields.label: client key
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains your private key to be used for SSL client authentication. Required if O(client_cert) is defined.

fields.description: The DN of the entry to add or remove.

fields.label: honor binary

fields.label: objectClass
fields.description: If O(state=present), value or list of values to use when creating the entry. It can either be a string or an actual list of strings.

fields.label: recursive
fields.description: If O(state=delete), a flag indicating whether a single entry or the whole branch must be deleted.

fields.label: referrals chasing
fields.description: Set the referrals chasing behavior. V(anonymous) follow referrals anonymously. This is the default behavior. V(disabled) disable referrals chasing. This sets C(OPT_REFERRALS) to off.

fields.label: sasl class
fields.description: The class to use for SASL authentication.

The default value lets the underlying LDAP client library look for a UNIX domain socket in its default location. Note that when using multiple URIs you cannot determine to which URI your client gets connected. For URIs containing additional fields, particularly when using commas, behavior is undefined.

fields.label: start tls
fields.description: Use the START_TLS LDAP extension if set to V(true).

fields.label: state
fields.description: The target state of the entry.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If set to V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: xorder discovery
fields.description: Set the behavior on how to process Xordered DNs. V(enable) performs a C(ONELEVEL) search below the superior RDN to find the matching DN. V(disable) always uses the DN unmodified (as passed by the O(dn) parameter). V(auto) only performs a search if the first RDN does not contain an index number (C({x})).

modules.label: ldap_inc
modules.tooltip: Use the Modify-Increment LDAP V3 feature to increment an attribute value

fields.label: attribute
fields.description: The attribute to increment.

fields.label: bind dn
fields.description: A DN to bind with. Try to use a SASL bind with the EXTERNAL mechanism as default when this parameter is omitted. Use an anonymous bind if the parameter is blank.

fields.label: bind pw
fields.description: The password to use with O(bind_dn).

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: Set the path to PEM file with CA certs.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: PEM formatted certificate chain file to be used for SSL client authentication. Required if O(client_key) is defined.

fields.label: client key
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains your private key to be used for SSL client authentication. Required if O(client_cert) is defined.

fields.label: dn
fields.description: The DN entry containing the attribute to increment.

fields.label: increment
fields.description: The value of the increment to apply.

fields.label: method
fields.description: If V(auto), the module determines automatically the method to use. If V(rfc4525) or V(legacy) force to use the corresponding method.

fields.label: referrals chasing
fields.description: Set the referrals chasing behavior. V(anonymous) follow referrals anonymously. This is the default behavior. V(disabled) disable referrals chasing. This sets C(OPT_REFERRALS) to off.

Note that when using multiple URIs you cannot determine to which URI your client gets connected. For URIs containing additional fields, particularly when using commas, behavior is undefined.

fields.label: dn
fields.description: The DN of the entry to add or remove.

fields.label: passwd
fields.description: The (plaintext) password to be set for O(dn).

fields.label: referrals chasing
fields.description: Set the referrals chasing behavior. V(anonymous) follow referrals anonymously. This is the default behavior. V(disabled) disable referrals chasing. This sets C(OPT_REFERRALS) to off.

Note that when using multiple URIs you cannot determine to which URI your client gets connected. For URIs containing additional fields, particularly when using commas, behavior is undefined.

modules.label: ldap_search
modules.tooltip: Search for entries in a LDAP server

fields.label: attrs
fields.description: A list of attributes for limiting the result. Use an actual list or a comma-separated string.

fields.label: base64 attributes
fields.description: If provided, all attribute values returned that are listed in this option are Base64 encoded. If the special value V(*) appears in this list, all attributes are Base64 encoded. All other attribute values are converted to UTF-8 strings. If they contain binary data, please note that invalid UTF-8 bytes are omitted.

fields.label: bind dn
fields.description: A DN to bind with. Try to use a SASL bind with the EXTERNAL mechanism as default when this parameter is omitted. Use an anonymous bind if the parameter is blank.

fields.label: bind pw
fields.description: The password to use with O(bind_dn).

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: Set the path to PEM file with CA certs.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: PEM formatted certificate chain file to be used for SSL client authentication. Required if O(client_key) is defined.

fields.label: client key
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains your private key to be used for SSL client authentication. Required if O(client_cert) is defined.

fields.label: dn
fields.description: The LDAP DN to search in.

fields.label: filter
fields.description: Used for filtering the LDAP search result.

fields.label: page size
fields.description: The page size when performing a simple paged result search (RFC 2696). This setting can be tuned to reduce issues with timeouts and server limits. Setting the page size to V(0) (default) disables paged searching.

fields.label: referrals chasing
fields.description: Set the referrals chasing behavior. V(anonymous) follow referrals anonymously. This is the default behavior. V(disabled) disable referrals chasing. This sets C(OPT_REFERRALS) to off.

fields.label: sasl class
fields.description: The class to use for SASL authentication.

fields.label: schema
fields.description: Set to V(true) to return the full attribute schema of entries, not their attribute values. Overrides O(attrs) when provided.

fields.label: scope
fields.description: The LDAP scope to use. V(subordinate) requires the LDAPv3 subordinate feature extension. V(children) is equivalent to a "subtree" scope.

fields.label: server uri
fields.description: The O(server_uri) parameter may be a comma- or whitespace-separated list of URIs containing only the schema, the host, and the port fields. The default value lets the underlying LDAP client library look for a UNIX domain socket in its default location. Note that when using multiple URIs you cannot determine to which URI your client gets connected. For URIs containing additional fields, particularly when using commas, behavior is undefined.

fields.label: start tls
fields.description: Use the START_TLS LDAP extension if set to V(true).

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If set to V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on sites using self-signed certificates.

V(disable) always uses the DN unmodified (as passed by the O(dn) parameter). V(auto) only performs a search if the first RDN does not contain an index number (C({x})).

fields.label: api key
fields.description: Librato account API key.

fields.label: description
fields.description: The description contains extra metadata about a particular annotation. The description should contain specifics on the individual annotation for example V(Deployed 9b562b2 shipped new feature foo!).

fields.label: end time
fields.description: The unix timestamp indicating the time at which the event referenced by this annotation ended. For events that have a duration, this is a useful way to annotate the duration of the event.

fields.label: links
fields.description: See examples.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The annotation stream name. If the annotation stream does not exist, it creates one automatically.

fields.label: source
fields.description: A string which describes the originating source of an annotation when that annotation is tracked across multiple members of a population.

fields.label: start time
fields.description: The unix timestamp indicating the time at which the event referenced by this annotation started.

fields.label: title
fields.description: The title of an annotation is a string and may contain spaces. The title should be a short, high-level summary of the annotation for example V(v45 Deployment).

fields.label: user
fields.description: Librato account username.

modules.label: linode
modules.tooltip: Manage instances on the Linode Public Cloud

fields.label: additional disks
fields.description: List of dictionaries for creating additional disks that are added to the Linode configuration settings. Dictionary takes Size, Label, Type. Size is in MB.

fields.label: alert bwin enabled
fields.description: Set status of bandwidth in alerts.

fields.label: alert bwin threshold
fields.description: Set threshold in MB of bandwidth in alerts.

fields.label: alert bwout enabled
fields.description: Set status of bandwidth out alerts.

fields.label: alert bwout threshold
fields.description: Set threshold in MB of bandwidth out alerts.

fields.label: alert bwquota enabled
fields.description: Set status of bandwidth quota alerts as percentage of network transfer quota.

fields.label: alert bwquota threshold
fields.description: Set threshold in MB of bandwidth quota alerts.

fields.label: alert cpu enabled
fields.description: Set status of receiving CPU usage alerts.

fields.label: alert cpu threshold
fields.description: Set percentage threshold for receiving CPU usage alerts. Each CPU core adds 100% to total.

fields.label: alert diskio enabled
fields.description: Set status of receiving disk IO alerts.

fields.label: alert diskio threshold
fields.description: Set threshold for average IO ops/sec over 2 hour period.

fields.label: api key
fields.description: Linode API key. E(LINODE_API_KEY) environment variable can be used instead.

fields.label: backupweeklyday
fields.description: Day of the week to take backups.

fields.label: backupwindow
fields.description: The time window in which backups are taken.

fields.label: datacenter
fields.description: Datacenter to create an instance in (Linode Datacenter).

fields.label: displaygroup
fields.description: Add the instance to a Display Group in Linode Manager.

fields.label: distribution
fields.description: Distribution to use for the instance (Linode Distribution).

fields.label: kernel id
fields.description: Kernel to use for the instance (Linode Kernel).

fields.label: linode id
fields.description: Unique ID of a Linode server. This value is read-only in the sense that if you specify it on creation of a Linode it is not used. The Linode API generates these IDs and we can those generated value here to reference a Linode more specifically. This is useful for idempotency.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name to give the instance (alphanumeric, dashes, underscore). To keep sanity on the Linode Web Console, name is prepended with C(LinodeID-).

fields.label: payment term
fields.description: Payment term to use for the instance (payment term in months).

fields.label: plan
fields.description: Plan to use for the instance (Linode plan).

fields.label: private ip
fields.description: Add private IPv4 address when Linode is created. Default is V(false).

fields.label: ssh pub key
fields.description: SSH public key applied to root user.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicate desired state of the resource.

fields.label: swap
fields.description: Swap size in MB.

fields.label: wait
fields.description: Wait for the instance to be in state V(running) before returning.

fields.label: wait timeout
fields.description: How long before wait gives up, in seconds.

fields.label: watchdog
fields.description: Set status of Lassie watchdog.

modules.label: linode_v4
modules.tooltip: Manage instances on the Linode cloud

fields.label: access token
fields.description: The Linode API v4 access token. It may also be specified by exposing the E(LINODE_ACCESS_TOKEN) environment variable. See U(https://www.linode.com/docs/api#access-and-authentication).

fields.label: authorized keys
fields.description: A list of SSH public key parts to deploy for the root user.

fields.label: group
fields.description: The group that the instance should be marked under. Please note, that group labelling is deprecated but still supported. The encouraged method for marking instances is to use tags.

fields.label: image
fields.description: The image of the instance. This is a required parameter only when creating Linode instances. See U(https://www.linode.com/docs/api/images/).

fields.label: label
fields.description: The instance label. This label is used as the main determiner for idempotency for the module and is therefore mandatory.

fields.label: private ip
fields.description: If V(true), the created Linode instance has private networking enabled and assigned a private IPv4 address.

fields.label: region
fields.description: The region of the instance. This is a required parameter only when creating Linode instances. See U(https://www.linode.com/docs/api/regions/).

fields.label: root pass
fields.description: The password for the root user. If not specified, it generates a new one. This generated password is available in the task success JSON.

fields.label: stackscript data
fields.description: An object containing arguments to any User Defined Fields present in the StackScript used when creating the instance. Only valid when a O(stackscript_id) is provided. See U(https://www.linode.com/docs/api/stackscripts/).

fields.label: stackscript id
fields.description: The numeric ID of the StackScript to use when creating the instance. See U(https://www.linode.com/docs/api/stackscripts/).

fields.label: state
fields.description: The desired instance state.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: The tags that the instance should be marked under. See U(https://www.linode.com/docs/api/tags/).

fields.label: type
fields.description: The type of the instance. This is a required parameter only when creating Linode instances. See U(https://www.linode.com/docs/api/linode-types/).

modules.label: listen_ports_facts
modules.tooltip: Gather facts on processes listening on TCP and UDP ports

fields.label: command
fields.description: Override which command to use for fetching listen ports. By default module uses first found supported command on the system (in alphanumerical order).

fields.label: include non listening
fields.description: Show both listening and non-listening sockets (for TCP this means established connections). Adds the return values RV(ansible_facts.tcp_listen[].state), RV(ansible_facts.udp_listen[].state), RV(ansible_facts.tcp_listen[].foreign_address), and RV(ansible_facts.udp_listen[].foreign_address) to the returned facts.

fields.label: multivalues
fields.description: If C(lldpctl) outputs an attribute multiple time represent all values as a list.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name and encoding of the locales, such as V(en_GB.UTF-8). Before community.general 9.3.0, this was a string. Using a string still works.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the locales shall be present.

modules.label: logentries
modules.tooltip: Module for tracking logs using U(logentries.com)

fields.label: logtype
fields.description: Type of the log.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the log.

fields.label: path
fields.description: Path to a log file.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Following state of the log.

fields.label: api
fields.description: API endpoint.

fields.label: msg
fields.description: The message body.

fields.label: token
fields.description: Log token.

fields.label: backup
fields.description: Create a backup of the existing configuration file before overwriting. The backup file is created in the same directory with a timestamp suffix.

fields.label: compress
fields.description: Compress rotated log files.

fields.label: compress options
fields.description: Options to pass to compression program. For O(compression_method=gzip), use V(-9) for best compression, V(-1) for fastest. For O(compression_method=xz), use V(-9) for best compression.

fields.label: compression method
fields.description: Compression method to use. Requires logrotate 3.18.0 or later for V(xz) and V(zstd).

fields.label: config dir
fields.description: Directory where logrotate configurations are stored. Using V(~/.logrotate.d) is recommended for user-specific configurations. This directory must exist before using the module.

fields.label: copy
fields.description: Copy the log file but do not truncate the original. Takes precedence over O(rename_copy) and O(copy_truncate).

fields.label: copy truncate
fields.description: Copy the log file and then truncate it in place. Useful for applications that cannot be told to close their logfile.

fields.label: create
fields.description: Create new log file with specified permissions after rotation. Format is V(mode owner group) (for example V(0640 root adm)). Set to V(null) or omit to use V(nocreate).

fields.label: create old dir
fields.description: Create O(old_dir) directory if it does not exist.

fields.label: date ext
fields.description: Use date as extension for rotated files (using the date format specified in O(date_format) instead of sequential numbers).

fields.label: date format
fields.description: Format for date extension. Use with O(date_ext=true). Format specifiers are V(%Y) year, V(%m) month, V(%d) day, V(%s) seconds since epoch.

fields.label: date yesterday
fields.description: Use yesterday's date for O(date_ext) instead of today's date. Useful for rotating logs that span midnight.

fields.label: delay compress
fields.description: Postpone compression of the previous log file to the next rotation cycle. Useful for applications that keep writing to the old log file for some time.

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Whether the configuration should be enabled. When V(false), adds V(.disabled) extension to the config file.

fields.label: extension
fields.description: Extension to use for rotated log files (including dot). Useful when O(compress=false).

fields.label: first action
fields.description: Commands to execute once before all log files that match the wildcard pattern are rotated.

fields.label: include
fields.description: Include additional configuration files from specified directory.

fields.label: last action
fields.description: Commands to execute once after all log files that match the wildcard pattern are rotated.

fields.label: mail
fields.description: Mail logs to specified address when removed. Set to V(null) or omit to not mail logs.

fields.label: mail first
fields.description: Mail just-created log file, not the about-to-expire one.

fields.label: mail last
fields.description: Mail about-to-expire log file (default).

fields.label: max age
fields.description: Remove rotated logs older than specified number of days.

fields.label: max size
fields.description: Rotate log file when it grows bigger than specified size, but at most once per O(rotation_period). Format is V(number[k|M|G]) (for example V(100M), V(1G)).

fields.label: min size
fields.description: Rotate log file only if it has grown bigger than specified size. Format is V(number[k|M|G]) (for example V(100M), V(1G)). Used with time-based rotation to avoid rotating too small files.

fields.label: missing ok
fields.description: Do not issue an error if the log file is missing.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the logrotate configuration. This creates a file in O(config_dir) with this name.

fields.label: no delay compress
fields.description: Opposite of O(delay_compress). Ensure compression happens immediately. Note that in logrotate, V(no_delay_compress) is the default behavior.

fields.label: no old dir
fields.description: Keep rotated logs in the same directory as the original log.

fields.label: not if empty
fields.description: Do not rotate the log file if it is empty. Set to V(false) to rotate even empty log files (equivalent to C(ifempty) in logrotate).

fields.label: old dir
fields.description: Move rotated logs into specified directory.

fields.label: paths
fields.description: List of log file paths or patterns to rotate. Can include wildcards (for example V(/var/log/app/*.log)). Required when creating a new configuration (O(state=present) and config file does not exist). Optional when modifying existing configuration (for example to enable/disable).

fields.label: post rotate
fields.description: Commands to execute after rotating the log file. Can be a single string or list of commands.

fields.label: pre remove
fields.description: Commands to execute before removing rotated log files.

fields.label: pre rotate
fields.description: Commands to execute before rotating the log file. Can be a single string or list of commands.

fields.label: rename copy
fields.description: Rename and copy the log file, leaving the original in place.

fields.label: rotate count
fields.description: Number of rotated log files to keep. Set to V(0) to disable rotation (keep only current log). Set to V(-1) to keep all rotated logs (not recommended).

fields.label: rotation period
fields.description: How often to rotate the logs. If not specified when modifying an existing configuration, the existing value is preserved. When creating a new configuration, this option is only included if specified. Choice V(hourly) was added in community.general 13.0.0.

fields.label: shared scripts
fields.description: Run O(pre_rotate) and O(post_rotate) scripts only once for all matching log files.

fields.label: shred
fields.description: Use C(shred) to securely delete rotated log files. Uses C(shred -u) to overwrite files before deleting.

fields.label: shred cycles
fields.description: Number of times to overwrite files when using O(shred=true). When omitted, logrotate uses C(shred)'s default.

fields.label: size
fields.description: Rotate log file when it grows bigger than specified size. Format is V(number[k|M|G]) (for example V(100M), V(1G)). Overrides O(rotation_period) when set. If not specified, existing value be preserved when modifying configuration. When creating new configuration, this option be omitted if not specified.

fields.label: start
fields.description: Base number for rotated files. Allowed values are from V(0) to V(999). For example, V(1) gives files C(.1), C(.2), and so on instead of C(.0), C(.1).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the configuration should be present or absent.

fields.label: su
fields.description: Set user and group for rotated files. Format is V(user group) (for example, V(www-data adm)). Set to V("") (empty string) to remove the directive from existing configurations. Set to V(null) or omit to leave the existing value unchanged (when modifying) or not set (when creating).

fields.label: syslog
fields.description: Send logrotate messages to syslog.

fields.label: taboo ext
fields.description: List of extensions that logrotate should not touch. Set to V(null) or empty list to clear defaults.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Install plugin with that name.

fields.label: plugin bin
fields.description: Specify logstash-plugin to use for plugin management.

fields.label: proxy host
fields.description: Proxy host to use during plugin installation. Can be specified as a hostname (for example, V(myproxy.example.com)) or as a URL (for example, V(http://myproxy.example.com)). When specified without a scheme, V(http://) is assumed. Sets the O(proxy_host):O(proxy_port) combination as the E(http_proxy) and E(https_proxy) environment variables when running the C(logstash-plugin) command.

fields.label: proxy port
fields.description: Proxy port to use during plugin installation.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Apply plugin state.

fields.label: version
fields.description: Specify version of the plugin to install. If the plugin exists with a previous version, it is B(not) updated.

fields.label: force
fields.description: If V(true), allows to remove volume group with logical volumes.

fields.label: pesize
fields.description: The size of the physical extent. O(pesize) must be a power of 2 of at least 1 sector (where the sector size is the largest sector size of the PVs currently used in the VG), or at least 128KiB. O(pesize) can be optionally suffixed by a UNIT (k/K/m/M/g/G), default unit is megabyte.

fields.label: pv options
fields.description: Additional options to pass to C(pvcreate) when creating the volume group.

fields.label: pvresize
fields.description: If V(true), resize the physical volume to the maximum available size.

fields.label: pvs
fields.description: List of comma-separated devices to use as physical devices in this volume group. Required when creating or resizing volume group. The module runs C(pvcreate) if needed. This parameter defines the B(desired state) of the physical volumes in the volume group. When the volume group already exists, physical volumes not listed here are removed from it by default. To add physical volumes without removing existing unlisted ones, set O(remove_extra_pvs=false).

fields.label: remove extra pvs
fields.description: Remove physical volumes from the volume group which are not in O(pvs).

fields.label: reset pv uuid
fields.description: Whether the volume group's physical volumes' UUIDs are regenerated. This is B(not idempotent). Specifying this parameter always results in a change.

fields.label: reset vg uuid
fields.description: Whether the volume group's UUID is regenerated. This is B(not idempotent). Specifying this parameter always results in a change.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Control if the volume group exists and its state. The states V(active) and V(inactive) implies V(present) state. Added in 7.1.0. If V(active) or V(inactive), the module manages the VG's logical volumes current state. The module also handles the VG's autoactivation state if supported unless when creating a volume group and the autoactivation option specified in O(vg_options).

fields.label: vg
fields.description: The name of the volume group.

fields.label: vg options
fields.description: Additional options to pass to C(vgcreate) when creating the volume group.

fields.label: vg
fields.description: The name or UUID of the source VG. See V(vgrename(8\)) for valid values.

fields.label: vg new
fields.description: The new name of the VG. See V(lvm(8\)) for valid names.

fields.label: device
fields.description: Path to the block device to manage.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Force the operation. When O(state=present) (creating a PV), this uses C(pvcreate -f) to force creation. When O(state=absent) (removing a PV), this uses C(pvremove -ff) to force removal even if part of a volume group.

fields.label: resize
fields.description: Resize PV to device size when O(state=present).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Control if the physical volume exists.

modules.label: lvm_pv_move_data
modules.tooltip: Move data between LVM Physical Volumes (PVs)

fields.label: atomic
fields.description: Makes the C(pvmove) operation atomic, ensuring that all affected LVs are moved to the destination PV, or none are if the operation is aborted.

fields.label: auto answer
fields.description: Answer yes to all prompts automatically.

fields.label: autobackup
fields.description: Automatically backup metadata before changes (strongly advised!).

fields.label: destination
fields.description: Path to the destination block device to move data to. Must be an existing PV with enough free space.

fields.label: source
fields.description: Path to the source block device to move data from. Must be an existing PV.

fields.label: active
fields.description: Whether the volume is active and visible to the host.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Shrink or remove operations of volumes requires this switch. Ensures that filesystems never get corrupted/destroyed by mistake.

fields.label: lv
fields.description: The name of the logical volume.

fields.label: opts
fields.description: Free-form options to be passed to the lvcreate command.

fields.label: pvs
fields.description: List of physical volumes (for example V(/dev/sda, /dev/sdb)).

fields.label: resizefs
fields.description: Resize the underlying filesystem together with the logical volume. Supported for C(ext2), C(ext3), C(ext4), C(reiserfs) and C(XFS) filesystems. Attempts to resize other filesystem types result in failure.

fields.label: shrink
fields.description: Shrink if current size is higher than size requested.

fields.label: size
fields.description: The size of the logical volume, according to lvcreate(8) C(--size), by default in megabytes or optionally with one of [bBsSkKmMgGtTpPeE] units; or according to lvcreate(8) C(--extents) as a percentage of [VG|PVS|FREE|ORIGIN]; Float values must begin with a digit. When resizing, apart from specifying an absolute size you may, according to lvextend(8)|lvreduce(8) C(--size), specify the amount to extend the logical volume with the prefix V(+) or the amount to reduce the logical volume by with prefix V(-). Resizing using V(+) or V(-) was not supported prior to community.general 3.0.0. Please note that when using V(+), V(-), or percentage of FREE, the module is B(not idempotent).

fields.label: snapshot
fields.description: The name of a snapshot volume to be configured. When creating a snapshot volume, the O(lv) parameter specifies the origin volume.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Control if the logical volume exists. If V(present) and the volume does not already exist then the O(size) option is required.

fields.label: thinpool
fields.description: The thin pool volume name. When you want to create a thin provisioned volume, specify a thin pool volume name.

fields.label: vg
fields.description: The volume group this logical volume is part of.

fields.label: archive
fields.description: When set to V(true) the system attempts to create a compressed tarball of the running container. The O(archive) option supports LVM backed containers and creates a snapshot of the running container when creating the archive.

fields.label: archive compression
fields.description: Type of compression to use when creating an archive of a running container.

fields.label: archive path
fields.description: Path the save the archived container. If the path does not exist the archive method attempts to create it.

fields.label: backing store
fields.description: Backend storage type for the container.

fields.label: clone name
fields.description: Name of the new cloned server. This is only used when state is clone.

fields.label: clone snapshot
fields.description: Create a snapshot a container when cloning. This is not supported by all container storage backends. Enabling this may fail if the backing store does not support snapshots.

fields.label: config
fields.description: Path to the LXC configuration file.

fields.label: container command
fields.description: Run a command within a container. The O(container_command) can be used with any state except V(absent). If used with state V(stopped) the container is V(started), the command executed, and then the container V(stopped) again. Likewise if O(state=stopped) and the container does not exist it is first created, V(started), the command executed, and then V(stopped). If you use a C(|) in the variable you can use common script formatting within the variable itself. The O(container_command) option always execute as C(bash). When using O(container_command), a log file is created in the C(/tmp/) directory which contains both RV(ignore:stdout) and RV(ignore:stderr) of any command executed.

fields.label: container config
fields.description: A list of C(key=value) options to use when configuring a container.

fields.label: container log
fields.description: Enable a container log for host actions to the container.

fields.label: container log level
fields.description: Set the log level for a container where O(container_log) was set.

fields.label: directory
fields.description: Place rootfs directory under DIR.

fields.label: fs size
fields.description: File system Size.

fields.label: fs type
fields.description: Create fstype TYPE.

fields.label: lv name
fields.description: Name of the logical volume, defaults to the container name. If not specified, it defaults to E(CONTAINER_NAME).

fields.label: lxc path
fields.description: Place container under E(PATH).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of a container.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Define the state of a container. If you clone a container using O(clone_name) the newly cloned container created in a stopped state. The running container is stopped while the clone operation is happening and upon completion of the clone the original container state is restored.

fields.label: template
fields.description: Name of the template to use within an LXC create.

fields.label: template options
fields.description: Template options when building the container.

fields.label: thinpool
fields.description: Use LVM thin pool called TP.

fields.label: vg name
fields.description: If backend store is lvm, specify the name of the volume group.

fields.label: zfs root
fields.description: Create zfs under given zfsroot.

modules.label: lxca_cmms
modules.tooltip: Custom module for lxca cmms inventory utility

fields.label: auth url
fields.description: Lxca HTTPS full web address.

fields.label: chassis
fields.description: UUID of chassis, this is string with length greater than 16.

fields.label: command options
fields.description: Options to filter nodes information.

fields.label: login password
fields.description: The password for use in HTTP basic authentication.

fields.label: login user
fields.description: The username for use in HTTP basic authentication.

fields.label: uuid
fields.description: UUID of device, this is string with length greater than 16.

modules.label: lxca_nodes
modules.tooltip: Custom module for lxca nodes inventory utility

fields.label: auth url
fields.description: Lxca HTTPS full web address.

fields.label: chassis
fields.description: UUID of chassis, this is string with length greater than 16.

fields.label: command options
fields.description: Options to filter nodes information.

fields.label: login password
fields.description: The password for use in HTTP basic authentication.

fields.label: login user
fields.description: The username for use in HTTP basic authentication.

fields.label: uuid
fields.description: UUID of device, this is string with length greater than 16.

fields.label: architecture
fields.description: The architecture for the instance (for example V(x86_64) or V(i686)). See U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/#/instances/instance_get).

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: The client certificate file path. If not specified, it defaults to C(${HOME}/.config/lxc/client.crt).

fields.label: client key
fields.description: The client certificate key file path. If not specified, it defaults to C(${HOME}/.config/lxc/client.key).

fields.label: config
fields.description: The config for the instance (for example V({"limits.cpu": "2"})). All values in O(config) must be strings, as required by the LXD/Incus API. Using non-string values (such as integers or booleans) will cause an API error. Make sure to quote numeric and boolean values in YAML (for example, use V("2") instead of V(2)). See U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/#/instances/instance_get). If the instance already exists and its "config" values in metadata obtained from the LXD API U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/#/instances/instance_get) are different, then this module tries to apply the configurations U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/#/instances/instance_put). The keys starting with C(volatile.) are ignored for this comparison when O(ignore_volatile_options=true).

fields.label: devices
fields.description: The devices for the instance (for example V({ "rootfs": { "path": "/dev/kvm", "type": "unix-char" }})). See U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/#/instances/instance_get).

fields.label: ephemeral
fields.description: Whether or not the instance is ephemeral (for example V(true) or V(false)). See U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/#/instances/instance_get).

fields.label: force stop
fields.description: If this is V(true), the C(lxd_container) forces to stop the instance when it stops or restarts the instance.

fields.label: ignore volatile options
fields.description: If set to V(true), options starting with C(volatile.) are ignored. As a result, they are reapplied for each execution. This default behavior can be changed by setting this option to V(false). The default value changed from V(true) to V(false) in community.general 6.0.0.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of an instance.

fields.label: profiles
fields.description: Profile to be used by the instance.

fields.label: project
fields.description: Project of an instance. See U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/projects/).

fields.label: snap url
fields.description: The unix domain socket path when LXD is installed by snap package manager.

fields.label: source
fields.description: The source for the instance (for example V({ "type": "image", "mode": "pull", "server": "https://cloud-images.ubuntu.com/releases/", "protocol": "simplestreams", "alias": "22.04" })). See U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/) for complete API documentation. Note that C(protocol) accepts two choices: V(lxd) or V(simplestreams).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Define the state of an instance.

fields.label: target
fields.description: For cluster deployments. It attempts to create an instance on a target node. If the instance exists elsewhere in a cluster, then it is not replaced nor moved. The name should respond to same name of the node you see in C(lxc cluster list).

fields.label: trust password
fields.description: The client trusted password. You need to set this password on the LXD server before running this module using the following command: C(lxc config set core.trust_password <some random password>). See U(https://www.stgraber.org/2016/04/18/lxd-api-direct-interaction/). If trust_password is set, this module send a request for authentication before sending any requests.

fields.label: type
fields.description: Instance type can be either V(virtual-machine) or V(container).

fields.label: url
fields.description: The unix domain socket path or the https URL for the LXD server.

fields.label: wait for container
fields.description: If set to V(true), the tasks wait until the task reports a success status when performing container operations.

fields.label: wait for ipv4 addresses
fields.description: If this is V(true), the C(lxd_container) waits until IPv4 addresses are set to the all network interfaces in the instance after starting or restarting.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: The client certificate file path. If not specified, it defaults to C($HOME/.config/lxc/client.crt).

fields.label: client key
fields.description: The client certificate key file path. If not specified, it defaults to C($HOME/.config/lxc/client.key).

fields.label: config
fields.description: The config for the instance (for example V({"limits.memory": "4GB"})). See U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/#/profiles/profile_get). If the profile already exists and its C(config) value in metadata obtained from GET /1.0/profiles/<name> U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/#/profiles/profile_get) are different, then this module tries to apply the configurations U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/#/profiles/profile_put). Not all config values are supported to apply the existing profile. Maybe you need to delete and recreate a profile.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Description of the profile.

fields.label: devices
fields.description: The devices for the profile (for example V({"rootfs": {"path": "/dev/kvm", "type": "unix-char"})). See U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/#/profiles/profile_get).

fields.label: merge profile
fields.description: Merge the configuration of the present profile with the new desired configuration, instead of replacing it.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of a profile.

fields.label: new name
fields.description: A new name of a profile. If this parameter is specified a profile is renamed to this name. See U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/#/profiles/profile_post).

fields.label: project
fields.description: Project of a profile. See U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/projects/).

fields.label: snap url
fields.description: The unix domain socket path when LXD is installed by snap package manager.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Define the state of a profile.

fields.label: trust password
fields.description: The client trusted password. You need to set this password on the LXD server before running this module using the following command: C(lxc config set core.trust_password <some random password>). See U(https://www.stgraber.org/2016/04/18/lxd-api-direct-interaction/). If O(trust_password) is set, this module send a request for authentication before sending any requests.

fields.label: url
fields.description: The unix domain socket path or the https URL for the LXD server.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: The client certificate file path. If not specified, it defaults to C($HOME/.config/lxc/client.crt).

fields.label: client key
fields.description: The client certificate key file path. If not specified, it defaults to C($HOME/.config/lxc/client.key).

fields.label: config
fields.description: The config for the project (for example V({"features.profiles": "true"})). See U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/#/projects/project_get). If the project already exists and its "config" value in metadata obtained from C(GET /1.0/projects/<name>) U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/#/projects/project_get) are different, then this module tries to apply the configurations U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/#/projects/project_put).

fields.label: description
fields.description: Description of the project.

fields.label: merge project
fields.description: Merge the configuration of the present project with the new desired configuration, instead of replacing it. If configuration is the same after merged, no change is made.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the project.

fields.label: new name
fields.description: A new name of a project. If this parameter is specified a project is renamed to this name. See U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/api/#/projects/project_post).

fields.label: snap url
fields.description: The Unix domain socket path when LXD is installed by snap package manager.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Define the state of a project.

fields.label: trust password
fields.description: The client trusted password. You need to set this password on the LXD server before running this module using the following command: C(lxc config set core.trust_password <some random password>) See U(https://www.stgraber.org/2016/04/18/lxd-api-direct-interaction/). If O(trust_password) is set, this module send a request for authentication before sending any requests.

fields.label: url
fields.description: The Unix domain socket path or the https URL for the LXD server.

modules.label: lxd_storage_pool_info
modules.tooltip: Retrieve information about LXD storage pools

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: The client certificate file path. If not specified, it defaults to C($HOME/.config/lxc/client.crt).

fields.label: client key
fields.description: The client certificate key file path. If not specified, it defaults to C($HOME/.config/lxc/client.key).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of a specific storage pool to retrieve information about. If not specified, information about all storage pools are returned.

fields.label: project
fields.description: Project of the storage pool. See U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/projects/).

fields.label: snap url
fields.description: The Unix domain socket path when LXD is installed by snap package manager.

fields.label: trust password
fields.description: The client trusted password. You need to set this password on the LXD server before running this module using the following command: C(lxc config set core.trust_password <some random password>) See U(https://www.stgraber.org/2016/04/18/lxd-api-direct-interaction/). If O(trust_password) is set, this module sends a request for authentication before sending any requests.

fields.label: type
fields.description: Filter storage pools by driver/type (for example V(dir), V(zfs), V(btrfs), V(lvm), or V(ceph)). If not specified, all storage pool types are returned. Can be a single type or a list of types.

fields.label: url
fields.description: The Unix domain socket path or the https URL for the LXD server.

modules.label: lxd_storage_volume_info
modules.tooltip: Retrieve information about LXD storage volumes

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: The client certificate file path. If not specified, it defaults to C($HOME/.config/lxc/client.crt).

fields.label: client key
fields.description: The client certificate key file path. If not specified, it defaults to C($HOME/.config/lxc/client.key).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of a specific storage volume to retrieve information about. If not specified, information about all volumes in the pool are returned.

fields.label: pool
fields.description: Name of the storage pool to query for volumes. This parameter is required.

fields.label: project
fields.description: Project of the storage volume. See U(https://documentation.ubuntu.com/lxd/en/latest/projects/).

fields.label: snap url
fields.description: The Unix domain socket path when LXD is installed by snap package manager.

fields.label: trust password
fields.description: The client trusted password. You need to set this password on the LXD server before running this module using the following command: C(lxc config set core.trust_password <some random password>) See U(https://www.stgraber.org/2016/04/18/lxd-api-direct-interaction/). If O(trust_password) is set, this module sends a request for authentication before sending any requests.

fields.label: type
fields.description: Filter volumes by type. Common types include V(container), V(virtual-machine), V(image), and V(custom). If not specified, all volume types are returned.

fields.label: url
fields.description: The Unix domain socket path or the https URL for the LXD server.

fields.label: name
fields.description: A list of port names.

fields.label: selfupdate
fields.description: Update Macports and the ports tree, either prior to installing ports or as a separate step. Equivalent to running C(port selfupdate).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicates the desired state of the port.

fields.label: upgrade
fields.description: Upgrade all outdated ports, either prior to installing ports or as a separate step. Equivalent to running C(port upgrade outdated).

fields.label: variant
fields.description: A port variant specification. O(variant) is only supported with O(state=installed) and O(state=present).

fields.label: attach
fields.description: A list of pathnames of files to attach to the message. Attached files have their content-type set to C(application/octet-stream).

fields.label: bcc
fields.description: The email-address(es) the mail is being 'blind' copied to. This is a list, which may contain address and phrase portions.

fields.label: body
fields.description: The body of the email being sent.

fields.label: cc
fields.description: The email-address(es) the mail is being copied to. This is a list, which may contain address and phrase portions.

fields.label: charset
fields.description: The character set of email being sent.

fields.label: ehlohost
fields.description: Allows for manual specification of host for EHLO.

fields.label: headers
fields.description: A list of headers which should be added to the message. Each individual header is specified as V(header=value) (see example below).

fields.label: message id domain
fields.description: The domain name to use for the L(Message-ID header, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Message-ID).

fields.label: secure
fields.description: If V(always), the connection only sends email if the connection is Encrypted. If the server does not accept the encrypted connection it fails. If V(try), the connection attempts to setup a secure SSL/TLS session, before trying to send. If V(never), the connection does not attempt to setup a secure SSL/TLS session, before sending. If V(starttls), the connection tries to upgrade to a secure SSL/TLS connection, before sending. If it is unable to do so it fails.

fields.label: sender
fields.description: The email-address the mail is sent from. May contain address and phrase.

fields.label: subject
fields.description: The subject of the email being sent.

fields.label: subtype
fields.description: The minor mime type, can be either V(plain) or V(html). The major type is always V(text).

fields.label: to
fields.description: The email-address(es) the mail is being sent to. This is a list, which may contain address and phrase portions.

fields.label: chdir
fields.description: Change to this directory before running make.

fields.label: file
fields.description: Use a custom Makefile.

fields.label: jobs
fields.description: Set the number of make jobs to run concurrently. Typically if set, this would be the number of processors and/or threads available to the machine. This is not supported by all make implementations.

fields.label: make
fields.description: Use a specific make binary.

fields.label: params
fields.description: Any extra parameters to pass to make. If the value is empty, only the key is used. For example, V(FOO:) produces V(FOO), not V(FOO=).

fields.label: target
fields.description: The target to run. Typically this would be something like V(install), V(test), or V(all). O(target) and O(targets) are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: targets
fields.description: The list of targets to run. Typically this would be something like V(install), V(test), or V(all). O(target) and O(targets) are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: alerts

fields.label: name

fields.label: notes

fields.label: resource type

fields.description: V(absent) - alert profile should not exist, V(present) - alert profile should exist.

fields.label: description

fields.label: expression

fields.label: options

fields.label: resource type

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(absent) - alert should not exist, V(present) - alert should exist.

fields.label: belongsto filters

fields.label: belongsto filters merge mode

Valid attributes are: - V(cpu_allocated) (int): use null to remove the quota. - V(mem_allocated) (GB): use null to remove the quota. - V(storage_allocated) (GB): use null to remove the quota. - V(vms_allocated) (int): use null to remove the quota. - V(templates_allocated) (int): use null to remove the quota.

fields.label: userid

modules.label: mas
