fields.label: id
fields.description: The Mac App Store identifier of the app(s) you want to manage. This can be found by running C(mas search APP_NAME) on your machine.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of the app installation. The V(absent) value requires root permissions, also see the examples.

fields.label: upgrade all
fields.description: Upgrade all installed Mac App Store apps.

fields.label: hs url
fields.description: URL of the homeserver, where the CS-API is reachable.

fields.label: msg html
fields.description: HTML form of the message to send to matrix.

fields.label: msg plain
fields.description: Plain text form of the message to send to matrix, usually markdown.

fields.label: room id
fields.description: ID of the room to send the notification to.

fields.label: token
fields.description: Authentication token for the API call. If provided, O(user_id) and O(password) are not required.

fields.label: user id
fields.description: The user ID of the user.

fields.label: api key
fields.description: Mattermost webhook API key. Log into your Mattermost site, go to Menu -> Integration -> Incoming Webhook -> Add Incoming Webhook. This gives you a full URL. O(api_key) is the last part. U(http://mattermost.example.com/hooks/API_KEY).

fields.label: attachments
fields.description: Define a list of attachments. For more information, see U(https://developers.mattermost.com/integrate/admin-guide/admin-message-attachments/). Required when O(text) is not set.

fields.label: channel
fields.description: Channel to send the message to. If absent, the message goes to the channel selected for the O(api_key).

fields.label: icon url
fields.description: URL for the message sender's icon.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: Set a priority for the message.

fields.label: text
fields.description: Text to send. Note that the module does not handle escaping characters. Required when O(attachments) is not set.

fields.label: url
fields.description: Mattermost URL (for example V(http://mattermost.yourcompany.com)).

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

modules.label: maven_artifact
modules.tooltip: Downloads an Artifact from a Maven Repository

fields.label: artifact id
fields.description: The maven artifactId coordinate.

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: The attributes the resulting filesystem object should have. To get supported flags look at the man page for I(chattr) on the target system. This string should contain the attributes in the same order as the one displayed by I(lsattr). The C(=) operator is assumed as default, otherwise C(+) or C(-) operators need to be included in the string.

fields.label: checksum alg
fields.description: If V(md5), checksums use the MD5 algorithm. This is the default. If V(sha1), checksums use the SHA1 algorithm. This can be used on systems configured to use FIPS-compliant algorithms, since MD5 is blocked on such systems.

fields.label: classifier
fields.description: The maven classifier coordinate.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: PEM formatted certificate chain file to be used for SSL client authentication. This file can also include the key as well, and if the key is included, O(client_key) is not required.

fields.label: client key
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains your private key to be used for SSL client authentication. If O(client_cert) contains both the certificate and key, this option is not required.

fields.label: dest
fields.description: The path where the artifact should be written to. If file mode or ownerships are specified and destination path already exists, they affect the downloaded file.

fields.label: directory mode
fields.description: Filesystem permission mode applied recursively to O(dest) when it is a directory.

fields.label: extension
fields.description: The maven type/extension coordinate.

fields.label: force basic auth
fields.description: C(httplib2), the library used by the URI module only sends authentication information when a webservice responds to an initial request with a 401 status. Since some basic auth services do not properly send a 401, logins fail. This option forces the sending of the Basic authentication header upon initial request.

fields.label: group
fields.description: Name of the group that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current group of the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership.

fields.label: group id
fields.description: The Maven groupId coordinate.

fields.label: headers
fields.description: Add custom HTTP headers to a request in hash/dict format.

fields.label: keep name
fields.description: If V(true), the downloaded artifact's name is preserved, in other words the version number remains part of it. This option only has effect when O(dest) is a directory and O(version) is set to V(latest) or O(version_by_spec) is defined.

fields.label: mode
fields.description: The permissions the resulting filesystem object should have. For those used to I(/usr/bin/chmod) remember that modes are actually octal numbers. You must give Ansible enough information to parse them correctly. For consistent results, quote octal numbers (for example, C('644') or C('1777')) so Ansible receives a string and can do its own conversion from string into number. Adding a leading zero (for example, C(0755)) works sometimes, but can fail in loops and some other circumstances. Giving Ansible a number without following either of these rules will end up with a decimal number which will have unexpected results. As of Ansible 1.8, the mode may be specified as a symbolic mode (for example, C(u+rwx) or C(u=rw,g=r,o=r)). If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does not) exist, the default C(umask) on the system will be used when setting the mode for the newly created filesystem object. If C(mode) is not specified and the destination filesystem object B(does) exist, the mode of the existing filesystem object will be used. Specifying C(mode) is the best way to ensure filesystem objects are created with the correct permissions. See CVE-2020-1736 for further details.

fields.label: owner
fields.description: Name of the user that should own the filesystem object, as would be fed to I(chown). When left unspecified, it uses the current user unless you are root, in which case it can preserve the previous ownership. Specifying a numeric username will be assumed to be a user ID and not a username. Avoid numeric usernames to avoid this confusion.

fields.label: repository url
fields.description: The URL of the Maven Repository to download from. Use V(s3://...) if the repository is hosted on Amazon S3. Use V(file://...) if the repository is local.

fields.label: selevel
fields.description: The level part of the SELinux filesystem object context. This is the MLS/MCS attribute, sometimes known as the C(range). When set to C(_default), it will use the C(level) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: serole
fields.description: The role part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(role) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: setype
fields.description: The type part of the SELinux filesystem object context. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(type) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: seuser
fields.description: The user part of the SELinux filesystem object context. By default it uses the C(system) policy, where applicable. When set to C(_default), it will use the C(user) portion of the policy if available.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The desired state of the artifact.

fields.label: unredirected headers
fields.description: A list of headers that should not be included in the redirection. This headers are sent to the C(fetch_url) function. On ansible-core version 2.12 or later, the default of this option is V([Authorization, Cookie]). Useful if the redirection URL does not need to have sensitive headers in the request. Requires ansible-core version 2.12 or later.

fields.label: unsafe writes
fields.description: Influence when to use atomic operation to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem object. By default this module uses atomic operations to prevent data corruption or inconsistent reads from the target filesystem objects, but sometimes systems are configured or just broken in ways that prevent this. One example is docker mounted filesystem objects, which cannot be updated atomically from inside the container and can only be written in an unsafe manner. This option allows Ansible to fall back to unsafe methods of updating filesystem objects when atomic operations fail (however, it doesn't force Ansible to perform unsafe writes). IMPORTANT! Unsafe writes are subject to race conditions and can lead to data corruption.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be set to V(false) when no other option exists.

fields.label: verify checksum
fields.description: If V(never), the MD5/SHA1 checksum is never downloaded and verified. If V(download), the MD5/SHA1 checksum is downloaded and verified only after artifact download. This is the default. If V(change), the MD5/SHA1 checksum is downloaded and verified if the destination already exist, to verify if they are identical. This was the behaviour before 2.6. Since it downloads the checksum before (maybe) downloading the artifact, and since some repository software, when acting as a proxy/cache, return a 404 error if the artifact has not been cached yet, it may fail unexpectedly. If you still need it, you should consider using V(always) instead - if you deal with a checksum, it is better to use it to verify integrity after download. V(always) combines V(download) and V(change).

fields.label: version
fields.description: The maven version coordinate. Mutually exclusive with O(version_by_spec).

fields.label: version by spec
fields.description: The maven dependency version ranges. See supported version ranges on U(https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/MAVENOLD/Dependency+Mediation+and+Conflict+Resolution). The range type V((,1.0],[1.2,\)) and V((,1.1\),(1.1,\)) is not supported. Mutually exclusive with O(version).

modules.label: memset_dns_reload

fields.label: api key
fields.description: The API key obtained from the Memset control panel.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The Memstore product name (that is, V(mstestyaa1)).

fields.label: api key

modules.label: memset_zone_record

fields.label: address

fields.label: api key

fields.label: priority

fields.description: The record's TTL in seconds (will inherit zone's TTL if not explicitly set). This must be a valid int from U(https://www.memset.com/apidocs/methods_dns.html#dns.zone_record_create).

fields.label: type

fields.label: zone
fields.description: The name of the zone to which to add the record to.

fields.label: backup crypt files
fields.description: Backup encrypted files.

fields.label: backup dmapi fs
fields.description: Back up DMAPI filesystem files.

fields.label: create map files
fields.description: Creates a new MAP files.

fields.label: exclude files
fields.description: Excludes files using C(/etc/rootvg.exclude).

fields.label: exclude wpar files
fields.description: Excludes WPAR files.

fields.label: extended attrs
fields.description: Backup extended attributes.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Backup name.

fields.label: new image data
fields.description: Creates a new file data.

fields.label: software packing
fields.description: Exclude files from packing option listed in C(/etc/exclude_packing.rootvg).

fields.label: storage path
fields.description: Storage path where the mksysb backup is stored.

fields.label: use snapshot
fields.description: Creates backup using snapshots.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of kernel module to manage.

fields.label: params
fields.description: Modules parameters.

fields.label: persistent
fields.description: Persistency between reboots for configured module. This option creates files in C(/etc/modules-load.d/) and C(/etc/modprobe.d/) that make your module configuration persistent during reboots. If V(present), adds module name to C(/etc/modules-load.d/) and params to C(/etc/modprobe.d/) so the module is loaded on next reboot. If V(absent), comments out module name from C(/etc/modules-load.d/) and comments out params from C(/etc/modprobe.d/) so the module is not loaded on next reboot. If V(disabled), does not touch anything and leaves C(/etc/modules-load.d/) and C(/etc/modprobe.d/) as it is. Note that it is usually a better idea to rely on the automatic module loading by PCI IDs, USB IDs, DMI IDs or similar triggers encoded in the kernel modules themselves instead of configuration like this. In fact, most modern kernel modules are prepared for automatic loading already. B(Note:) This option works only with distributions that use C(systemd) when set to values other than V(disabled).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the module should be present or absent.

modules.label: monit
modules.tooltip: Manage the state of a program monitored using Monit

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the C(monit) program/process to manage.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of service.

modules.label: mqtt
modules.tooltip: Publish a message on an MQTT topic for the IoT

fields.label: ca cert
fields.description: The path to the Certificate Authority certificate files that are to be treated as trusted by this client. If this is the only option given then the client operates in a similar manner to a web browser. That is to say it requires the broker to have a certificate signed by the Certificate Authorities in ca_certs and communicates using TLS v1, but does not attempt any form of authentication. This provides basic network encryption but may not be sufficient depending on how the broker is configured.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: The path pointing to the PEM encoded client certificate. If this is set it is used as client information for TLS based authentication. Support for this feature is broker dependent.

fields.label: client id
fields.description: MQTT client identifier. If not specified, it uses a value C(hostname + pid).

fields.label: client key
fields.description: The path pointing to the PEM encoded client private key. If this is set it is used as client information for TLS based authentication. Support for this feature is broker dependent.

fields.label: payload
fields.description: Payload. The special string V("None") may be used to send a NULL (that is, empty) payload which is useful to simply notify with the O(topic) or to clear previously retained messages.

fields.label: qos
fields.description: QoS (Quality of Service).

fields.label: retain
fields.description: Setting this flag causes the broker to retain (in other words keep) the message so that applications that subsequently subscribe to the topic can received the last retained message immediately.

fields.label: server
fields.description: MQTT broker address/name.

fields.label: tls version
fields.description: Specifies the version of the SSL/TLS protocol to be used. By default (if the python version supports it) the highest TLS version is detected. If unavailable, TLS v1 is used.

fields.label: topic
fields.description: MQTT topic name.

modules.label: mssql_db
modules.tooltip: Add or remove MSSQL databases from a remote host

fields.label: autocommit
fields.description: Automatically commit the change only if the import succeed. Sometimes it is necessary to use autocommit=true, since some content can not be changed within a transaction.

fields.label: login host
fields.description: Host running the database. For named instances, use the format V(server\\instance). In that case, do not use O(login_port).

fields.label: login password
fields.description: The password used to authenticate with.

fields.label: login port
fields.description: Port of the MSSQL server. Requires login_host be defined as other than localhost if login_port is used. Cannot be used together with a named instance in O(login_host) (that is, V(server\\instance) format). If O(login_host) is not a named instance and O(login_port) is not specified, it defaults to V(1433).

fields.label: login user
fields.description: The username used to authenticate with.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the database to add or remove.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The database state.

fields.label: target
fields.description: Location, on the remote host, of the dump file to read from or write to. Uncompressed SQL files (C(.sql)) files are supported.

modules.label: mssql_script
modules.tooltip: Execute SQL scripts on a MSSQL database

fields.label: login host
fields.description: Host running the database. For named instances, use the format V(server\\instance). In that case, do not use O(login_port).

fields.label: login password
fields.description: The password used to authenticate with.

fields.label: login port
fields.description: Port of the MSSQL server. Requires O(login_host) to be defined as well. Cannot be used together with a named instance in O(login_host) (that is, V(server\\instance) format). If O(login_host) is not a named instance and O(login_port) is not specified, it defaults to V(1433).

fields.label: login user
fields.description: The username used to authenticate with.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Database to run script against.

fields.label: output
fields.description: With V(default) each row is returned as a list of values. See RV(query_results). Output format V(dict) returns dictionary with the column names as keys. See RV(query_results_dict). V(dict) requires named columns to be returned by each query otherwise an error is thrown.

fields.label: params
fields.description:
Parameters passed to the script as SQL parameters.
(Query V('SELECT %(name\)s"') with V(example: '{"name": "John Doe"}).)'.

fields.label: script
fields.description: The SQL script to be executed. Script can contain multiple SQL statements. Multiple Batches can be separated by V(GO) command. Each batch must return at least one result set.

fields.label: transaction
fields.description: If transactional mode is requested, start a transaction and commit the change only if the script succeed. Otherwise, rollback the transaction. If transactional mode is not requested (default), automatically commit the change.

modules.label: nagios
modules.tooltip: Perform common tasks in Nagios related to downtime and notifications

fields.label: action
fields.description: Action to take. The V(acknowledge) and V(forced_check) actions were added in community.general 1.2.0.

fields.label: author
fields.description: Author to leave downtime comments as. Only used when O(action) is V(downtime) or V(acknowledge).

fields.label: cmdfile
fields.description: Path to the nagios I(command file) (FIFO pipe). Only required if auto-detection fails.

fields.label: command
fields.description: The raw command to send to Nagios, which should not include the submitted time header or the line-feed. B(Required) option when O(action=command).

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Comment when O(action) is V(downtime) or V(acknowledge).

fields.label: minutes
fields.description: Minutes to schedule downtime for. Only usable with O(action=downtime).

fields.label: servicegroup
fields.description: The Servicegroup we want to set downtimes/alerts for. B(Required) option when using the V(servicegroup_service_downtime) and V(servicegroup_host_downtime) O(action).

fields.label: services
fields.description: What to manage downtime/alerts for. Separate multiple services with commas. B(Required) option when O(action) is one of: V(downtime), V(acknowledge), V(forced_check), V(enable_alerts), V(disable_alerts). When specifying what O(services) to handle there is a special service value, V(host), which handles alerts/downtime/acknowledge for the I(host itself), for example O(services=host). This keyword may not be given with other services at the same time. B(Setting alerts/downtime/acknowledge for a host does not affect alerts/downtime/acknowledge for any of the services running on it.) To schedule downtime for all O(services) on particular host use keyword V(all), for example O(services=all). Before community.general 11.2.0, one could specify multiple services at once by separating them with commas, for example O(services=httpd,nfs,puppet). Since community.general 11.2.0, there can be spaces around the commas, and an actual list can be provided.

fields.label: start
fields.description: When downtime should start, in C(time_t) format (epoch seconds).

fields.label: api key
fields.description: API key for authentication, must be obtained using the netcup CCP (U(https://ccp.netcup.net)).

fields.label: api password
fields.description: API password for authentication, must be obtained using the netcup CCP (U(https://ccp.netcup.net)).

fields.label: customer id
fields.description: Netcup customer ID.

fields.label: domain
fields.description: Domainname the records should be added / removed.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: Record priority. Required for O(type=MX).

fields.label: record
fields.description: Record to add or delete, supports wildcard (V(*)). Default is V(@) (that is, the zone name).

fields.label: solo
fields.description: Whether the record should be the only one for that record type and record name. Only use with O(state=present). This deletes all other records with the same record name and type.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the record should exist or not.

fields.label: type
fields.description: Record type. Support for V(OPENPGPKEY), V(SMIMEA) and V(SSHFP) was added in community.general 8.1.0. Record types V(OPENPGPKEY) and V(SMIMEA) require nc-dnsapi >= 0.1.5. Record type V(SSHFP) requires nc-dnsapi >= 0.1.6.

fields.label: value
fields.description: Record value.

modules.label: newrelic_deployment
modules.tooltip: Notify New Relic about app deployments

fields.label: app name
fields.description: The value of C(app_name) in the C(newrelic.yml) file used by the application. One of O(app_name) or O(application_id) is required.

fields.label: app name exact match
fields.description: If this flag is set to V(true) then the application ID lookup by name would only work for an exact match. If set to V(false) it returns the first result.

fields.label: application id
fields.description: The application ID found in the metadata of the application in APM. One of O(app_name) or O(application_id) is required.

fields.label: changelog
fields.description: A list of changes for this deployment.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Text annotation for the deployment - notes for you.

fields.label: revision
fields.description: A revision number (for example, git commit SHA).

fields.label: token
fields.description: API token to place in the Api-Key header.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The name of the user/process that triggered this deployment.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: api key
fields.description: Nexmo API Key.

fields.label: api secret
fields.description: Nexmo API Secret.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: PEM formatted certificate chain file to be used for SSL client authentication. This file can also include the key as well, and if the key is included, C(client_key) is not required.

fields.label: client key
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains your private key to be used for SSL client authentication. If C(client_cert) contains both the certificate and key, this option is not required.

fields.label: dest
fields.description: Phone number(s) to send SMS message to.

fields.label: force
fields.description: If C(yes) do not get a cached copy.

fields.label: force basic auth
fields.description: Credentials specified with I(url_username) and I(url_password) should be passed in HTTP Header.

fields.label: http agent
fields.description: Header to identify as, generally appears in web server logs.

fields.label: msg
fields.description: Message text to send. Messages longer than 160 characters are split into multiple messages.

fields.label: src
fields.description: Nexmo Number to send from.

fields.label: url
fields.description: HTTP, HTTPS, or FTP URL in the form (http|https|ftp)://[user[:pass]]@host.domain[:port]/path

fields.label: url password
fields.description: The password for use in HTTP basic authentication. If the I(url_username) parameter is not specified, the I(url_password) parameter will not be used.

fields.label: url username
fields.description: The username for use in HTTP basic authentication. This parameter can be used without I(url_password) for sites that allow empty passwords

fields.label: use gssapi
fields.description: Use GSSAPI to perform the authentication, typically this is for Kerberos or Kerberos through Negotiate authentication. Requires the Python library L(gssapi,https://github.com/pythongssapi/python-gssapi) to be installed. Credentials for GSSAPI can be specified with I(url_username)/I(url_password) or with the GSSAPI env var C(KRB5CCNAME) that specified a custom Kerberos credential cache. NTLM authentication is C(not) supported even if the GSSAPI mech for NTLM has been installed.

fields.label: use proxy
fields.description: If C(no), it will not use a proxy, even if one is defined in an environment variable on the target hosts.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: url
fields.description: URL of the nginx status.

modules.label: nictagadm
modules.tooltip: Manage nic tags on SmartOS systems

fields.label: etherstub
fields.description: Specifies that the nic tag is attached to a created O(etherstub). Parameter O(etherstub) is mutually exclusive with both O(mtu), and O(mac).

fields.label: force
fields.description: When O(state=absent) this switch uses the C(-f) parameter and delete the nic tag regardless of existing VMs.

fields.label: mac
fields.description: Specifies the O(mac) address to attach the nic tag to when not creating an O(etherstub). Parameters O(mac) and O(etherstub) are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: mtu
fields.description: Specifies the size of the O(mtu) of the desired nic tag. Parameters O(mtu) and O(etherstub) are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the nic tag.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Create or delete a SmartOS nic tag.

fields.label: addr gen mode6
fields.description: Configure method for creating the address for use with IPv6 Stateless Address Autoconfiguration. V(default) and V(default-or-eui64) have been added in community.general 6.5.0.

fields.label: ageingtime
fields.description: This is only used with bridge - [ageing-time <0-1000000>] the Ethernet MAC address aging time, in seconds.

fields.label: arp interval
fields.description: This is only used with bond - ARP interval.

fields.label: arp ip target
fields.description: This is only used with bond - ARP IP target.

fields.label: autoconnect
fields.description: Whether the connection should start on boot. Whether the connection profile can be automatically activated.

fields.label: autoconnect priority
fields.description: The priority of the connection profile for autoconnect. If set, connection profiles with higher priority are preferred.

fields.label: autoconnect retries
fields.description: The number of times to retry autoconnecting.

fields.label: conn name
fields.description: The name used to call the connection. Pattern is V(<type>[-<ifname>][-<num>]).

fields.label: conn reload
fields.description: Whether the connection should be reloaded if it was modified.

fields.label: dhcp client id
fields.description: DHCP Client Identifier sent to the DHCP server.

fields.label: dns4
fields.description: A list of up to 3 DNS servers. The entries must be IPv4 addresses, for example V(192.0.2.53).

fields.label: dns4 ignore auto
fields.description: Ignore automatically configured IPv4 name servers.

fields.label: dns4 options
fields.description: A list of DNS options.

fields.label: dns4 search
fields.description: A list of DNS search domains.

fields.label: dns6
fields.description: A list of up to 3 DNS servers. The entries must be IPv6 addresses, for example V(2001:4860:4860::8888).

fields.label: dns6 ignore auto
fields.description: Ignore automatically configured IPv6 name servers.

fields.label: dns6 options
fields.description: A list of DNS options.

fields.label: dns6 search
fields.description: A list of DNS search domains.

fields.label: downdelay
fields.description: This is only used with bond - downdelay.

fields.label: egress
fields.description: This is only used with VLAN - VLAN egress priority mapping.

fields.label: fail over mac
fields.description: This is only used with bond - fail_over_mac.

fields.label: flags
fields.description: This is only used with VLAN - flags.

fields.label: forwarddelay
fields.description: This is only used with bridge - [forward-delay <2-30>] STP forwarding delay, in seconds.

fields.label: gw4
fields.description: The IPv4 gateway for this interface. Use the format V(192.0.2.1). This parameter is mutually_exclusive with never_default4 parameter.

fields.label: gw4 ignore auto
fields.description: Ignore automatically configured IPv4 routes.

fields.label: gw6
fields.description: The IPv6 gateway for this interface. Use the format V(2001:db8::1).

fields.label: gw6 ignore auto
fields.description: Ignore automatically configured IPv6 routes.

fields.label: hairpin
fields.description: This is only used with 'bridge-slave' - 'hairpin mode' for the slave, which allows frames to be sent back out through the slave the frame was received on. The default change to V(false) in community.general 7.0.0. It used to be V(true) before.

fields.label: hellotime
fields.description: This is only used with bridge - [hello-time <1-10>] STP hello time, in seconds.

fields.label: ifname
fields.description: The interface to bind the connection to. The connection is only applicable to this interface name. A special value of V(*) can be used for interface-independent connections. The O(ifname) argument is mandatory for all connection types except bond, team, bridge, vlan and vpn. This parameter defaults to O(conn_name) when left unset for all connection types except vpn that removes it.

fields.label: ignore unsupported suboptions
fields.description: Ignore suboptions which are invalid or unsupported by the version of NetworkManager/nmcli installed on the host. Only O(wifi) and O(wifi_sec) options are currently affected.

fields.label: infiniband mac
fields.description: MAC address of the Infiniband IPoIB devices.

fields.label: ingress
fields.description: This is only used with VLAN - VLAN ingress priority mapping.

fields.label: ip4
fields.description: List of IPv4 addresses to this interface. Use the format V(192.0.2.24/24) or V(192.0.2.24). If defined and O(method4) is not specified, automatically set C(ipv4.method) to V(manual).

fields.label: ip6
fields.description: List of IPv6 addresses to this interface. Use the format V(abbe::cafe/128) or V(abbe::cafe). If defined and O(method6) is not specified, automatically set C(ipv6.method) to V(manual).

fields.label: ip privacy6
fields.description: If enabled, it makes the kernel generate a temporary IPv6 address in addition to the public one.

fields.label: ip tunnel dev
fields.description: This is used with GRE/IPIP/SIT - parent device this GRE/IPIP/SIT tunnel, can use ifname.

fields.label: ip tunnel input key
fields.description: The key used for tunnel input packets. Only used when O(type=gre).

fields.label: ip tunnel local
fields.description: This is used with GRE/IPIP/SIT - GRE/IPIP/SIT local IP address.

fields.label: ip tunnel output key
fields.description: The key used for tunnel output packets. Only used when O(type=gre).

fields.label: ip tunnel remote
fields.description: This is used with GRE/IPIP/SIT - GRE/IPIP/SIT destination IP address.

fields.label: mac
fields.description: MAC address of the connection. Note this requires a recent kernel feature, originally introduced in 3.15 upstream kernel.

fields.label: master
fields.description: Master <master (ifname, or connection UUID or conn_name) of bridge, team, bond, ovs-port master connection profile. Mandatory if O(slave_type) is defined.

fields.label: maxage
fields.description: This is only used with bridge - [max-age <6-42>] STP maximum message age, in seconds.

fields.label: may fail4
fields.description: If you need O(ip4) configured before C(network-online.target) is reached, set this option to V(false). This option applies when O(method4) is not V(disabled).

fields.label: method4
fields.description: Configuration method to be used for IPv4. If O(ip4) is set, C(ipv4.method) is automatically set to V(manual) and this parameter is not needed.

fields.label: method6
fields.description: Configuration method to be used for IPv6. If O(ip6) is set, C(ipv6.method) is automatically set to V(manual) and this parameter is not needed. V(disabled) was added in community.general 3.3.0.

fields.label: miimon
fields.description: This is only used with bond - miimon. This parameter defaults to V(100) when unset.

fields.label: mode
fields.description: This is the type of device or network connection that you wish to create for a bond or bridge.

fields.label: mtu
fields.description: The connection MTU, for example V(9000). This can not be applied when creating the interface and is done once the interface has been created. Can be used when modifying Team, VLAN, Ethernet (Future plans to implement wifi, gsm, pppoe, infiniband). This parameter defaults to V(1500) when unset.

fields.label: never default4
fields.description: Set as default route. This parameter is mutually_exclusive with gw4 parameter.

fields.label: path cost
fields.description: This is only used with 'bridge-slave' - [<1-65535>] - STP port cost for destinations using this slave.

fields.label: primary
fields.description: This is only used with bond and is the primary interface name (for "active-backup" mode), this is the usually the 'ifname'.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: This is only used with 'bridge' - sets STP priority.

fields.label: route metric4
fields.description: Set metric level of ipv4 routes configured on interface.

fields.label: route metric6
fields.description: Set metric level of IPv6 routes configured on interface.

fields.label: routes4
fields.description: The list of IPv4 routes. Use the format V(192.0.3.0/24 192.0.2.1). To specify more complex routes, use the O(routes4_extended) option.

fields.label: routes6
fields.description: The list of IPv6 routes. Use the format V(fd12:3456:789a:1::/64 2001:dead:beef::1). To specify more complex routes, use the O(routes6_extended) option.

fields.label: routing rules4
fields.description: Is the same as in an C(ip rule add) command, except always requires specifying a priority.

fields.label: routing rules6
fields.description: Is the same as in an C(ip rule add) command, except always requires specifying a priority.

fields.label: runner
fields.description: This is the type of device or network connection that you wish to create for a team.

fields.label: runner fast rate
fields.description: Option specifies the rate at which our link partner is asked to transmit LACPDU packets. If this is V(true) then packets are sent once per second. Otherwise they are sent every 30 seconds. Only allowed for O(runner=lacp).

fields.label: runner hwaddr policy
fields.description: This defines the policy of how hardware addresses of team device and port devices should be set during the team lifetime.

fields.label: slave type
fields.description: Type of the device of this slave's master connection (for example V(bond)). Type V(ovs-port) is added in community.general 8.6.0.

fields.label: slavepriority
fields.description: This is only used with 'bridge-slave' - [<0-63>] - STP priority of this slave.

fields.label: ssid
fields.description: Name of the Wireless router or the access point.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the device should exist or not, taking action if the state is different from what is stated. Using O(state=present) creates connection set to be brought up automatically. Using O(state=up) and O(state=down) does not modify connection with other parameters. These states have been added in community.general 9.5.0.

fields.label: stp
fields.description: This is only used with bridge and controls whether Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) is enabled for this bridge.

fields.label: table
fields.description: This is only used with VRF - VRF table number.

fields.label: transport mode
fields.description: This option sets the connection type of Infiniband IPoIB devices.

fields.label: type
fields.description: This is the type of device or network connection that you wish to create or modify. Type V(dummy) is added in community.general 3.5.0. Type V(gsm) is added in community.general 3.7.0. Type V(infiniband) is added in community.general 2.0.0. Type V(loopback) is added in community.general 8.1.0. Type V(macvlan) is added in community.general 6.6.0. Type V(ovs-bridge) is added in community.general 8.6.0. Type V(ovs-interface) is added in community.general 8.6.0. Type V(ovs-port) is added in community.general 8.6.0. Type V(wireguard) is added in community.general 4.3.0. Type V(vpn) is added in community.general 5.1.0. Type V(vrf) is added in community.general 10.4.0. Using V(bond-slave), V(bridge-slave), or V(team-slave) implies V(ethernet) connection type with corresponding O(slave_type) option. If you want to control non-ethernet connection attached to V(bond), V(bridge), or V(team) consider using O(slave_type) option.

fields.label: updelay
fields.description: This is only used with bond - updelay.

fields.label: vlandev
fields.description: This is only used with VLAN - parent device this VLAN is on, can use ifname.

fields.label: vlanid
fields.description: This is only used with VLAN - VLAN ID in range <0-4095>.

fields.label: vxlan id
fields.description: This is only used with VXLAN - VXLAN ID.

fields.label: vxlan local
fields.description: This is only used with VXLAN - VXLAN local IP address.

fields.label: vxlan parent
fields.description: This is only used with VXLAN - VXLAN parent device (required when using a multicast remote address).

fields.label: vxlan remote
fields.description: This is only used with VXLAN - VXLAN destination IP address.

fields.label: xmit hash policy
fields.description: This is only used with bond - xmit_hash_policy type.

fields.label: zone
fields.description: The trust level of the connection. When updating this property on a currently activated connection, the change takes effect immediately.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: Path of certificate for TLS/SSL.

fields.label: client key
fields.description: Path of certificate's private key for TLS/SSL.

fields.label: content
fields.description: Content of Nomad job. Either this or O(name) must be specified.

fields.label: content format
fields.description: Type of content of Nomad job.

fields.label: force start
fields.description: Force job to started.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of job for delete, stop and start job without source. Name of job for delete, stop and start job without source. Either this or O(content) must be specified.

fields.label: namespace
fields.description: Namespace for Nomad.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Deploy or remove job.

fields.label: token
fields.description: ACL token for authentication.

fields.label: use ssl
fields.description: Use TLS/SSL connection.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Enable TLS/SSL certificate validation.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: Path of certificate for TLS/SSL.

fields.label: client key
fields.description: Path of certificate's private key for TLS/SSL.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of job for Get info. If not specified, lists all jobs.

fields.label: namespace
fields.description: Namespace for Nomad.

fields.label: token
fields.description: ACL token for authentication.

fields.label: use ssl
fields.description: Use TLS/SSL connection.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Enable TLS/SSL certificate validation.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: Path of certificate for TLS/SSL.

fields.label: client key
fields.description: Path of certificate's private key for TLS/SSL.

fields.label: global replicated
fields.description: Indicates whether or not the token was created with the C(--global).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of ACL token to create.

fields.label: namespace
fields.description: Namespace for Nomad.

fields.label: policies
fields.description: A list of the policies assigned to the token.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Create or remove ACL token.

fields.label: token
fields.description: ACL token for authentication.

fields.label: token type
fields.description: The type of the token can be V(client), V(management), or V(bootstrap).

fields.label: use ssl
fields.description: Use TLS/SSL connection.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Enable TLS/SSL certificate validation.

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Enable or disable the service, independently of C(*.preset) file preference or running state. Mutually exclusive with O(preset). It takes effect prior to O(state=reset).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the service to manage.

fields.label: preset
fields.description: Enable or disable the service according to local preferences in C(*.preset) files. Mutually exclusive with O(enabled). Only has an effect if set to true. It takes effect prior to O(state=reset).

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(started)/V(stopped) are idempotent actions that do not run commands unless necessary. V(restarted) always bounces the service. V(reloaded) sends a SIGHUP or starts the service. V(reset) starts or stops the service according to whether it is enabled or not.

fields.label: user
fields.description: Run system-control talking to the calling user's service manager, rather than the system-wide service manager.

fields.label: ci
fields.description: Install packages based on package-lock file, same as running C(npm ci).

fields.label: executable
fields.description: The executable location for npm. This is useful if you are using a version manager, such as nvm.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Use the C(--force) flag when installing.

fields.label: global
fields.description: Install the node.js library globally. When O(global=true), then O(path) is not used to install the packages.

fields.label: ignore scripts
fields.description: Use the C(--ignore-scripts) flag when installing.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of a node.js library to install.

fields.label: no bin links
fields.description: Use the C(--no-bin-links) flag when installing.

fields.label: no optional
fields.description: Use the C(--no-optional) flag when installing.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The base path where to install the node.js libraries. When O(global=true), then O(path) is not used to install the packages.

fields.label: production
fields.description: Install dependencies in production mode, excluding devDependencies.

fields.label: registry
fields.description: The registry to install modules from.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of the node.js library.

fields.label: unsafe perm
fields.description: Use the C(--unsafe-perm) flag when installing.

fields.label: version
fields.description: The version to be installed.

fields.label: key algorithm
fields.description: Specify key algorithm used by O(key_secret). Use V(gss-tsig) for GSS-TSIG authentication (requires the gssapi library and Kerberos credentials). V(gss-tsig) was added in community.general 12.3.0.

fields.label: key name
fields.description: Use TSIG key name to authenticate against DNS O(server). Not required when using O(key_algorithm=gss-tsig).

fields.label: key secret
fields.description: Use TSIG key secret, associated with O(key_name), to authenticate against O(server). Not required when using O(key_algorithm=gss-tsig).

fields.label: protocol
fields.description: Sets the transport protocol (TCP or UDP). TCP is the recommended and a more robust option.

fields.label: record
fields.description: Sets the DNS record to modify. When zone is omitted this has to be absolute (ending with a dot).

fields.label: server
fields.description: Apply DNS modification on this server, specified by IPv4/IPv6 address or FQDN. FQDNs are supported since community.general 12.3.0.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Manage DNS record.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: Sets the record TTL.

fields.label: type
fields.description: Sets the record type.

fields.label: value
fields.description: Sets the record value.

fields.label: zone
fields.description: DNS record is modified on this O(zone). When omitted, DNS is queried to attempt finding the correct zone.

modules.label: ocapi_command
modules.tooltip: Manages Out-Of-Band controllers using Open Composable API (OCAPI)

fields.label: baseuri
fields.description: Base URI of OOB controller.

fields.label: category
fields.description: Category to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: command
fields.description: Command to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: job name
fields.description: For O(command=DeleteJob) command, the name of the job to delete.

fields.label: proxy slot number
fields.description: For proxied inband requests, the slot number of the IOM. Only applies if O(baseuri) is a proxy server.

fields.label: update image path
fields.description: For O(command=FWUpload), the path on the local filesystem of the firmware update image.

modules.label: ocapi_info
modules.tooltip: Manages Out-Of-Band controllers using Open Composable API (OCAPI)

fields.label: baseuri
fields.description: Base URI of OOB controller.

fields.label: category
fields.description: Category to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: command
fields.description: Command to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: job name
fields.description: Name of job for fetching status.

fields.label: proxy slot number
fields.description: For proxied inband requests, the slot number of the IOM. Only applies if O(baseuri) is a proxy server.

fields.label: commit
fields.description: Perform a commit after the execution of the SQL query. Some databases allow a commit after a select whereas others raise an exception. Default is V(true) to support legacy module behavior.

fields.label: dsn
fields.description: The connection string passed into ODBC.

fields.label: params
fields.description: Parameters to pass to the SQL query.

fields.label: query
fields.description: The SQL query to perform.

modules.label: office_365_connector_card
modules.tooltip: Use webhooks to create Connector Card messages within an Office 365 group

fields.label: actions
fields.description: This array of objects is used to power the action links found at the bottom of the card.

fields.label: color
fields.description: Accent color used for branding or indicating status in the card.

fields.label: sections
fields.description: Contains a list of sections to display in the card. For more information see U(https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/outlook/actionable-messages/message-card-reference#section-fields).

fields.label: summary
fields.description: A string used for summarizing card content. This is the message subject. This is required if the text parameter is not populated.

fields.label: text
fields.description: The main text of the card. This is rendered below the sender information and optional title, And above any sections or actions present.

fields.label: title
fields.description: A title for the Connector message. Shown at the top of the message.

fields.label: webhook
fields.description: The webhook URL is given to you when you create a new Connector.

fields.label: ddns
fields.description: Enable dynamic DNS updates for this host.

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: Sets the host lease hostname (mandatory if O(state=present)).

fields.label: ip
fields.description: Sets the lease host IP address.

fields.label: key
fields.description: Sets the TSIG key content for authenticating against OMAPI server.

fields.label: key name
fields.description: Sets the TSIG key name for authenticating against OMAPI server.

fields.label: macaddr
fields.description: Sets the lease host MAC address.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Create or remove OMAPI host.

fields.label: statements
fields.description: Attach a list of OMAPI DHCP statements with host lease (without ending semicolon).

fields.label: api password
fields.description: The password or token for XMLRPC authentication. If not specified then the value of the E(ONE_PASSWORD) environment variable, if any, is used.

fields.label: api url
fields.description: The ENDPOINT URL of the XMLRPC server. If not specified then the value of the E(ONE_URL) environment variable, if any, is used.

fields.label: api username
fields.description: The name of the user for XMLRPC authentication. If not specified then the value of the E(ONE_USERNAME) environment variable, if any, is used.

fields.label: cluster id
fields.description: The cluster ID.

fields.label: cluster name
fields.description: The cluster specified by name.

fields.label: im mad name
fields.description: The name of the information manager, this values are taken from the oned.conf with the tag name IM_MAD (name).

fields.label: labels
fields.description: The labels for this host.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Hostname of the machine to manage.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Takes the host to the desired lifecycle state. If V(absent) the host is deleted from the cluster. If V(present) the host is created in the cluster (includes V(enabled), V(disabled) and V(offline) states). If V(enabled) the host is fully operational. V(disabled), for example to perform maintenance operations. V(offline), host is totally offline.

fields.label: template
fields.description: The template or attribute changes to merge into the host template.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to validate the TLS/SSL certificates or not. This parameter is ignored if E(PYTHONHTTPSVERIFY) environment variable is used.

fields.label: vmm mad name
fields.description: The name of the virtual machine manager mad name, this values are taken from the oned.conf with the tag name VM_MAD (name).

fields.label: wait timeout
fields.description: Time to wait for the desired state to be reached before timeout, in seconds.

fields.label: api password
fields.description: The password or token for XMLRPC authentication. If not specified then the value of the E(ONE_PASSWORD) environment variable, if any, is used.

fields.label: api url
fields.description: The ENDPOINT URL of the XMLRPC server. If not specified then the value of the E(ONE_URL) environment variable, if any, is used.

fields.label: api username
fields.description: The name of the user for XMLRPC authentication. If not specified then the value of the E(ONE_USERNAME) environment variable, if any, is used.

fields.label: create
fields.description: Whether the image should be created if not present. This is ignored if O(state=absent).

fields.label: datastore id
fields.description: Use with O(create=true) to specify datastore for image.

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Whether the image should be enabled or disabled.

fields.label: id
fields.description: A O(id) of the image you would like to manage.

fields.label: name
fields.description: A O(name) of the image you would like to manage. Required if O(create=true).

fields.label: new name
fields.description: A name that is assigned to the existing or new image. In the case of cloning, by default O(new_name) is set to the name of the origin image with the prefix 'Copy of'.

fields.label: persistent
fields.description: Whether the image should be persistent or non-persistent.

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(present) - state that is used to manage the image. V(absent) - delete the image. V(cloned) - clone the image. V(renamed) - rename the image to the O(new_name).

fields.label: template
fields.description: Use with O(create=true) to specify image template.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to validate the TLS/SSL certificates or not. This parameter is ignored if E(PYTHONHTTPSVERIFY) environment variable is used.

fields.label: wait timeout
fields.description: Seconds to wait until image is ready, deleted or cloned.

fields.label: api password
fields.description: The password or token for XMLRPC authentication. If not specified then the value of the E(ONE_PASSWORD) environment variable, if any, is used.

fields.label: api url
fields.description: The ENDPOINT URL of the XMLRPC server. If not specified then the value of the E(ONE_URL) environment variable, if any, is used.

fields.label: api username
fields.description: The name of the user for XMLRPC authentication. If not specified then the value of the E(ONE_USERNAME) environment variable, if any, is used.

fields.label: ids
fields.description: A list of images IDs whose facts you want to gather. Module can use integers too.

fields.label: name
fields.description: A O(name) of the image whose facts is gathered. If the O(name) begins with V(~) the O(name) is used as regex pattern, which restricts the list of images (whose facts is returned) whose names match specified regex. Also, if the O(name) begins with V(~*) case-insensitive matching is performed. See examples for more details.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to validate the TLS/SSL certificates or not. This parameter is ignored if E(PYTHONHTTPSVERIFY) environment variable is used.

fields.label: wait timeout
fields.description: Time to wait for the desired state to be reached before timeout, in seconds.

fields.label: api password
fields.description: Password of the user to login into OpenNebula OneFlow API server. If not set then the value of the E(ONEFLOW_PASSWORD) environment variable is used.

fields.label: api url
fields.description: URL of the OpenNebula OneFlow API server. It is recommended to use HTTPS so that the username/password are not transferred over the network unencrypted. If not set then the value of the E(ONEFLOW_URL) environment variable is used.

fields.label: api username
fields.description: Name of the user to login into the OpenNebula OneFlow API server. If not set then the value of the E(ONEFLOW_USERNAME) environment variable is used.

fields.label: cardinality
fields.description: Number of VMs for the specified role.

fields.label: custom attrs
fields.description: Dictionary of key/value custom attributes which is used when instantiating a new service.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Force the new cardinality even if it is outside the limits.

fields.label: group id
fields.description: ID of the group which is set as the group of the service.

fields.label: mode
fields.description: Set permission mode of a service instance in octet format, for example V(0600) to give owner C(use) and C(manage) and nothing to group and others.

fields.label: owner id
fields.description: ID of the user which is set as the owner of the service.

fields.label: role
fields.description: Name of the role whose cardinality should be changed.

fields.label: service id
fields.description: ID of a service instance that you would like to manage.

fields.label: service name
fields.description: Name of a service instance that you would like to manage.

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(present) - instantiate a service from a template specified with O(template_id) or O(template_name). V(absent) - terminate an instance of a service specified with O(template_id) or O(template_name).

fields.label: template id
fields.description: ID of a service template to use to create a new instance of a service.

fields.label: template name
fields.description: Name of service template to use to create a new instance of a service.

fields.label: unique
fields.description: Setting O(unique=true) ensures that there is only one service instance running with a name set with O(service_name) when instantiating a service from a template specified with O(template_id) or O(template_name). Check examples below.

fields.label: wait
fields.description: Wait for the instance to reach RUNNING state after DEPLOYING or COOLDOWN state after SCALING.

fields.label: wait timeout
fields.description: How long before wait gives up, in seconds.

fields.label: api password
fields.description: The password or token for XMLRPC authentication. If not specified then the value of the E(ONE_PASSWORD) environment variable, if any, is used.

fields.label: api url
fields.description: The ENDPOINT URL of the XMLRPC server. If not specified then the value of the E(ONE_URL) environment variable, if any, is used.

fields.label: api username
fields.description: The name of the user for XMLRPC authentication. If not specified then the value of the E(ONE_USERNAME) environment variable, if any, is used.

fields.label: filter
fields.description: V(user_primary_group) - Resources belonging to the user's primary group. V(user) - Resources belonging to the user. V(all) - All resources. V(user_groups) - Resources belonging to the user and any of his groups.

fields.label: id
fields.description: A O(id) of the template you would like to manage. If not set then a new template is created with the given O(name).

fields.label: name
fields.description: A O(name) of the template you would like to manage. If a template with the given name does not exist it is created, otherwise it is managed by this module.

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(present) - state that is used to manage the template. V(absent) - delete the template.

fields.label: template
fields.description: A string containing the template contents.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to validate the TLS/SSL certificates or not. This parameter is ignored if E(PYTHONHTTPSVERIFY) environment variable is used.

fields.label: wait timeout
fields.description: Time to wait for the desired state to be reached before timeout, in seconds.

fields.label: api password
fields.description: Password of the user to login into OpenNebula RPC server. If not set then the value of the E(ONE_PASSWORD) environment variable is used. if both O(api_username) or O(api_password) are not set, then it tries to authenticate with ONE auth file. Default path is C(~/.one/one_auth). Set environment variable E(ONE_AUTH) to override this path.

fields.label: api url
fields.description: URL of the OpenNebula RPC server. It is recommended to use HTTPS so that the username/password are not transferred over the network unencrypted. If not set then the value of the E(ONE_URL) environment variable is used.

fields.label: api username
fields.description: Name of the user to login into the OpenNebula RPC server. If not set then the value of the E(ONE_USERNAME) environment variable is used.

fields.label: attributes
fields.description: A dictionary of key/value attributes to add to new instances, or for setting C(state) of instances with these attributes. Keys are case insensitive and OpenNebula automatically converts them to upper case. Be aware V(NAME) is a special attribute which sets the name of the VM when it is deployed. C(#) character(s) can be appended to the C(NAME) and the module automatically adds indexes to the names of VMs. For example: V(NAME: foo-###) would create VMs with names V(foo-000), V(foo-001),... When used with O(count_attributes) and O(exact_count) the module matches the base name without the index part.

fields.label: count
fields.description: Number of instances to launch.

fields.label: count attributes
fields.description: A dictionary of key/value attributes that can only be used with O(exact_count) to determine how many nodes based on a specific attributes criteria should be deployed. This can be expressed in multiple ways and is shown in the EXAMPLES section.

fields.label: count labels
fields.description: A list of labels that can only be used with O(exact_count) to determine how many nodes based on a specific labels criteria should be deployed. This can be expressed in multiple ways and is shown in the EXAMPLES section.

fields.label: cpu
fields.description: Percentage of CPU divided by 100 required for the new instance. Half a processor is written 0.5.

fields.label: datastore id
fields.description: Name of Datastore to use to create a new instance.

fields.label: datastore name
fields.description: Name of Datastore to use to create a new instance.

fields.label: disk saveas
fields.description: Creates an image from a VM disk. It is a dictionary where you have to specify C(name) of the new image. Optionally you can specify C(disk_id) of the disk you want to save. By default C(disk_id) is 0. B(NOTE:) This operation is only performed on the first VM (if more than one VM ID is passed) and the VM has to be in the C(poweredoff) state. Also this operation fails if an image with specified C(name) already exists.

fields.label: disk size
fields.description: The size of the disk created for new instances (in MB, GB, TB,...). B(NOTE:) If The Template hats Multiple Disks the Order of the Sizes is matched against the order specified in O(template_id)/O(template_name).

fields.label: exact count
fields.description: Indicates how many instances that match O(count_attributes) and O(count_labels) parameters should be deployed. Instances are either created or terminated based on this value. B(NOTE:) Instances with the least IDs are terminated first.

fields.label: group id
fields.description: ID of the group which is set as the group of the instance.

fields.label: hard
fields.description: Reboot, power-off or terminate instances C(hard).

fields.label: instance ids
fields.description: A list of instance IDs used for states: V(absent), V(running), V(rebooted), V(poweredoff).

fields.label: labels
fields.description: A list of labels to associate with new instances, or for setting C(state) of instances with these labels.

fields.label: memory
fields.description: The size of the memory for new instances (in MB, GB, ..).

fields.label: mode
fields.description: Set permission mode of the instance in octet format, for example V(0600) to give owner C(use) and C(manage) and nothing to group and others.

fields.label: networks
fields.description: A list of dictionaries with network parameters. See examples for more details.

fields.label: owner id
fields.description: ID of the user which is set as the owner of the instance.

fields.label: persistent
fields.description: Create a private persistent copy of the template plus any image defined in DISK, and instantiate that copy.

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(present) - create instances from a template specified with C(template_id)/C(template_name). V(running) - run instances. V(poweredoff) - power-off instances. V(rebooted) - reboot instances. V(absent) - terminate instances.

fields.label: template id
fields.description: ID of a VM template to use to create a new instance.

fields.label: template name
fields.description: Name of VM template to use to create a new instance.

fields.label: updateconf
fields.description: When O(instance_ids) is provided, updates running VMs with the C(updateconf) API call. When new VMs are being created, emulates the C(updateconf) API call using direct template merge. Allows for complete modifications of the C(CONTEXT) attribute. Supported attributes include: B(BACKUP_CONFIG:) V(BACKUP_VOLATILE), V(FS_FREEZE), V(INCREMENT_MODE), V(KEEP_LAST), V(MODE); B(CONTEXT:) (Any value, except V(ETH*). Variable substitutions are made); B(CPU_MODEL:) V(FEATURES), V(MODEL); B(FEATURES:) V(ACPI), V(APIC), V(GUEST_AGENT), V(HYPERV), V(IOTHREADS), V(LOCALTIME), V(PAE), V(VIRTIO_BLK_QUEUES), V(VIRTIO_SCSI_QUEUES); B(GRAPHICS:) V(COMMAND), V(KEYMAP), V(LISTEN), V(PASSWD), V(PORT), V(TYPE); B(INPUT:) V(BUS), V(TYPE); B(OS:) V(ARCH), V(BOOT), V(BOOTLOADER), V(FIRMWARE), V(INITRD), V(KERNEL), V(KERNEL_CMD), V(MACHINE), V(ROOT), V(SD_DISK_BUS), V(UUID); B(RAW:) V(DATA), V(DATA_VMX), V(TYPE), V(VALIDATE); B(VIDEO:) V(ATS), V(IOMMU), V(RESOLUTION), V(TYPE), V(VRAM).

fields.label: vcpu
fields.description: Number of CPUs (cores) the new VM uses.

fields.label: vm start on hold
fields.description: Set to true to put VM on hold while creating.

fields.label: wait
fields.description: Wait for the instance to reach its desired state before returning. Keep in mind if you are waiting for instance to be in running state it does not mean that you are able to SSH on that machine only that boot process have started on that instance. See the example using the M(ansible.builtin.wait_for) module for details.

fields.label: wait timeout
fields.description: How long before wait gives up, in seconds.

fields.label: api password
fields.description: The password or token for XMLRPC authentication. If not specified then the value of the E(ONE_PASSWORD) environment variable, if any, is used.

fields.label: api url
fields.description: The ENDPOINT URL of the XMLRPC server. If not specified then the value of the E(ONE_URL) environment variable, if any, is used.

fields.label: api username
fields.description: The name of the user for XMLRPC authentication. If not specified then the value of the E(ONE_USERNAME) environment variable, if any, is used.

fields.label: id
fields.description: A O(id) of the network you would like to manage. If not set then a new network is created with the given O(name).

fields.label: name
fields.description: A O(name) of the network you would like to manage. If a network with the given name does not exist it, then is created, otherwise it is managed by this module.

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(present) - state that is used to manage the network. V(absent) - delete the network.

fields.label: template
fields.description: A string containing the network template contents.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Whether to validate the TLS/SSL certificates or not. This parameter is ignored if E(PYTHONHTTPSVERIFY) environment variable is used.

fields.label: wait timeout
fields.description: Time to wait for the desired state to be reached before timeout, in seconds.

fields.label: cli path
fields.description: Used to specify the exact path to the C(op) command line interface.

modules.label: oneview_datacenter_info

fields.label: api version
fields.description: OneView API Version.

fields.label: config
fields.description: Path to a JSON configuration file containing the OneView client configuration. The configuration file is optional and when used should be present in the host running the ansible commands. If the file path is not provided, the configuration is loaded from environment variables. For links to example configuration files or how to use the environment variables verify the notes section.

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: IP address or hostname for the appliance.

fields.label: image streamer hostname
fields.description: IP address or hostname for the HPE Image Streamer REST API.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Data Center name.

fields.label: options
fields.description: Retrieve additional information. Options available: V(visualContent).

fields.label: params
fields.description: List of parameters to delimit, filter and sort the list of resources. Parameter keys allowed are: V(start): The first item to return, using 0-based indexing. V(count): The number of resources to return. V(filter): A general filter/query string to narrow the list of items returned. V(sort): The sort order of the returned data set.

The configuration file is optional and when used should be present in the host running the ansible commands. If the file path is not provided, the configuration is loaded from environment variables. For links to example configuration files or how to use the environment variables verify the notes section.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Logical Interconnect Group name.

fields.label: params
fields.description: List of parameters to delimit, filter and sort the list of resources. Parameter keys allowed are: V(start): The first item to return, using 0-based indexing. V(count): The number of resources to return. V(filter): A general filter/query string to narrow the list of items returned. V(sort): The sort order of the returned data set.

fields.description: Path to a JSON configuration file containing the OneView client configuration. V(connection_information_set) updates the connection information for the SAN Manager. This operation is non-idempotent.

The configuration file is optional and when used should be present in the host running the ansible commands. If the file path is not provided, the configuration is loaded from environment variables. For links to example configuration files or how to use the environment variables verify the notes section.

fields.label: hostname

fields.label: image streamer hostname

fields.label: params
fields.description: List of params to delimit, filter and sort the list of resources. Params allowed: V(start): The first item to return, using 0-based indexing. V(count): The number of resources to return. V(query): A general query string to narrow the list of resources returned. V(sort): The sort order of the returned data set.

fields.label: provider display name
fields.description: Provider Display Name.

fields.label: api timeout
fields.description: HTTP timeout to Online API in seconds.

fields.label: api token
fields.description: Online OAuth token.

fields.label: api url
fields.description: Online API URL.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Validate SSL certs of the Online API.

fields.label: api timeout
fields.description: HTTP timeout to Online API in seconds.

fields.label: api token
fields.description: Online OAuth token.

fields.label: api url
fields.description: Online API URL.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Validate SSL certs of the Online API.

fields.label: auto node startup
fields.description: Whether the target node should be automatically connected at startup.

fields.label: auto portal startup
fields.description: Whether the target node portal should be automatically connected at startup.

fields.label: discover
fields.description: Whether the list of target nodes on the portal should be (re)discovered and added to the persistent iSCSI database. Keep in mind that C(iscsiadm) discovery resets configuration, like C(node.startup) to manual, hence combined with O(auto_node_startup=true) always returns a changed state.

fields.label: login
fields.description: Whether the target node should be connected. When O(target) is omitted, it logins to all available.

fields.label: node auth
fields.description: The value for C(node.session.auth.authmethod).

fields.label: node pass
fields.description: The value for C(node.session.auth.password).

fields.label: node pass in
fields.description: The value for C(node.session.auth.password_in).

fields.label: node user
fields.description: The value for C(node.session.auth.username).

fields.label: node user in
fields.description: The value for C(node.session.auth.username_in).

fields.label: portal
fields.description: The domain name or IP address of the iSCSI target.

fields.label: rescan
fields.description: Rescan an established session for discovering new targets. When O(target) is omitted, it rescans all sessions.

fields.label: show nodes
fields.description: Whether the list of nodes in the persistent iSCSI database should be returned by the module.

fields.label: target
fields.description: The iSCSI target name.

fields.label: autoremove
fields.description: Calls C(pkg_delete -a) to remove automatically installed packages which are no longer needed.

fields.label: build
fields.description: Build the package from source instead of downloading and installing a binary. Requires that the port source tree is already installed. Automatically builds and installs the C(sqlports) package, if it is not already installed. Mutually exclusive with O(snapshot).

fields.label: clean
fields.description: When updating or removing packages, delete the extra configuration file(s) in the old packages which are annotated with C(@extra) in the packaging-list.

fields.label: name
fields.description: A name or a list of names of the packages.

fields.label: ports dir
fields.description: When used in combination with the O(build) option, allows overriding the default ports source directory.

fields.label: quick
fields.description: Replace or delete packages quickly; do not bother with checksums before removing normal files.

fields.label: snapshot
fields.description: Force C(%c) and C(%m) to expand to C(snapshots), even on a release kernel. Mutually exclusive with O(build).

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(present) ensures the package is installed. V(latest) ensures the latest version of the package is installed. V(absent) ensures the specified package is not installed.

modules.label: opendj_backendprop
modules.tooltip: Update the backend configuration of OpenDJ using the dsconfig set-backend-prop command

fields.label: backend
fields.description: The name of the backend on which the property needs to be updated.

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: The hostname of the OpenDJ server.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The configuration setting to update.

fields.label: opendj bindir
fields.description: The path to the bin directory of OpenDJ.

fields.label: passwordfile
fields.description: Location to the password file which holds the password for O(username). Either O(password) or O(passwordfile) is needed.

fields.label: state
fields.description: If configuration needs to be added/updated.

fields.label: value
fields.description: The value for the configuration item.

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Whether the service should start on boot. B(At least one) of O(state) and O(enabled) are required.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the service.

fields.label: pattern
fields.description: If the service does not respond to the C(running) command, name a substring to look for as would be found in the output of the C(ps) command as a stand-in for a C(running) result. If the string is found, the service is assumed to be running.

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(started)/V(stopped) are idempotent actions that do not run commands unless necessary. V(restarted) always bounces the service. V(reloaded) always reloads.

modules.label: opkg
modules.tooltip: Package manager for OpenWrt and Openembedded/Yocto based Linux distributions

fields.label: executable
fields.description: The executable location for C(opkg).

fields.label: force
fields.description: The C(opkg --force) parameter used.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of package(s) to install/remove. V(NAME=VERSION) syntax is also supported to install a package in a certain version. See the examples. This only works on Yocto based Linux distributions (opkg>=0.3.2) and not for OpenWrt. This is supported since community.general 6.2.0.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the package.

fields.label: update cache
fields.description: Update the package DB first.

fields.label: array add
fields.description: Add new elements to the array for a key which has an array as its value.

fields.label: check type
fields.description: Checks if the type of the provided O(value) matches the type of an existing default. If the types do not match, raises an error.

fields.label: dict mode
fields.description: Defines the write behavior for O(type=dict) values. V(replace) writes the full dictionary, replacing any existing value. V(add) merges only the specified keys into the existing dictionary, leaving other keys untouched.

fields.label: domain
fields.description: The domain is a domain name of the form C(com.companyname.appname).

fields.label: key
fields.description: The key of the user preference.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The path in which to search for C(defaults).

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state of the user defaults. If set to V(list) it queries the given parameter specified by O(key). Returns V(null) is nothing found or misspelled.

fields.label: type
fields.description: The type of value to write. V(dict) has been added in community.general 12.5.0.

fields.label: value
fields.description: The value to write. Only required when O(state=present).

fields.label: application key
fields.description: The applicationKey to use.

fields.label: application secret
fields.description: The application secret to use.

fields.label: consumer key
fields.description: The consumer key to use.

fields.label: endpoint
fields.description: The endpoint to use (for instance V(ovh-eu)).

fields.label: name
fields.description: The IP address to manage (can be a single IP like V(1.1.1.1) or a block like V(1.1.1.1/28)).

fields.label: service
fields.description: The name of the OVH service this IP address should be routed.

fields.label: wait completion
fields.description: If V(true), the module waits for the IP address to be moved. If false, exit without waiting. The C(taskId) is returned in module output.

fields.label: wait task completion
fields.description: If not V(0), the module waits for this task ID to be completed. Use O(wait_task_completion) if you want to wait for completion of a previously executed task with O(wait_completion=false). You can execute this module repeatedly on a list of failover IPs using O(wait_completion=false) (see examples).

fields.label: application key
fields.description: The applicationKey to use.

fields.label: application secret
fields.description: The application secret to use.

fields.label: backend
fields.description: The IP address of the backend to update / modify / delete.

fields.label: consumer key
fields.description: The consumer key to use.

fields.label: endpoint
fields.description: The endpoint to use (for instance V(ovh-eu)).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the LoadBalancing internal name (V(ip-X.X.X.X)).

fields.label: probe
fields.description: Determines the type of probe to use for this backend.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Determines whether the backend is to be created/modified or deleted.

fields.label: weight
fields.description: Determines the weight for this backend.

fields.label: application key
fields.description: The applicationKey to use.

fields.label: application secret
fields.description: The application secret to use.

fields.label: consumer key
fields.description: The consumer key to use.

fields.label: endpoint
fields.description: The endpoint to use (for instance V(ovh-eu)).

fields.label: instance id
