fields.description: ID of the instance, get it with U(https://api.ovh.com/console/#/cloud/project/%7BserviceName%7D/instance#GET).

fields.label: project id
fields.description: ID of the project, get it with U(https://api.ovh.com/console/#/cloud/project#GET).

fields.label: force
fields.description: Force the change of the cluster state.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specify which node of the cluster you want to manage. V(null) == the cluster status itself, V(all) == check the status of all nodes.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicate desired state of the cluster. The value V(maintenance) has been added in community.general 11.1.0.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specify the resource name to create or clone to. This is required if O(state=present), O(state=absent), O(state=enabled), or O(state=disabled).

fields.label: resource clone ids
fields.description: List of clone resource IDs to clone from.

fields.label: resource clone meta
fields.description: List of metadata to associate with clone resource.

fields.label: resource meta
fields.description: List of meta to associate with resource.

fields.label: resource option
fields.description: Specify the resource option to create.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicate desired state for cluster resource. The states V(cleanup) and V(cloned) have been added in community.general 11.3.0. If O(state=cloned) or O(state=present), you can set O(resource_clone_ids) and O(resource_clone_meta) to determine exactly what and how to clone.

fields.label: wait
fields.description: Timeout period for polling the resource creation.

fields.label: agent validation
fields.description: Enabled agent validation for STONITH creation.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Specify the STONITH name to create.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicate desired state for cluster STONITH.

fields.label: stonith metas
fields.description: List of metadata to associate with STONITH.

fields.label: stonith options
fields.description: Specify the STONITH option to create.

fields.label: stonith type
fields.description: Specify the STONITH device type.

fields.label: wait
fields.description: Timeout period for polling the STONITH creation.

modules.label: packet_device
modules.tooltip: Manage a bare metal server in the Packet Host

fields.label: always pxe
fields.description: Persist PXE as the first boot option. Normally, the PXE process happens only on the first boot. Set this arg to have your device continuously boot to iPXE.

fields.label: auth token
fields.description: Packet API token. You can also supply it in environment variable E(PACKET_API_TOKEN).

fields.label: count
fields.description: The number of devices to create. Count number can be included in hostname using the C(%d) string formatter.

fields.label: count offset
fields.description: From which number to start the count.

fields.label: device ids
fields.description: List of device IDs on which to operate.

fields.label: facility
fields.description: Facility slug for device creation. See Packet API for current list - U(https://www.packet.net/developers/api/facilities/).

fields.label: features
fields.description: Dict with "features" for device creation. See Packet API docs for details.

fields.label: hostnames
fields.description: A hostname of a device, or a list of hostnames. If given string or one-item list, you can use the C("%d") Python string format to expand numbers from O(count). If only one hostname, it might be expanded to list if O(count)>1.

fields.label: ipxe script url
fields.description: URL of custom iPXE script for provisioning. More about custom iPXE for Packet devices at U(https://help.packet.net/technical/infrastructure/custom-ipxe).

fields.label: locked
fields.description: Whether to lock a created device.

fields.label: operating system
fields.description: OS slug for device creation. See Packet API for current list - U(https://www.packet.net/developers/api/operatingsystems/).

fields.label: plan
fields.description: Plan slug for device creation. See Packet API for current list - U(https://www.packet.net/developers/api/plans/).

fields.label: project id
fields.description: ID of project of the device.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of the device. If set to V(present) (the default), the module call returns immediately after the device-creating HTTP request successfully returns. If set to V(active), the module call blocks until all the specified devices are in state active due to the Packet API, or until O(wait_timeout).

fields.label: tags
fields.description: List of device tags. Currently implemented only for device creation.

fields.label: user data
fields.description: Userdata blob made available to the machine.

fields.label: wait for public IPv
fields.description: Whether to wait for the instance to be assigned a public IPv4/IPv6 address. If set to V(4), it waits until IPv4 is assigned to the instance. If set to V(6), it waits until public IPv6 is assigned to the instance.

fields.label: wait timeout
fields.description: How long (seconds) to wait either for automatic IP address assignment, or for the device to reach the V(active) state. If O(wait_for_public_IPv) is set and O(state=active), the module waits for both events consequently, applying the timeout twice.

modules.label: packet_ip_subnet
modules.tooltip: Assign IP subnet to a bare metal server

fields.label: auth token
fields.description: Packet API token. You can also supply it in environment variable E(PACKET_API_TOKEN).

fields.label: cidr
fields.description: IPv4 or IPv6 subnet which you want to manage. It must come from a reserved block for your project in the Packet Host.

fields.label: device count
fields.description: The number of devices to retrieve from the project. The max allowed value is 1000. See U(https://www.packet.com/developers/api/#retrieve-all-devices-of-a-project) for more info.

fields.label: device id
fields.description: UUID of a device to/from which to assign/remove a subnet.

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: A hostname of a device to/from which to assign/remove a subnet.

fields.label: project id
fields.description: UUID of a project of the device to/from which to assign/remove a subnet.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of the IP subnet on the specified device. With O(state=present), you must specify either O(hostname) or O(device_id). Subnet with given CIDR is then assigned to the specified device. With O(state=absent), you can specify either O(hostname) or O(device_id). The subnet is then removed from specified devices. If you leave both O(hostname) and O(device_id) empty, the subnet is then removed from any device it is assigned to.

modules.label: packet_project
modules.tooltip: Create/delete a project in Packet host

fields.label: auth token
fields.description: Packet API token. You can also supply it in environment variable E(PACKET_API_TOKEN).

fields.label: custom data
fields.description: Custom data about the project to create.

fields.label: id
fields.description: UUID of the project which you want to remove.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name for/of the project.

fields.label: org id
fields.description: UUID of the organization to create a project for. When blank, the API assumes the default organization.

fields.label: payment method
fields.description: Payment method is name of one of the payment methods available to your user. When blank, the API assumes the default payment method.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicate desired state of the target.

modules.label: packet_sshkey
modules.tooltip: Create/delete an SSH key in Packet host

fields.label: auth token
fields.description: Packet API token. You can also supply it in environment variable E(PACKET_API_TOKEN).

fields.label: fingerprint
fields.description: Fingerprint of the key which you want to remove.

fields.label: id
fields.description: UUID of the key which you want to remove.

fields.label: key
fields.description: Public Key string (V({type} {base64 encoded key} {description})).

fields.label: key file
fields.description: File with the public key.

fields.label: label
fields.description: Label for the key. If you keep it empty, it is read from key string.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicate desired state of the target.

modules.label: packet_volume
modules.tooltip: Create/delete a volume in Packet host

fields.label: auth token
fields.description: Packet API token. You can also supply it in environment variable E(PACKET_API_TOKEN).

fields.label: billing cycle
fields.description: Billing cycle for new volume.

fields.label: description
fields.description: User-defined description attribute for Packet volume. It is used used as idempotent identifier - if volume with given description exists, new one is not created.

fields.label: facility
fields.description: Location of the volume. Volumes can only be attached to device in the same location.

fields.label: id
fields.description: UUID of a volume.

fields.label: locked
fields.description: Create new volume locked.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Selector for API-generated name of the volume.

fields.label: plan
fields.description: V(storage_1) for standard tier, V(storage_2) for premium (performance) tier. Tiers are described at U(https://www.packet.com/cloud/storage/).

fields.label: project id
fields.description: ID of project of the device.

fields.label: size
fields.description: Size of the volume in gigabytes.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of the volume.

modules.label: packet_volume_attachment
modules.tooltip: Attach/detach a volume to a device in the Packet host

fields.label: auth token
fields.description: Packet API token. You can also supply it in environment variable E(PACKET_API_TOKEN).

fields.label: device
fields.description: Selector for the device. It can be a UUID of the device, or a hostname. Example values: 98a14f7a-3d27-4478-b7cf-35b5670523f3, "my device".

fields.label: project id
fields.description: UUID of the project to which the device and volume belong.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicate desired state of the attachment.

fields.label: volume
fields.description: Selector for the volume. It can be a UUID, an API-generated volume name, or user-defined description string. Example values: V(4a347482-b546-4f67-8300-fb5018ef0c5), V(volume-4a347482), V(my volume).

fields.label: cachedir
fields.description: An alternative package cache directory, passed as C(--cachedir) to all pacman commands. Only used when O(root) is also specified.

fields.label: config
fields.description: Path to an alternative pacman configuration file, passed as C(--config) to all pacman commands.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: Path of the binary to use. This can either be C(pacman) or a pacman compatible AUR helper. Pacman compatibility is unfortunately ill defined, in particular, this modules makes extensive use of the C(--print-format) directive which is known not to be implemented by some AUR helpers (notably, C(yay)). Beware that AUR helpers might behave unexpectedly and are therefore not recommended.

fields.label: extra args
fields.description: Additional option to pass to pacman when enforcing O(state).

fields.label: force
fields.description: When removing packages, forcefully remove them, without any checks. Same as O(extra_args="--nodeps --nodeps"). When combined with O(update_cache), force a refresh of all package databases. Same as O(update_cache_extra_args="--refresh --refresh").

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name or list of names of the package(s) or file(s) to install, upgrade, or remove. Cannot be used in combination with O(upgrade).

fields.label: reason
fields.description: The install reason to set for the packages.

fields.label: reason for
fields.description: Set the install reason for V(all) packages or only for V(new) packages. In case of O(state=latest) already installed packages which are updated to a newer version are not counted as V(new).

fields.label: remove nosave
fields.description: When removing packages, do not save modified configuration files as C(.pacsave) files. (passes C(--nosave) to pacman).

fields.label: root
fields.description: An alternative installation root directory, passed as C(--root) to all pacman commands. Useful for installing packages into a chroot or a new system root, similarly to how C(pacstrap) works.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to install (V(present) or V(installed), V(latest)), or remove (V(absent) or V(removed)) a package. V(present) and V(installed) simply ensure that a desired package is installed. V(latest) updates the specified package if it is not of the latest available version. V(absent) and V(removed) remove the specified package.

fields.label: update cache
fields.description: Whether or not to refresh the master package lists. This can be run as part of a package installation or as a separate step. If not specified, it defaults to V(false). Please note that this option only had an influence on the module's RV(ignore:changed) state if O(name) and O(upgrade) are not specified before community.general 5.0.0. See the examples for how to keep the old behavior.

fields.label: update cache extra args
fields.description: Additional option to pass to pacman when enforcing O(update_cache).

fields.label: upgrade
fields.description: Whether or not to upgrade the whole system. Cannot be used in combination with O(name). If not specified, it defaults to V(false).

fields.label: upgrade extra args
fields.description: Additional option to pass to pacman when enforcing O(upgrade).

modules.label: pacman_key
modules.tooltip: Manage pacman's list of trusted keys

fields.label: data
fields.description: The keyfile contents to add to the keyring. Must be of C(PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK) type.

fields.label: ensure trusted
fields.description: Ensure that the key is trusted (signed by the Pacman machine key and not expired).

fields.label: file
fields.description: The path to a keyfile on the remote server to add to the keyring. Remote file must be of C(PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK) type.

fields.label: force update
fields.description: This forces the key to be updated if it already exists in the keyring.

fields.label: id
fields.description: The 40 character identifier of the key. Including this allows check mode to correctly report the changed state. Do not specify a subkey ID, instead specify the primary key ID.

fields.label: keyring
fields.description: The full path to the keyring folder on the remote server. If not specified, module uses pacman's default (V(/etc/pacman.d/gnupg)). Useful if the remote system requires an alternative gnupg directory.

fields.label: keyserver
fields.description: The keyserver used to retrieve key from.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Ensures that the key is V(present) (added) or V(absent) (revoked).

fields.label: url
fields.description: The URL to retrieve keyfile from. Remote file must be of C(PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK) type.

fields.label: verify
fields.description: Whether or not to verify the keyfile's key ID against specified key ID.

fields.label: desc
fields.description: Short description of maintenance window.

fields.label: hours
fields.description: Length of maintenance window in hours.

fields.label: minutes
fields.description: Maintenance window in minutes (this is added to the hours).

fields.label: name
fields.description: PagerDuty unique subdomain. Obsolete. It is not used with PagerDuty REST v2 API.

fields.label: requester id
fields.description: ID of user making the request. Only needed when creating a maintenance_window.

fields.label: service
fields.description: A comma separated list of PagerDuty service IDs.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Create a maintenance window or get a list of ongoing windows.

fields.label: token
fields.description: A pagerduty token, generated on the pagerduty site. It is used for authorization.

fields.label: user
fields.description: PagerDuty user ID. Obsolete. Please, use O(token) for authorization.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: window id
fields.description: ID of maintenance window. Only needed when absent a maintenance_window.

modules.label: pagerduty_alert
modules.tooltip: Trigger, acknowledge or resolve PagerDuty incidents

fields.label: api key
fields.description: The pagerduty API key (readonly access), generated on the pagerduty site. Required if O(api_version=v1).

fields.label: api version
fields.description: The API version we want to use to run the module. V1 is more limited with option we can provide to trigger incident. V2 has more variables for example, O(severity), O(source), O(custom_details) and so on.

fields.label: client
fields.description: The name of the monitoring client that is triggering this event.

fields.label: client url
fields.description: The URL of the monitoring client that is triggering this event.

fields.label: component
fields.description: Component of the source machine that is responsible for the event, for example C(mysql) or C(eth0).

fields.label: custom details
fields.description: Additional details about the event and affected system. A dictionary with custom keys and values.

fields.label: desc
fields.description: For O(state=triggered) - Required. Short description of the problem that led to this trigger. This field (or a truncated version) is used when generating phone calls, SMS messages and alert emails. It also appears on the incidents tables in the PagerDuty UI. The maximum length is 1024 characters. For O(state=acknowledged) or O(state=resolved) - Text that appears in the incident's log associated with this event.

fields.label: incident class
fields.description: The class/type of the event, for example C(ping failure) or C(cpu load).

fields.label: incident key
fields.description: Identifies the incident to which this O(state) should be applied. For O(state=triggered) - If there is no open (in other words unresolved) incident with this key, a new one is created. If there is already an open incident with a matching key, this event is appended to that incident's log. The event key provides an easy way to 'de-dup' problem reports. If no O(incident_key) is provided, then it is generated by PagerDuty. For O(state=acknowledged) or O(state=resolved) - This should be the incident_key you received back when the incident was first opened by a trigger event. Acknowledge events referencing resolved or nonexistent incidents is discarded.

fields.label: integration key
fields.description: The GUID of one of your 'Generic API' services. This is the 'integration key' listed on a 'Integrations' tab of PagerDuty service.

fields.label: link text
fields.description: A short description of the O(link_url).

fields.label: link url
fields.description: Relevant link URL to the alert. For example, the website or the job link.

fields.label: name
fields.description: PagerDuty unique subdomain. Obsolete. It is not used with PagerDuty REST v2 API.

fields.label: service id
fields.description: ID of PagerDuty service when incidents are triggered, acknowledged or resolved. Required if O(api_version=v1).

fields.label: service key
fields.description: The GUID of one of your 'Generic API' services. Obsolete. Please use O(integration_key).

fields.label: severity
fields.description: The perceived severity of the status the event is describing with respect to the affected system. Required in case of O(state=trigger) and O(api_version=v2).

fields.label: source
fields.description: The unique location of the affected system, preferably a hostname or FQDN. Required in case of O(state=trigger) and O(api_version=v2).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Type of event to be sent.

modules.label: pagerduty_change
modules.tooltip: Track a code or infrastructure change as a PagerDuty change event

fields.label: environment
fields.description: The environment name, typically V(production), V(staging), and so on.

fields.label: integration key
fields.description: The integration key that identifies the service the change was made to. This can be found by adding an integration to a service in PagerDuty.

fields.label: link text
fields.description: Descriptive text for a URL where more information about the deployment can be obtained.

fields.label: link url
fields.description: A URL where more information about the deployment can be obtained.

fields.label: repo
fields.description: The URL of the project repository.

fields.label: revision
fields.description: An identifier of the revision being deployed, typically a number or SHA from a version control system.

fields.label: source
fields.description: The source of the change event.

fields.label: summary
fields.description: A short description of the change that occurred.

fields.label: url
fields.description: URL to submit the change event to.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The name of the user or process that triggered this deployment.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates for the target URL are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

modules.label: pagerduty_user
modules.tooltip: Manage a user account on PagerDuty

fields.label: access token
fields.description: An API access token to authenticate with the PagerDuty REST API.

fields.label: pd email
fields.description: The user's email address. O(pd_email) is the unique identifier used and cannot be updated using this module.

fields.label: pd role
fields.description: The user's role.

fields.label: pd teams
fields.description: The teams to which the user belongs. Required if O(state=present).

fields.label: pd user
fields.description: Name of the user in PagerDuty.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the user. On V(present), it creates a user if the user does not exist. On V(absent), it removes a user if the account exists.

fields.label: backup
fields.description: Create a backup file including the timestamp information so you can get the original file back if you somehow clobbered it incorrectly.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Comment associated with the limit.

fields.label: dest
fields.description: Modify the limits.conf path.

fields.label: domain
fields.description: A username, @groupname, wildcard, UID/GID range.

fields.label: limit item
fields.description: The limit to be set.

fields.label: limit type
fields.description: Limit type, see C(man 5 limits.conf) for an explanation.

fields.label: use max
fields.description: If set to V(true), the maximal value is used or conserved. If the specified value is superior to the value in the file, file content is replaced with the new value, else content is not modified.

fields.label: use min
fields.description: If set to V(true), the minimal value is used or conserved. If the specified value is inferior to the value in the file, file content is replaced with the new value, else content is not modified.

fields.label: value
fields.description: The value of the limit. Value must either be V(unlimited), V(infinity) or V(-1), all of which indicate no limit, or a limit of 0 or larger. Value must be a number in the range -20 to 19 inclusive, if O(limit_item) is set to V(nice) or V(priority). Refer to the C(man 5 limits.conf) manual pages for more details.

fields.label: backup
fields.description: Create a backup file including the timestamp information so you can get the original file back if you somehow clobbered it incorrectly.

fields.label: control
fields.description: The control of the PAM rule being modified. This may be a complicated control with brackets. If this is the case, be sure to put "[bracketed controls]" in quotes. The O(type), O(control), and O(module_path) options all must match a rule to be modified.

fields.label: module arguments
fields.description: When O(state=updated), the O(module_arguments) replace existing module_arguments. When O(state=args_absent) args matching those listed in O(module_arguments) are removed. When O(state=args_present) any args listed in O(module_arguments) are added if missing from the existing rule. Furthermore, if the module argument takes a value denoted by C(=), the value changes to that specified in module_arguments.

fields.label: module path
fields.description: The module path of the PAM rule being modified. The O(type), O(control), and O(module_path) options all must match a rule to be modified.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name generally refers to the PAM service file to change, for example system-auth.

fields.label: new control
fields.description: The new control to assign to the new rule.

fields.label: new module path
fields.description: The new module path to be assigned to the new rule.

fields.label: new type
fields.description: The new type to assign to the new rule.

fields.label: path
fields.description: This is the path to the PAM service files.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The default of V(updated) modifies an existing rule if type, control and module_path all match an existing rule. With V(before), the new rule is inserted before a rule matching type, control and module_path. Similarly, with V(after), the new rule is inserted after an existing rulematching type, control and module_path. With either V(before) or V(after) O(new_type), O(new_control), and O(new_module_path) must all be specified. If state is V(args_absent) or V(args_present), O(new_type), O(new_control), and O(new_module_path) are ignored. State V(absent) removes the rule.

fields.label: type
fields.description: The type of the PAM rule being modified. The O(type), O(control), and O(module_path) options all must match a rule to be modified.

fields.label: align
fields.description: Set alignment for newly created partitions. Use V(undefined) for parted default alignment.

fields.label: device
fields.description: The block device (disk) where to operate. Regular files can also be partitioned, but it is recommended to create a loopback device using C(losetup) to easily access its partitions.

fields.label: flags
fields.description: A list of the flags that has to be set on the partition.

fields.label: fs type
fields.description: If specified and the partition does not exist, sets filesystem type to given partition. Parameter optional, but see notes below about negative O(part_start) values.

fields.label: label
fields.description: Disk label type or partition table to use. If O(device) already contains a different label, it is changed to O(label) and any previous partitions are lost. A O(name) must be specified for a V(gpt) partition table.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Sets the name for the partition number (GPT, Mac, MIPS and PC98 only).

fields.label: number
fields.description: The partition number being affected. Required when performing any action on the disk, except fetching information.

fields.label: part end
fields.description: Where the partition ends as offset from the beginning of the disk, that is, the "distance" from the start of the disk. Negative numbers specify distance from the end of the disk. The distance can be specified with all the units supported by parted (except compat) and it is case sensitive, for example V(10GiB), V(15%).

fields.label: part start
fields.description: Where the partition starts as offset from the beginning of the disk, that is, the "distance" from the start of the disk. Negative numbers specify distance from the end of the disk. The distance can be specified with all the units supported by parted (except compat) and it is case sensitive, for example V(10GiB), V(15%). Using negative values may require setting of O(fs_type) (see notes).

fields.label: part type
fields.description: May be specified only with O(label=msdos) or O(label=dvh). Neither O(part_type) nor O(name) may be used with O(label=sun).

fields.label: resize
fields.description: Call C(resizepart) on existing partitions to match the size specified by O(part_end).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to create or delete a partition. If set to V(info) the module only returns the device information.

fields.label: unit
fields.description: Selects the current default unit that Parted uses to display locations and capacities on the disk and to interpret those given by the user if they are not suffixed by an unit. When fetching information about a disk, it is recommended to always specify a unit. O(unit_preserve_case) controls the case of the units in the return values. It used to be all lower case, but using that option you can make it return the units textually equal to the choices used in O(unit).

fields.label: unit preserve case
fields.description: Controls the case of the V(unit) field in the module output (RV(ignore:partition_info.disk.unit), RV(ignore:partition_info.partitions[].unit)). When V(true), the unit is returned in its original mixed case, for example V(KiB) or V(MiB). This matches the values accepted by O(unit), making it safe to feed the output back as input. When V(false), the unit is returned in lowercase (legacy behavior), for example V(kib) or V(mib).

fields.label: executable
fields.description: Path to the pear executable.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the package to install, upgrade, or remove.

fields.label: prompts
fields.description: List of regular expressions that can be used to detect prompts during pear package installation to answer the expected question. Prompts are processed in the same order as the packages list. You can optionally specify an answer to any question in the list. If no answer is provided, the list item must contain only the regular expression. To specify an answer, the item must be a dictionary with the regular expression as key and the answer as value C(my_regular_expression: 'an_answer'). You can provide a list containing items with or without answer. A prompt list can be shorter or longer than the packages list but it issues a warning. If you want to specify that a package does not need prompts in the middle of a list, V(null).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of the package.

modules.label: pids
modules.tooltip: Retrieves process IDs list if the process is running otherwise return empty list

fields.label: ignore case
fields.description: Ignore case in pattern if using the O(pattern) option.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the process(es) you want to get PID(s) for.

fields.label: pattern
fields.description: The pattern (regular expression) to match the process(es) you want to get PID(s) for.

fields.label: checkid
fields.description: Pingdom ID of the check.

fields.label: key
fields.description: Pingdom API key.

fields.label: passwd
fields.description: Pingdom user password.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Define whether or not the check should be running or paused.

fields.label: uid
fields.description: Pingdom user ID.

fields.label: clients
fields.description: A list of the pip executables that are used to get the packages. They can be supplied with the full path or just the executable name, for example V(pip3.7).

fields.label: editable
fields.description: Install the project in editable mode.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: Path to the C(pipx) installed in the system. If not specified, the module uses C(python -m pipx) to run the tool, using the same Python interpreter as ansible itself.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Force modification of the application's virtual environment. See C(pipx) for details. Only used when O(state=install), O(state=upgrade), O(state=upgrade_all), O(state=latest), or O(state=inject). The module is not idempotent when O(force=true).

fields.label: global
fields.description: The module passes the C(--global) argument to C(pipx), to execute actions in global scope.

fields.label: include injected
fields.description: Upgrade the injected packages along with the application. Only used when O(state=upgrade), O(state=upgrade_all), or O(state=latest). This is used with O(state=upgrade) and O(state=latest) since community.general 6.6.0.

fields.label: index url
fields.description: Base URL of Python Package Index. Only used when O(state=install), O(state=upgrade), O(state=latest), or O(state=inject).

fields.label: inject packages
fields.description: Packages to be injected into an existing virtual environment. Only used when O(state=inject).

fields.label: install apps
fields.description: Add apps from the injected packages. Only used when O(state=inject).

fields.label: install deps
fields.description: Include applications of dependent packages. Only used when O(state=install), O(state=latest), or O(state=inject).

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the application and also the name of the Python package being installed. In C(pipx) documentation it is also referred to as the name of the virtual environment where the application is installed. If O(name) is a simple package name without version specifiers, then that name is used as the Python package name to be installed. Starting in community.general 10.7.0, you can use package specifiers when O(state=present) or O(state=install). For example, O(name=tox<4.0.0) or O(name=tox>3.0.27). Please note that when you use O(state=present) and O(name) with version specifiers, contrary to the behavior of C(pipx), this module honors the version specifier and installs a version of the application that satisfies it. If you want to ensure the reinstallation of the application even when the version specifier is met, then you must use O(force=true), or perhaps use O(state=upgrade) instead. Use O(source) for installing from URLs or directories.

fields.label: pip args
fields.description: Arbitrary arguments to pass directly to C(pip).

fields.label: python
fields.description: Python version to be used when creating the application virtual environment. Must be 3.6+. Only used when O(state=install), O(state=latest), O(state=reinstall), or O(state=reinstall_all).

fields.label: source
fields.description: Source for the package. This option is used when O(state=install) or O(state=latest), and it is ignored with other states. Use O(source) when installing a Python package with version specifier, or from a local path, from a VCS URL or compressed file. The value of this option is passed as-is to C(pipx). O(name) is still required when using O(source) to establish the application name without fetching the package from a remote source. The module is not idempotent when using O(source).

fields.label: spec metadata
fields.description: Spec metadata file for O(state=install_all). This content of the file is usually generated with C(pipx list --json), and it can be obtained with M(community.general.pipx_info) with O(community.general.pipx_info#module:include_raw=true) and obtaining the content from the RV(community.general.pipx_info#module:raw_output).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state for the application. The states V(present) and V(absent) are aliases to V(install) and V(uninstall), respectively. The state V(latest) is equivalent to executing the task twice, with state V(install) and then V(upgrade). It was added in community.general 5.5.0. The states V(install_all), V(uninject), V(upgrade_shared), V(pin) and V(unpin) are only available in C(pipx>=1.6.0), make sure to have a compatible version when using this option. These states have been added in community.general 9.4.0.

fields.label: suffix
fields.description: Optional suffix for virtual environment and executable names. B(Warning:) C(pipx) documentation states this is an B(experimental) feature subject to change.

fields.label: system site packages
fields.description: Give application virtual environment access to the system site-packages directory. Only used when O(state=install) or O(state=latest).

modules.label: pipx_info
modules.tooltip: Rretrieves information about applications installed with pipx

fields.label: executable
fields.description: Path to the C(pipx) installed in the system. If not specified, the module uses C(python -m pipx) to run the tool, using the same Python interpreter as ansible itself.

fields.label: global
fields.description: The module passes the C(--global) argument to C(pipx), to execute actions in global scope.

fields.label: include deps
fields.description: Include dependent packages in the output.

fields.label: include injected
fields.description: Include injected packages in the output.

fields.label: include raw
fields.description: Returns the raw output of C(pipx list --json). The raw output is not affected by O(include_deps) or O(include_injected).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of an application installed with C(pipx).

modules.label: pkg5
modules.tooltip: Manages packages with the Solaris 11 Image Packaging System

fields.label: accept licenses
fields.description: Accept any licences.

fields.label: be name
fields.description: Creates a new boot environment with the given name.

fields.label: name
fields.description: An FRMI of the package(s) to be installed/removed/updated. Multiple packages may be specified, separated by V(,).

fields.label: refresh
fields.description: Refresh publishers before execution.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to install (V(present), V(latest)), or remove (V(absent)) a package.

fields.label: verbose
fields.description: Set to V(true) to disable quiet execution.

modules.label: pkg5_publisher
modules.tooltip: Manages Solaris 11 Image Packaging System publishers

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Is the repository enabled or disabled?

fields.label: mirror
fields.description: A path or URL to the repository mirror. Multiple values may be provided.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The publisher's name.

fields.label: origin
fields.description: A path or URL to the repository. Multiple values may be provided.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to ensure that a publisher is present or absent.

fields.label: sticky
fields.description: Packages installed from a sticky repository can only receive updates from that repository.

modules.label: pkgin
modules.tooltip: Package manager for SmartOS, NetBSD, et al

fields.label: clean
fields.description: Clean packages cache.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Force package reinstall.

fields.label: full upgrade
fields.description: Upgrade all packages to their newer versions.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of package to install/remove; Multiple names may be given, separated by commas.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Intended state of the package.

fields.label: update cache
fields.description: Update repository database. Can be run with other steps or on its own.

fields.label: upgrade
fields.description: Upgrade main packages to their newer versions.

fields.label: annotation
fields.description: A list of keyvalue-pairs of the form C(<+/-/:><key>[=<value>]). A V(+) denotes adding an annotation, a V(-) denotes removing an annotation, and V(:) denotes modifying an annotation. If setting or modifying annotations, a value must be provided.

fields.label: autoremove
fields.description: Remove automatically installed packages which are no longer needed.

fields.label: cached
fields.description: Use local package base instead of fetching an updated one.

fields.label: chroot
fields.description: Pkg chroots in the specified environment. Can not be used together with O(rootdir) or O(jail) options.

fields.label: ignore osver
fields.description: Ignore FreeBSD OS version check, useful on C(-STABLE) and C(-CURRENT) branches. Defines the E(IGNORE_OSVERSION) environment variable.

fields.label: jail
fields.description: Pkg executes in the given jail name or ID. Can not be used together with O(chroot) or O(rootdir) options.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name or list of names of packages to install/remove. With O(name=*), O(state=latest) operates, but O(state=present) and O(state=absent) are noops.

fields.label: pkgsite
fields.description: For C(pkgng) versions before 1.1.4, specify C(packagesite) to use for downloading packages. If not specified, use settings from C(/usr/local/etc/pkg.conf). For newer C(pkgng) versions, specify a the name of a repository configured in C(/usr/local/etc/pkg/repos).

fields.label: rootdir
fields.description: For C(pkgng) versions 1.5 and later, pkg installs all packages within the specified root directory. Can not be used together with O(chroot) or O(jail) options.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the package.

fields.label: use globs
fields.description: Treat the package names as shell glob patterns.

fields.label: force
fields.description: To allow the update process to downgrade packages to match what is present in the repository, set this to V(true). This is useful for rolling back to stable from testing, or similar operations.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the package. When using O(state=latest), this can be V('*'), which updates all installed packages managed by pkgutil.

fields.label: site
fields.description: The repository path to install the package from. Its global definition is in C(/etc/opt/csw/pkgutil.conf).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to install (V(present)/V(installed)), or remove (V(absent)/V(removed)) packages. The upgrade (V(latest)) operation updates/installs the packages to the latest version available.

fields.label: update catalog
fields.description: If you always want to refresh your catalog from the mirror, even when it is not stale, set this to V(true).

modules.label: pmem
modules.tooltip: Configure Intel Optane Persistent Memory modules

fields.label: appdirect
fields.description: Percentage of the total capacity to use in AppDirect Mode (V(0)-V(100)). Create AppDirect capacity utilizing hardware interleaving across the requested PMem modules if applicable given the specified target. Total of O(appdirect), O(memorymode) and O(reserved) must be V(100).

fields.label: appdirect interleaved
fields.description: Create AppDirect capacity that is interleaved any other PMem modules.

fields.label: memorymode
fields.description: Percentage of the total capacity to use in Memory Mode (V(0)-V(100)).

fields.label: namespace append
fields.description: Enable to append the new namespaces to the system. The default is V(false) so the all existing namespaces not listed in O(namespace) are removed.

fields.label: reserved
fields.description: Percentage of the capacity to reserve (V(0)-V(100)). O(reserved) is not mapped into the system physical address space and is presented as reserved capacity with Show Device and Show Memory Resources Commands. O(reserved) is set automatically if this is not configured.

fields.label: alias
fields.description: Alias of the Node.js library.

fields.label: dev
fields.description: Install dependencies in development mode. Pnpm ignores any regular dependencies in C(package.json).

fields.label: executable
fields.description: The executable location for pnpm. The default location it searches for is E(PATH), fails if not set.

fields.label: global
fields.description: Install the Node.js library globally.

fields.label: ignore scripts
fields.description: Use the C(--ignore-scripts) flag when installing.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of a Node.js library to install. All packages in C(package.json) are installed if not provided.

fields.label: no optional
fields.description: Do not install optional packages, equivalent to C(--no-optional).

fields.label: optional
fields.description: Install dependencies in optional mode.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The base path to install the Node.js libraries.

fields.label: production
fields.description: Install dependencies in production mode. Pnpm ignores any dependencies under C(devDependencies) in package.json.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Installation state of the named Node.js library. If V(absent) is selected, a name option must be provided.

fields.label: version
fields.description: The version of the library to be installed, in semver format.

fields.label: backtrack
fields.description: Set backtrack value (C(--backtrack)).

fields.label: changed deps
fields.description: Tells emerge to replace installed packages for which the ebuild dependencies have changed since the packages were built (C(--changed-deps)).

fields.label: changed use
fields.description: Include installed packages where USE flags have changed, except when. Flags that the user has not enabled are added or removed. (C(--changed-use)).

fields.label: deep
fields.description: Consider the entire dependency tree of packages (C(--deep)).

fields.label: depclean
fields.description: Remove packages not needed by explicitly merged packages (C(--depclean)). If no package is specified, clean up the world's dependencies. Otherwise, C(--depclean) serves as a dependency aware version of C(--unmerge).

fields.label: getbinpkg
fields.description: Prefer packages specified at C(PORTAGE_BINHOST) in C(make.conf).

fields.label: getbinpkgonly
fields.description: Merge only packages specified at C(PORTAGE_BINHOST) in C(make.conf).

fields.label: jobs
fields.description: Specifies the number of packages to build simultaneously. Since version 2.6: Value of V(0) or V(false) resets any previously added C(--jobs) setting values.

fields.label: keepgoing
fields.description: Continue as much as possible after an error.

fields.label: loadavg
fields.description: Specifies that no new builds should be started if there are other builds running and the load average is at least LOAD. Since version 2.6: Value of 0 or False resets any previously added C(--load-average) setting values.

fields.label: newuse
fields.description: Include installed packages where USE flags have changed (C(--newuse)).

fields.label: nodeps
fields.description: Only merge packages but not their dependencies (C(--nodeps)).

fields.label: noreplace
fields.description: Do not re-emerge installed packages (C(--noreplace)).

fields.label: oneshot
fields.description: Do not add the packages to the world file (C(--oneshot)).

fields.label: onlydeps
fields.description: Only merge packages' dependencies but not the packages (C(--onlydeps)).

fields.label: package
fields.description: Package atom or set, for example V(sys-apps/foo) or V(>foo-2.13) or V(@world).

fields.label: quiet
fields.description: Run emerge in quiet mode (C(--quiet)).

fields.label: quietbuild
fields.description: Redirect all build output to logs alone, and do not display it on stdout (C(--quiet-build)).

fields.label: quietfail
fields.description: Suppresses display of the build log on stdout (--quiet-fail). Only the die message and the path of the build log are displayed on stdout.

fields.label: select
fields.description: If set to V(true), explicitely add the package to the world file. Please note that this option is not used for idempotency, it is only used when actually installing a package.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the package atom.

fields.label: sync
fields.description: Sync package repositories first. If V(yes), perform C(emerge --sync). If V(web), perform C(emerge-webrsync).

fields.label: update
fields.description: Update packages to the best version available (C(--update)).

fields.label: usepkg
fields.description: Tries to use the binary package(s) in the locally available packages directory.

fields.label: usepkgonly
fields.description: Merge only binaries (no compiling).

fields.label: verbose
fields.description: Run emerge in verbose mode (C(--verbose)).

fields.label: withbdeps
fields.description: Specifies that build time dependencies should be installed.

modules.label: portinstall
modules.tooltip: Installing packages from FreeBSD's ports system

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of package to install/remove.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the package.

fields.label: use packages
fields.description: Use packages instead of ports whenever available.

modules.label: pritunl_org
modules.tooltip: Manages Pritunl Organizations using the Pritunl API

fields.label: force
fields.description: If O(force) is V(true) and O(state) is V(absent), the module deletes the organization, no matter if it contains users or not. By default O(force) is V(false), which causes the module to fail the deletion of the organization when it contains users.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the organization to manage in Pritunl.

fields.label: pritunl api secret
fields.description: API Secret found in Administrators > USERNAME > API Secret.

fields.label: pritunl api token
fields.description: API Token of a Pritunl admin user. It needs to be enabled in Administrators > USERNAME > Enable Token Authentication.

fields.label: pritunl url
fields.description: URL and port of the Pritunl server on which the API is enabled.

fields.label: state
fields.description: If V(present), the module adds organization O(name) to Pritunl. If V(absent), attempt to delete the organization from Pritunl (please read about O(force) usage).

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If certificates should be validated or not. This should never be set to V(false), except if you are very sure that your connection to the server can not be subject to a Man In The Middle attack.

modules.label: pritunl_org_info
modules.tooltip: List Pritunl Organizations using the Pritunl API

fields.label: organization
fields.description: Name of the Pritunl organization to search for. If none provided, the module returns all Pritunl organizations.

fields.label: pritunl api secret
fields.description: API Secret found in Administrators > USERNAME > API Secret.

fields.label: pritunl api token
fields.description: API Token of a Pritunl admin user. It needs to be enabled in Administrators > USERNAME > Enable Token Authentication.

fields.label: pritunl url
fields.description: URL and port of the Pritunl server on which the API is enabled.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If certificates should be validated or not. This should never be set to V(false), except if you are very sure that your connection to the server can not be subject to a Man In The Middle attack.

modules.label: pritunl_user
modules.tooltip: Manage Pritunl Users using the Pritunl API

fields.label: organization
fields.description: The name of the organization the user is part of.

fields.label: pritunl api secret
fields.description: API Secret found in Administrators > USERNAME > API Secret.

fields.label: pritunl api token
fields.description: API Token of a Pritunl admin user. It needs to be enabled in Administrators > USERNAME > Enable Token Authentication.

fields.label: pritunl url
fields.description: URL and port of the Pritunl server on which the API is enabled.

fields.label: state
fields.description: If V(present), the module adds user O(user_name) to the Pritunl O(organization). If V(absent), removes the user O(user_name) from the Pritunl O(organization).

fields.label: user disabled
fields.description: Enable/Disable the user O(user_name).

fields.label: user email
fields.description: Email address associated with the user O(user_name).

fields.label: user gravatar
fields.description: Enable/Disable Gravatar usage for the user O(user_name).

fields.label: user groups
fields.description: List of groups associated with the user O(user_name).

fields.label: user mac addresses
fields.description: Allowed MAC addresses for the user O(user_name).

fields.label: user name
fields.description: Name of the user to create or delete from Pritunl.

fields.label: user type
fields.description: Type of the user O(user_name).

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If certificates should be validated or not. This should never be set to V(false), except if you are very sure that your connection to the server can not be subject to a Man In The Middle attack.

modules.label: pritunl_user_info
modules.tooltip: List Pritunl Users using the Pritunl API

fields.label: organization
fields.description: The name of the organization the user is part of.

fields.label: pritunl api secret
fields.description: API Secret found in Administrators > USERNAME > API Secret.

fields.label: pritunl api token
fields.description: API Token of a Pritunl admin user. It needs to be enabled in Administrators > USERNAME > Enable Token Authentication.

fields.label: pritunl url
fields.description: URL and port of the Pritunl server on which the API is enabled.

fields.label: user name
fields.description: Name of the user to filter on Pritunl.

fields.label: user type
fields.description: Type of the user O(user_name).

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If certificates should be validated or not. This should never be set to V(false), except if you are very sure that your connection to the server can not be subject to a Man In The Middle attack.

fields.label: account
fields.description: Name of PubNub account for from which O(application) is used to manage blocks. User's account is used if value not set or empty.

fields.label: application
fields.description: Name of target PubNub application for which blocks configuration on specific O(keyset) is done.

fields.label: cache
fields.description: In case if single play use blocks management module few times it is preferred to enabled 'caching' by making previous module to share gathered artifacts and pass them to this parameter.

fields.label: changes
fields.description: List of fields which should be changed by block itself (does not affect any event handlers). Possible options for change is: O(name).

fields.label: description
fields.description: Short block description which is later visible on U(https://admin.pubnub.com). Used only if block does not exists and does not change description for existing block.

fields.label: email
fields.description: Email from account for which new session should be started. Not required if O(cache) contains result of previous module call (in same play).

fields.label: event handlers
fields.description: List of event handlers which should be updated for specified block O(name). Each entry for new event handler should contain: V(name), V(src), V(channels), V(event). V(name) used as event handler name which can be used later to make changes to it. C(src) is full path to file with event handler code. V(channels) is name of channel from which event handler is waiting for events. V(event) is type of event which is able to trigger event handler: V(js-before-publish), V(js-after-publish), V(js-after-presence). Each entry for existing handlers should contain C(name) (so target handler can be identified). Rest parameters (C(src), C(channels) and C(event)) can be added if changes required for them. It is possible to rename event handler by adding C(changes) key to event handler payload and pass dictionary, which contains single key C(name), where new name should be passed. To remove particular event handler it is possible to set C(state) for it to C(absent) and it is removed.

fields.label: keyset
fields.description: Name of application's keys set which is bound to managed blocks.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of managed block which is later visible on admin.pubnub.com.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Intended block state after event handlers creation / update process is completed.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: This key allow to try skip certificates check when performing REST API calls. Sometimes host may have issues with certificates on it and this causes problems to call PubNub REST API. If check should be ignored V(false) should be passed to this parameter.

modules.label: pulp_repo
modules.tooltip: Add or remove Pulp repos from a remote host

fields.label: add export distributor
fields.description: Whether or not to add the export distributor to new C(rpm) repositories.

fields.label: client cert
fields.description: PEM formatted certificate chain file to be used for SSL client authentication. This file can also include the key as well, and if the key is included, C(client_key) is not required.

fields.label: client key
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains your private key to be used for SSL client authentication. If C(client_cert) contains both the certificate and key, this option is not required.

fields.label: feed
fields.description: Upstream feed URL to receive updates from.

fields.label: feed ca cert
fields.description: CA certificate string used to validate the feed source SSL certificate. This can be the file content or the path to the file.

fields.label: feed client cert
fields.description: Certificate used as the client certificate when synchronizing the repository. This is used to communicate authentication information to the feed source. The value to this option must be the full path to the certificate. The specified file may be the certificate itself or a single file containing both the certificate and private key. This can be the file content or the path to the file.

fields.label: feed client key
fields.description: Private key to the certificate specified in O(feed_client_cert), assuming it is not included in the certificate file itself. This can be the file content or the path to the file.

fields.label: force
fields.description: If C(yes) do not get a cached copy.

fields.label: force basic auth
fields.description: C(httplib2), the library used by the M(ansible.builtin.uri) module only sends authentication information when a webservice responds to an initial request with a 401 status. Since some basic auth services do not properly send a 401, logins fail. This option forces the sending of the Basic authentication header upon initial request.

fields.label: generate sqlite
fields.description: Boolean flag to indicate whether sqlite files should be generated during a repository publish.

fields.label: http agent
fields.description: Header to identify as, generally appears in web server logs.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the repo to add or remove. This correlates to repo-id in Pulp.

fields.label: proxy host
fields.description: Proxy URL setting for the pulp repository importer. This is in the format V(scheme://host).

fields.label: proxy password
fields.description: Proxy password for the pulp repository importer.

fields.label: proxy port
fields.description: Proxy port setting for the pulp repository importer.

fields.label: proxy username
fields.description: Proxy username for the pulp repository importer.

fields.label: publish distributor
fields.description: Distributor to use when O(state=publish). The default is to publish all distributors.

fields.label: pulp host
fields.description: URL of the pulp server to connect to.

fields.label: relative url
fields.description: Relative URL for the local repository. It's required when state=present.

fields.label: repo type
fields.description: Repo plugin type to use (that is, V(rpm), V(docker)).

fields.label: repoview
fields.description: Whether to generate repoview files for a published repository. Setting this to V(true) automatically activates O(generate_sqlite).

fields.label: serve http
fields.description: Make the repo available over HTTP.

fields.label: serve https
fields.description: Make the repo available over HTTPS.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The repo state. A state of V(sync) queues a sync of the repo. This is asynchronous but not delayed like a scheduled sync. A state of V(publish) uses the repository's distributor to publish the content.

fields.label: url
fields.description: HTTP, HTTPS, or FTP URL in the form (http|https|ftp)://[user[:pass]]@host.domain[:port]/path

fields.label: url password
fields.description: The password for use in HTTP basic authentication to the pulp API. If the O(url_username) parameter is not specified, the O(url_password) parameter is not used.

fields.label: url username
fields.description: The username for use in HTTP basic authentication to the pulp API.

fields.label: use gssapi
fields.description: Use GSSAPI to perform the authentication, typically this is for Kerberos or Kerberos through Negotiate authentication. Requires the Python library L(gssapi,https://github.com/pythongssapi/python-gssapi) to be installed. Credentials for GSSAPI can be specified with I(url_username)/I(url_password) or with the GSSAPI env var C(KRB5CCNAME) that specified a custom Kerberos credential cache. NTLM authentication is C(not) supported even if the GSSAPI mech for NTLM has been installed.

fields.label: use proxy
fields.description: If C(no), it will not use a proxy, even if one is defined in an environment variable on the target hosts.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: wait for completion
fields.description: Wait for asynchronous tasks to complete before returning.

fields.label: certname
fields.description: The name to use when handling certificates.

fields.label: confdir
fields.description: Path to the directory containing the puppet.conf file.

fields.label: debug
fields.description: Enable full debugging.

fields.label: environment
fields.description: Puppet environment to be used.

fields.label: environment lang
fields.description: The lang environment to use when running the puppet agent. The default value, V(C), is supported on every system, but can lead to encoding errors if UTF-8 is used in the output. Use V(C.UTF-8) or V(en_US.UTF-8) or similar UTF-8 supporting locales in case of problems. You need to make sure the selected locale is supported on the system the puppet agent runs on. Starting with community.general 9.1.0, you can use the value V(auto) and the module tries to determine the best parseable locale to use.

fields.label: execute
fields.description: Execute a specific piece of Puppet code. It has no effect with a puppetmaster.

fields.label: facter basename
fields.description: Basename of the facter output file.

fields.label: facts
fields.description: A dict of values to pass in as persistent external facter facts.

fields.label: logdest
fields.description: Where the puppet logs should go, if puppet apply is being used. V(all) goes to both C(console) and C(syslog). V(stdout) is deprecated and replaced by C(console).

fields.label: manifest
fields.description: Path to the manifest file to run puppet apply on.

fields.label: modulepath
fields.description: Path to an alternate location for puppet modules.

fields.label: noop
fields.description: Override puppet.conf noop mode. When V(true), run Puppet agent with C(--noop) switch set. When V(false), run Puppet agent with C(--no-noop) switch set. When unset (default), use default or puppet.conf value if defined.

fields.label: puppetmaster
fields.description: The hostname of the puppetmaster to contact.

fields.label: show diff
fields.description: Whether to print file changes details.

fields.label: skip tags
fields.description: A list of puppet tags to be excluded.

fields.label: summarize
fields.description: Whether to print a transaction summary.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: A list of puppet tags to be used.

fields.label: use srv records
fields.description: Toggles use_srv_records flag.

fields.label: verbose
fields.description: Print extra information.

fields.label: waitforlock
fields.description: The maximum amount of time C(puppet) should wait for an already running C(puppet) agent to finish before starting. If a number without unit is provided, it is assumed to be a number of seconds. Allowed units are V(m) for minutes and V(h) for hours.

fields.label: app token
fields.description: Pushover issued token identifying your pushover app.

fields.label: device
fields.description: A device the message should be sent to. Multiple devices can be specified, separated by a comma.

fields.label: msg
fields.description: What message you wish to send.

fields.label: pri
fields.description: Message priority (see U(https://pushover.net) for details).

fields.label: title
fields.description: Message title.

fields.label: user key
fields.description: Pushover issued authentication key for your user.

modules.label: python_requirements_info
modules.tooltip: Show python path and assert dependency versions

fields.label: dependencies
fields.description: A list of version-likes or module names to check for installation. Supported operators: C(<), C(>), C(<=), C(>=), or C(==). The bare module name like V(ansible), the module with a specific version like V(boto3==1.6.1), or a partial version like V(requests>2) are all valid specifications.

fields.label: delimiter
fields.description: A one-character string used to separate fields. When using this parameter, you change the default value used by O(dialect). The default value depends on the dialect used.

fields.label: dialect
fields.description: The CSV dialect to use when parsing the CSV file. Possible values include V(excel), V(excel-tab) or V(unix).

fields.label: fieldnames
fields.description: A list of field names for every column. This is needed if the CSV does not have a header.

fields.label: key
fields.description: The column name used as a key for the resulting dictionary. If O(key) is unset, the module returns a list of dictionaries, where each dictionary is a row in the CSV file.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The CSV filename to read data from.

fields.label: skipinitialspace
fields.description: Whether to ignore any whitespaces immediately following the delimiter. When using this parameter, you change the default value used by O(dialect). The default value depends on the dialect used.

fields.label: strict
fields.description: Whether to raise an exception on bad CSV input. When using this parameter, you change the default value used by O(dialect). The default value depends on the dialect used.

fields.label: unique
fields.description: Whether the O(key) used is expected to be unique.

modules.label: redfish_command
modules.tooltip: Manages Out-Of-Band controllers using Redfish APIs

fields.label: account properties
fields.description: Properties of account service to update.

fields.label: account types
fields.description: Array of account types to apply to a user account.

fields.label: auth token
fields.description: Security token for authenticating to OOB controller.

fields.label: baseuri
fields.description: Base URI of OOB controller.

fields.label: bios attributes
fields.description: BIOS attributes that needs to be verified in the given server.

fields.label: boot next
fields.description: BootNext target when bootdevice is "UefiBootNext".

fields.label: boot override mode
fields.description: Boot mode when using an override.

fields.label: bootdevice
fields.description: Boot device when setting boot configuration.

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains a CA certificate to be used for validation. Only used if O(validate_certs=true).

fields.label: category
fields.description: Category to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: ciphers
fields.description: TLS/SSL Ciphers to use for the request. When a list is provided, all ciphers are joined in order with V(:). See the L(OpenSSL Cipher List Format,https://www.openssl.org/docs/manmaster/man1/openssl-ciphers.html#CIPHER-LIST-FORMAT) for more details. The available ciphers is dependent on the Python and OpenSSL/LibreSSL versions.

fields.label: command
fields.description: List of commands to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: id
fields.description: ID of account to delete/modify. Can also be used in account creation to work around vendor issues where the ID of the new user is required in the POST request.

fields.label: new password
fields.description: New password of account to add/modify.

fields.label: new username
fields.description: Username of account to add/delete/modify.

fields.label: oem account types
fields.description: Array of OEM account types to apply to a user account.

fields.label: reset to defaults mode
fields.description: Mode to apply when reseting to default.

fields.label: resource id
fields.description: ID of the System, Manager or Chassis to modify.

fields.label: roleid
fields.description: Role of account to add/modify.

fields.label: session uri
fields.description: URI of the session resource.

fields.label: strip etag quotes
fields.description: Removes surrounding quotes of etag used in C(If-Match) header of C(PATCH) requests. Only use this option to resolve bad vendor implementation where C(If-Match) only matches the unquoted etag string.

fields.label: uefi target
fields.description: UEFI boot target when bootdevice is "UefiTarget".

fields.label: update apply time
fields.description: Time when to apply the update.

fields.label: update custom oem header
fields.description: Optional OEM header, sent as separate form-data for the Multipart HTTP push update. The header shall start with "Oem" according to DMTF Redfish spec 12.6.2.2. For more details, see U(https://www.dmtf.org/sites/default/files/standards/documents/DSP0266_1.21.0.html). If set, then O(update_custom_oem_params) is required too.

fields.label: update custom oem mime type
fields.description: MIME Type for custom OEM properties for HTTP Multipart Push updates.

fields.label: update custom oem params
fields.description: Custom OEM properties for HTTP Multipart Push updates. If set, then O(update_custom_oem_header) is required too. The properties are passed raw without any validation or conversion by Ansible. This means the content can be a file, a string, or any other data. If the content is a dictionary that should be converted to JSON, then the content must be converted to JSON before passing it to this module using the P(ansible.builtin.to_json#filter) filter.

fields.label: update handle
fields.description: Handle to check the status of an update in progress.

fields.label: update image file
fields.description: Filename, with optional path, of the image for the update.

fields.label: update image uri
fields.description: URI of the image for the update.

fields.label: update oem params
fields.description: Properties for HTTP Multipart Push Updates.

fields.label: update protocol
fields.description: Protocol for the update.

fields.label: update targets
fields.description: List of target resource URIs to apply the update to.

fields.label: update username
fields.description: New user name for updating account_username.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), TLS/SSL certificates are not validated. Set this to V(true) to enable certificate checking. Should be used together with O(ca_path).

fields.label: wait
fields.description: Block until the service is ready again.

fields.label: wait timeout
fields.description: How long to block until the service is ready again before giving up.

modules.label: redfish_config
modules.tooltip: Manages Out-Of-Band controllers using Redfish APIs

fields.label: auth token
fields.description: Security token for authenticating to OOB controller.

fields.label: baseuri
fields.description: Base URI of OOB controller.

fields.label: bios attributes
fields.description: Dictionary of BIOS attributes to update.

fields.label: boot order
fields.description: List of BootOptionReference strings specifying the BootOrder.

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains a CA certificate to be used for validation. Only used if O(validate_certs=true).

fields.label: category
fields.description: Category to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: ciphers
fields.description: TLS/SSL Ciphers to use for the request. When a list is provided, all ciphers are joined in order with V(:). See the L(OpenSSL Cipher List Format,https://www.openssl.org/docs/manmaster/man1/openssl-ciphers.html#CIPHER-LIST-FORMAT) for more details. The available ciphers is dependent on the Python and OpenSSL/LibreSSL versions.

fields.label: command
fields.description: List of commands to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: hostinterface config
fields.description: Setting dict of HostInterface on OOB controller.

fields.label: hostinterface id
fields.description: Redfish HostInterface instance ID if multiple HostInterfaces are present.

fields.label: network protocols
fields.description: Setting dict of manager services to update.

fields.label: nic addr
fields.description: EthernetInterface Address string on OOB controller.

fields.label: nic config
fields.description: Setting dict of EthernetInterface on OOB controller.

fields.label: power restore policy
fields.description: The desired power state of the system when power is restored after a power loss.

fields.label: resource id
fields.description: ID of the System, Manager or Chassis to modify.

fields.label: secure boot enable
fields.description: Setting parameter to enable or disable SecureBoot.

fields.label: service id
fields.description: ID of the manager to update.

fields.label: sessions config
fields.description: Setting dict of Sessions.

fields.label: storage none volume deletion
fields.description: Indicates if all non-RAID volumes are automatically deleted prior to creating the new volume.

fields.label: storage subsystem id
fields.description: ID of the Storage Subsystem on which the volume is to be created.

fields.label: strip etag quotes
fields.description: Removes surrounding quotes of etag used in C(If-Match) header of C(PATCH) requests. Only use this option to resolve bad vendor implementation where C(If-Match) only matches the unquoted etag string.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), TLS/SSL certificates are not validated. Set this to V(true) to enable certificate checking. Should be used together with O(ca_path).

fields.label: volume details
fields.description: Setting dictionary of volume to be created. If C(CapacityBytes) key is not specified in this dictionary, the size of the volume is determined by the Redfish service. It is possible the size is not the maximum available size.

fields.label: volume ids
fields.description: List of IDs of volumes to be deleted.

modules.label: redfish_info
modules.tooltip: Manages Out-Of-Band controllers using Redfish APIs

fields.label: auth token
fields.description: Security token for authenticating to OOB controller.

fields.label: baseuri
fields.description: Base URI of OOB controller.

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains a CA certificate to be used for validation. Only used if O(validate_certs=true).

fields.label: category
fields.description: List of categories to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: ciphers
fields.description: TLS/SSL Ciphers to use for the request. When a list is provided, all ciphers are joined in order with V(:). See the L(OpenSSL Cipher List Format,https://www.openssl.org/docs/manmaster/man1/openssl-ciphers.html#CIPHER-LIST-FORMAT) for more details. The available ciphers is dependent on the Python and OpenSSL/LibreSSL versions.

fields.label: command
fields.description: List of commands to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: manager
fields.description: Name of manager on OOB controller to target.

fields.label: update handle
fields.description: Handle to check the status of an update in progress.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), TLS/SSL certificates are not validated. Set this to V(true) to enable certificate checking. Should be used together with O(ca_path).

modules.label: redhat_subscription
modules.tooltip: Manage registration and subscriptions to RHSM using C(subscription-manager)

fields.label: activationkey
fields.description: Supply an activation key for use with registration.

fields.label: auto attach
fields.description: Upon successful registration, auto-consume available subscriptions. Please note that the alias O(ignore:autosubscribe) was removed in community.general 9.0.0.

fields.label: consumer id
fields.description: References an existing consumer ID to resume using a previous registration for this system. If the system's identity certificate is lost or corrupted, this option allows it to resume using its previous identity and subscriptions. The default is to not specify a consumer ID so a new ID is created.

fields.label: consumer name
fields.description: Name of the system to register, defaults to the hostname.

fields.label: consumer type
fields.description: The type of unit to register, defaults to system.

fields.label: environment
fields.description: Register with a specific environment in the destination org. Used with Red Hat Satellite or Katello.

fields.label: force register
fields.description: Register the system even if it is already registered.

fields.label: org id
fields.description: Organization ID to use in conjunction with activationkey.

fields.label: pool ids
fields.description: Specify subscription pool IDs to consume. A pool ID may be specified as a C(string) - just the pool ID (for example V(0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef)), or as a C(dict) with the pool ID as the key, and a quantity as the value (for example V(0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef: 2). If the quantity is provided, it is used to consume multiple entitlements from a pool (the pool must support this).

Credentials for GSSAPI can be specified with I(url_username)/I(url_password) or with the GSSAPI env var C(KRB5CCNAME) that specified a custom Kerberos credential cache.
