modules.label: sefcontext
modules.tooltip: Manages SELinux file context mapping definitions

fields.label: ftype
fields.description: The file type that should have SELinux contexts applied. The following file type options are available: V(a) for all files, V(b) for block devices, V(c) for character devices, V(d) for directories, V(f) for regular files, V(l) for symbolic links, V(p) for named pipes, V(s) for socket files.

fields.label: ignore selinux state
fields.description: Useful for scenarios (chrooted environment) that you cannot get the real SELinux state.

fields.label: reload
fields.description: Reload SELinux policy after commit. Note that this does not apply SELinux file contexts to existing files.

fields.label: selevel
fields.description: SELinux range for the specified O(target). Defaults to V(s0) for new file contexts and to existing value when modifying file contexts.

fields.label: setype
fields.description: SELinux type for the specified O(target).

fields.label: seuser
fields.description: SELinux user for the specified O(target). Defaults to V(system_u) for new file contexts and to existing value when modifying file contexts.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the SELinux file context must be V(absent) or V(present). Specifying V(absent) without either O(setype) or O(substitute) deletes both SELinux type or path substitution mappings that match O(target).

fields.label: substitute
fields.description: Path to use to substitute file context(s) for the specified O(target). The context labeling for the O(target) subtree is made equivalent to this path. This is also referred to as SELinux file context equivalence and it implements the C(equal) functionality of the SELinux management tools.

fields.label: target
fields.description: Target path (expression).

modules.label: selinux_permissive
modules.tooltip: Change permissive domain in SELinux policy

fields.label: domain
fields.description: The domain that is added or removed from the list of permissive domains.

fields.label: no reload
fields.description: Disable reloading of the SELinux policy after making change to a domain's permissive setting. The default is V(false), which causes policy to be reloaded when a domain changes state. Reloading the policy does not work on older versions of the C(policycoreutils-python) library, for example in EL 6.".

fields.label: permissive
fields.description: Indicate if the domain should or should not be set as permissive.

fields.label: store
fields.description: Name of the SELinux policy store to use.

modules.label: selogin
modules.tooltip: Manages linux user to SELinux user mapping

fields.label: ignore selinux state
fields.description: Run independent of selinux runtime state.

fields.label: login
fields.description: A Linux user.

fields.label: reload
fields.description: Reload SELinux policy after commit.

fields.label: selevel
fields.description: MLS/MCS Security Range (MLS/MCS Systems only) SELinux Range for SELinux login mapping defaults to the SELinux user record range.

fields.label: seuser
fields.description: SELinux user name.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired mapping value.

modules.label: sendgrid
modules.tooltip: Sends an email with the SendGrid API

fields.label: api key
fields.description: Sendgrid API key to use instead of username/password.

fields.label: attachments
fields.description: A list of relative or explicit paths of files you want to attach (7MB limit as per SendGrid docs).

fields.label: bcc
fields.description: A list of email addresses to bcc.

fields.label: body
fields.description: The e-mail body content.

fields.label: cc
fields.description: A list of email addresses to cc.

fields.label: from address
fields.description: The address in the "from" field for the email.

fields.label: from name
fields.description: The name you want to appear in the from field, for example V(John Doe).

fields.label: headers
fields.description: A dict to pass on as headers.

fields.label: html body
fields.description: Whether the body is HTML content that should be rendered.

fields.label: subject
fields.description: The desired subject for the email.

fields.label: to addresses
fields.description: A list with one or more recipient email addresses.

modules.label: seport
modules.tooltip: Manages SELinux network port type definitions

fields.label: ignore selinux state
fields.description: Run independent of selinux runtime state.

fields.label: local
fields.description: Work with local modifications only.

fields.label: ports
fields.description: Ports or port ranges. Can be a list (since 2.6) or comma separated string.

fields.label: proto
fields.description: Protocol for the specified port. Support for V(dccp) and V(sctp) has been added in community.general 12.4.0.

fields.label: reload
fields.description: Reload SELinux policy after commit.

fields.label: setype
fields.description: SELinux type for the specified port.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired boolean value.

fields.label: deploy
fields.description: Whether or not to deploy artifacts after building them. When this option is V(false) all the functions are built, but no stack update is run to send them out. This is mostly useful for generating artifacts to be stored/deployed elsewhere.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Whether or not to force full deployment, equivalent to serverless C(--force) option.

fields.label: region
fields.description: AWS region to deploy the service to. This parameter defaults to V(us-east-1).

fields.label: serverless bin path
fields.description: The path of a serverless framework binary relative to the O(service_path), for example V(node_module/.bin/serverless).

fields.label: service path
fields.description: The path to the root of the Serverless Service to be operated on.

fields.label: stage
fields.description: The name of the serverless framework project stage to deploy to. This uses the serverless framework default "dev".

fields.label: state
fields.description: Goal state of given stage/project.

fields.label: verbose
fields.description: Shows all stack events during deployment, and display any Stack Output.

fields.label: delay
fields.description: Seconds to wait before shutdown. Passed as a parameter to the shutdown command. On Linux, macOS and OpenBSD, this is converted to minutes and rounded down. If less than 60, it is set to 0. On Solaris and FreeBSD, this represents seconds.

fields.label: msg
fields.description: Message to display to users before shutdown.

fields.label: search paths
fields.description: Paths to search on the remote machine for the C(shutdown) command. I(Only) these paths are searched for the C(shutdown) command. E(PATH) is ignored in the remote node when searching for the C(shutdown) command.

modules.label: simpleinit_msb
modules.tooltip: Manage services on Source Mage GNU/Linux

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Whether the service should start on boot. At least one of O(state) and O(enabled) are required.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the service.

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(started)/V(stopped) are idempotent actions that do not run commands unless necessary. V(restarted) always bounces the service. V(reloaded) always reloads. At least one of O(state) and O(enabled) are required. Note that V(reloaded) starts the service if it is not already started, even if your chosen init system would not normally.

modules.label: sl_vm
modules.tooltip: Create or cancel a virtual instance in SoftLayer

fields.label: cpus
fields.description: Count of cpus to be assigned to new virtual instance.

fields.label: datacenter
fields.description: Datacenter for the virtual instance to be deployed.

fields.label: dedicated
fields.description: Flag to determine if the instance should be deployed in dedicated space.

fields.label: disks
fields.description: List of disk sizes to be assigned to new virtual instance.

fields.label: domain
fields.description: Domain name to be provided to a virtual instance.

fields.label: flavor
fields.description: Specify which SoftLayer flavor template to use instead of cpus and memory.

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: Hostname to be provided to a virtual instance.

fields.label: hourly
fields.description: Flag to determine if the instance should be hourly billed.

fields.label: image id
fields.description: Image Template to be used for new virtual instance.

fields.label: instance id
fields.description: Instance ID of the virtual instance to perform action option.

fields.label: local disk
fields.description: Flag to determine if local disk should be used for the new instance.

fields.label: memory
fields.description: Amount of memory to be assigned to new virtual instance.

fields.label: nic speed
fields.description: NIC Speed to be assigned to new virtual instance.

fields.label: os code
fields.description: OS Code to be used for new virtual instance.

fields.label: post uri
fields.description: URL of a post provisioning script to be loaded and executed on virtual instance.

fields.label: private
fields.description: Flag to determine if the instance should be private only.

fields.label: private vlan
fields.description: VLAN by its ID to be assigned to the private NIC.

fields.label: public vlan
fields.description: VLAN by its ID to be assigned to the public NIC.

fields.label: ssh keys
fields.description: List of ssh keys by their ID to be assigned to a virtual instance.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Create, or cancel a virtual instance. Specify V(present) for create, V(absent) to cancel.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: Tag or list of tags to be provided to a virtual instance.

fields.label: wait
fields.description: Flag used to wait for active status before returning.

fields.label: wait time
fields.description: Time in seconds before wait returns.

fields.label: attachments
fields.description: Define a list of attachments. This list mirrors the Slack JSON API. For more information, see U(https://api.slack.com/docs/attachments).

fields.label: blocks
fields.description: Define a list of blocks. This list mirrors the Slack JSON API. For more information, see U(https://api.slack.com/block-kit).

fields.label: channel
fields.description: Channel to send the message to. If absent, the message goes to the channel selected for the O(token).

fields.label: color
fields.description: Allow text to use default colors - use the default of V(normal) to not send a custom color bar at the start of the message. Allowed values for color can be one of V(normal), V(good), V(warning), V(danger), any valid 3 digit or 6 digit hex color value.

fields.label: domain
fields.description: When using new format 'Webhook token' and WebAPI tokens: this can be V(slack.com) or V(slack-gov.com) and is ignored otherwise. When using old format 'Webhook token': Slack (sub)domain for your environment without protocol. (For example V(example.slack.com).) in Ansible 1.8 and beyond, this is deprecated and may be ignored. See token documentation for information.

fields.label: fail on file error
fields.description: If V(true), the module fails if a file is missing or encounters an upload error. If V(false), the module issues a warning and continue processing the next file.

fields.label: icon emoji
fields.description: Emoji for the message sender. See Slack documentation for options. If O(icon_emoji) is set, O(icon_url) is not used.

fields.label: icon url
fields.description: URL for the message sender's icon.

fields.label: link names
fields.description: Automatically create links for channels and usernames in O(msg).

fields.label: message id
fields.description: Optional. Message ID to edit, instead of posting a new message. If supplied O(channel) must be in form of C(C0xxxxxxx). use C({{ slack_response.channel }}) to get RV(ignore:channel) from previous task run. The token needs history scope to get information on the message to edit (C(channels:history,groups:history,mpim:history,im:history)). Corresponds to C(ts) in the Slack API (U(https://api.slack.com/messaging/modifying)).

fields.label: msg
fields.description: Message to send. Note that the module does not handle escaping characters. Plain-text angle brackets and ampersands should be converted to HTML entities (for example C(&) to C(&)) before sending. See Slack's documentation (U(https://api.slack.com/docs/message-formatting)) for more.

fields.label: parse
fields.description: Setting for the message parser at Slack.

fields.label: prepend hash
fields.description: Setting for automatically prepending a V(#) symbol on the passed in O(channel). The V(auto) method prepends a V(#) unless O(channel) starts with one of V(#), V(@), V(C0), V(GF), V(G0), V(CP). These prefixes only cover a small set of the prefixes that should not have a V(#) prepended. Since an exact condition which O(channel) values must not have the V(#) prefix is not known, the value V(auto) for this option is deprecated in the future. It is best to explicitly set O(prepend_hash=always) or O(prepend_hash=never) to obtain the needed behavior. Before community.general 12.0.0, the default was V(auto). It has been deprecated since community.general 10.2.0. Note that V(auto) will be deprecated in a future version.

fields.label: thread id
fields.description: Optional. Timestamp of parent message to thread this message, see U(https://api.slack.com/docs/message-threading).

fields.label: token
fields.description: Slack integration token. This authenticates you to the Slack service. Make sure to use the correct type of token, depending on what method you use. Webhook token: Prior to Ansible 1.8, a token looked like V(3Ffe373sfhRE6y42Fg3rvf4GlK). In Ansible 1.8 and above, Ansible adapts to the new Slack API where tokens look like V(G922VJP24/D921DW937/3Ffe373sfhRE6y42Fg3rvf4GlK). If tokens are in the new format then Slack ignores any value of domain except V(slack.com) or V(slack-gov.com). If the token is in the old format the domain is required. Ansible has no control of when Slack is going to remove the old API. When Slack does that the old format is going to cease working. B(Please keep in mind the tokens are not the API tokens but are the webhook tokens.) In Slack these are found in the webhook URL which are obtained under the apps and integrations. The incoming webhooks can be added in that area. In some cases this may be locked by your Slack admin and you must request access. It is there that the incoming webhooks can be added. The key is on the end of the URL given to you in that section. WebAPI token: Slack WebAPI requires a personal, bot or work application token. These tokens start with V(xoxp-), V(xoxb-) or V(xoxa-), for example V(xoxb-1234-56789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz). WebAPI token is required if you intend to receive thread_id. See Slack's documentation (U(https://api.slack.com/docs/token-types)) for more information.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of package to install/remove.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the package, you can use V(installed) as an alias for V(present) and V(removed) as one for V(absent).

fields.label: update cache
fields.description: Update the package database first.

fields.label: filters
fields.description: Criteria for selecting image. Can be any value from image manifest and V(published_date), V(published), V(source), V(clones), and V(size). More information can be found at U(https://smartos.org/man/8/imgadm) under C(imgadm list).

fields.label: channel
fields.description: Define which release of a snap is installed and tracked for updates. This option can only be specified if there is a single snap in the task. If not passed, the C(snap) command defaults to V(stable). If the value passed does not contain the C(track), it defaults to C(latest). For example, if V(edge) is passed, the module assumes the channel to be V(latest/edge). See U(https://snapcraft.io/docs/channels) for more details about snap channels.

fields.label: classic
fields.description: Install a snap that has classic confinement. This option corresponds to the C(--classic) argument of the C(snap install) command. This level of confinement is permissive, granting full system access, similar to that of traditionally packaged applications that do not use sandboxing mechanisms. This option can only be specified when the task involves a single snap. See U(https://snapcraft.io/docs/snap-confinement) for more details about classic confinement and confinement levels.

fields.label: dangerous
fields.description: Install the snap in dangerous mode, without validating its assertions and signatures. This is useful when installing local snaps that are either unsigned or have signatures that have not been acknowledged. See U(https://snapcraft.io/docs/install-modes) for more details about installation modes.

fields.label: devmode
fields.description: Install the snap in developer mode, granting the snap full system access and disabling security confinement. See U(https://snapcraft.io/docs/install-modes) for more details about installation modes.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the snaps to be installed. Any named snap accepted by the C(snap) command is valid. O(dangerous=true) may be necessary when installing C(.snap) files. See O(dangerous) for more details. The special name V(system) refers to the snapd system-wide configuration namespace. When used with O(options), it runs C(snap set system <key=value>). It is always considered present and only V(state=present) and O(options) are meaningful for it. Support for V(system) was added in community.general 13.0.0.

fields.label: options
fields.description: Set options with pattern C(key=value) or C(snap:key=value). If a snap name is given, the option is applied to that snap only. If the snap name is omitted, the options are applied to all snaps listed in O(name). Options are only applied to active snaps. Options are only applied when C(state) is set to V(present). This is done after the necessary installation or refresh (upgrade/downgrade) of all the snaps listed in O(name). See U(https://snapcraft.io/docs/configuration-in-snaps) for more details about snap configuration options.

fields.label: revision
fields.description: Install a specific revision of the snap. This option can only be specified if there is a single snap in the task. Mutually exclusive with O(channel). When a specific revision is set, the snap is held (C(snap refresh --hold)) to prevent automatic updates from overriding the pinned revision. B(Note:) running C(snap refresh <name>) manually bypasses the hold and will update the snap regardless. The hold only prevents snapd's automatic background refreshes. See U(https://snapcraft.io/docs/how-to-guides/manage-snaps/manage-updates/) for details. See U(https://snapcraft.io/docs/revisions) for more details about snap revisions.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of the package. When O(state=present) the module uses C(snap install) if the snap is not installed, and C(snap refresh) if it is installed but from a different channel.

fields.label: alias
fields.description: Aliases to be created or removed.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the snap.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of the alias.

fields.label: plug
fields.description: The plug endpoint in the format C(<snap>:<plug>).

fields.label: slot
fields.description: The slot endpoint in the format C(<snap>:<slot>) or C(:<slot>) for system slots. If not specified, snapd will attempt to find a matching slot automatically.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of the connection.

modules.label: snmp_facts
modules.tooltip: Retrieve facts for a device using SNMP

fields.label: authkey
fields.description: Authentication key. Required O(version=v3).

fields.label: community
fields.description: The SNMP community string, required if O(version) is V(v2) or V(v2c).

fields.label: integrity
fields.description: Hashing algorithm. Required if O(version=v3).

fields.label: level
fields.description: Authentication level. Required if O(version=v3).

fields.label: privacy
fields.description: Encryption algorithm. Required if O(level=authPriv).

fields.label: privkey
fields.description: Encryption key. Required if O(level=authPriv).

fields.label: retries
fields.description: Maximum number of request retries, 0 retries means just a single request.

fields.label: version
fields.description: SNMP Version to use, V(v2), V(v2c) or V(v3).

fields.label: attach options
fields.description: Extra options to the zoneadm attach command. For example, this can be used to specify whether a minimum or full update of packages is required and if any packages need to be deleted. For valid values, see zoneadm(1M).

fields.label: config
fields.description: The C(zonecfg) configuration commands for this zone. See zonecfg(1M) for the valid options and syntax. Typically this is a list of options separated by semi-colons or new lines, for example V(set auto-boot=true;add net;set physical=bge0;set address=10.1.1.1;end).

fields.label: create options
fields.description: Extra options to the zonecfg(1M) create command.

fields.label: install options
fields.description: Extra options to the zoneadm(1M) install command. To automate Solaris 11 zone creation, use this to specify the profile XML file, for example O(install_options=-c sc_profile.xml).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Zone name. A zone name must be unique name. A zone name must begin with an alphanumeric character. The name can contain alphanumeric characters, underscores V(_), hyphens V(-), and periods V(.). The name cannot be longer than 64 characters.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The path where the zone is created. This is required when the zone is created, but not used otherwise.

fields.label: root password
fields.description: The password hash for the root account. If not specified, the zone's root account does not have a password.

fields.label: sparse
fields.description: Whether to create a sparse (V(true)) or whole root (V(false)) zone.

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(present), configure and install the zone. V(installed), synonym for V(present). V(running), if the zone already exists, boot it, otherwise, configure and install the zone first, then boot it. V(started), synonym for V(running). V(stopped), shutdown a zone. V(absent), destroy the zone. V(configured), configure the ready so that it is to be attached. V(attached), attach a zone, but do not boot it. V(detached), shutdown and detach a zone.

modules.label: sorcery
modules.tooltip: Package manager for Source Mage GNU/Linux

fields.label: cache valid time
fields.description: Time in seconds to invalidate grimoire collection on update. Especially useful for SCM and rsync grimoires. Makes sense only in pair with O(update_cache).

fields.label: depends
fields.description: Comma-separated list of _optional_ dependencies to build a spell (or make sure it is built) with; use V(+)/V(-) in front of dependency to turn it on/off (V(+) is optional though). This option is ignored if O(name) parameter is equal to V(*) or contains more than one spell. Providers must be supplied in the form recognized by Sorcery, for example 'V(openssl(SSL\))'.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the spell or grimoire. Multiple names can be given, separated by commas. Special value V(*) in conjunction with states V(latest) or V(rebuild) updates or rebuilds the whole system respectively. The alias O(grimoire) was added in community.general 7.3.0.

fields.label: repository
fields.description: Repository location. If specified, O(name) represents grimoire(s) instead of spell(s). Special value V(*) pulls grimoire from the official location. Only single item in O(name) in conjunction with V(*) can be used. O(state=absent) must be used with a special value V(*).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to cast, dispel or rebuild a package. State V(cast) is an equivalent of V(present), not V(latest). State V(rebuild) implies cast of all specified spells, not only those existed before.

fields.label: update
fields.description: Whether or not to update sorcery scripts at the very first stage.

fields.label: update cache
fields.description: Whether or not to update grimoire collection before casting spells.

fields.label: agentport
fields.description: UDP port used for SNMP discovery.

fields.label: community
fields.description: SNMP community used for device discovery. Required when O(state=present).

fields.label: device
fields.description: IP address of the device. If a hostname is given, it is resolved to the IP address.

fields.label: landscape
fields.description: Landscape handle of the SpectroServer to which add or remove the device.

fields.label: state
fields.description: On V(present) creates the device when it does not exist. On V(absent) removes the device when it exists.

fields.label: url
fields.description: HTTP, HTTPS URL of the Oneclick server in the form V((http|https\)://host.domain[:port]).

fields.label: url password
fields.description: Oneclick user password.

fields.label: url username
fields.description: Oneclick user name.

fields.label: use proxy
fields.description: If V(false), it does not use a proxy, even if one is defined in an environment variable on the target hosts.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), SSL certificates are not validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

modules.label: spectrum_model_attrs
modules.tooltip: Enforce a model's attributes in CA Spectrum

fields.label: name
fields.description: Model name.

fields.label: type
fields.description: Model type.

fields.label: url
fields.description: URL of OneClick server.

fields.label: url password
fields.description: OneClick password.

fields.label: url username
fields.description: OneClick username.

fields.label: use proxy
fields.description: If V(false), it does not use a proxy, even if one is defined in an environment variable on the target hosts.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Validate SSL certificates. Only change this to V(false) if you can guarantee that you are talking to the correct endpoint and there is no man-in-the-middle attack happening.

fields.label: cpg name
fields.description: Name of the CPG.

fields.label: disk type
fields.description: Specifies that physical disks must have the specified device type.

fields.label: domain
fields.description: Specifies the name of the domain in which the object resides.

fields.label: growth increment
fields.description: Specifies the growth increment(in MiB, GiB or TiB) the amount of logical disk storage created on each auto-grow operation.

fields.label: growth limit
fields.description: Specifies that the autogrow operation is limited to the specified storage amount that sets the growth limit (in MiB, GiB or TiB).

fields.label: growth warning
fields.description: Specifies that the threshold (in MiB, GiB or TiB) of used logical disk space when exceeded results in a warning alert.

fields.label: high availability
fields.description: Specifies that the layout must support the failure of one port pair, one cage, or one magazine.

fields.label: raid type
fields.description: Specifies the RAID type for the logical disk.

fields.label: secure
fields.description: Specifies whether the certificate needs to be validated while communicating.

fields.label: set size
fields.description: Specifies the set size in the number of chunklets.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the specified CPG should exist or not.

fields.label: storage system ip
fields.description: The storage system IP address.

fields.label: storage system password
fields.description: The storage system password.

fields.label: storage system username
fields.description: The storage system user name.

fields.label: add keys to agent
fields.description: Sets the C(AddKeysToAgent) option.

fields.label: address family
fields.description: Sets the C(AddressFamily) option.

fields.label: controlmaster
fields.description: Sets the C(ControlMaster) option.

fields.label: controlpath
fields.description: Sets the C(ControlPath) option.

fields.label: controlpersist
fields.description: Sets the C(ControlPersist) option.

fields.label: dynamicforward
fields.description: Sets the C(DynamicForward) option.

fields.label: forward agent
fields.description: Sets the C(ForwardAgent) option.

fields.label: group
fields.description: Which group this configuration file belongs to. If none given, O(user) is used.

fields.label: host key algorithms
fields.description: Sets the C(HostKeyAlgorithms) option.

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: The actual host to connect to when connecting to the host defined.

fields.label: identities only
fields.description: Specifies that SSH should only use the configured authentication identity and certificate files (either the default files, or those explicitly configured in the C(ssh_config) files or passed on the ssh command-line), even if C(ssh-agent) or a C(PKCS11Provider) or C(SecurityKeyProvider) offers more identities.

fields.label: identity file
fields.description: The path to an identity file (SSH private key) that is used when connecting to this host. File need to exist and have mode V(0600) to be valid.

fields.label: other options
fields.description: Allows specifying arbitrary SSH config entry options using a dictionary. The key names must be lower case. Keys with upper case values are rejected. The values must be strings. Other values are rejected.

fields.label: proxycommand
fields.description: Sets the C(ProxyCommand) option. Mutually exclusive with O(proxyjump).

fields.label: proxyjump
fields.description: Sets the C(ProxyJump) option. Mutually exclusive with O(proxycommand).

fields.label: remote user
fields.description: Specifies the user to log in as.

fields.label: ssh config file
fields.description: SSH config file. If O(user) and this option are not specified, C(/etc/ssh/ssh_config) is used. Mutually exclusive with O(user).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether a host entry should exist or not.

fields.label: strict host key checking
fields.description: Whether to strictly check the host key when doing connections to the remote host. The value V(accept-new) is supported since community.general 8.6.0.

fields.label: user
fields.description: Which user account this configuration file belongs to. If none given and O(ssh_config_file) is not specified, C(/etc/ssh/ssh_config) is used. If a user is given, C(~/.ssh/config) is used. Mutually exclusive with O(ssh_config_file).

fields.label: user known hosts file
fields.description: Sets the user known hosts file option.

modules.label: sssd_info
modules.tooltip: Check SSSD domain status using D-Bus

fields.label: action
fields.description: The action to perform.

fields.label: domain
fields.description: Domain name to check. Required unless O(action=domain_list). When O(action=domain_list), this parameter is ignored and the module returns a list of all configured domains.

fields.label: server type
fields.description: Required parameter when O(action=active_servers) and O(action=list_servers). Optional and ignored for all other actions. At this point, the module supports ONLY the types C(IPA) for FreeIPA servers and C(AD).

modules.label: stacki_host
modules.tooltip: Add or remove host to stacki front-end

fields.label: appliance
fields.description: Appliance to be used in host creation. Required if O(state=present) and host does not yet exist.

fields.label: force install
fields.description: Set value to V(true) to force node into install state if it already exists in stacki.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the host to be added to Stacki.

fields.label: network
fields.description: Network to be configured in the host. Currently not used by the module.

fields.label: prim intf
fields.description: Name of the primary network interface. Currently not used by the module.

fields.label: prim intf ip
fields.description: IP Address for the primary network interface. Currently not used by the module.

fields.label: prim intf mac
fields.description: MAC Address for the primary PXE boot network interface. Currently not used by the module.

fields.label: rack
fields.description: Rack to be used in host creation. Required if O(state=present) and host does not yet exist.

fields.label: rank
fields.description: Rank to be used in host creation. In Stacki terminology, the rank is the position of the machine in a rack. Required if O(state=present) and host does not yet exist.

fields.label: stacki endpoint
fields.description: URL for the Stacki API Endpoint.

fields.label: stacki password
fields.description: Password for authenticating with Stacki API, but if not specified, the environment variable E(stacki_password) is used instead.

fields.label: stacki user
fields.description: Username for authenticating with Stacki API, but if not specified, the environment variable E(stacki_user) is used instead.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Set value to the desired state for the specified host.

fields.label: delta
fields.description: If the metric is of type V(gauge), change the value by O(delta).

fields.label: metric
fields.description: The name of the metric.

fields.label: metric prefix
fields.description: The prefix to add to the metric.

fields.label: metric type
fields.description: The type of metric.

fields.label: protocol
fields.description: The transport protocol to send metrics over.

fields.label: state
fields.description: State of the check, only V(present) makes sense.

fields.label: value
fields.description: The value of the metric.

modules.label: statusio_maintenance
modules.tooltip: Create maintenance windows for your status.io dashboard

fields.label: all infrastructure affected
fields.description: If it affects all components and containers.

fields.label: api id
fields.description: Your unique API ID from status.io.

fields.label: api key
fields.description: Your unique API Key from status.io.

fields.label: automation
fields.description: Automatically start and end the maintenance window.

fields.label: components
fields.description: The given name of your component (server name).

fields.label: containers
fields.description: The given name of your container (data center).

fields.label: desc
fields.description: Message describing the maintenance window.

fields.label: maintenance id
fields.description: The maintenance ID number when deleting a maintenance window.

fields.label: maintenance notify 1 hr
fields.description: Notify subscribers 1 hour before maintenance start time.

fields.label: maintenance notify 24 hr
fields.description: Notify subscribers 24 hours before maintenance start time.

fields.label: maintenance notify 72 hr
fields.description: Notify subscribers 72 hours before maintenance start time.

fields.label: maintenance notify now
fields.description: Notify subscribers now.

fields.label: minutes
fields.description: The duration of the maintenance window (starting from playbook runtime).

fields.label: start date
fields.description: Date maintenance is expected to start (Month/Day/Year) (UTC). End Date is worked out from O(start_date) + O(minutes).

fields.label: start time
fields.description: Time maintenance is expected to start (Hour:Minutes) (UTC). End Time is worked out from O(start_time) + O(minutes).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of the package.

fields.label: statuspage
fields.description: Your unique StatusPage ID from status.io.

fields.label: title
fields.description: A descriptive title for the maintenance window.

fields.label: url
fields.description: Status.io API URL. A private apiary can be used instead.

fields.label: commands
fields.description: The commands allowed by the sudoers rule. Multiple can be added by passing a list of commands. Use V(ALL) for all commands.

fields.label: defaults
fields.description: A list of C(Defaults) directives to include in the sudoers rule file. Each entry is written as a C(Defaults) line scoped to the user or group specified in the rule. For example, V(!targetpw) becomes C(Defaults:%group !targetpw) for a group rule or C(Defaults:user !targetpw) for a user rule. The directives are placed before the privilege rule in the generated file.

fields.label: group
fields.description: The name of the group for the sudoers rule. This option cannot be used in conjunction with O(user).

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the sudoers rule. This is used for the filename for the sudoers file managed by this rule.

fields.label: noexec
fields.description: Whether a command is prevented to run further commands itself.

fields.label: nopassword
fields.description: Whether a password is not required when command is run with sudo.

fields.label: runas
fields.description: Specify the target user the command(s) runs as.

fields.label: setenv
fields.description: Whether to allow keeping the environment when command is run with sudo.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the rule should exist or not.

fields.label: sudoers path
fields.description: The path which sudoers config files are managed in.

fields.label: user
fields.description: The name of the user for the sudoers rule. This option cannot be used in conjunction with O(group).

fields.label: validation
fields.description: If V(absent), the sudoers rule is added without validation. If V(detect) and C(visudo) is available, then the sudoers rule is validated by C(visudo). If V(required), C(visudo) must be available to validate the sudoers rule.

modules.label: supervisorctl
modules.tooltip: Manage the state of a program or group of programs managed by C(supervisord)

fields.label: config
fields.description: The supervisor configuration file path.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the supervisord program or group to manage. The name is taken as group name when it ends with a colon V(:). If O(name=all), all programs and program groups are managed.

fields.label: server url
fields.description: URL on which supervisord server is listening.

fields.label: signal
fields.description: The signal to send to the program/group, when combined with the V(signalled) state. Required when l(state=signalled).

fields.label: state
fields.description: The desired state of program/group.

fields.label: stop before removing
fields.description: Use O(stop_before_removing=true) to stop the program/group before removing it.

fields.label: supervisorctl path
fields.description: Path to C(supervisorctl) executable.

fields.label: downed
fields.description: Should a C(down) file exist or not, if it exists it disables auto startup. Defaults to V(false). Downed does not imply stopped.

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Whether the service is enabled or not, if disabled it also implies O(state=stopped). Take note that a service can be enabled and downed (no auto restart).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the service to manage.

fields.label: service dir
fields.description: Directory C(svscan) watches for services.

fields.label: service src
fields.description: Directory where services are defined, the source of symlinks to O(service_dir).

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(started)/V(stopped) are idempotent actions that do not run commands unless necessary. V(restarted) always bounces the svc (svc -t) and V(killed) always bounces the svc (svc -k). V(reloaded) sends a sigusr1 (svc -1). V(once) runs a normally downed svc once (svc -o), not really an idempotent operation.

fields.label: category
fields.description: Install/Remove category instead of a single package.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Package name, for example V(SUNWcsr).

fields.label: proxy
fields.description: HTTP[s] proxy to be used if O(src) is a URL.

fields.label: response file
fields.description: Specifies the location of a response file to be used if package expects input on install.

fields.label: src
fields.description: Specifies the location to install the package from. Required when O(state=present). Can be any path acceptable to the C(pkgadd) command's C(-d) option. For example: V(somefile.pkg), V(/dir/with/pkgs), V(http://server/mypkgs.pkg). If using a file or directory, they must already be accessible by the host. See the M(ansible.builtin.copy) module for a way to get them there.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to install (V(present)), or remove (V(absent)) a package. If the package is to be installed, then O(src) is required. The SVR4 package system does not provide an upgrade operation. You need to uninstall the old, then install the new package.

fields.label: zone
fields.description: Whether to install the package only in the current zone, or install it into all zones. The installation into all zones works only if you are working with the global zone.

modules.label: swdepot
modules.tooltip: Manage packages with swdepot package manager (HP-UX)

fields.label: depot
fields.description: The source repository from which install or upgrade a package.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Package name.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to install (V(present), V(latest)), or remove (V(absent)) a package.

modules.label: swupd
modules.tooltip: Manages updates and bundles in ClearLinux systems

fields.label: contenturl
fields.description: URL pointing to the contents of available bundles. If not specified, the contents are retrieved from clearlinux.org.

fields.label: format
fields.description: The format suffix for version file downloads. For example V(1), V(2), V(3), and so on, or the special value V(staging). If not specified, the default format is used.

fields.label: manifest
fields.description: The manifest contains information about the bundles at certain version of the OS. Specify a Manifest version to verify against that version or leave unspecified to verify against the current version.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the (I)bundle to install or remove.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Indicates the desired (I)bundle state. V(present) ensures the bundle is installed while V(absent) ensures the (I)bundle is not installed.

fields.label: update
fields.description: Updates the OS to the latest version.

fields.label: url
fields.description: Overrides both O(contenturl) and O(versionurl).

fields.label: verify
fields.description: Verify content for OS version.

fields.label: versionurl
fields.description: URL for version string download.

fields.label: facility
fields.description: Set the log facility.

fields.label: ident
fields.description: Specify the name of application name which is sending the log to syslog.

fields.label: log pid
fields.description: Log the PID in brackets.

fields.label: msg
fields.description: This is the message to place in syslog.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: Set the log priority.

fields.label: revert
fields.description: Revert system patches.

fields.label: delim
fields.description: Delimiter to be used instead of V(" ") (space). Only used when O(state=value_present) or O(state=value_absent).

fields.label: jail
fields.description: Name or ID of the jail to operate on.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of variable in C(/etc/rc.conf) to manage.

fields.label: path
fields.description: Path to file to use instead of V(/etc/rc.conf).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Use V(present) to add the variable. Use V(absent) to remove the variable. Use V(value_present) to add the value to the existing variable. Use V(value_absent) to remove the value from the existing variable.

fields.label: value
fields.description: The value to set when O(state=present). The value to add when O(state=value_present). The value to remove when O(state=value_absent).

fields.label: name
fields.description: The credential name to validate the embedded credential name.

fields.label: newline
fields.description: Whether to add a trailing newline character to the end of the output, if not present.

fields.label: secret
fields.description: The secret to decrypt.

fields.label: timestamp
fields.description: The timestamp to use to validate the V(not-after) timestamp that was used during encryption. Takes a timestamp specification in the format described in V(systemd.time(7\)).

fields.label: transcode
fields.description: Whether to transcode the output before returning it.

fields.label: user
fields.description: A user name or numeric UID when decrypting from a specific user context. If set to the special string V(self) it sets the user to the user of the calling process. Requires C(systemd) 256 or later.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The credential name to embed in the encrypted credential data.

fields.label: not after
fields.description: The time when the credential shall not be used anymore. Takes a timestamp specification in the format described in V(systemd.time(7\)).

fields.label: pretty
fields.description: Pretty print the output so that it may be pasted directly into a unit file.

fields.label: secret
fields.description: The secret to encrypt.

fields.label: timestamp
fields.description: The timestamp to embed into the encrypted credential. Takes a timestamp specification in the format described in V(systemd.time(7\)).

fields.label: user
fields.description: A user name or numeric UID to encrypt the credential for. If set to the special string V(self) it sets the user to the user of the calling process. Requires C(systemd) 256 or later.

fields.label: extra properties
fields.description: Additional properties to retrieve (appended to the default ones). Note that all property names are converted to lower-case.

fields.label: unitname
fields.description: List of unit names to process. It supports C(.service), C(.target), C(.socket), C(.mount) and C(.timer) units type. C(.timer) units are supported since community.general 10.5.0. Each name must correspond to the full name of the C(systemd) unit or to a wildcard expression like V('ssh*') and V('*.service'). Wildcard expressions in O(unitname) are supported since community.general 10.5.0.

fields.label: fetch only
fields.description: Fetch and verify files and create C(/bsd.upgrade) but do not reboot. Set to V(false) if you want C(sysupgrade) to reboot. This causes the module to fail. See the examples.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Force upgrade (for snapshots only).

fields.label: installurl
fields.description: OpenBSD mirror top-level URL for fetching an upgrade. By default, the mirror URL is pulled from C(/etc/installurl).

fields.label: keep files
fields.description: Keep the files under C(/home/_sysupgrade). By default, the files are deleted after the upgrade.

fields.label: snapshot
fields.description: Apply the latest snapshot. Otherwise release is applied.

modules.label: taiga_issue
modules.tooltip: Creates/deletes an issue in a Taiga Project Management Platform

fields.label: attachment
fields.description: Path to a file to be attached to the issue.

fields.label: attachment description
fields.description: A string describing the file to be attached to the issue.

fields.label: description
fields.description: The issue description.

fields.label: issue type
fields.description: The issue type. Must exist previously.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: The issue priority. Must exist previously.

fields.label: project
fields.description: Name of the project containing the issue. Must exist previously.

fields.label: severity
fields.description: The issue severity. Must exist previously.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the issue should be present or not.

fields.label: status
fields.description: The issue status. Must exist previously.

fields.label: subject
fields.description: The issue subject.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: A lists of tags to be assigned to the issue.

fields.label: taiga host
fields.description: The hostname of the Taiga instance.

fields.label: api args
fields.description: Any parameters for the method. For reference to default method, V(SendMessage), see U(https://core.telegram.org/bots/api#sendmessage).

fields.label: api host
fields.description: Custom telegram API host.

fields.label: api method
fields.description: Bot API method. For reference, see U(https://core.telegram.org/bots/api).

fields.label: token
fields.description: Token identifying your telegram bot.

fields.label: backend config
fields.description: A group of key-values to provide at init stage to the -backend-config parameter.

fields.label: backend config files
fields.description: The path to a configuration file to provide at init state to the -backend-config parameter. This can accept a list of paths to multiple configuration files.

fields.label: binary path
fields.description: The path of a C(terraform) binary to use, relative to the 'service_path' unless you supply an absolute path.

fields.label: check destroy
fields.description: Apply only when no resources are destroyed. Note that this only prevents "destroy" actions, but not "destroy and re-create" actions. This option is ignored when O(state=absent).

fields.label: complex vars
fields.description: Enable/disable capability to handle complex variable structures for C(terraform). If V(true) the O(variables) also accepts dictionaries, lists, and booleans to be passed to C(terraform). Strings that are passed are correctly quoted. When disabled, supports only simple variables (strings, integers, and floats), and passes them on unquoted.

fields.label: force init
fields.description: To avoid duplicating infra, if a state file cannot be found this forces a C(terraform init). Generally, this should be turned off unless you intend to provision an entirely new Terraform deployment.

fields.label: init reconfigure
fields.description: Forces backend reconfiguration during init.

fields.label: lock
fields.description: Enable statefile locking, if you use a service that accepts locks (such as S3+DynamoDB) to store your statefile.

fields.label: lock timeout
fields.description: How long to maintain the lock on the statefile, if you use a service that accepts locks (such as S3+DynamoDB).

fields.label: no color
fields.description: If V(true), suppress color codes in output from Terraform commands. If V(false), allows Terraform to use color codes in its output.

fields.label: overwrite init
fields.description: Run init even if C(.terraform/terraform.tfstate) already exists in O(project_path).

fields.label: parallelism
fields.description: Restrict concurrent operations when Terraform applies the plan.

fields.label: plan file
fields.description: The path to an existing Terraform plan file to apply. If this is not specified, Ansible builds a new TF plan and execute it. Note that this option is required if O(state=planned).

fields.label: plugin paths
fields.description: List of paths containing Terraform plugin executable files. Plugin executables can be downloaded from U(https://releases.hashicorp.com/). When set, the plugin discovery and auto-download behavior of Terraform is disabled. The directory structure in the plugin path can be tricky. The Terraform docs U(https://learn.hashicorp.com/tutorials/terraform/automate-terraform#pre-installed-plugins) show a simple directory of files, but actually, the directory structure has to follow the same structure you would see if Terraform auto-downloaded the plugins. See the examples below for a tree output of an example plugin directory.

fields.label: project path
fields.description: The path to the root of the Terraform directory with the C(vars.tf)/C(main.tf)/etc to use.

fields.label: provider upgrade
fields.description: Allows Terraform init to upgrade providers to versions specified in the project's version constraints.

fields.label: purge workspace
fields.description: Only works with state = absent. If V(true), the O(workspace) is deleted after the C(terraform destroy) action. If O(workspace=default) then it is not deleted.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Goal state of given stage/project.

fields.label: state file
fields.description: The path to an existing Terraform state file to use when building plan. If this is not specified, the default C(terraform.tfstate) is used. This option is ignored when plan is specified.

fields.label: targets
fields.description: A list of specific resources to target in this plan/application. The resources selected here are also auto-include any dependencies.

fields.label: variables
fields.description: A group of key-values pairs to override template variables or those in variables files. By default, only string and number values are allowed, which are passed on unquoted. Support complex variable structures (lists, dictionaries, numbers, and booleans) to reflect terraform variable syntax when O(complex_vars=true). Ansible integers or floats are mapped to terraform numbers. Ansible strings are mapped to terraform strings. Ansible dictionaries are mapped to terraform objects. Ansible lists are mapped to terraform lists. Ansible booleans are mapped to terraform booleans. B(Note) passwords passed as variables are visible in the log output. Make sure to use C(no_log=true) in production!.

fields.label: variables files
fields.description: The path to a variables file for Terraform to fill into the TF configurations. This can accept a list of paths to multiple variables files.

fields.label: workspace
fields.description: The terraform workspace to work with. This sets the E(TF_WORKSPACE) environmental variable that is used to override workspace selection. For more information about workspaces have a look at U(https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/state/workspaces).

fields.label: hwclock
fields.description: Whether the hardware clock is in UTC or in local timezone. Default is to keep current setting. Note that this option is recommended not to change and may fail to configure, especially on virtual environments such as AWS. B(At least one) of O(name) and O(hwclock) are required. I(Only used on Linux).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the timezone for the system clock. Default is to keep current setting. B(At least one) of O(name) and O(hwclock) are required.

modules.label: twilio
modules.tooltip: Sends a text message to a mobile phone through Twilio

fields.label: account sid
fields.description: User's Twilio account token found on the account page.

fields.label: auth token
fields.description: User's Twilio authentication token.

fields.label: from number
fields.description: The Twilio number to send the text message from, format C(+15551112222).

fields.label: media url
fields.description: A URL with a picture, video or sound clip to send with an MMS (multimedia message) instead of a plain SMS.

fields.label: msg
fields.description: The body of the text message.

fields.label: to numbers
fields.description: One or more phone numbers to send the text message to, format C(+15551112222).

modules.label: udm_dns_record
modules.tooltip: Manage DNS entries on a univention corporate server

fields.label: data
fields.description: Additional data for this record, for example V({'a': '192.0.2.1'}). Required if O(state=present).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the record, this is also the DNS record. For example V(www) for www.example.com. For PTR records this has to be the IP address.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the DNS record is present or not.

fields.label: type
fields.description: Define the record type. V(host_record) is a A or AAAA record, V(alias) is a CNAME, V(ptr_record) is a PTR record, V(srv_record) is a SRV record and V(txt_record) is a TXT record. The available choices are: V(host_record), V(alias), V(ptr_record), V(srv_record), V(txt_record).

fields.label: zone
fields.description: Corresponding DNS zone for this record, for example V(example.com). For PTR records this has to be the full reverse zone (for example V(1.1.192.in-addr.arpa)).

modules.label: udm_dns_zone
modules.tooltip: Manage DNS zones on a univention corporate server

fields.label: contact
fields.description: Contact person in the SOA record.

fields.label: expire
fields.description: Specifies the upper limit on the time interval that can elapse before the zone is no longer authoritative.

fields.label: interfaces
fields.description: List of interface IP addresses, on which the server should response this zone. Required if O(state=present).

fields.label: mx
fields.description: List of MX servers. (Must declared as A or AAAA records).

fields.label: nameserver
fields.description: List of appropriate name servers. Required if O(state=present).

fields.label: refresh
fields.description: Interval before the zone should be refreshed.

fields.label: retry
fields.description: Interval that should elapse before a failed refresh should be retried.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the DNS zone is present or not.

fields.label: ttl
fields.description: Minimum TTL field that should be exported with any RR from this zone.

fields.label: type
fields.description: Define if the zone is a forward or reverse DNS zone. The available choices are: V(forward_zone), V(reverse_zone).

fields.label: zone
fields.description: DNS zone name, for example V(example.com).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the group is present or not.

fields.label: subpath
fields.description: Subpath inside the OU, for example V(cn=classes,cn=students,cn=groups).

fields.label: title
fields.description: Title, for example V(Prof.).

fields.label: unixhome
fields.description: Unix home directory. If not specified, it defaults to C(/home/$USERNAME).

fields.label: update password
fields.description: V(always) updates passwords if they differ. V(on_create) only sets the password for newly created users.

fields.label: userexpiry
fields.description: Account expiry date, for example V(1999-12-31). If not specified, it defaults to the current day plus one year.

fields.label: comment
fields.description: Add a comment to the rule. Requires UFW version >=0.35.

fields.label: default
fields.description: Change the default policy for incoming or outgoing traffic.

fields.label: delete
fields.description: Delete rule. If O(delete=true) and a value is provided for O(insert), then O(insert) is ignored.

fields.label: direction
fields.description: Select direction for a rule or default policy command. Mutually exclusive with O(interface_in) and O(interface_out).

fields.label: from ip
fields.description: Source IP address.

fields.label: from port
fields.description: Source port.

fields.label: insert
fields.description: Insert the corresponding rule as rule number NUM. Note that ufw numbers rules starting with 1. If O(delete=true) and a value is provided for O(insert), then O(insert) is ignored.

fields.label: insert relative to
fields.description: Allows to interpret the index in O(insert) relative to a position. V(zero) interprets the rule number as an absolute index (that is, 1 is the first rule). V(first-ipv4) interprets the rule number relative to the index of the first IPv4 rule, or relative to the position where the first IPv4 rule would be if there is currently none. V(last-ipv4) interprets the rule number relative to the index of the last IPv4 rule, or relative to the position where the last IPv4 rule would be if there is currently none. V(first-ipv6) interprets the rule number relative to the index of the first IPv6 rule, or relative to the position where the first IPv6 rule would be if there is currently none. V(last-ipv6) interprets the rule number relative to the index of the last IPv6 rule, or relative to the position where the last IPv6 rule would be if there is currently none.

fields.label: interface
fields.description: Specify interface for the rule. The direction (in or out) used for the interface depends on the value of O(direction). See O(interface_in) and O(interface_out) for routed rules that needs to supply both an input and output interface. Mutually exclusive with O(interface_in) and O(interface_out).

fields.label: interface in
fields.description: Specify input interface for the rule. This is mutually exclusive with O(direction) and O(interface). However, it is compatible with O(interface_out) for routed rules.

fields.label: interface out
fields.description: Specify output interface for the rule. This is mutually exclusive with O(direction) and O(interface). However, it is compatible with O(interface_in) for routed rules.

fields.label: log
fields.description: Log new connections matched to this rule.

fields.label: logging
fields.description: Toggles logging. Logged packets use the LOG_KERN syslog facility.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Use profile located in C(/etc/ufw/applications.d).

fields.label: proto
fields.description: TCP/IP protocol. The value V(vrrp) is supported since community.general 10.3.0.

fields.label: route
fields.description: Apply the rule to routed/forwarded packets.

fields.label: rule
fields.description: Add firewall rule.

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(enabled) reloads firewall and enables firewall on boot. V(disabled) unloads firewall and disables firewall on boot. V(reloaded) reloads firewall. V(reset) disables and resets firewall to installation defaults.

fields.label: to ip
fields.description: Destination IP address.

fields.label: to port
fields.description: Destination port.

modules.label: uv_python
modules.tooltip: Manage Python versions and installations using the C(uv) Python package manager

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of the specified Python version. V(present) ensures the specified version is installed. If you specify a full patch version (for example O(version=3.12.3)), that exact version is be installed if not already present. If you only specify a minor version (for example V(3.12)), the latest available patch version for that minor release is installed only if no patch version for that minor release is currently installed (including patch versions not managed by C(uv)). RV(python_versions) and RV(python_paths) lengths are always equal to one for this state. V(absent) ensures the specified version is removed. If you specify a full patch version, only that exact patch version is removed. If you only specify a minor version (for example V(3.12)), all installed patch versions for that minor release are removed. If you specify a version that is not installed, no changes are made. RV(python_versions) and RV(python_paths) lengths can be higher or equal to one in this state. V(latest) ensures the latest available patch version for the specified version is installed. If you only specify a minor version (for example V(3.12)), the latest available patch version for that minor release is always installed. If another patch version is already installed but is not the latest, the latest patch version is installed. The latest patch version installed depends on the C(uv) version, since available Python versions are frozen per C(uv) release. RV(python_versions) and RV(python_paths) lengths are always equal to one in this state. This state does not use C(uv python upgrade).

fields.label: version
fields.description: Python version to manage. Not all canonical Python versions are supported in this release. Valid version numbers consist of two or three dot-separated numeric components, with an optional 'pre-release' tag at the end such as V(3.12), V(3.12.3), V(3.15.0a5). Advanced uv selectors such as V(>=3.12,<3.13) or V(cpython@3.12) are not supported in this release. When you specify only a major.minor version, make sure the number is enclosed in quotes so that it gets parsed correctly. Note that in case only a major.minor version are specified behavior depends on the O(state) parameter.

fields.label: ackthreads
fields.description: Specifies the number of threads to use for acknowledging completion of requested VDO I/O operations. Valid values are integer values from V(1) to V(100) (lower numbers are preferable due to overhead). The default is V(1). Existing volumes maintain their previously configured setting unless a different value is specified in the playbook.

fields.label: activated
fields.description: The C(activate) status for a VDO volume. If this is set to V(false), the VDO volume cannot be started, and it does not start on system startup. However, on initial creation, a VDO volume with O(activated=false) is set to be running until stopped. This is the default behavior of the C(vdo create) command; it provides the user an opportunity to write a base amount of metadata (filesystem, LVM headers, and so on) to the VDO volume prior to stopping the volume, and leaving it deactivated until ready to use.

fields.label: biothreads
fields.description: Specifies the number of threads to use for submitting I/O operations to the storage device. Valid values are integer values from V(1) to V(100) (lower numbers are preferable due to overhead). The default is V(4). Existing volumes maintain their previously configured setting unless a different value is specified in the playbook.

fields.label: blockmapcachesize
fields.description: The amount of memory allocated for caching block map pages, in megabytes (or may be issued with an LVM-style suffix of K, M, G, or T). The default (and minimum) value is V(128M). The value specifies the size of the cache; there is a 15% memory usage overhead. Each 1.25G of block map covers 1T of logical blocks, therefore a small amount of block map cache memory can cache a significantly large amount of block map data. Existing volumes maintain their previously configured setting unless a different value is specified in the playbook.

fields.label: compression
fields.description: Configures whether compression is enabled. The default for a created volume is V(enabled). Existing volumes maintain their previously configured setting unless a different value is specified in the playbook.

fields.label: cputhreads
fields.description: Specifies the number of threads to use for CPU-intensive work such as hashing or compression. Valid values are integer values from V(1) to V(100) (lower numbers are preferable due to overhead). The default is V(2). Existing volumes maintain their previously configured setting unless a different value is specified in the playbook.

fields.label: deduplication
fields.description: Configures whether deduplication is enabled. The default for a created volume is V(enabled). Existing volumes maintain their previously configured setting unless a different value is specified in the playbook.

fields.label: device
fields.description: The full path of the device to use for VDO storage. This is required if O(state=present).

fields.label: emulate512
fields.description: Enables 512-byte emulation mode, allowing drivers or filesystems to access the VDO volume at 512-byte granularity, instead of the default 4096-byte granularity. Only recommended when a driver or filesystem requires 512-byte sector level access to a device. This option is only available when creating a new volume, and cannot be changed for an existing volume.

fields.label: force
fields.description: When creating a volume, ignores any existing file system or VDO signature already present in the storage device. When stopping or removing a VDO volume, first unmounts the file system stored on the device if mounted. B(Warning:) Since this parameter removes all safety checks it is important to make sure that all parameters provided are accurate and intentional.

fields.label: growphysical
fields.description: Specifies whether to attempt to execute a C(growphysical) operation, if there is enough unused space on the device. A C(growphysical) operation is executed if there is at least 64 GB of free space, relative to the previous physical size of the affected VDO volume.

fields.label: indexmem
fields.description: Specifies the amount of index memory in gigabytes. The default is V(0.25). The special decimal values V(0.25), V(0.5), and V(0.75) can be used, as can any positive integer. This option is only available when creating a new volume, and cannot be changed for an existing volume.

fields.label: indexmode
fields.description: Specifies the index mode of the Albireo index. The default is V(dense), which has a deduplication window of 1 GB of index memory per 1 TB of incoming data, requiring 10 GB of index data on persistent storage. The V(sparse) mode has a deduplication window of 1 GB of index memory per 10 TB of incoming data, but requires 100 GB of index data on persistent storage. This option is only available when creating a new volume, and cannot be changed for an existing volume.

fields.label: logicalsize
fields.description: The logical size of the VDO volume (in megabytes, or LVM suffix format). If not specified for a new volume, this defaults to the same size as the underlying storage device, which is specified in the O(device) parameter. Existing volumes maintain their size if the O(logicalsize) parameter is not specified, or is smaller than or identical to the current size. If the specified size is larger than the current size, a C(growlogical) operation is performed.

fields.label: logicalthreads
fields.description: Specifies the number of threads across which to subdivide parts of the VDO processing based on logical block addresses. Valid values are integer values from V(1) to V(100) (lower numbers are preferable due to overhead). The default is V(1). Existing volumes maintain their previously configured setting unless a different value is specified in the playbook.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the VDO volume.

fields.label: physicalthreads
fields.description: Specifies the number of threads across which to subdivide parts of the VDO processing based on physical block addresses. Valid values are integer values from V(1) to V(16) (lower numbers are preferable due to overhead). The physical space used by the VDO volume must be larger than (O(slabsize) * O(physicalthreads)). The default is V(1). Existing volumes maintain their previously configured setting unless a different value is specified in the playbook.

fields.label: readcache
fields.description: Enables or disables the read cache. The default is V(disabled). Choosing V(enabled) enables a read cache which may improve performance for workloads of high deduplication, read workloads with a high level of compression, or on hard disk storage. Existing volumes maintain their previously configured setting unless a different value is specified in the playbook. The read cache feature is available in VDO 6.1 and older.

fields.label: readcachesize
fields.description: Specifies the extra VDO device read cache size in megabytes. This is in addition to a system-defined minimum. Using a value with a suffix of K, M, G, or T is optional. The default value is V(0). 1.125 MB of memory per bio thread is used per 1 MB of read cache specified (for example, a VDO volume configured with 4 bio threads has a read cache memory usage overhead of 4.5 MB per 1 MB of read cache specified). Existing volumes maintain their previously configured setting unless a different value is specified in the playbook. The read cache feature is available in VDO 6.1 and older.

fields.label: running
fields.description: Whether this VDO volume is running. A VDO volume must be activated in order to be started.

fields.label: slabsize
fields.description: The size of the increment by which the physical size of a VDO volume is grown, in megabytes (or may be issued with an LVM-style suffix of K, M, G, or T). Must be a power of two between 128M and 32G. The default is V(2G), which supports volumes having a physical size up to 16T. The maximum, V(32G), supports a physical size of up to 256T. This option is only available when creating a new volume, and cannot be changed for an existing volume.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether this VDO volume should be V(present) or V(absent). If a V(present) VDO volume does not exist, it is created. If a V(present) VDO volume already exists, it is modified by updating the configuration, which takes effect when the VDO volume is restarted. Not all parameters of an existing VDO volume can be modified; the C(statusparamkeys) list in the code contains the parameters that can be modified after creation. If an V(absent) VDO volume does not exist, it is not removed.

fields.label: writepolicy
fields.description: Specifies the write policy of the VDO volume. The V(sync) mode acknowledges writes only after data is on stable storage. The V(async) mode acknowledges writes when data has been cached for writing to stable storage. The default (and highly recommended) V(auto) mode checks the storage device to determine whether it supports flushes. Devices that support flushes result in a VDO volume in V(async) mode, while devices that do not support flushes run in V(sync) mode. Existing volumes maintain their previously configured setting unless a different value is specified in the playbook.
