modules.label: vmadm
modules.tooltip: Manage SmartOS virtual machines and zones

fields.label: archive on delete
fields.description: When enabled, the zone dataset is mounted on C(/zones/archive) upon removal.

fields.label: autoboot
fields.description: Whether or not a VM is booted when the system is rebooted.

fields.label: boot
fields.description: Set the boot order for KVM VMs.

fields.label: brand
fields.description: Type of virtual machine. The V(bhyve) option was added in community.general 0.2.0.

fields.label: cpu cap
fields.description: Sets a limit on the amount of CPU time that can be used by a VM. Use V(0) for no cap.

fields.label: cpu shares
fields.description: Sets a limit on the number of fair share scheduler (FSS) CPU shares for a VM. This limit is relative to all other VMs on the system.

fields.label: cpu type
fields.description: Control the type of virtual CPU exposed to KVM VMs.

fields.label: customer metadata
fields.description: Metadata to be set and associated with this VM, this contain customer modifiable keys.

fields.label: delegate dataset
fields.description: Whether to delegate a ZFS dataset to an OS VM.

fields.label: disk driver
fields.description: Default value for a virtual disk model for KVM guests.

fields.label: disks
fields.description: A list of disks to add, valid properties are documented in vmadm(1M).

fields.label: dns domain
fields.description: Domain value for C(/etc/hosts).

fields.label: docker
fields.description: Docker images need this flag enabled along with the O(brand) set to C(lx).

fields.label: filesystems
fields.description: Mount additional filesystems into an OS VM.

fields.label: firewall enabled
fields.description: Enables the firewall, allowing fwadm(1M) rules to be applied.

fields.label: flexible disk size
fields.description: This sets an upper bound for the amount of space that a bhyve instance may use for its disks and snapshots of those disks (in MiBs).

fields.label: force
fields.description: Force a particular action (in other words, stop or delete a VM).

fields.label: fs allowed
fields.description: Comma separated list of filesystem types this zone is allowed to mount.

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: Zone/VM hostname.

fields.label: image uuid
fields.description: Image UUID.

fields.label: indestructible delegated
fields.description: Adds an C(@indestructible) snapshot to delegated datasets.

fields.label: indestructible zoneroot
fields.description: Adds an C(@indestructible) snapshot to zoneroot.

fields.label: internal metadata
fields.description: Metadata to be set and associated with this VM, this contains operator generated keys.

fields.label: internal metadata namespace
fields.description: List of namespaces to be set as C(internal_metadata-only); these namespaces come from O(internal_metadata) rather than O(customer_metadata).

fields.label: kernel version
fields.description: Kernel version to emulate for LX VMs.

fields.label: limit priv
fields.description: Set (comma separated) list of privileges the zone is allowed to use.

fields.label: maintain resolvers
fields.description: Resolvers in C(/etc/resolv.conf) are updated when updating the O(resolvers) property.

fields.label: max locked memory
fields.description: Total amount of memory (in MiBs) on the host that can be locked by this VM.

fields.label: max lwps
fields.description: Maximum number of lightweight processes this VM is allowed to have running.

fields.label: max physical memory
fields.description: Maximum amount of memory (in MiBs) on the host that the VM is allowed to use.

fields.label: max swap
fields.description: Maximum amount of virtual memory (in MiBs) the VM is allowed to use.

fields.label: mdata exec timeout
fields.description: Timeout in seconds (or 0 to disable) for the C(svc:/smartdc/mdata:execute) service that runs user-scripts in the zone.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the VM. vmadm(1M) uses this as an optional name.

fields.label: nic driver
fields.description: Default value for a virtual NIC model for KVM guests.

fields.label: nics
fields.description: A list of nics to add, valid properties are documented in vmadm(1M).

fields.label: nowait
fields.description: Consider the provisioning complete when the VM first starts, rather than when the VM has rebooted.

fields.label: owner uuid
fields.description: Define the UUID of the owner of the VM.

fields.label: qemu extra opts
fields.description: Additional qemu cmdline arguments for KVM guests.

fields.label: qemu opts
fields.description: Additional qemu arguments for KVM guests. This overwrites the default arguments provided by vmadm(1M) and should only be used for debugging.

fields.label: quota
fields.description: Quota on zone filesystems (in MiBs).

fields.label: ram
fields.description: Amount of virtual RAM for a KVM guest (in MiBs).

fields.label: resolvers
fields.description: List of resolvers to be put into C(/etc/resolv.conf).

fields.label: routes
fields.description: Dictionary that maps destinations to gateways, these are set as static routes in the VM.

fields.label: spice opts
fields.description: Addition options for SPICE-enabled KVM VMs.

fields.label: spice password
fields.description: Password required to connect to SPICE. By default no password is set. Please note this can be read from the Global Zone.

fields.label: state
fields.description: States for the VM to be in. Please note that V(present), V(stopped) and V(restarted) operate on a VM that is currently provisioned. V(present) means that the VM is created if it was absent, and that it is in a running state. V(absent) shutdowns the zone before removing it. V(stopped) means the zone is created if it does not exist already, before shutting it down.

fields.label: tmpfs
fields.description: Amount of memory (in MiBs) that is available in the VM for the C(/tmp) filesystem.

fields.label: uuid
fields.description: UUID of the VM. Can either be a full UUID or V(*) for all VMs.

fields.label: vcpus
fields.description: Number of virtual CPUs for a KVM guest.

fields.label: vga
fields.description: Specify VGA emulation used by KVM VMs.

fields.label: virtio txburst
fields.description: Number of packets that can be sent in a single flush of the tx queue of virtio NICs.

fields.label: virtio txtimer
fields.description: Timeout (in nanoseconds) for the TX timer of virtio NICs.

fields.label: vnc password
fields.description: Password required to connect to VNC. By default no password is set. Please note this can be read from the Global Zone.

fields.label: vnc port
fields.description: TCP port to listen of the VNC server. Or set V(0) for random, or V(-1) to disable.

fields.label: zfs data compression
fields.description: Specifies compression algorithm used for this VMs data dataset. This option only has effect on delegated datasets.

fields.label: zfs data recsize
fields.description: Suggested block size (power of 2) for files in the delegated dataset's filesystem.

fields.label: zfs filesystem limit
fields.description: Maximum number of filesystems the VM can have.

fields.label: zfs io priority
fields.description: IO throttle priority value relative to other VMs.

fields.label: zfs root compression
fields.description: Specifies compression algorithm used for this VMs root dataset. This option only has effect on the zoneroot dataset.

fields.label: zfs root recsize
fields.description: Suggested block size (power of 2) for files in the zoneroot dataset's filesystem.

fields.label: zfs snapshot limit
fields.description: Number of snapshots the VM can have.

fields.label: zpool
fields.description: ZFS pool the VM's zone dataset is created in.

modules.label: wakeonlan
modules.tooltip: Send a magic Wake-on-LAN (WoL) broadcast packet

fields.label: broadcast
fields.description: Network broadcast address to use for broadcasting magic Wake-on-LAN packet.

fields.label: mac
fields.description: MAC address to send Wake-on-LAN broadcast packet for.

modules.label: wdc_redfish_command
modules.tooltip: Manages WDC UltraStar Data102 Out-Of-Band controllers using Redfish APIs

fields.label: auth token
fields.description: Security token for authentication with OOB controller.

fields.label: baseuri
fields.description: Base URI of OOB controller. Must include this or O(ioms).

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains a CA certificate to be used for validation. Only used if O(validate_certs=true).

fields.label: category
fields.description: Category to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: ciphers
fields.description: TLS/SSL Ciphers to use for the request. When a list is provided, all ciphers are joined in order with V(:). See the L(OpenSSL Cipher List Format,https://www.openssl.org/docs/manmaster/man1/openssl-ciphers.html#CIPHER-LIST-FORMAT) for more details. The available ciphers is dependent on the Python and OpenSSL/LibreSSL versions.

fields.label: command
fields.description: List of commands to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: ioms
fields.description: List of IOM FQDNs for the enclosure. Must include this or O(baseuri).

fields.label: resource id
fields.description: ID of the component to modify, such as V(Enclosure), V(IOModuleAFRU), V(PowerSupplyBFRU), V(FanExternalFRU3), or V(FanInternalFRU).

fields.label: update image uri
fields.description: The URI of the image for the update.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), TLS/SSL certificates are not validated. Set this to V(true) to enable certificate checking. Should be used together with O(ca_path).

modules.label: wdc_redfish_info
modules.tooltip: Manages WDC UltraStar Data102 Out-Of-Band controllers using Redfish APIs

fields.label: auth token
fields.description: Security token for authentication with OOB controller.

fields.label: baseuri
fields.description: Base URI of OOB controller. Must include this or O(ioms).

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains a CA certificate to be used for validation. Only used if O(validate_certs=true).

fields.label: category
fields.description: Category to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: ciphers
fields.description: TLS/SSL Ciphers to use for the request. When a list is provided, all ciphers are joined in order with V(:). See the L(OpenSSL Cipher List Format,https://www.openssl.org/docs/manmaster/man1/openssl-ciphers.html#CIPHER-LIST-FORMAT) for more details. The available ciphers is dependent on the Python and OpenSSL/LibreSSL versions.

fields.label: command
fields.description: List of commands to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: ioms
fields.description: List of IOM FQDNs for the enclosure. Must include this or O(baseuri).

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), TLS/SSL certificates are not validated. Set this to V(true) to enable certificate checking. Should be used together with O(ca_path).

fields.label: follow
fields.description: If V(true), dereferences symlinks and sets/gets attributes on symlink target, otherwise acts on symlink itself.

fields.label: key
fields.description: The name of a specific Extended attribute key to set/retrieve.

fields.label: namespace
fields.description: Namespace of the named name/key.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The full path of the file/object to get the facts of.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Defines which state you want to do. V(read) retrieves the current value for a O(key). V(present) sets O(path) to O(value), default if value is set. V(all) dumps all data. V(keys) retrieves all keys. V(absent) deletes the key.

fields.label: value
fields.description: The value to set the named name/key to, it automatically sets the O(state) to V(present).

fields.label: accept pubkey
fields.description: Whether or not repository signing keys should be automatically accepted.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the package to install, upgrade, or remove.

fields.label: recurse
fields.description: When removing a package, also remove its dependencies, provided that they are not required by other packages and were not explicitly installed by a user.

fields.label: repositories
fields.description: Repository URL(s) to prepend to the repository list for the package installation. The URL can be remote repositories or paths for local repositories.

fields.label: root
fields.description: The full path for the target root directory.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Desired state of the package.

fields.label: update cache
fields.description: Whether or not to refresh the master package lists. This can be run as part of a package installation or as a separate step.

fields.label: upgrade
fields.description: Whether or not to upgrade whole system.

fields.label: upgrade xbps
fields.description: Whether or not to upgrade the C(xbps) package when necessary. Before installing new packages, C(xbps) requires the user to update itself. Thus when this option is set to V(false), upgrades and installations fail when C(xbps) is not up to date.

modules.label: xcc_redfish_command
modules.tooltip: Manages Lenovo Out-Of-Band controllers using Redfish APIs

fields.label: auth token
fields.description: Security token for authentication with OOB controller.

fields.label: baseuri
fields.description: Base URI of OOB controller.

fields.label: ca path
fields.description: PEM formatted file that contains a CA certificate to be used for validation. Only used if O(validate_certs=true).

fields.label: category
fields.description: Category to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: ciphers
fields.description: TLS/SSL Ciphers to use for the request. When a list is provided, all ciphers are joined in order with V(:). See the L(OpenSSL Cipher List Format,https://www.openssl.org/docs/manmaster/man1/openssl-ciphers.html#CIPHER-LIST-FORMAT) for more details. The available ciphers is dependent on the Python and OpenSSL/LibreSSL versions.

fields.label: command
fields.description: List of commands to execute on OOB controller.

fields.label: request body
fields.description: The request body to patch or post.

fields.label: resource id
fields.description: The ID of the System, Manager or Chassis to modify.

fields.label: resource uri
fields.description: The resource URI to get or patch or post.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: If V(false), TLS/SSL certificates are not validated. Set this to V(true) to enable certificate checking. Should be used together with O(ca_path).

modules.label: xdg_mime
modules.tooltip: Set default handler for MIME types, for applications using XDG tools

fields.label: handler
fields.description: Sets the default handler for the specified MIME types. The desktop file must be installed in the system. If the desktop file is not installed, the module does not fail, but the handler is not set either. You must pass a handler in the form V(*.desktop), otherwise the module fails.

fields.label: mime types
fields.description: One or more MIME types for which a default handler is set.

modules.label: xenserver_guest
modules.tooltip: Manages virtual machines running on Citrix Hypervisor/XenServer host or pool

fields.label: folder
fields.description: Destination folder for VM. This parameter is case sensitive. Example: O(folder=/folder1/folder2).

fields.label: force
fields.description: Ignore warnings and complete the actions. This parameter is useful for removing VM in running state or reconfiguring VM params that require VM to be shut down.

fields.label: home server
fields.description: Name of a XenServer host that is a Home Server for the VM. This parameter is case sensitive.

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: The hostname or IP address of the XenServer host or XenServer pool master. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(XENSERVER_HOST) is used instead.

fields.label: is template
fields.description: Convert VM to template.

fields.label: linked clone
fields.description: Whether to create a Linked Clone from the template, existing VM or snapshot. If V(false), it creates a full copy. This is equivalent to C(Use storage-level fast disk clone) option in XenCenter.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the VM to work with. VMs running on XenServer do not necessarily have unique names. The module fails if multiple VMs with same name are found. In case of multiple VMs with same name, use O(uuid) to uniquely specify VM to manage. This parameter is case sensitive.

fields.label: name desc
fields.description: VM description.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Specify the state VM should be in. If O(state) is set to V(present) and VM exists, ensure the VM configuration conforms to given parameters. If O(state) is set to V(present) and VM does not exist, then VM is deployed with given parameters. If O(state) is set to V(absent) and VM exists, then VM is removed with its associated components. If O(state) is set to V(poweredon) and VM does not exist, then VM is deployed with given parameters and powered on automatically.

fields.label: state change timeout
fields.description: By default, the module waits indefinitely for VM to acquire an IP address if O(wait_for_ip_address=true). If this parameter is set to a positive value, the module instead waits the specified number of seconds for the state change. In case of timeout, module generates an error message.

fields.label: template
fields.description: Name of a template, an existing VM (must be shut down) or a snapshot that should be used to create VM. Templates/VMs/snapshots on XenServer do not necessarily have unique names. The module fails if multiple templates with same name are found. In case of multiple templates/VMs/snapshots with same name, use O(template_uuid) to uniquely specify source template. If VM already exists, this setting is ignored. This parameter is case sensitive.

fields.label: template uuid
fields.description: UUID of a template, an existing VM or a snapshot that should be used to create VM. It is required if template name is not unique.

fields.label: uuid
fields.description: UUID of the VM to manage if known. This is XenServer's unique identifier. It is required if name is not unique. Please note that a supplied UUID is ignored on VM creation, as XenServer creates the UUID internally.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Allows connection when SSL certificates are not valid. Set to V(false) when certificates are not trusted. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(XENSERVER_VALIDATE_CERTS) is used instead.

fields.label: wait for ip address
fields.description: Wait until XenServer detects an IP address for the VM. If O(state) is set to V(absent), this parameter is ignored. This requires XenServer Tools to be preinstalled on the VM to work properly.

modules.label: xenserver_guest_info
modules.tooltip: Gathers information for virtual machines running on Citrix Hypervisor/XenServer host or pool

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: The hostname or IP address of the XenServer host or XenServer pool master. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(XENSERVER_HOST) is used instead.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the VM to gather facts from. VMs running on XenServer do not necessarily have unique names. The module fails if multiple VMs with same name are found. In case of multiple VMs with same name, use O(uuid) to uniquely specify VM to manage. This parameter is case sensitive.

fields.label: uuid
fields.description: UUID of the VM to gather fact of. This is XenServer's unique identifier. It is required if name is not unique.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Allows connection when SSL certificates are not valid. Set to V(false) when certificates are not trusted. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(XENSERVER_VALIDATE_CERTS) is used instead.

modules.label: xenserver_guest_powerstate
modules.tooltip: Manages power states of virtual machines running on Citrix Hypervisor/XenServer host or pool

fields.label: hostname
fields.description: The hostname or IP address of the XenServer host or XenServer pool master. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(XENSERVER_HOST) is used instead.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the VM to manage. VMs running on XenServer do not necessarily have unique names. The module fails if multiple VMs with same name are found. In case of multiple VMs with same name, use O(uuid) to uniquely specify VM to manage. This parameter is case sensitive.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Specify the state VM should be in. If O(state) is set to value other than V(present), then VM is transitioned into required state and facts are returned. If O(state) is set to V(present), then VM is just checked for existence and facts are returned.

fields.label: state change timeout
fields.description: By default, module waits indefinitely for VM to change state or acquire an IP address if O(wait_for_ip_address=true). If this parameter is set to positive value, the module instead waits specified number of seconds for the state change. In case of timeout, module generates an error message.

fields.label: uuid
fields.description: UUID of the VM to manage if known. This is XenServer's unique identifier. It is required if name is not unique.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: Allows connection when SSL certificates are not valid. Set to V(false) when certificates are not trusted. If the value is not specified in the task, the value of environment variable E(XENSERVER_VALIDATE_CERTS) is used instead.

fields.label: wait for ip address
fields.description: Wait until XenServer detects an IP address for the VM. This requires XenServer Tools to be preinstalled on the VM to work properly.

fields.label: channel
fields.description: A Xfconf preference channel is a top-level tree key, inside of the Xfconf repository that corresponds to the location for which all application properties/keys are stored. See man xfconf-query(1).

fields.label: force array
fields.description: Force array even if only one element.

fields.label: property
fields.description: A Xfce preference key is an element in the Xfconf repository that corresponds to an application preference. See man xfconf-query(1).

fields.label: state
fields.description: The action to take upon the property/value. The state V(get) has been removed in community.general 5.0.0. Please use the module M(community.general.xfconf_info) instead. Xfce4 may, and usually does, have default values that come with the system packages. You can set new values for these default properties and override the their values. However, whey you use O(state=absent), the module executes the command C(xfconf-query reset) for the specified property and that only removes user-configured properties, so those properties are B(not removed), but rather they go back to the default values.

fields.label: value
fields.description: Preference properties typically have simple values such as strings, integers, or lists of strings and integers. See man xfconf-query(1).

fields.label: value type
fields.description: The type of value being set. When providing more than one O(value_type), the length of the list must be equal to the length of O(value). If only one O(value_type) is provided, but O(value) contains more than on element, that O(value_type) is applied to all elements of O(value). If the O(property) being set is an array and it can possibly have only one element in the array, then O(force_array=true) must be used to ensure that C(xfconf-query) interprets the value as an array rather than a scalar. Support for V(uchar), V(char), V(uint64), and V(int64) has been added in community.general 4.8.0.

fields.label: channel
fields.description: A Xfconf preference channel is a top-level tree key, inside of the Xfconf repository that corresponds to the location for which all application properties/keys are stored. If not provided, the module lists all available channels.

fields.label: property
fields.description: A Xfce preference key is an element in the Xfconf repository that corresponds to an application preference. If provided, then O(channel) is required. If not provided and a O(channel) is provided, then the module lists all available properties in that O(channel).

fields.label: bhard
fields.description: Hard blocks quota limit. This argument supports human readable sizes.

fields.label: bsoft
fields.description: Soft blocks quota limit. This argument supports human readable sizes.

fields.label: ihard
fields.description: Hard inodes quota limit.

fields.label: isoft
fields.description: Soft inodes quota limit.

fields.label: mountpoint
fields.description: The mount point on which to apply the quotas.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of the user, group or project to apply the quota to, if other than default.

fields.label: rtbhard
fields.description: Hard realtime blocks quota limit. This argument supports human readable sizes.

fields.label: rtbsoft
fields.description: Soft realtime blocks quota limit. This argument supports human readable sizes.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to apply the limits or remove them. When removing limit, they are set to 0, and not quite removed.

fields.label: type
fields.description: The XFS quota type.

modules.label: xml
modules.tooltip: Manage bits and pieces of XML files or strings

fields.label: add children
fields.description: Add additional child-element(s) to a selected element for a given O(xpath). Child elements must be given in a list and each item may be either a string (for example C(children=ansible) to add an empty C(<ansible/>) child element), or a hash where the key is an element name and the value is the element value. This parameter requires O(xpath) to be set.

fields.label: attribute
fields.description: The attribute to select when using parameter O(value). This is a string, not prepended with V(@).

fields.label: backup
fields.description: Create a backup file including the timestamp information so you can get the original file back if you somehow clobbered it incorrectly.

fields.label: content
fields.description: Search for a given O(xpath) and get content. This parameter requires O(xpath) to be set.

fields.label: count
fields.description: Search for a given O(xpath) and provide the count of any matches. This parameter requires O(xpath) to be set.

fields.label: create if missing
fields.description: When using O(value) and the O(xpath) matches no nodes, create the node. When set to V(false), a no-match is silently ignored instead of creating a new node.

fields.label: huge tree
fields.description: Disable libxml2 security restrictions on XML node size or document depth, allowing processing of very large XML files. This option should only be activated when needed, as it disables internal safety limits.

fields.label: input type
fields.description: Type of input for O(add_children) and O(set_children).

fields.label: insertafter
fields.description: Add additional child-element(s) after the last selected element for a given O(xpath). Child elements must be given in a list and each item may be either a string (for example C(children=ansible) to add an empty C(<ansible/>) child element), or a hash where the key is an element name and the value is the element value. This parameter requires O(xpath) to be set.

fields.label: insertbefore
fields.description: Add additional child-element(s) before the first selected element for a given O(xpath). Child elements must be given in a list and each item may be either a string (for example C(children=ansible) to add an empty C(<ansible/>) child element), or a hash where the key is an element name and the value is the element value. This parameter requires O(xpath) to be set.

fields.label: namespaces
fields.description: The namespace C(prefix:uri) mapping for the XPath expression. Needs to be a C(dict), not a C(list) of items.

fields.label: path
fields.description: Path to the file to operate on. This file must exist ahead of time. This parameter is required, unless O(xmlstring) is given.

fields.label: pretty print
fields.description: Pretty print XML output.

fields.label: print match
fields.description: Search for a given O(xpath) and print out any matches. This parameter requires O(xpath) to be set.

fields.label: set children
fields.description: Set the child-element(s) of a selected element for a given O(xpath). Removes any existing children. Child elements must be specified as in O(add_children). This parameter requires O(xpath) to be set.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Set or remove an xpath selection (node(s), attribute(s)).

fields.label: strip cdata tags
fields.description: Remove CDATA tags surrounding text values. Note that this might break your XML file if text values contain characters that could be interpreted as XML.

fields.label: value
fields.description: Desired state of the selected attribute. Either a string, or to unset a value, the Python V(None) keyword (YAML Equivalent, V(null)). Elements default to no value (but present). Attributes default to an empty string.

fields.label: xmlstring
fields.description: A string containing XML on which to operate. This parameter is required, unless O(path) is given.

fields.label: xpath
fields.description: A valid XPath expression describing the item(s) you want to manipulate. Operates on the document root, V(/), by default.

fields.label: executable
fields.description: The executable location for yarn.

fields.label: global
fields.description: Install the Node.js library globally.

fields.label: ignore scripts
fields.description: Use the C(--ignore-scripts) flag when installing.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The name of a Node.js library to install. If omitted all packages in package.json are installed. To globally install from local Node.js library. Prepend C(file:) to the path of the Node.js library.

fields.label: path
fields.description: The base path where Node.js installs libraries. This is where the C(node_modules) folder lives.

fields.label: production
fields.description: Install dependencies in production mode. C(yarn) ignores any dependencies under devDependencies in C(package.json).

fields.label: registry
fields.description: The registry to install modules from.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Installation state of the named Node.js library. If V(absent) is selected, a O(name) option must be provided.

fields.label: version
fields.description: The version of the library to be installed. Must be in semver format. If "latest" is desired, use O(state) arg instead.

modules.label: yum_versionlock
modules.tooltip: Locks / unlocks a installed package(s) from being updated by yum package manager

fields.label: name
fields.description: Package name or a list of package names with optional version or wildcards. Specifying versions is supported since community.general 7.2.0.

fields.label: state
fields.description: If state is V(present), package(s) is/are added to yum versionlock list. If state is V(absent), package(s) is/are removed from yum versionlock list.

fields.label: extra zfs properties
fields.description: A dictionary of zfs properties to be set. See the zfs(8) man page for more information.

fields.label: name
fields.description: File system, snapshot or volume name, for example V(rpool/myfs).

fields.label: origin
fields.description: Snapshot from which to create a clone.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to create (V(present)), or remove (V(absent)) a file system, snapshot or volume. All parents/children are created/destroyed as needed to reach the desired state.

modules.label: zfs_delegate_admin
modules.tooltip: Manage ZFS delegated administration (user admin privileges)

fields.label: descendents
fields.description: Apply permissions to O(name)'s descendents (C(zfs allow -d)).

fields.label: everyone
fields.description: Apply permissions to everyone.

fields.label: groups
fields.description: List of groups to whom permission(s) should be granted.

fields.label: local
fields.description: Apply permissions to O(name) locally (C(zfs allow -l)).

fields.label: name
fields.description: File system or volume name, for example V(rpool/myfs).

fields.label: permissions
fields.description: The list of permission(s) to delegate (required if O(state=present)). Supported permissions depend on the ZFS version in use. See for example U(https://openzfs.github.io/openzfs-docs/man/8/zfs-allow.8.html) for OpenZFS.

fields.label: recursive
fields.description: Unallow permissions recursively (ignored when O(state=present)).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to allow (V(present)), or unallow (V(absent)) a permission. When set to V(present), at least one "entity" param of O(users), O(groups), or O(everyone) are required. When set to V(absent), removes permissions from the specified entities, or removes all permissions if no entity params are specified.

fields.label: users
fields.description: List of users to whom permission(s) should be granted.

fields.label: depth
fields.description: Specifies recursion depth.

fields.label: name
fields.description: ZFS dataset name.

fields.label: parsable
fields.description: Specifies if property values should be displayed in machine friendly format.

fields.label: properties
fields.description: Specifies which dataset properties should be queried in comma-separated format. For more information about dataset properties, check zfs(1M) man page.

fields.label: recurse
fields.description: Specifies if properties for any children should be recursively displayed.

fields.label: type
fields.description: Specifies which datasets types to display. Multiple values have to be provided as a list or in comma-separated form. Value V(all) cannot be used together with other values.

modules.label: znode
modules.tooltip: Create, delete, retrieve, and update znodes using ZooKeeper

fields.label: auth credential
fields.description: The authentication credential value. Depends on O(auth_scheme). The format for O(auth_scheme=digest) is C(user:password), and the format for O(auth_scheme=sasl) is C(user:password).

fields.label: auth scheme
fields.description: Authentication scheme.

fields.label: hosts
fields.description: A list of ZooKeeper servers (format V([server]:[port])).

fields.label: name
fields.description: The path of the znode.

fields.label: op
fields.description: An operation to perform. Mutually exclusive with state.

fields.label: recursive
fields.description: Recursively delete node and all its children.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The state to enforce. Mutually exclusive with op.

fields.label: use tls
fields.description: Using TLS/SSL or not.

fields.label: value
fields.description: The value assigned to the znode.

fields.label: altroot
fields.description: Alternate root for mounting filesystems.

fields.label: disable new features
fields.description: If V(true), disable new ZFS feature flags when creating.

fields.label: filesystem properties
fields.description: Dictionary of ZFS filesystem properties to set on the root dataset (for example V(compression), V(dedup)).

fields.label: force
fields.description: If V(true), force operations (for example overwrite existing devices).

fields.label: mountpoint
fields.description: Filesystem mountpoint for the root dataset.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name of the zpool to manage.

fields.label: pool properties
fields.description: Dictionary of ZFS pool properties to set (for example V(autoexpand), V(cachefile)).

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the pool should exist.

fields.label: temp name
fields.description: Temporary name used during pool creation.

fields.label: name
fields.description: ZFS pool name.

fields.label: parsable
fields.description: Specifies if property values should be displayed in machine friendly format.

fields.label: properties
fields.description: Specifies which dataset properties should be queried in comma-separated format. For more information about dataset properties, check zpool(1M) man page.

modules.label: zypper
modules.tooltip: Manage packages on SUSE and openSUSE

fields.label: allow vendor change
fields.description: Adds C(--allow_vendor_change) option to I(zypper) dist-upgrade command.

fields.label: auto import keys
fields.description: Whether to automatically import new repository signing keys. Adds C(--gpg-auto-import-keys) option to I(zypper). Is only used when installing.

fields.label: clean deps
fields.description: Adds C(--clean-deps) option to I(zypper) remove command.

fields.label: disable gpg check
fields.description: Whether to disable to GPG signature checking of the package signature being installed. Has an effect only if O(state) is V(present) or V(latest).

fields.label: disable recommends
fields.description: Corresponds to the C(--no-recommends) option for I(zypper). Default behavior (V(true)) modifies zypper's default behavior; V(false) does install recommended packages.

fields.label: extra args
fields.description: Add additional options to C(zypper) command. Options should be supplied in a single line as if given in the command line.

fields.label: extra args precommand
fields.description: Add additional global target options to C(zypper). Options should be supplied in a single line as if given in the command line.

fields.label: force
fields.description: Adds C(--force) option to I(zypper). Allows to downgrade packages and change vendor or architecture.

fields.label: force resolution
fields.description: Adds C(--force-resolution) option to I(zypper). Allows to (un)install packages with conflicting requirements (resolver chooses a solution).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Package name V(name) or package specifier or a list of either. Can include a version like V(name=1.0), V(name>3.4) or V(name<=2.7). If a version is given, V(oldpackage) is implied and zypper is allowed to update the package within the version range given. You can also pass a URL or a local path to a rpm file. When using O(state=latest), this can be V(*), which updates all installed packages.

fields.label: oldpackage
fields.description: Adds C(--oldpackage) option to I(zypper). Allows to downgrade packages with less side-effects than force. This is implied as soon as a version is specified as part of the package name.

fields.label: quiet
fields.description: Adds C(--quiet) option to I(zypper) install/update command.

fields.label: replacefiles
fields.description: Adds C(--replacefiles) option to I(zypper) install/update command.

fields.label: simple errors
fields.description: When set to V(true), provide a simplified error output (parses only the C(<message>) tag text in the XML output).

fields.label: skip post errors
fields.description: When set to V(true), ignore I(zypper) return code 107 (post install script errors).

fields.label: state
fields.description: V(present) makes sure the package is installed. V(latest) makes sure the latest version of the package is installed. V(absent) makes sure the specified package is not installed. V(dist-upgrade) makes sure the latest version of all installed packages from all enabled repositories is installed. When using V(dist-upgrade), O(name) should be V(*).

fields.label: type
fields.description: The type of package to be operated on.

fields.label: update cache
fields.description: Run the equivalent of C(zypper refresh) before the operation. Disabled in check mode.

fields.label: auto import keys
fields.description: Automatically import the gpg signing key of the new or changed repository. Has an effect only if O(state=present). Has no effect on existing (unchanged) repositories or in combination with O(state=absent). Implies O(runrefresh). Only works with C(.repo) files if O(name) is given explicitly.

fields.label: autorefresh
fields.description: Enable autorefresh of the repository. When not specified, the value from the C(.repo) file is used if available, otherwise it defaults to V(true).

fields.label: description
fields.description: A description of the repository.

fields.label: disable gpg check
fields.description: Whether to disable GPG signature checking of all packages. Has an effect only if O(state=present). Needs C(zypper) version >= 1.6.2. When not specified, the value from the C(.repo) file is used if available, otherwise it defaults to V(false).

fields.label: enabled
fields.description: Set repository to enabled (or disabled). When not specified, the value from the C(.repo) file is used if available, otherwise it defaults to V(true).

fields.label: name
fields.description: A name for the repository. Not required when adding repofiles.

fields.label: overwrite multiple
fields.description: Overwrite multiple repository entries, if repositories with both name and URL already exist.

fields.label: priority
fields.description: Set priority of repository. Packages are always installed from the repository with the smallest priority number. Needs C(zypper) version >= 1.12.25.

fields.label: repo
fields.description: URI of the repository or full path of a C(.repo) file. Required when O(state=present).

fields.label: runrefresh
fields.description: Refresh the package list of the given repository. Can be used with O(repo=*) to refresh all repositories.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the repository should exist or not. A source string state.
